name: CNC Milling — Cutting Force, Specific Cutting Energy & Spindle Power
subtitle: CNC Machining / Metal Cutting — Sandvik Coromant C-2920 2024, ISO 513:2012 (cutting tool classification), CIRP Annals Vol.57 (2008) §2

inScope: CNC Milling — Cutting Force, Specific Cutting Energy & Spindle Power
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Cutting Speed (V_c)
inputs.description: From Sandvik C-2920 Table 1 or tool manufacturer data for workpiece/tool material combination

inputs.label: Specific Cutting Force at 1 mm² Chip Area (k_c1)
inputs.description: Sandvik C-2920: Steel 0.2C → k_c1=1900; Stainless 316L → 2700; Ti6Al4V → 1700; Al7075 → 700 MPa

inputs.label: Chip Thickness Exponent (m_c)
inputs.description: Sandvik: steel = 0.22–0.27; cast iron = 0.26; aluminium = 0.25

standards.name: CNC Milling — Cutting Force, Specific Cutting Energy & Spindle Power
standards.description: CNC Milling — Cutting Force, Specific Cutting Energy & Spindle Power — CNC Machining / Metal Cutting

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: ae_mm > D_mm
fmea.effect: Radial depth a_e cannot exceed cutter diameter D_c — reduce a_e or select larger cutter
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Pc_kw > 50
fmea.effect: Spindle power demand > 50 kW: verify machine spindle rating; add 25% mechanical loss for machine requirement
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: hm_mm < 0.01
fmea.effect: Mean chip thickness < 0.01 mm: rubbing regime — built-up edge and tool wear risk; increase f_z or a_e
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: a_e/D_c > 70%: full or near-full engagement — tool deflection and chatter risk severe; reduce radial depth to ≤ 50% and use finishing pass strategy
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: ae_mm / D_mm > 0.7

fmea.failureMode: Specific cutting force > 3500 MPa: hard material or worn tool — verify k_c1 input and check for built-up edge; consider coated carbide grade
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: kc_mpa > 3500

fmea.failureMode: Spindle speed > 20,000 rpm: HSM regime — verify spindle bearing rating, tool balance (ISO 1940 G2.5), and coolant pressure capacity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: n_rpm > 20000

fmea.failureMode: Table feed > 5000 mm/min: high-feed machining — verify CNC controller acceleration and servo drive current limits
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Vf_mm_min > 5000

fmea.failureMode: Deep axial cut with many teeth: high chip volume — verify chip evacuation; coolant-through-tool strongly recommended
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: ap_mm > D_mm * 1.5 && z_teeth > 4

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: CNC Turning — Cutting Force, MRR, Surface Roughness & Tool Life Estimation
subtitle: CNC Machining / Metal Cutting — Sandvik Coromant C-2920 2024, ISO 3685:1993 (tool life), ISO 4287:1997 (surface texture)

inScope: CNC Turning — Cutting Force, MRR, Surface Roughness & Tool Life Estimation
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Specific Cutting Force (k_c1) at h=1 mm
Insert Nose Radius (r_ε)
inputs.description: ISO 1832 insert designation — 4th character; R = 0.4 → standard; R = 0.8 → medium

inputs.label: Taylor Tool Life Constant (C) at Reference Conditions
inputs.description: From Sandvik TMMS or manufacturer data; steel P15 grade: C ≈ 180–250 m/min at n=0.25

inputs.label: Taylor Exponent (n)
inputs.description: Carbide turning steel: n=0.20–0.30; ceramic: n=0.40–0.50; CBN hardened steel: n=0.45

standards.name: CNC Turning — Cutting Force, MRR, Surface Roughness & Tool Life Estimation
standards.description: CNC Turning — Cutting Force, MRR, Surface Roughness & Tool Life Estimation — CNC Machining / Metal Cutting

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: T_min < 5
fmea.effect: Tool life < 5 min: rapid tool wear regime — reduce V_c by 20% or select higher-grade insert coating (PVD AlTiN, CVD Al₂O₃)
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Ra_um > 6.3
fmea.effect: Theoretical Ra > 6.3 µm: rough finish — reduce feed rate or increase nose radius for finish passes
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Pc_kw > 80
fmea.effect: Cutting power > 80 kW: verify lathe spindle motor rating; excessive power causes thermal distortion in workpiece
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Tool life < 10 min: uneconomical — optimise V_c using Taylor equation; target T = 15–20 min for productive insert change interval
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_min < 10

fmea.failureMode: High feed with low Ra requirement conflict — theoretical roughness model predicts Ra > specification; reduce f or apply wiper insert geometry
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: f_mm_rev > 1.0 && Ra_um < 3.2

fmea.failureMode: Low speed + high k_c: built-up edge risk on sticky materials — increase V_c above BUE threshold or apply coated grade with sharp edge prep
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Vc_m_min < 50 && kc_mpa > 3000

fmea.failureMode: MRR > 500 cm³/min: heavy roughing — verify workpiece fixture rigidity and coolant pressure; chip control critical at high MRR
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: MRR_cm3_min > 500

fmea.failureMode: Deep cut with fine feed: check radial force F_p — deep cuts generate large passive forces that deflect thin workpieces; verify L/D ratio ≤ 4
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: ap_mm > 10 && f_mm_rev < 0.2

name: OEE & TPM Analysis — Availability, Performance, Quality & Six Big Loss Breakdown
subtitle: Manufacturing Operations / Lean / TPM — ISO 22400-2:2014, SEMI E10-0304, JIPM TPM Excellence Award Criteria

inScope: OEE & TPM Analysis — Availability, Performance, Quality & Six Big Loss Breakdown
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

standards.name: OEE & TPM Analysis — Availability, Performance, Quality & Six Big Loss Breakdown
standards.description: OEE & TPM Analysis — Availability, Performance, Quality & Six Big Loss Breakdown — Manufacturing Operations / Lean / TPM

formulas.expression: OT_h = planned_production_time_h - downtime_unplanned_h - downtime_setup_h; A = OT_h / planned_production_time_h
good_parts = total_parts_produced - rejected_parts - rework_parts; Q = good_parts / total_parts_produced
WC_OEE = 0.85; actual_good = good_parts; wc_good = Math.round(total_parts_produced * WC_OEE / OEE); parts_lost = wc_good - actual_good
calendar_hours = 24 * Math.ceil(planned_production_time_h / 8); utilisation = planned_production_time_h / calendar_hours; TEEP = OEE * utilisation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: downtime_unplanned_h + downtime_setup_h > planned_production_time_h
fmea.effect: Total downtime exceeds planned production time — check hours input
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: rejected_parts + rework_parts > total_parts_produced
fmea.effect: Defect + rework count exceeds total production — verify part counts
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: P > 1.05
fmea.effect: Performance rate > 105%: physically implausible — verify ideal cycle time; it may be set too conservative
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: OEE < 65%: below minimum acceptable threshold — initiate full Six Big Loss analysis; priority losses: breakdowns (Loss 1) and setup time (Loss 2)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: OEE < 0.65

fmea.failureMode: Availability < 80%: unplanned downtime dominant loss — implement Planned Maintenance (PM Step 3) and predictive maintenance programme
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: A < 0.80

fmea.failureMode: Quality rate < 95%: >5% defect/rework — conduct root cause analysis per 8D; check tooling wear schedule and incoming material certification
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Q < 0.95

fmea.failureMode: Performance < 85%: speed loss and minor stoppages — install cycle time monitoring; target micro-stoppage elimination through Autonomous Maintenance (AM Step 2)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: P < 0.85

fmea.failureMode: TEEP < 50%: significant unused capacity — evaluate shift extension or weekend running before CAPEX investment per JIPM TPM §6
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: TEEP < 0.50

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: VDI 2230 Bolted Joint — Tightening Torque, Preload Loss & Fatigue Safety Factor
subtitle: Mechanical Design / Bolted Connections — VDI 2230:2015 Part 1, ISO 898-1:2013, DIN 946:1990 (friction coefficients)

inScope: VDI 2230 Bolted Joint — Tightening Torque, Preload Loss & Fatigue Safety Factor
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Thread & Under-Head Friction Coefficient (μ_total)
inputs.description: VDI 2230 Table A5: dry steel = 0.14–0.18; MoS₂ = 0.06–0.10; zinc-phosphate = 0.09–0.14

inputs.label: Bolt Utilisation at Assembly (% of proof load)
inputs.description: VDI 2230 §5.3.1: controlled torque tightening → 90%; torque-angle → 95–100%

inputs.label: Expected Embedding / Relaxation Loss (% of initial preload)
inputs.description: VDI 2230 Table 5: machined surfaces = 5–10%; rough cast = 15–25%; plastic inserter = 20%

inputs.label: External Axial Working Load per Bolt (F_A)
Load Introduction Factor (Φ)
inputs.description: VDI 2230 §4.2: concentric bolt through long joint → Φ≈0.1; direct shear plane → Φ≈0.5

standards.name: VDI 2230 Bolted Joint — Tightening Torque, Preload Loss & Fatigue Safety Factor
standards.description: VDI 2230 Bolted Joint — Tightening Torque, Preload Loss & Fatigue Safety Factor — Mechanical Design / Bolted Connections

formulas.expression: const d = parseFloat(bolt_size.replace('M','')); const p = {M6:1.0,M8:1.25,M10:1.5,M12:1.75,M16:2.0,M20:2.5,M24:3.0,M30:3.5}[bolt_size]; MA_Nm = FM_kn * 1000 * (p/(2*Math.PI) + mu_friction * d * 0.583 + mu_friction * d * 0.5) / 1000
dFA_kn = phi_force_ratio * FA_kn; sigma_a_mpa = dFA_kn * 1000 / A_s

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: SA < 1.0
fmea.effect: Joint separation: clamp force exhausted by working load — increase preload (larger bolt, higher class), reduce F_A, or increase bolt count
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: FM_kn > F_proof_kn
fmea.effect: Assembly preload exceeds proof load: bolt will yield on tightening — reduce utilisation to max 95% or select 10.9/12.9 class
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: SA < 1.2
fmea.effect: Separation safety factor < 1.2: marginal joint — VDI 2230 minimum S_A = 1.2 for reliable sealing and fatigue resistance
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Bolt fatigue amplitude > 50 MPa: high fatigue loading — check against VDI 2230 Haigh diagram; apply thread-rolling (fatigue limit improvement ~30%) or increase bolt diameter
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: sigma_a_mpa > 50

fmea.failureMode: Very low friction coefficient: self-loosening risk in vibration environments — evaluate NORD-LOCK washers or anaerobic adhesive per VDI 2230 §3.5
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: mu_friction < 0.08

fmea.failureMode: 12.9 class with low friction: hydrogen embrittlement risk in acidic environments — verify plating and service environment compatibility per ISO 4042
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: property_class === '12.9' && mu_friction < 0.10

fmea.failureMode: High embedding loss (>15%): rough or soft interface — machine mating surfaces or add hardened washer to reduce embedding; re-torque protocol recommended after 48h
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: preload_loss_pct > 15

fmea.failureMode: Low utilisation (<70%): bolt is underloaded — susceptible to fatigue and loosening; increase preload per VDI 2230 §5.3 or reduce to smaller bolt diameter
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: utilisation_pct < 70

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: WPS Preheat Temperature & PWHT Requirements — Carbon Equivalent & Hydrogen Risk
subtitle: Welding Engineering / WPS / PQR — AWS D1.1-2020 §3.5, Table 3.2, EN 1011-2:2001 Annex C, ISO 13916:2017

inScope: WPS Preheat Temperature & PWHT Requirements — Carbon Equivalent & Hydrogen Risk
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Carbon Content (C)
inputs.description: From mill certificate; structural steel: 0.10–0.25%; HSLA: 0.05–0.18%

inputs.label: Welding Heat Input (HI)
inputs.description: HI = U × I × η / (1000 × v); SMAW η=0.80; GMAW η=0.80; SAW η=0.99

standards.name: WPS Preheat Temperature & PWHT Requirements — Carbon Equivalent & Hydrogen Risk
standards.description: WPS Preheat Temperature & PWHT Requirements — Carbon Equivalent & Hydrogen Risk — Welding Engineering / WPS / PQR

formulas.expression: PWHT_required = (CE_IIW > 0.45 && plate_thickness_mm > 32) || (plate_thickness_mm > 38 && property === 'Class D/E') ? 'PWHT REQUIRED per ASME IX / AWS D1.1 §5.8' : 'PWHT not mandatory — verify per design code'

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: CE_IIW > 0.65
fmea.effect: CE_IIW > 0.65: very high hardenability — cold cracking risk is severe; mandatory H2.5 consumables, preheat ≥ 150°C, and post-heat treatment required
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: T_p_governing_c > 250
fmea.effect: Preheat temperature > 250°C: verify interpass temperature control ≤ 300°C — excessive interpass causes heat-affected zone toughness loss per EN 1011-2
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: HI_kj_mm < 0.5 && CE_IIW > 0.45
fmea.effect: Very low heat input on hardenable steel: rapid cooling rate — HAZ hardness may exceed 350 HV; increase HI or raise preheat to slow cooling
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: CE > 0.50 + H_d > 10 ml/100g: hydrogen-assisted cold cracking risk — switch to H5 or H2.5 consumables immediately; minimum 48h inspection delay before NDT
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: CE_IIW > 0.50 && H_content > 10

fmea.failureMode: High preheat + high heat input: creep in HAZ possible during multipass welding — implement interpass temperature monitoring ≤ T_p + 100°C
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_p_governing_c > 150 && HI_kj_mm > 3.0

fmea.failureMode: Heavy section with low calculated preheat: heat sink effect underestimated — add 25°C for plates > 50 mm per AWS D1.1 Commentary §C-3.5
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: plate_thickness_mm > 50 && T_p_governing_c < 100

fmea.failureMode: CET > 0.45: HSLA or alloy steel with elevated cold cracking susceptibility — document in WPS and PQR; hydrogen bake-out at 200°C × 2h strongly recommended
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: CET > 0.45

fmea.failureMode: Standard hydrogen consumable on alloy steel: upgrade to H10 minimum — AWS D1.1 Table 4.5 restricts H15 for CE > 0.40 without mandatory elevated preheat
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: H_content === 15 && CE_IIW > 0.40

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Capital Investment Appraisal — NPV, IRR, Payback & Profitability Index
subtitle: Financial Engineering / Project Finance — ICMA Financial Appraisal Guide, ISO 15686-5:2017 (LCC), IAS 36 Impairment

inScope: Capital Investment Appraisal — NPV, IRR, Payback & Profitability Index
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Discount Rate / WACC (r)
inputs.description: WACC = weighted cost of equity + debt; industrial: typically 8–15%; emerging markets: 12–20%

inputs.label: Terminal / Salvage Value at End of Life
Annual Cash Flow Escalation Rate (inflation adj.)

standards.name: Capital Investment Appraisal — NPV, IRR, Payback & Profitability Index
standards.description: Capital Investment Appraisal — NPV, IRR, Payback & Profitability Index — Financial Engineering / Project Finance

formulas.expression: r = discount_rate_pct / 100; g = annual_escalation_pct / 100; PVA = (Math.abs(g - r) < 0.001) ? CF_after_tax_usd * project_life_yr / (1+r) : CF_after_tax_usd * (1 - Math.pow((1+g)/(1+r), project_life_yr)) / (r - g); PV_salvage = salvage_value_usd / Math.pow(1+r, project_life_yr); NPV_usd = PVA + PV_salvage - initial_investment_usd

let irr = 0.10; for(let i=0;i<1000;i++){let f=-initial_investment_usd; let df=0; for(let t=1;t<=project_life_yr;t++){let cf=CF_after_tax_usd*Math.pow(1+annual_escalation_pct/100,t-1); f+=cf/Math.pow(1+irr,t); df-=t*cf/Math.pow(1+irr,t+1);} if(t===project_life_yr) f+=salvage_value_usd/Math.pow(1+irr,project_life_yr); let di=f/df; irr-=di; if(Math.abs(di)<1e-8) break;} IRR_pct = irr * 100

let cumPV = 0; let DPBP = project_life_yr; for(let t=1; t<=project_life_yr; t++){cumPV += CF_after_tax_usd * Math.pow(1 + annual_escalation_pct/100, t-1) / Math.pow(1 + discount_rate_pct/100, t); if(cumPV >= initial_investment_usd){DPBP = t - 1 + (initial_investment_usd - (cumPV - CF_after_tax_usd / Math.pow(1+discount_rate_pct/100, t))) / (CF_after_tax_usd / Math.pow(1+discount_rate_pct/100, t)); break;}} DPBP_yr = DPBP

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: NPV_usd < 0
fmea.effect: Negative NPV: project destroys value at given WACC — evaluate cost reduction, revenue enhancement, or alternative investment
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: IRR_pct < discount_rate_pct
fmea.effect: IRR < WACC: project return insufficient — do not proceed unless strategic rationale overrides financial return
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: PBP_yr > project_life_yr
fmea.effect: Payback period exceeds project life: investment never recovers in nominal terms — re-examine cost structure or financing
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: IRR > 50%: unusually high — verify cash flow projections for optimism bias; conduct sensitivity analysis on key revenue assumptions
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: IRR_pct > 50

fmea.failureMode: Discounted payback > 70% of project life: late-life cash flow dependence — project is sensitive to abandonment risk; assess terminal value robustness
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DPBP_yr > project_life_yr * 0.7

fmea.failureMode: Profitability Index 1.0–1.1: marginal value creation — compare alternative uses of capital; opportunity cost may be higher
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: PI < 1.1

fmea.failureMode: High escalation assumption (>5%): NPV is sensitive to inflation forecast — run downside at g=0% and upside at g=3% for scenario range
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: annual_escalation_pct > 5

fmea.failureMode: Large salvage value (>30% of CAPEX): NPV materially dependent on terminal residual — verify asset market and contractual exit options before committing
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: salvage_value_usd > initial_investment_usd * 0.3

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Pump NPSH Available, Cavitation Index & System Head Curve Analysis
subtitle: Fluid Systems / Pump Engineering — API 610 12th Ed. §6.1.14, ANSI/HI 9.6.1-2012, ISO 9906:2012 §6.4

inScope: Pump NPSH Available, Cavitation Index & System Head Curve Analysis
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Atmospheric Pressure at Installation (P_atm)
inputs.description: Sea level = 1.013 bar; altitude 1000m ≈ 0.899 bar; altitude 2000m ≈ 0.795 bar

inputs.label: Liquid Vapour Pressure at Pumping Temperature (P_v)
Pump Required NPSH (NPSHr) from Manufacturer Curve
inputs.description: At operating flow point from pump data sheet; API 610 NPSHr = 3% head drop criterion

inputs.label: Total Pipe Friction & Minor Losses at Design Flow (H_f)

standards.name: Pump NPSH Available, Cavitation Index & System Head Curve Analysis
standards.description: Pump NPSH Available, Cavitation Index & System Head Curve Analysis — Fluid Systems / Pump Engineering

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: NPSHa_m < NPSHr_m
fmea.effect: NPSHa < NPSHr: cavitation will occur — pump will cavitate, causing impeller erosion and vibration; lower pump elevation or reduce suction losses immediately
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: NPSHa_m < 0
fmea.effect: NPSHa ≤ 0: liquid will flash in suction — verify vapour pressure and elevation inputs; flood suction (pump below tank) required
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: MR < 1.1
fmea.effect: NPSH margin < 10% (API 610 minimum): cavitation damage risk — increase suction pipe diameter, reduce suction losses, or lower pump installation elevation
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Low NPSH margin on hot/volatile liquid: API 610 requires minimum 1.3× margin for T > 65°C or volatile hydrocarbons — redesign suction system
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: MR < 1.3 && P_vapour_bar > 0.2

fmea.failureMode: Suction lift > 8 m: practical centrifugal pump limit in most water applications — consider submersible pump or lower pump installation
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Z_s_m < -8

fmea.failureMode: Suction losses > 50% of NPSHr: suction pipe undersized — upsize suction pipe by one nominal size or eliminate elbows; target h_Ls < 0.3×NPSHr
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: hL_suction_m > NPSHr_m * 0.5

fmea.failureMode: Low atmospheric pressure (altitude > 1500 m): NPSHa significantly reduced — altitude correction mandatory per HI 9.6.1 §2.3 for installations above 1500 m ASL
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: P_atm_bar < 0.85

fmea.failureMode: Thoma σ < 0.10: pump operating near cavitation onset on system curve — verify cavitation-free zone on pump curve and consider variable speed operation
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: sigma < 0.10

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Steel Column Axial Compression — Slenderness, Critical Stress & Euler Buckling Load
subtitle: Structural Engineering / Steel Design — AISC 360-22 Chapter E, EN 1993-1-1:2022 §6.3.1, ASCE 7-22 load combinations

inScope: Steel Column Axial Compression — Slenderness, Critical Stress & Euler Buckling Load
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Effective Column Length (KL)
inputs.description: Effective length = K × L; AISC Commentary C-C2.2: pinned-pinned K=1.0; fixed-pinned K=0.7; fixed-fixed K=0.5

inputs.label: Minimum Radius of Gyration (r_min)
inputs.description: From section tables; r_y for wide-flange; r_min for angle — typically controls buckling

inputs.label: End Condition — Effective Length Factor (K)
inputs.description: Used if KL not pre-calculated; L_eff = K × physical length

standards.name: Steel Column Axial Compression — Slenderness, Critical Stress & Euler Buckling Load
standards.description: Steel Column Axial Compression — Slenderness, Critical Stress & Euler Buckling Load — Structural Engineering / Steel Design

formulas.expression: lambda_c = Math.sqrt(Fy_mpa / Fe_mpa); Fcr_mpa = (lambda_c <= 1.5) ? Math.pow(0.658, lambda_c * lambda_c) * Fy_mpa : (0.877 / (lambda_c * lambda_c)) * Fy_mpa
I_min_mm4 = A_g_mm2 * Math.pow(r_min_mm, 2); Pcr_kn = Math.pow(Math.PI, 2) * 200000 * I_min_mm4 / Math.pow(L_eff_m * 1000, 2) / 1000

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: DCR > 1.0
fmea.effect: Column overstressed: axial demand exceeds φP_n — increase section area, reduce effective length, or reduce axial load
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: KL_r > 200
fmea.effect: KL/r > 200: exceeds AISC 360 maximum slenderness limit for compression members — add intermediate bracing or select deeper section
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Fe_mpa < Fy_mpa * 0.5
fmea.effect: Elastic buckling stress < 50% of yield: slender column dominated by buckling — verify effective length assumptions and add lateral bracing
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: DCR > 90%: minimal reserve capacity — no additional loads or load combination factors can be accommodated; redesign before construction
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DCR > 0.90

fmea.failureMode: Very slender column (KL/r > 150): highly sensitive to eccentricity and initial imperfections — apply additional amplification per AISC Appendix 8 or second-order analysis
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: KL_r > 150

fmea.failureMode: High-strength steel with moderate slenderness: F_cr may be governed by elastic buckling, not yield — verify cost benefit of S460 vs S355 at this slenderness level
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Fy_mpa > 355 && KL_r > 80

fmea.failureMode: Applied load > 80% of Euler critical load: very close to elastic bifurcation point — P-delta effects and geometric imperfections become critical; perform second-order analysis
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Pu_kn > Pcr_kn * 0.8

fmea.failureMode: Cantilever column (K=2.0) over 5 m: very high effective length — verify fixed base connection rotational stiffness; partial fixity significantly increases effective K
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: end_condition === 2.0 && L_eff_m > 5

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Pressure Vessel Wall Thickness Sizing (ASME VIII / PED)
subtitle: Mechanical Design / Pressure Vessels — ASME VIII Div.1, ASME VIII UG-27, ASME VIII UG-99

inScope: Pressure Vessel Wall Thickness Sizing (ASME VIII / PED)
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

standards.name: Pressure Vessel Wall Thickness Sizing (ASME VIII / PED)
standards.description: Pressure Vessel Wall Thickness Sizing (ASME VIII / PED) — Mechanical Design / Pressure Vessels

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: design_pressure_mpa >= (0.385 * allowable_stress_mpa * joint_efficiency_val)
fmea.effect: Thin wall formula invalid: P >= 0.385*S*E threshold violated. Thick wall (Lamé) equation must be applied (ASME VIII UG-27 footnote).
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: corrosion_allowance_mm === 0 && design_temp_c > 50
fmea.effect: High temperature + zero corrosion allowance: corrosion cannot be neglected in services >50°C. Check API 571 damage mechanisms.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: design_temp_c > 400 && allowable_stress_mpa > 140
fmea.effect: Above 400°C, the allowable stress for SA-516 Gr.70 drops below 138 MPa. Use ASME Sec. II Part D temperature tables.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: hydro_test_factor < 1.3
fmea.effect: ASME VIII UG-99: Hydrostatic test factor must be minimum 1.3. This is a regulatory requirement.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Low corrosion allowance: CA < 1.5mm. Minimum 3mm is recommended for general service. Increase to 6mm if process fluid is corrosive (acid, H2S, Cl-). Insufficient CA leads to premature pinholes.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: corrosion_allowance_mm < 1.5

fmea.failureMode: Critical: Hoop stress exceeds 90% of allowable stress. Safety margin is insufficient. Increase wall thickness or select material with higher S value.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Hoop_Stress > allowable_stress_mpa * 0.90

fmea.failureMode: Factor of safety is below 3.5. ASME VIII targets minimum SF=4.0 for static design. Design should be re-evaluated.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Safety_Factor < 3.5

fmea.failureMode: Full RT inspection is not performed (E < 1.0). The calculated thickness requires 15-43% more material. Total cost may decrease with full RT.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: joint_efficiency_val < 1.0

fmea.failureMode: Low temperature service: Impact testing (Charpy V-notch) and MDMT (Minimum Design Metal Temperature) analysis are mandatory. General carbon steel poses embrittlement risk.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: design_temp_c < 0

fmea.failureMode: Thickness > 50mm: PWHT (Post-Weld Heat Treatment) may become mandatory. Check ASME VIII UCS-56 tables. Evaluate cost and schedule impact of heat treatment.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: t_ordered > 50

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Compressed Air Leakage Energy Loss Audit (Sonic Flow)
subtitle: Energy Efficiency / Compressed Air Systems — ISO 1217:2009 Annex C, ISO 11011, CAGI ADF-100

inScope: Compressed Air Leakage Energy Loss Audit (Sonic Flow)
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

standards.name: Compressed Air Leakage Energy Loss Audit (Sonic Flow)
standards.description: Compressed Air Leakage Energy Loss Audit (Sonic Flow) — Energy Efficiency / Compressed Air Systems

formulas.expression: Leak_Flow_kg_s = discharge_coefficient_Cd * Orifice_Area_m2 * SQRT(polytropic_index_n * Air_Density_kg_m3 * P_abs_bar * 100000 * POWER(2 / (polytropic_index_n + 1), (polytropic_index_n + 1) / (polytropic_index_n - 1)))

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: polytropic_index_n < 1.0 || polytropic_index_n > 1.4
fmea.effect: Polytropic index n must be 1.00–1.40. Isothermal (ideal intercooling): 1.00 | Adiabatic: 1.40. Values outside this range are physically impossible for air compression.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Total_Leak_Flow_m3min > compressor_fad_m3min
fmea.effect: Total leak flow exceeds compressor FAD. System cannot maintain pressure — compressor runs continuously at full load. Data check required.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: SONIC (CHOKED) FLOW confirmed: At this system pressure, air exits the leak point at the speed of sound. Flow rate is independent of downstream pressure — leakage cannot be reduced by lowering system pressure. Fix is mechanical repair only.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: P_abs_bar / P_atm_bar > 1 / Critical_Pressure_Ratio

fmea.failureMode: CRITICAL SYSTEM LOSS: Leakage exceeds 30% of compressor output. Industry average is 20–30% in poorly maintained systems. At this level, an additional compressor may be running solely to compensate for losses. Immediate audit and repair program mandatory.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Leak_Pct_of_FAD > 30

fmea.failureMode: Significant leakage: 10–30% of FAD lost. Well-maintained systems achieve <10%. Schedule leak repair program. Typical payback: 3–12 months.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Leak_Pct_of_FAD > 10 && Leak_Pct_of_FAD <= 30

fmea.failureMode: Annual financial loss exceeds $5,000 USD from this leak source alone. Ultrasonic leak detection survey and systematic repair program will deliver rapid ROI.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Annual_Cost_USD > 5000

fmea.failureMode: Large orifice leak (≥3mm): This is a fitting, joint, or valve failure — not a pinhole. Immediate isolation required. At 6 bar(g), a 3mm orifice leaks ~4 m³/min FAD continuously.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: leak_diameter_mm >= 3.0

fmea.failureMode: Carbon impact: Leakage responsible for significant Scope 2 CO₂ emissions. Relevant for ISO 14001, ESG reporting, and EU CBAM supply chain exposure.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Annual_CO2_tonnes > 10

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Multi-Product Break-Even Analysis and Degree of Operating Leverage (DOL)
subtitle: Corporate Finance / Managerial Accounting — CIMA Management Accounting, IFRS 8 Segment Reporting, CVP Analysis Framework

inScope: Multi-Product Break-Even Analysis and Degree of Operating Leverage (DOL)
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Product A — Variable Cost per Unit
Product B — Variable Cost per Unit

standards.name: Multi-Product Break-Even Analysis and Degree of Operating Leverage (DOL)
standards.description: Multi-Product Break-Even Analysis and Degree of Operating Leverage (DOL) — Corporate Finance / Managerial Accounting

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Mix_Sum < 0.98 || Mix_Sum > 1.02
fmea.effect: Product mix percentages must sum to 100% (±2% tolerance). Current sum is invalid — WACM calculation will be distorted.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: WACM <= 0
fmea.effect: Weighted Average Contribution Margin ≤ 0: Variable costs exceed selling prices on a weighted basis. Break-even is mathematically unreachable — this product mix destroys value at every unit sold.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: CM_A < 0 || CM_B < 0
fmea.effect: At least one product has negative contribution margin (price < variable cost). Selling more units increases losses. Price revision or discontinuation must be evaluated immediately.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: corporate_tax_rate_pct >= 100
fmea.effect: Tax rate cannot equal or exceed 100%. Net profit would be zero or negative by definition.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: EXTREME OPERATING LEVERAGE (DOL > 5): A 10% revenue decline will reduce operating income by more than 50%. Fixed cost structure is dangerously high relative to volume. Immediate fixed cost reduction or revenue diversification required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DOL > 5

fmea.failureMode: High Operating Leverage (DOL 3–5): Business is highly sensitive to volume fluctuations. In an economic downturn, profitability will erode rapidly. Stress-test at -15% and -30% volume scenarios.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DOL > 3 && DOL <= 5

fmea.failureMode: CRITICAL: Margin of Safety < 10%. Actual sales are within 10% of break-even. Any demand reduction, price pressure, or cost increase could push the business into loss. Pricing, cost structure, or volume must be addressed immediately.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Margin_of_Safety_Pct < 10

fmea.failureMode: Low Margin of Safety (10–25%): Business has limited buffer above break-even. Acceptable for stable industries; concerning for cyclical manufacturing or export-dependent operations.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Margin_of_Safety_Pct >= 10 && Margin_of_Safety_Pct < 25

fmea.failureMode: Contribution Margin Ratio < 20% on one or more products: Very thin margin structure. Small cost increases (raw materials, energy) will immediately eliminate profitability. Price elasticity analysis required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: CM_A / product_A_price < 0.20 || CM_B / product_B_price < 0.20

fmea.failureMode: Break-even revenue exceeds $10M: High absolute break-even level. Ensure sales pipeline and contracted revenue cover this threshold before capacity expansion.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: BEP_Revenue > 10000000

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Mass Concrete Volume, Thermal Crack Risk & Pour Cost (ACI 207)
subtitle: Civil & Structural Engineering / Concrete Works — ACI 207.1R-96 Mass Concrete, ACI 207.2R Thermal Effects, EN 1992-3 Liquid Retaining

inScope: Mass Concrete Volume, Thermal Crack Risk & Pour Cost (ACI 207)
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Cement Type / Heat Class
options: CEM III/B (GGBS ≥66%) — Very low heat: factor 0.55

standards.name: Mass Concrete Volume, Thermal Crack Risk & Pour Cost (ACI 207)
standards.description: Mass Concrete Volume, Thermal Crack Risk & Pour Cost (ACI 207) — Civil & Structural Engineering / Concrete Works

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Gross_Volume_m3 <= 0
fmea.effect: Total concrete volume is zero. Enter at least one non-zero volume (slab, footing, or column).
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: cement_content_kg_m3 < 150
fmea.effect: Cement content < 150 kg/m³: Below minimum for structural concrete workability and strength. ACI 318 minimum for structural applications: 250 kg/m³.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: min_section_thickness_mm >= 900 && cement_heat_factor >= 0.85
fmea.effect: MASS CONCRETE CONDITIONS: Section ≥ 900mm with high-heat cement (CEM I/II). ACI 207.1R mass concrete protocols mandatory. Thermal control plan required before pouring.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: THERMAL CRACKING RISK CONFIRMED: Core-to-surface ΔT exceeds 20°C threshold. Restrained thermal shrinkage will generate tensile stresses exceeding concrete tensile strength. Through-cracking expected. Mandatory mitigations: (1) Use CEM III/B or pozzolanic cement, (2) Pre-cool mix water or aggregate, (3) Limit pour lifts to 600mm, (4) Apply thermal insulation blanket to retain surface heat.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Delta_T_Core_Surface > 20

fmea.failureMode: SEVERE THERMAL CRACKING — ΔT > 25°C: At this differential, macro-cracks of 0.2–0.5mm width are near-certain. Structure integrity and watertightness compromised. Redesign concrete mix with low-heat cement and thermal monitoring sensors (embedded thermocouples) required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Delta_T_Core_Surface > 25

fmea.failureMode: HOT WEATHER CONCRETING: Ambient temperature > 32°C. Concrete may set prematurely. ACI 305R requirements: (1) Fresh concrete temperature ≤ 35°C at point of discharge, (2) Use chilled water or ice substitution, (3) Pour at night, (4) Increase slump via plasticizer — not water.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: ambient_temp_c > 32

fmea.failureMode: COLD WEATHER CONCRETING: Ambient < 5°C. Hydration will stall or freeze. ACI 306R: Fresh concrete must be maintained at ≥ 10°C for 7 days minimum. Insulated formwork, heated enclosure, or anti-freeze admixtures required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: ambient_temp_c < 5

fmea.failureMode: DELAYED ETTRINGITE FORMATION (DEF) RISK: Peak core temperature > 70°C. Ettringite phase destroyed during curing and reforms later causing expansive cracking. Use water-reducing admixtures and low-C₃A cements. This risk is irreversible once temperature is exceeded.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Peak_Core_Temp_C > 70

fmea.failureMode: Waste allowance < 3%: Likely underestimated for construction site conditions. Insufficient waste factor leads to under-ordering, schedule delays, and cold joint formation. Minimum 5% recommended for poured-in-place work.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: pour_waste_pct < 3

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Calibration Drift Analysis & ISO 17025 Conformity Decision Risk (TUR / Guard Band)
subtitle: Metrology & Quality / Calibration Management — ISO/IEC 17025:2017, ILAC G8:09/2019, JCGM 100:2008 (GUM)

inScope: Calibration Drift Analysis & ISO 17025 Conformity Decision Risk (TUR / Guard Band)
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Guard Band Decision Rule
inputs.default: ILAC G8 Rule — w = U (full guard band)
options: ILAC G8 Rule — w = U (full guard band)

standards.name: Calibration Drift Analysis & ISO 17025 Conformity Decision Risk (TUR / Guard Band)
standards.description: Calibration Drift Analysis & ISO 17025 Conformity Decision Risk (TUR / Guard Band) — Metrology & Quality / Calibration Management

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: U_expanded > process_tolerance
fmea.effect: CRITICAL: Expanded measurement uncertainty exceeds the process tolerance. Conformity decisions are statistically meaningless — the instrument cannot determine if the process is in-spec. Lab recertification or instrument replacement required.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: CRITICAL DECISION RISK — TUR < 4:1: The 4:1 minimum Test Uncertainty Ratio is not met. Probability of false accept (in-tolerance equipment measuring out-of-spec parts as conforming) is statistically significant. ISO 17025 cl.7.8.6 requires this risk to be disclosed in the conformity statement.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: TUR < 4

fmea.failureMode: EXTREME RISK — TUR < 2:1: False accept probability may exceed 5–15% depending on distribution. Guard band must be applied and expanded. Product liability exposure is severe. Internal audit required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: TUR < 2

fmea.failureMode: DRIFT EXCEEDS 50% OF TOLERANCE before next calibration is due. At the current drift rate, instrument will be out of tolerance before planned recalibration. Shorten calibration interval to maximum half of current interval.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Predicted_Drift_Next_Interval > process_tolerance * 0.5

fmea.failureMode: High drift rate detected: Instrument drifts more than 1% of tolerance per 90 days. Current calibration interval may be too long. Review against measurement criticality and reduce interval.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Drift_Rate_per_Day > ABS(process_tolerance) / 90

fmea.failureMode: CONDITIONAL CONFORMITY: Instrument is within raw tolerance but fails guarded acceptance limit. Under ILAC G8, this is a NON-CONFORM result when guard banding is applied. Measurements made since last calibration may contain false accepts.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: ABS(current_error) > Acceptance_Limit && ABS(current_error) <= process_tolerance
fmea.control_measure: ILAC G8 Guard Band — Conditional Pass

fmea.failureMode: OUT-OF-TOLERANCE FOUND: Instrument exceeded calibration specification. Mandatory action: (1) Quarantine all measurements made since last in-tolerance calibration, (2) Risk assessment of affected product/decisions, (3) Root cause analysis, (4) Customer notification if product was released.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: ABS(current_error) > process_tolerance

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: CBAM Financial Exposure Calculator (EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism)
subtitle: Environmental Engineering & Corporate Finance / GHG Compliance — EU Regulation 2023/956 (CBAM), GHG Protocol Corporate Standard, EU Regulation 2021/447 Benchmarks

inScope: CBAM Financial Exposure Calculator (EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism)

inputs.label: Carbon Tax / ETS Already Paid in Origin Country

standards.name: CBAM Financial Exposure Calculator (EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism)
standards.description: CBAM Financial Exposure Calculator (EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism) — Environmental Engineering & Corporate Finance / GHG Compliance

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: eu_export_volume_tonnes > annual_production_tonnes
fmea.effect: EU export volume cannot exceed total production volume. Data input error.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: eu_sector_benchmark_tco2_per_tonne <= 0
fmea.effect: EU sector benchmark must be positive. Check EU Regulation 2021/447 for your product category benchmark value.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: CBAM EXPOSURE CONFIRMED: Your carbon intensity exceeds the EU sector benchmark. CBAM certificates must be purchased for every tonne exported to the EU above this threshold. This is a legal obligation from 2026 onwards. Decarbonisation investment or EU market exit must be evaluated.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Specific_Embedded_tCO2_per_tonne > eu_sector_benchmark_tco2_per_tonne

fmea.failureMode: MATERIAL FINANCIAL EXPOSURE: Annual CBAM liability exceeds €100,000. This represents a significant margin erosion risk for EU-destined exports. Quantify in CAPEX planning for decarbonisation. EU customers will increasingly demand lower-carbon supply chains.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Net_CBAM_Cost_EUR > 100000

fmea.failureMode: EXTREME CARBON INTENSITY: Your product emits 50%+ more CO₂ than the EU benchmark. At current EU ETS trajectory (projected €100+/t by 2030), export competitiveness will be critically impaired. Immediate industrial decarbonisation roadmap required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Carbon_Intensity_vs_Benchmark_Pct > 150
fmea.control_measure: EU Green Deal Supply Chain Requirements

fmea.failureMode: High-carbon electricity grid (>0.5 kgCO₂/kWh): Scope 2 emissions are a major CBAM driver. Corporate PPA (Power Purchase Agreement) with renewable energy can significantly reduce embedded carbon and improve CBAM position. Model the impact using market-based Scope 2 method.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: scope2_grid_factor_kg_kwh > 0.5

fmea.failureMode: No local carbon tax offset applicable. Countries with carbon pricing (UK, Switzerland, Korea) can deduct equivalent carbon costs from CBAM liability. Monitoring Turkey's carbon market development is recommended.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: local_carbon_tax_eur_tco2 === 0

fmea.failureMode: More than 50% of production exported to EU: High CBAM concentration risk. Any regulatory tightening of EU benchmarks or ETS price spike will have disproportionate financial impact. Diversify export markets or accelerate low-carbon transition.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: eu_export_volume_tonnes / annual_production_tonnes > 0.5
