name: Machining Chatter Stability, Surface Roughness & Tool Deflection Analysis
subtitle: CNC Manufacturing / Dynamics — ISO 3002-1 Turning/Milling Roughness, ISO 1302 Surface Texture Indication, Tobias-Fishwick Chatter Theory

inScope: Machining Chatter Stability, Surface Roughness & Tool Deflection Analysis
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Machine / Fixture Rigidity Class
options: HIGH — Heavy machining centre, short toolholder, vise
MEDIUM — Standard VMC, standard extension, milling fixture
LOW — Long overhang, thin wall part, weak fixture, worn spindle

standards.name: Machining Chatter Stability, Surface Roughness & Tool Deflection Analysis
standards.description: Machining Chatter Stability, Surface Roughness & Tool Deflection Analysis — CNC Manufacturing / Dynamics

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: radial_depth_ae > tool_diameter_mm
fmea.effect: Radial depth (ae) exceeds tool diameter — geometrically impossible. ae must be ≤ D.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: axial_depth_ap > tool_diameter_mm * 0.8
fmea.effect: Axial depth (ap) exceeds 80% of tool diameter. Catastrophic tool breakage risk. Reduce ap.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: rigidity_factor < 1.0
fmea.effect: Dynamic rigidity factor (Kd) cannot be less than 1.0. Ideal (chatter-free) conditions = 1.0.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: SURFACE FINISH VIOLATION: Predicted Ra exceeds drawing specification. Reduce fz or use a wiper insert (larger rε). Safe fz for spec compliance is shown below.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Ra_approx > ra_specification_um

fmea.failureMode: CHATTER RISK HIGH — L/D > 4: At this overhang ratio, regenerative chatter is likely. Symptoms: harmonic surface marks, tool noise, premature wear. Mitigations: (1) Use anti-vibration toolholder, (2) Reduce ap by 30–40%, (3) Change spindle speed ±10% to shift from resonance zone.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: L_D_ratio > 4

fmea.failureMode: DIMENSIONAL INACCURACY: Tool tip deflection exceeds 0.05mm (50μm). Workpiece dimensional tolerance will be violated. Reduce overhang, increase tool diameter, or reduce cutting force.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Tool_Deflection_mm > 0.05

fmea.failureMode: Elevated tool deflection (20–50μm): Monitor dimensional output. For tight tolerances (IT6 or better), this deflection will cause out-of-spec parts. Consider shorter toolholder or reduced Fc.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Tool_Deflection_mm > 0.02 && Tool_Deflection_mm <= 0.05

fmea.failureMode: Tooth passing frequency > 1000 Hz: May excite spindle bearing natural frequencies. If chatter occurs, shift RPM by ±5–10% or change number of flutes to detune from structural resonance.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Tooth_Passing_Freq_Hz > 1000

fmea.failureMode: COMBINED HIGH-RISK: Weak fixture + long overhang simultaneously. Predicted Ra and deflection values are unreliable — actual results may be significantly worse. Mandatory: reduce overhang to L/D ≤ 3 before proceeding.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: rigidity_factor > 2.0 && L_D_ratio > 3

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Bolted Joint Tightening Torque, Preload & Von Mises Utilisation (VDI 2230)
subtitle: Mechanical Engineering / Fastener Design — VDI 2230 Part 1:2015, ISO 898-1 Mechanical Properties, ISO 724 Metric Thread Geometry

inScope: Bolted Joint Tightening Torque, Preload & Von Mises Utilisation (VDI 2230)
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

standards.name: Bolted Joint Tightening Torque, Preload & Von Mises Utilisation (VDI 2230)
standards.description: Bolted Joint Tightening Torque, Preload & Von Mises Utilisation (VDI 2230) — Mechanical Engineering / Fastener Design

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: thread_pitch_mm >= nominal_diameter_mm
fmea.effect: Thread pitch cannot equal or exceed nominal diameter — geometrically impossible for ISO metric threads.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: bearing_diameter_dw_mm < nominal_diameter_mm
fmea.effect: Bearing face diameter (dw) must be greater than nominal diameter. Typical: dw ≈ 1.4–1.7 × d.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: preload_utilisation_pct > 95
fmea.effect: Preload utilisation > 95%: Torsional component will cause yielding during tightening. VDI 2230 maximum for torque wrench: 90%. Use yield-controlled (TTY) method if 100% is required.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: yield_strength_mpa > 1400
fmea.effect: Yield strength > 1400 MPa: Exceeds standard property class range (12.9 = 1080 MPa). Hydrogen embrittlement risk is significant above 1200 MPa. Verify material specification.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: BOLT YIELDS DURING ASSEMBLY: Combined Von Mises stress exceeds yield strength. Bolt will neck and lose preload or fracture during tightening. Actions: (1) Lubricate to reduce μG and μK, (2) Reduce preload utilisation target to 80%, (3) Use higher-grade bolt.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Utilisation_pct > 100

fmea.failureMode: Near-yield tightening: Utilisation 90–100%. Any friction variation above nominal will cause bolt yield. Calibrated torque wrench mandatory. Re-verify after first thermal cycle.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Utilisation_pct > 90 && Utilisation_pct <= 100

fmea.failureMode: High thread friction (μG > 0.16): Most of applied torque is consumed overcoming friction — actual preload achieved will be much lower than calculated. Use thread lubricant or anti-seize compound. Re-measure μ with torque-angle method.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: friction_thread_mu_g > 0.16

fmea.failureMode: High scatter factor (αA > 1.6): Actual preload may be only 60% of target. Joint may not achieve required clamp force. Switch to hydraulic tensioning (αA = 1.2) or angle-controlled tightening (αA = 1.05).
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: alpha_val > 1.6

fmea.failureMode: FATIGUE JOINT — INSUFFICIENT PRELOAD: Dynamic joints require high preload to prevent fretting and self-loosening (Junker effect). Preload below 80% Rp0.2 with vibration loading is a known failure mode. Use thread-locking compound or prevailing torque nut.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: joint_type === 'Dynamic / Fatigue — vibration, alternating load' && preload_utilisation_pct < 80

fmea.failureMode: High K-factor (>0.25): Friction-dominated joint. Standard torque tables assume K≈0.20. Your actual preload from nameplate torque values may be 25–40% lower than expected. Re-calibrate using measured K-factor for this specific surface combination.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: K_nut_factor > 0.25

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: CNC Cycle Time Breakdown, Spindle Utilisation & OEE-Spindle Analysis
subtitle: CNC Manufacturing / Operations — ISO 13399 Tool Representation, ISO 22400-2 OEE Definitions, JIPM TPM Spindle OEE

inScope: CNC Cycle Time Breakdown, Spindle Utilisation & OEE-Spindle Analysis
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

standards.name: CNC Cycle Time Breakdown, Spindle Utilisation & OEE-Spindle Analysis
standards.description: CNC Cycle Time Breakdown, Spindle Utilisation & OEE-Spindle Analysis — CNC Manufacturing / Operations

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Vf_feed_rate > 100000
fmea.effect: Calculated feed rate exceeds 100,000 mm/min — physically impossible. Check Vc, fz, z, and D inputs.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: CRITICAL SPINDLE WASTE: Only cutting for less than 30% of cycle. Machine spends over 70% of time on non-value-adding operations (rapids, ATC, load/unload). Immediate actions: (1) Zero-point clamping system, (2) Pre-set offline tooling, (3) Optimise G00 paths, (4) Pallet changer evaluation.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Spindle_Utilisation_pct < 30

fmea.failureMode: Low spindle utilisation (30–50%): Significant non-cutting time losses. Target is >65% for general machining. Review ATC time, rapid distances, and fixture strategy.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Spindle_Utilisation_pct >= 30 && Spindle_Utilisation_pct < 50

fmea.failureMode: Tool change time >30% of cutting time: ATC is a major cycle-time driver. Evaluate: (1) Reduce tool count via multi-function tools, (2) Sub-spindle or twin-pallet, (3) Upgrade to faster ATC (arm-type vs. umbrella).
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_toolchange_min > T_cut_min * 0.3

fmea.failureMode: Required RPM exceeds machine maximum. Actual cutting speed reduced. Recalculate with Vc_actual shown. Consider: (1) Smaller tool diameter, (2) Higher-RPM spindle machine, (3) Reduce cutting speed to match machine limit.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: n_rpm > machine_max_rpm

fmea.failureMode: Auxiliary time (load/unload + probing) exceeds actual cutting time. Automation of part handling or in-process gauging should be evaluated for ROI.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_aux_min > T_cut_min

fmea.failureMode: Machine spindle speed capability underused (<40% of max RPM). Machine may be oversized for this operation, or cutting parameters are conservative. Review tool and material data for speed increase potential.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: RPM_Utilisation_pct < 40

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: CNC Machining True Unit Cost (TCO) — Material, Tooling, Overhead & Scrap
subtitle: CNC Manufacturing / Cost Engineering — Sandvik Coromant Machining Economics, ISO 3685 Tool Life Criteria, CIMA Management Accounting

inScope: CNC Machining True Unit Cost (TCO) — Material, Tooling, Overhead & Scrap
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Number of Cutting Edges per Insert

standards.name: CNC Machining True Unit Cost (TCO) — Material, Tooling, Overhead & Scrap
standards.description: CNC Machining True Unit Cost (TCO) — Material, Tooling, Overhead & Scrap — CNC Manufacturing / Cost Engineering

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: finished_part_volume_m3 >= raw_material_volume_m3
fmea.effect: Finished part volume cannot be equal to or larger than raw billet volume. Material is removed during machining.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Buy_to_Fly_Ratio > 20
fmea.effect: Buy-to-fly ratio > 20: Over 95% of material becomes swarf. Verify volumes. For aerospace titanium forgings, ratios up to 10 are common — above 20 is exceptional.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: TOOLING COST EXCEEDS MACHINE TIME COST: Cutting speed is too high — tools are being consumed faster than economically optimal. Reduce Vc to extend tool life. Optimal Vc for minimum cost (Vcopt) is typically 20–30% below Vcmax.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Tooling_Cost_per_Part_USD > Machining_Cost_USD

fmea.failureMode: High buy-to-fly ratio (>5): More than 80% of raw material becomes chip. Material waste is a dominant cost driver. Evaluate: (1) Near-net-shape forging or casting, (2) Additive manufacturing + hybrid machining, (3) Billet size optimisation.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Buy_to_Fly_Ratio > 5

fmea.failureMode: Material cost >60% of total unit cost: Material is the dominant cost element. Price negotiation, alternative alloys, or supply chain changes will have greater impact than process optimisation.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Material_Cost_Fraction_pct > 60

fmea.failureMode: Scrap rate > 5%: Significant quality cost. At this rate, scrap penalty is adding meaningful cost per part. Root cause analysis (8D / Ishikawa) required. Target: <1% for mature CNC processes.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: scrap_rate_pct > 5

fmea.failureMode: Scrap rate 1–5%: Above world-class target. Each percentage point of scrap adds direct cost penalty. SPC implementation and tooling condition monitoring recommended.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: scrap_rate_pct > 1 && scrap_rate_pct <= 5

fmea.failureMode: Tool life < 15 minutes per edge: Very short tool life indicates aggressive cutting conditions or workpiece hardness issues. Economic tool life target is typically 20–45 min. Reduce Vc or check for built-up edge (BUE) conditions.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: tool_life_per_edge_min < 15

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Process Capability (Cp / Cpk / Pp / Ppk), PPM Defect Rate & Quality Loss Function
subtitle: Quality Engineering / Six Sigma — AIAG SPC 2nd Edition, IATF 16949:2016 Clause 8.3.3, ISO 22514-2 Process Capability
standardsBasis: ASQ Six Sigma Body of Knowledge

inScope: Process Capability (Cp / Cpk / Pp / Ppk), PPM Defect Rate & Quality Loss Function
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Cost per Defective Part (scrap + rework + warranty)

standards.name: Process Capability (Cp / Cpk / Pp / Ppk), PPM Defect Rate & Quality Loss Function
standards.description: Process Capability (Cp / Cpk / Pp / Ppk), PPM Defect Rate & Quality Loss Function — Quality Engineering / Six Sigma

formulas.expression: Ppk = overall_sigma > 0 ? MIN((usl - process_mean) / (3 * overall_sigma), (process_mean - lsl) / (3 * overall_sigma)) : Cpk
Centering_Index = 1 - ABS(2 * process_mean - usl - lsl) / Tolerance_Band

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: lsl >= usl
fmea.effect: LSL must be strictly less than USL. Check specification limits — bilateral tolerance requires LSL < target < USL.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: process_mean > usl || process_mean < lsl
fmea.effect: Process mean is outside specification limits. PPM = 500,000+. Process is fundamentally out of control — all output may be non-conforming. Stop production and investigate immediately.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: total_samples_N < 25
fmea.effect: AIAG SPC requires minimum 25 subgroups for statistically valid Cpk. Current sample insufficient — Cpk estimate unreliable.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: PROCESS INCAPABLE — Cpk < 1.0: Process cannot meet specification. Defect rate is measured in percent, not PPM. Immediate containment (100% inspection) mandatory. Root cause analysis and process redesign required before resuming normal production.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Cpk < 1.0

fmea.failureMode: CAPABILITY BELOW REQUIREMENT: Cpk is below the required target. Customer PPAP submission will be rejected. For automotive (IATF 16949): Cpk ≥ 1.33 is mandatory for production approval. Variance reduction required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Cpk >= 1.0 && Cpk < target_cpk_val

fmea.failureMode: PROCESS OFF-CENTRE: Cp is significantly higher than Cpk. Process has good potential width but is shifted from nominal. Re-centering alone (tool offset, fixture adjustment) could reduce PPM dramatically without improving process variation.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Cp > Cpk * 1.33

fmea.failureMode: SIGNIFICANT Cpk–Ppk GAP (>0.3): Long-term variation substantially exceeds short-term. Special causes (tool wear, material lot changes, operator shifts, temperature drift) are present. SPC control chart review and MSA re-evaluation required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Cpk > 0 && Ppk > 0 && (Cpk - Ppk) > 0.3
fmea.control_measure: AIAG SPC — Cp vs Pp Analysis

fmea.failureMode: Annual quality cost exceeds $50,000: This Cpk level is generating significant financial loss. Investment in process improvement, SPC implementation, or machine capability upgrade will deliver measurable ROI. Present to management with this annual cost figure.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Annual_Quality_Cost_USD > 50000

fmea.failureMode: Poor process centering (index < 0.70): Mean is more than 30% of tolerance from nominal. Asymmetric defect distribution — one side generating significantly more rejects. Investigate systematic bias: fixture wear, tool offset error, or raw material dimensional shift.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Centering_Index < 0.7

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: CPM Schedule Delay, Liquidated Damages & Acceleration (Crashing) Analysis
subtitle: Project Management / Contract Engineering — FIDIC Red/Yellow/Silver Book 2017, PMBOK 7th Edition, NEC4 Engineering Contract

inScope: CPM Schedule Delay, Liquidated Damages & Acceleration (Crashing) Analysis
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

standards.name: CPM Schedule Delay, Liquidated Damages & Acceleration (Crashing) Analysis
standards.description: CPM Schedule Delay, Liquidated Damages & Acceleration (Crashing) Analysis — Project Management / Contract Engineering

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: excusable_delay_days > Gross_Delay_days
fmea.effect: Excusable delay cannot exceed total gross delay. Check inputs — contractor may be entitled to EOT only up to the actual overrun.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: max_crashable_days > Gross_Delay_days && Gross_Delay_days > 0
fmea.effect: Maximum crashable days exceeds total delay. You cannot recover more time than the delay incurred.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: CRASHING NOT ECONOMICAL: Acceleration cost per day exceeds LD rate. Paying liquidated damages is financially preferable to accelerating — unless client relationship, repeat business, or reputational risk justifies the premium.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: crash_cost_per_day_usd > daily_ld_rate_usd

fmea.failureMode: LD CAP REACHED: Liquidated damages have hit the contractual maximum. Any additional delay beyond this point no longer increases LD exposure — however, client may pursue general damages if LD is found to be a penalty clause. Seek legal advice.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Base_LD_Exposure_USD >= LD_Cap_USD && LD_Cap_USD > 0
fmea.control_measure: FIDIC / Contract Law — LD Cap Reached

fmea.failureMode: CRITICAL DELAY: Non-excusable overrun exceeds 20% of planned duration. This is a major contract performance failure. Formal delay analysis (TIA — Time Impact Analysis) required immediately. Consider formal acceleration notice and schedule recovery plan submission.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: NonExcusable_Delay_days > planned_duration_days * 0.2

fmea.failureMode: RECOVERABLE CLAIM EXCEEDS LD: Prolongation costs attributable to client-caused delays exceed LD exposure. Net position may be a client payment obligation to contractor. Formal contractual notice and substantiation records are essential.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Prolongation_Claim_USD > Capped_LD_USD && compensable_delay_days > 0

fmea.failureMode: SCHEDULE CANNOT BE RECOVERED: No crashable days available on critical path. Full LD exposure is unavoidable. Focus on EOT entitlement claims, LD cap, and dispute avoidance strategy.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Gross_Delay_days > 0 && max_crashable_days === 0

fmea.failureMode: HIGH CRASH ROI (>3×): Acceleration investment yields more than 3× return in LD savings. Full crash programme to maximum compressible days is strongly recommended on financial grounds alone.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Crash_ROI_ratio > 3

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Industrial Roof Structural Load, Area Calculation & Material Cost (ASCE 7 / EN 1991)
subtitle: Civil & Structural Engineering / Industrial Buildings — ASCE 7-22 Minimum Design Loads, EN 1991-1-3 Snow Loads, EN 1991-1-4 Wind Actions

inScope: Industrial Roof Structural Load, Area Calculation & Material Cost (ASCE 7 / EN 1991)
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Terrain / Exposure Category
options: C / III — Open terrain, scattered obstructions (Cp=0.85)
D / IV — Flat open coast, unobstructed (Cp=1.0)

standards.name: Industrial Roof Structural Load, Area Calculation & Material Cost (ASCE 7 / EN 1991)
standards.description: Industrial Roof Structural Load, Area Calculation & Material Cost (ASCE 7 / EN 1991) — Civil & Structural Engineering / Industrial Buildings

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: building_length_m <= 0 || building_width_m <= 0
fmea.effect: Building dimensions must be positive values.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: roof_pitch_deg < 0 || roof_pitch_deg > 60
fmea.effect: Roof pitch must be 0–60°. Angles above 60° are structurally unconventional for industrial buildings.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: importance_factor_Is < 0.8 || importance_factor_Is > 1.2
fmea.effect: Importance factor must be 0.8–1.2 per ASCE 7 Table 1.5-2.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: TOTAL GRAVITY LOAD > 3.0 kN/m²: Heavy combined loading. Structural engineer must design purlins, rafters, and columns specifically for this load. Standard light steel industrial roof frames (Z/C purlins) are typically designed for 0.5–1.5 kN/m². DO NOT use standard span tables.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Total_Gravity_Load_kN_m2 > 3.0

fmea.failureMode: HIGH SNOW LOAD > 1.5 kN/m²: Significant structural implication. Roof framing must be designed for snow drift accumulation (ASCE 7 Section 7.7) at parapets and higher-level roof adjacencies. Progressive collapse analysis may be required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Roof_Snow_Load_kN_m2 > 1.5

fmea.failureMode: FLAT/LOW-PITCH ROOF WITH SNOW: Pitch < 5° prevents snow from sliding. Full unbalanced snow load accumulates. Ponding risk from blocked drainage must also be assessed. Provide minimum 2% fall to drains.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: roof_pitch_deg < 5 && Roof_Snow_Load_kN_m2 > 0.5

fmea.failureMode: HIGH WIND PRESSURE > 1.2 kN/m²: Cladding fixings and sheet overlaps must be designed for this uplift. Standard trapezoidal sheet fixings rated 0.8–1.0 kN/m² may be insufficient. Verify manufacturer's approved fixing schedule.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Dynamic_Wind_Pressure_kN_m2 > 1.2

fmea.failureMode: Waste allowance < 5% on steeply pitched roof: Hip ends, valleys, and ridge cuts generate significant offcuts. Minimum 8–10% recommended for pitches above 20°. Under-ordering causes installation delays and material price premium on re-order.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: waste_allowance_pct < 5 && roof_pitch_deg > 20

fmea.failureMode: WIND UPLIFT EXCEEDS DEAD LOAD: Net uplift force will lift roof structure. Roof-to-wall connections and hold-down anchors are structurally critical elements. Structural engineer must design specific uplift connections — this is not a standard detail.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Uplift_Wind_kN_m2 * -1 > dead_load_kN_m2

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: TOC Bottleneck Throughput Analysis & Constraint Upgrade ROI (Goldratt / APICS)
subtitle: Lean Manufacturing / Theory of Constraints — Goldratt Theory of Constraints (TOC), APICS CPIM — Constraint Management, ISO 22400-2 OEE KPI Standard

inScope: TOC Bottleneck Throughput Analysis & Constraint Upgrade ROI (Goldratt / APICS)
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Target Bottleneck Cycle Time After Investment
Throughput per Unit (Revenue minus Truly Variable Cost)

standards.name: TOC Bottleneck Throughput Analysis & Constraint Upgrade ROI (Goldratt / APICS)
standards.description: TOC Bottleneck Throughput Analysis & Constraint Upgrade ROI (Goldratt / APICS) — Lean Manufacturing / Theory of Constraints

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: bottleneck_ct_target_min >= bottleneck_ct_current_min
fmea.effect: Target cycle time must be less than current cycle time. If no improvement is expected, investment analysis is not applicable.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: wacc_pct <= 0
fmea.effect: WACC must be positive. Zero or negative discount rate is not valid for NPV calculation.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: OVER-INVESTMENT: Target cycle time is faster than customer takt time. Output beyond takt rate creates overproduction waste (Muda #1). System is demand-constrained — additional throughput cannot be sold. Reduce investment scope to target CT = Takt Time exactly.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: bottleneck_ct_target_min < takt_time_min

fmea.failureMode: NEGATIVE NPV: Investment destroys value at the given discount rate. Review: (1) Is throughput per unit correctly calculated? (2) Is project life realistic? (3) Can CAPEX be reduced? (4) Is there a phased investment option?
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Project_NPV_USD < 0

fmea.failureMode: Payback period exceeds 36 months: Above typical manufacturing investment approval threshold (12–24 months for constraint upgrades). Present risk-adjusted scenario analysis to management.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Payback_Months > 36

fmea.failureMode: Low demand headroom (<10%): Market is nearly saturated at current output. Throughput gain from constraint removal is limited by demand, not capacity. Validate sales forecast before committing CAPEX — demand growth must be confirmed.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Demand_Headroom_pct < 10

fmea.failureMode: More than 50% of incremental throughput comes from OEE improvement (not cycle time reduction). Consider TPM/autonomous maintenance programme as low-cost alternative before CAPEX. OEE improvement is typically 5–10× cheaper than hardware upgrade.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: OEEImprovementUnits > Incremental_Units_yr * 0.5

fmea.failureMode: BOTTLENECK OEE < 65%: Constraint station is severely underperforming before investment. Adding capacity to a poorly-maintained machine multiplies waste. Mandatory: reach OEE ≥ 75% through TPM before hardware upgrade. Otherwise investment ROI will be materially worse than projected.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: current_oee_pct < 65

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: SMED Setup Time Reduction, Capacity Recovery & EOQ Optimisation (Shingo / Harris)
subtitle: Lean Manufacturing / Setup Reduction — Shingo Prize SMED Methodology, Harris EOQ Model (1913) — Wilson Formula, APICS CPIM Module 3 — Inventory Management

inScope: SMED Setup Time Reduction, Capacity Recovery & EOQ Optimisation (Shingo / Harris)
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Current External Setup Time (pre/post while running)

standards.name: SMED Setup Time Reduction, Capacity Recovery & EOQ Optimisation (Shingo / Harris)
standards.description: SMED Setup Time Reduction, Capacity Recovery & EOQ Optimisation (Shingo / Harris) — Lean Manufacturing / Setup Reduction

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: internal_to_external_conversion_pct === 0 && streamlining_reduction_pct === 0
fmea.effect: Both conversion and streamlining reductions are zero — no SMED improvement defined. Enter expected improvement percentages.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: internal_to_external_conversion_pct > 100
fmea.effect: Conversion rate cannot exceed 100% of internal time.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: holding_cost_rate_pct < 5
fmea.effect: Holding cost rate < 5% is unrealistically low. Minimum components: capital cost (WACC) + warehousing + insurance. Typical industrial: 20–30%.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: BATCH PRODUCTION MODE: Current EOQ exceeds 2 months of demand. High setup cost is forcing large batch sizes, creating excess WIP, long lead times, and poor flexibility. SMED is the correct intervention — reducing setup cost directly enables smaller, leaner batches.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Current_EOQ_units > annual_demand_units / 6

fmea.failureMode: Setup reduction < 50%: Below typical SMED first-pass target. Shingo's methodology targets >75% reduction in internal time. Review: Have all convertible internal activities been identified? Are quick-clamp fixtures and standardised toolholder systems being used?
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Setup_Reduction_Pct < 50

fmea.failureMode: Significant capacity recovered (>8 hours equivalent): At this machine rate, recovered capacity has high monetary value. If demand exists, this freed capacity can be converted to throughput rather than cost saving — model the revenue upside separately.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Annual_Capacity_Recovered_min > 480 && machine_hourly_rate_usd > 50

fmea.failureMode: SMED enables weekly or sub-weekly batch sizes: Approaching one-piece-flow conditions. Consider implementing Pull / Kanban replenishment system to replace EOQ-based push scheduling.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: New_EOQ_units < annual_demand_units / 52

fmea.failureMode: Low conversion rate with long internal setup: SMED Stage 1 (separation of internal/external) is incomplete. Most setups > 30 min have 40–60% of activities convertible to external. Conduct video-based setup analysis to identify conversion opportunities.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: internal_to_external_conversion_pct < 30 && internal_setup_current_min > 30

fmea.failureMode: WORKING CAPITAL RELEASE EXCEEDS PROJECT COST: Inventory value released from smaller batch sizes alone covers the SMED investment. Present to finance as a working capital project, not just a productivity initiative.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Inventory_Value_Released_USD > smed_project_cost_usd && smed_project_cost_usd > 0

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Warehouse 3D Cube Utilisation, Pallet Position Count & Storage Capacity Analysis
subtitle: Logistics & Supply Chain / Warehouse Engineering — VDI 3618 Intralogistics Planning, FEM 10.2.02 Rack Safety, SEMA Code of Practice for Static Racking
standardsBasis: SEMA Code of Practice for Static Racking

inScope: Warehouse 3D Cube Utilisation, Pallet Position Count & Storage Capacity Analysis
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Racking / Storage System Type
options: Double-deep rack — access factor 0.5 (50% directly accessible)

standards.name: Warehouse 3D Cube Utilisation, Pallet Position Count & Storage Capacity Analysis
standards.description: Warehouse 3D Cube Utilisation, Pallet Position Count & Storage Capacity Analysis — Logistics & Supply Chain / Warehouse Engineering

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Required_Height_m > clear_height_m
fmea.effect: PHYSICAL IMPOSSIBILITY: Required rack height exceeds available clear height. Reduce rack levels, decrease loaded pallet height, or increase building clear height.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: net_storage_ratio_pct > 75
fmea.effect: Net storage ratio > 75% is physically unrealistic. Minimum space is needed for: dock doors, charging bays, staging areas, columns, fire exits. Maximum achievable: 70% for automated warehouses.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: aisle_width_m < 1.5
fmea.effect: Aisle width < 1.5m is below minimum safe passage for any powered truck. Fire safety regulations also require minimum 1.2m emergency egress width.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: WIDE AISLE SYSTEM (≥3.5m): Aisle width suitable for counterbalance forklift only. Approximately 35–40% of floor area is dedicated to aisles. Switching to reach truck (aisle 2.6–2.8m) or VNA (1.6–1.8m) could increase pallet positions by 20–40% in the same building.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: aisle_width_m >= 3.5

fmea.failureMode: POOR CUBE UTILISATION (<25%): Building volume is severely underused. Root causes: insufficient rack height, too many aisles, low stacking, or low occupancy. World-class warehouses achieve 40–60% cube utilisation. Evaluate: (1) Taller racking, (2) Narrower aisles, (3) Double-deep or mobile racking.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Cube_Utilisation_pct < 25

fmea.failureMode: OCCUPANCY TARGET > 90%: Operating above 90% causes severe congestion, pick path blockages, and forces multiple product relocations per pick. WMS systems degrade significantly above 85%. Required buffer for operational flexibility: minimum 15% empty positions.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: occupancy_target_pct > 90

fmea.failureMode: Only 1–2 rack levels achievable: Building clear height is limiting vertical density. If building modification is not feasible, consider mezzanine floor installation or multi-tier picking to improve m³ utilisation.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Max_Rack_Levels <= 2

fmea.failureMode: Storage density < 0.8 positions/m² gross: Below industry benchmark for conventional racking (target: 1.0–1.5 positions/m²). Analyse: wide aisle ratios, low stacking height, and low net storage ratio as primary contributors.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Positions_per_m2 < 0.8

fmea.failureMode: Low accessibility + small absolute accessible count: With drive-in or pushback systems, FIFO rotation becomes operationally difficult. For perishable goods, pharmaceuticals, or date-coded products, minimum selectivity factor 0.8 is required to ensure FEFO compliance.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: selectivity_factor < 0.5 && accessible_positions < 200

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Belt, Pulley and Gear Ratio Calculator — Speed Ratio, Belt Length, Center Distance and Power
subtitle: Mechanical Design / Power Transmission / Machine Elements — ISO 5294:2012, ISO 10823:2007, AGMA 9002-B16

inScope: Belt, Pulley and Gear Ratio Calculator — Speed Ratio, Belt Length, Center Distance and Power

name: Digital Twin Prototype ROI vs Physical Build — Cost Avoidance, TTM & NPV (ISO 23247)
subtitle: Industry 4.0 / R&D Engineering — ISO 23247 Digital Twin Manufacturing Framework, Gartner Hype Cycle Industry 4.0, IEC 61360 Digital Twin Properties

inScope: Digital Twin Prototype ROI vs Physical Build — Cost Avoidance, TTM & NPV (ISO 23247)
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Expected Digital Iterations Before First Physical Build
Remaining Physical Builds Required After DT Validation
Field Test Cost Reduction from DT Preflight

standards.name: Digital Twin Prototype ROI vs Physical Build — Cost Avoidance, TTM & NPV (ISO 23247)
standards.description: Digital Twin Prototype ROI vs Physical Build — Cost Avoidance, TTM & NPV (ISO 23247) — Industry 4.0 / R&D Engineering

formulas.expression: NPV_USD = Prototype_Cost_Avoidance_USD + TTM_Revenue_Gain_USD + Warranty_Saving_USD * Annuity_Factor - DT_Total_Investment_USD

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: physical_iterations_with_dt >= physical_iterations_avg
fmea.effect: Physical builds after DT must be less than baseline. If DT reduces zero physical iterations, there is no prototype cost avoidance benefit.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: programme_life_years < 1 || programme_life_years > 15
fmea.effect: Programme life must be 1–15 years for meaningful NPV analysis.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: NEGATIVE NPV: Digital Twin investment does not recover its cost at this discount rate and programme scale. Review: (1) Increase programme reuse across multiple products, (2) Negotiate platform licence sharing, (3) Quantify previously excluded benefits (regulatory approval acceleration, recall avoidance).
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: NPV_USD < 0

fmea.failureMode: Engineering labour >60% of DT investment: Model build effort is dominant cost. Evaluate: (1) Reuse existing simulation models, (2) Use vendor-supplied component models, (3) Phased modelling — start with highest-risk subsystems only.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DT_Engineering_Cost_USD > DT_Total_Investment_USD * 0.6

fmea.failureMode: Zero TTM gain entered: Time-to-market acceleration is typically the highest-value DT benefit. If parallel virtual/physical development is not planned, significant ROI is being excluded. Replan development timeline to capture TTM benefit.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: ttm_gain_days === 0

fmea.failureMode: Payback >24 months: Above typical threshold for product development tool investment. Consider: (1) Shared licence across multiple simultaneous programmes, (2) Phased IoT sensor rollout, (3) Open-source simulation platforms to reduce licence cost.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Simple_Payback_Months > 24

fmea.failureMode: Low virtual iteration count (<5): DT value scales with simulation depth. Less than 5 virtual iterations suggests the model is not being used to its potential. Target 10–20 virtual design cycles per physical build for full benefit realisation.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: dt_iterations_expected < 5
fmea.control_measure: Digital Twin Best Practice — McKinsey / Deloitte

fmea.failureMode: IoT CAPEX exceeds 50% of prototype cost baseline: Sensor investment may be disproportionate. Evaluate: (1) Virtual sensor placement using FEA instead of physical measurement, (2) Shared sensor infrastructure across production lines, (3) Edge AI for data reduction before cloud transmission.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: iot_sensor_capex_usd > physical_proto_unit_cost_usd * physical_iterations_avg * 0.5

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Assembly Line Balancing, SMV Analysis & Takt Compliance (IE / MTM-2 / MOST)
subtitle: Industrial Engineering / Production Planning — IE Line Balancing (Kilbridge & Wester / RPW Method), MTM-2 Standard Data System, MOST (Maynard Operation Sequence Technique)
standardsBasis: IE Line Balancing (Kilbridge & Wester / RPW Method)

inScope: Assembly Line Balancing, SMV Analysis & Takt Compliance (IE / MTM-2 / MOST)
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

standards.code: IE Line Balancing (Kilbridge & Wester / RPW Method)
standards.name: Assembly Line Balancing, SMV Analysis & Takt Compliance (IE / MTM-2 / MOST)
standards.description: Assembly Line Balancing, SMV Analysis & Takt Compliance (IE / MTM-2 / MOST) — Industrial Engineering / Production Planning

formulas.expression: Total_SMV_min = station_1_smv + station_2_smv + station_3_smv + station_4_smv + station_5_smv + station_6_smv + station_7_smv + station_8_smv
Bottleneck_SMV_min = MAX(station_1_smv, station_2_smv, station_3_smv, station_4_smv, station_5_smv, station_6_smv, station_7_smv, station_8_smv)

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: station_1_smv <= 0
fmea.effect: Station 1 SMV must be greater than zero — at least one workstation must have content.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: actual_operators < 1
fmea.effect: At least 1 operator is required on the line.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: TAKT VIOLATION: Bottleneck station cycle time exceeds takt time. Meeting daily demand target is MATHEMATICALLY IMPOSSIBLE without: (1) splitting the bottleneck task, (2) adding a parallel station, or (3) increasing shift time. Calculate: required output deficit = (Bottleneck_SMV - Takt) × demand units.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Bottleneck_SMV_min > Takt_Time_min

fmea.failureMode: LOW LINE EFFICIENCY (<75%): Over 25% of operator time is idle due to imbalance. Rebalance by: (1) relocating work elements from overloaded to underloaded stations, (2) applying RPW or Kilbridge-Wester algorithm, (3) combining or splitting stations. Target: >85% for high-volume lines.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Line_Efficiency_pct < 75

fmea.failureMode: EXCESS OPERATORS IDENTIFIED: Line has more operators than theoretical minimum. Each excess operator represents avoidable annual labour cost. Conduct kaizen event to redistribute work elements before adding headcount.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Idle_Operator_Equivalent >= 1

fmea.failureMode: CAPACITY SHORTFALL: Line cannot meet daily demand at current bottleneck rate. Immediate actions: (1) Overtime authorisation, (2) Bottleneck relief (parallel resource), (3) Demand smoothing with customer. Quantify units short per day and escalate to production manager.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Capacity_vs_Demand_pct < 100

fmea.failureMode: Low allowance (<10%): ILO recommends minimum 10% for light assembly work. Insufficient allowance leads to: (1) fatigue-induced quality defects, (2) absenteeism increase, (3) operator injury claims. Verify time study method includes all required rest periods.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: allowance_pct < 10

fmea.failureMode: VERY SHORT TAKT (<0.5 min): Cycle time under 30 seconds. Ergonomic risk of repetitive strain injury is high at this pace. EU Directive 2002/44/EC requires risk assessment. Consider task variety rotation and automation for highly repetitive operations.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Takt_Time_min < 0.5

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Power Factor, Reactive Energy Penalty & Industrial Electricity Bill Analysis (ISO 50001)
subtitle: Energy Efficiency / Facility Management — ISO 50001:2018 Energy Management, IEC 61000-3-2 Harmonics, IEC 60831 Shunt Capacitors

inScope: Power Factor, Reactive Energy Penalty & Industrial Electricity Bill Analysis (ISO 50001)
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Power Factor Correction Capacitor Bank Cost

standards.name: Power Factor, Reactive Energy Penalty & Industrial Electricity Bill Analysis (ISO 50001)
standards.description: Power Factor, Reactive Energy Penalty & Industrial Electricity Bill Analysis (ISO 50001) — Energy Efficiency / Facility Management

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Power_Factor > 1.0
fmea.effect: Calculated power factor exceeds 1.0 — physically impossible. Check reactive energy meter readings. Possible cause: capacitive reactive (leading) is over-reported.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: contracted_demand_kw > 0 && peak_demand_kw > contracted_demand_kw * 1.1
fmea.effect: Peak demand exceeds contracted demand by >10%. Grid operator will apply demand overage penalty. Immediate load management or contract renegotiation required.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: POOR POWER FACTOR (<0.90): Significant reactive energy penalty and oversized distribution equipment. Automatic power factor correction (APFC) capacitor bank is almost certainly economically justified. Every 0.01 improvement in PF reduces reactive penalty and may enable demand reduction.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Power_Factor < 0.90

fmea.failureMode: Suboptimal power factor (0.90–0.95): Below most utility target of PF ≥ 0.95. Reactive penalty charges are accumulating. PFC capacitor payback is typically 12–24 months at this level.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Power_Factor >= 0.90 && Power_Factor < 0.95

fmea.failureMode: REACTIVE PENALTY >10% OF ACTIVE COST: Reactive energy is adding over 10% to your energy bill. This is avoidable with power factor correction hardware. Annual penalty cost shown — compare to PFC CAPEX for immediate payback analysis.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Inductive_Penalty_USD > Active_Energy_Cost_USD * 0.1

fmea.failureMode: LOW LOAD FACTOR (<40%): High peak demand relative to average consumption. Demand charge is disproportionately large. Strategies: (1) Peak shaving with battery storage or generator, (2) Load shifting to off-peak hours, (3) Demand response programme, (4) Contract demand renegotiation.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Load_Factor_pct < 40

fmea.failureMode: Demand charge >30% of energy cost: Peak demand management has high financial leverage. Each kW of peak demand reduction saves demand_charge_usd_kw USD per month permanently. Industrial battery storage or VFD-based soft starters should be evaluated.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Demand_Charge_USD > Active_Energy_Cost_USD * 0.3

fmea.failureMode: OVER-COMPENSATION DETECTED: Capacitive reactive energy penalty is being charged. Existing PFC capacitor bank is over-sized for current load. Partial deactivation of capacitor stages during light-load periods required. Check APFC controller thresholds.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Capacitive_Penalty_USD > 0

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Project Cost Escalation, Real Discount Rate & Inflation-Adjusted Budget (AACE / Fisher Equation)
subtitle: Corporate Finance / Project Cost Engineering — AACE International RP 18R-97 Cost Escalation, AACE RP 10S-90 Cost Estimate Classification, PMBOK 7th Edition — Cost Baseline

inScope: Project Cost Escalation, Real Discount Rate & Inflation-Adjusted Budget (AACE / Fisher Equation)
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Nominal Discount Rate (WACC or project hurdle)

standards.name: Project Cost Escalation, Real Discount Rate & Inflation-Adjusted Budget (AACE / Fisher Equation)
standards.description: Project Cost Escalation, Real Discount Rate & Inflation-Adjusted Budget (AACE / Fisher Equation) — Corporate Finance / Project Cost Engineering

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: base_material_cost_usd + base_labour_cost_usd <= 0
fmea.effect: Total base cost must be positive. Both material and labour cannot be zero simultaneously.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: general_inflation_rate_pct >= nominal_discount_rate_pct
fmea.effect: Inflation rate equals or exceeds nominal discount rate — Real discount rate is zero or negative (Fisher Equation). This represents hyperinflationary conditions. NPV analysis in real terms becomes unreliable. Use nominal cash flows instead.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: NEGATIVE REAL DISCOUNT RATE: Inflation exceeds nominal WACC. Every deferred cost actually costs more in real terms — there is no financial benefit to delaying project expenditure. This typically signals a hyperinflationary environment. Nominal cash flow analysis is required instead of real.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Real_Discount_Rate_pct < 0

fmea.failureMode: HIGH ESCALATION (>20%): Cost escalation exceeds 20% of base estimate. This level typically represents a material budget risk. Project sanction budget must include full escalation provision — underfunded projects are the primary cause of cost overruns in capital projects. Escalate to project sponsor.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Escalation_Pct > 20

fmea.failureMode: MATERIAL ESCALATION OUTPACING LABOUR >2×: Commodity price volatility is the dominant risk. Evaluate: (1) Fixed-price supply contracts with early procurement, (2) Material price escalation clauses in contracts, (3) Alternative materials or supplier diversification.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: material_escalation_pct_yr > labour_escalation_pct_yr * 2

fmea.failureMode: LOW CONTINGENCY (<5%): Insufficient contingency for any estimate class. AACE Class 3 (budget estimate) requires 10–20%. Underfunding contingency is the most common cause of cost overruns on capital projects.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: risk_contingency_pct < 5

fmea.failureMode: NO MANAGEMENT RESERVE on multi-year project: Projects >3 years face significant scope uncertainty. Zero management reserve leaves no headroom for authorised scope additions. PMBOK recommends 5–10% management reserve separate from risk contingency.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: project_duration_years > 3 && management_reserve_pct === 0

fmea.failureMode: ESCALATION EXCEEDS CONTINGENCY: Price escalation alone is larger than the entire contingency reserve. Contingency will be fully consumed by inflation before any technical risk event. Increase contingency or renegotiate fixed-price contracts to include escalation clauses.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Escalation_Amount_USD > Contingency_USD

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Industrial Waste Stream Cost, Circularity Rate & ESG Compliance Analysis (ISO 14001 / EU Taxonomy)

inScope: Industrial Waste Stream Cost, Circularity Rate & ESG Compliance Analysis (ISO 14001 / EU Taxonomy)

standards.name: Industrial Waste Stream Cost, Circularity Rate & ESG Compliance Analysis (ISO 14001 / EU Taxonomy)
standards.description: Industrial Waste Stream Cost, Circularity Rate & ESG Compliance Analysis (ISO 14001 / EU Taxonomy) — Environmental Engineering / Sustainability

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: recycled_diverted_t > non_hazardous_waste_t + hazardous_waste_t
fmea.effect: Diverted waste cannot exceed total waste generated. Check input data — diverted fraction is a subset of total waste.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: violation_probability_pct < 0 || violation_probability_pct > 100
fmea.effect: Violation probability must be 0–100%.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: hazardous_waste_t > 0 && hazardous_disposal_rate_usd_t < 50
fmea.effect: Hazardous disposal rate below $50/tonne is unrealistically low. Licensed hazardous waste disposal is strictly regulated. Check units or confirm contractor rate — legal disposal typically costs $200–800/tonne.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: HIGH HAZARDOUS WASTE FRACTION (>20%): Hazardous waste disposal represents a significant cost and liability. Mandatory: (1) Waste audit to identify substitution opportunities (REACH compliance), (2) Solvent recovery system ROI analysis, (3) Licensed contractor insurance verification. Regulatory exposure is elevated.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Hazardous_Fraction_pct > 20

fmea.failureMode: CIRCULARITY RATE <20%: EU Taxonomy and CSRD require disclosure of circularity performance. Institutional investors and EU supply chain customers will classify this as a sustainability red flag. Circular economy action plan required for continued EU market access and ESG-linked financing eligibility.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Circularity_Rate_pct < 20
fmea.control_measure: EU CSRD / ESRS E5 / EU Taxonomy

fmea.failureMode: EXPECTED REGULATORY PENALTY EXCEEDS RECYCLING REVENUE: Compliance risk is financially material. Investment in waste management systems (proper segregation, licensed storage, treatment capacity) should be evaluated against risk-weighted fine cost. Environmental insurance may be warranted.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Risk_Weighted_Fine_USD > Recycling_Revenue_USD

fmea.failureMode: SIGNIFICANT WASTE-RELATED EMISSIONS (>100 tCO₂e/year): This waste stream generates reportable Scope 3 emissions under GHG Protocol Category 5. Must be disclosed in CDP, GRI 306, and CSRD reporting. Diversion from landfill (composting, energy recovery, recycling) reduces both cost and carbon footprint.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Carbon_Footprint_Waste_tCO2e > 100

fmea.failureMode: UNTAPPED RECYCLING REVENUE: Scrap value of recyclable materials exceeds current landfill cost, but less than 50% of non-hazardous waste is being diverted. Increasing recovery rate will reduce costs AND generate additional revenue. Conduct waste composition audit to identify high-value streams.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Recycling_Revenue_USD > Landfill_Cost_USD && recycled_diverted_t < non_hazardous_waste_t * 0.5

fmea.failureMode: HIGH ALL-IN WASTE COST (>$200/tonne generated): Above typical manufacturing benchmark. Dominant driver may be hazardous waste, wastewater treatment, or regulatory risk premium. Waste minimisation at source (lean process, material substitution) delivers permanent cost reduction.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Cost_per_Tonne_Generated_USD > 200

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Pump System Curve & BEP Deviation — Hydraulic Power Loss & Cavitation Risk
subtitle: Fluid Systems / Rotating Equipment — HI 9.6.3-2017, ISO 9906:2012, API 610 12th Ed.

standards.name: Pump System Curve & BEP Deviation — Hydraulic Power Loss & Cavitation Risk
standards.description: Pump System Curve & BEP Deviation — Hydraulic Power Loss & Cavitation Risk — Fluid Systems / Rotating Equipment

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: actual_flow_m3h < rated_flow_m3h * 0.5
fmea.effect: Operation below 50% BEP flow: recirculation risk — increase flow or install bypass
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: actual_flow_m3h > rated_flow_m3h * 1.2
fmea.effect: Operation above 120% BEP: cavitation and overload risk — throttle or trim impeller
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: npsha_m < npshr_m * 1.1
fmea.effect: NPSHa margin < 1.1× NPSHr: cavitation onset likely — lower suction lift or increase inlet pressure
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Severe left-of-BEP operation: axial thrust reversal and bearing fatigue risk per HI 9.6.3
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Q_ratio < 0.7

fmea.failureMode: Right-of-BEP runout: NPSH curve steep rise — verify NPSHa at this flow
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Q_ratio > 1.15

fmea.failureMode: NPSHa < NPSHr: cavitation active — impeller erosion will occur within 500 operating hours
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: npsh_margin < 1.0

fmea.failureMode: Motor overload >5%: thermal trip risk — verify service factor or upsize motor
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: P_overload_pct > 5

fmea.failureMode: Operating efficiency <75% of BEP: energy waste exceeds ISO 50001 baseline threshold
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: eta_act_pct < rated_efficiency_pct * 0.75

fmea.failureMode: Large pump at low BEP efficiency: consider hydraulic redesign or variable-speed drive
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: rated_efficiency_pct < 60 && motor_power_kw > 75

name: Bolted Flange Joint Integrity — Gasket Seating Stress, Bolt Load & Leak Tightness Class
subtitle: Pressure Equipment / Mechanical Integrity — ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 App.2, ASME PCC-1-2019, ASME B16.5-2017

inScope: Bolted Flange Joint Integrity — Gasket Seating Stress, Bolt Load & Leak Tightness Class
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Gasket Material Factor (m)
inputs.description: ASME VIII App 2 Table 2-5.1: spiral-wound SS/graphite m=3.0, full-face rubber m=0.5

inputs.label: Minimum Gasket Seating Stress (y)
inputs.description: ASME VIII App 2 Table 2-5.1

inputs.label: Bolt Material Yield Strength (S_y)
inputs.description: ASTM A193 B7: S_y=724 MPa at ambient

standards.name: Bolted Flange Joint Integrity — Gasket Seating Stress, Bolt Load & Leak Tightness Class
standards.description: Bolted Flange Joint Integrity — Gasket Seating Stress, Bolt Load & Leak Tightness Class — Pressure Equipment / Mechanical Integrity

formulas.expression: H_p_kN = 2*Math.PI*G_mean_m*b_eff_mm*1e-3 * gasket_m_factor * design_pressure_bar*1e5/1000; W_op_total_kN = H_kN + H_p_kN

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: gasket_od_mm <= gasket_id_mm
fmea.effect: G_OD must exceed G_ID — check gasket dimension inputs
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: gasket_od_mm > flange_od_mm
fmea.effect: Gasket OD cannot exceed flange OD — invalid geometry
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: bolt_ratio < 1.0
fmea.effect: Available bolt load insufficient — joint will leak or gasket will not seat
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Bolt load deficit: add bolts, upgrade bolt grade, or use lower-y gasket per ASME App.2 §2-5
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: bolt_ratio < 1.0

fmea.failureMode: Excessive bolt load: gasket crush risk — verify gasket crush limit per manufacturer data
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: bolt_ratio > 2.5

fmea.failureMode: High-pressure service with low m-factor: soft gasket inadequate — use spiral-wound or ring-type joint
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: design_pressure_bar > 100 && gasket_m_factor < 2.5

fmea.failureMode: Wide effective seating width: bolt load demand increases sharply — verify torque values per PCC-1-2019
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: b_eff_mm > 19

fmea.failureMode: Few bolts at elevated pressure: flange rotation risk — minimum 8 bolts recommended per ASME B16.5
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: bolt_count < 8 && design_pressure_bar > 40

fmea.failureMode: High-strength bolting: stress corrosion cracking susceptibility in H₂S service per NACE MR0175
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: bolt_material_yield_mpa > 1200

name: Shell & Tube Heat Exchanger Thermal Sizing — LMTD, NTU-ε & Fouling Margin
subtitle: Heat Transfer / Process Equipment — TEMA C/B/R 10th Ed., ISO 15547-1:2005, ASME BPVC VIII Div.1

inScope: Shell & Tube Heat Exchanger Thermal Sizing — LMTD, NTU-ε & Fouling Margin
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Overall Clean Heat Transfer Coefficient (U_clean)
inputs.description: Typical: water/water 800–1500, gas/gas 50–300, condensers 500–2000

inputs.label: Hot-Side Fouling Resistance (R_f,hot)
inputs.description: TEMA Table RGP-T-2.4: cooling water R_f = 0.176 m²K/kW

inputs.label: LMTD Correction Factor (F) — Multi-Pass
inputs.description: F=1 for true counterflow; F≥0.75 required per TEMA §7

standards.name: Shell & Tube Heat Exchanger Thermal Sizing — LMTD, NTU-ε & Fouling Margin
standards.description: Shell & Tube Heat Exchanger Thermal Sizing — LMTD, NTU-ε & Fouling Margin — Heat Transfer / Process Equipment

formulas.expression: dT1 = T_hot_in_c - T_cold_out_c; dT2 = T_hot_out_c - T_cold_in_c; LMTD_cf_K = (Math.abs(dT1 - dT2) < 0.001) ? dT1 : (dT1 - dT2) / Math.log(dT1/dT2)
Q_max = Math.min(Q_duty_kw / Math.abs(T_hot_in_c - T_cold_in_c)) * Math.abs(T_hot_in_c - T_cold_in_c); epsilon = Q_duty_kw / (Q_duty_kw / (1 - (T_hot_out_c - T_hot_in_c)/(T_cold_in_c - T_hot_in_c)))

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: T_hot_out_c >= T_hot_in_c
fmea.effect: Hot-side outlet must be cooler than inlet — check terminal temperatures
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: T_cold_out_c <= T_cold_in_c
fmea.effect: Cold-side outlet must be hotter than inlet — check terminal temperatures
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: T_hot_out_c < T_cold_in_c + 5
fmea.effect: Hot outlet approaches cold inlet temperature: cross-temperature pinch — reduce duty or add surface area
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: LMTD F-factor < 0.75: thermal inefficiency and flow maldistribution risk — split into two units or use pure counterflow
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: F_correction < 0.75

fmea.failureMode: Cleanliness factor < 85%: fouling penalty severe — schedule CIP or mechanical cleaning within 6 months
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: cleanliness_factor_pct < 85

fmea.failureMode: Effective LMTD < 5 K: very large surface area required — evaluate stream mixing or upstream temperature shift
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: LMTD_eff_K < 5

fmea.failureMode: Low U-value for high-duty service: excessive area — verify fluid velocities and baffling
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: U_clean_w_m2k < 200 && Q_duty_kw > 1000

fmea.failureMode: High combined fouling resistance: fouling margin > 35% — verify TEMA class selection for this service
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Rf_hot_m2k_kw + Rf_cold_m2k_kw > 0.5

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

fmea.failureMode: VFD payback < 2 years: strong ROI — prioritize for next capital cycle
