standards.name: Compressed Air System Leak Loss Quantification & Repair ROI
standards.description: Compressed Air System Leak Loss Quantification & Repair ROI — Utilities / Energy Management

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: load_unload_ratio < baseline_demand_pct/100
fmea.effect: Duty cycle below demand estimate: leak fraction is zero — re-measure duty cycle during production
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: leak_fraction > 0.4
fmea.effect: Leak fraction >40%: measurement or demand estimate may be unreliable — validate with ultrasonic leak detection
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: system_pressure_bar > 10 && specific_power_kw_m3min < 6
fmea.effect: High pressure with low specific power: verify compressor data — possible instrument error
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Leak fraction >20%: ISO 11011 threshold exceeded — immediate ultrasonic leak detection survey mandatory
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: leak_fraction > 0.2

fmea.failureMode: Repair payback < 6 months: high-priority action — schedule within current maintenance cycle
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: payback_months < 6

fmea.failureMode: Repair payback > 24 months: re-evaluate repair scope — prioritise high-flow leaks first (orifice Q ∝ d²)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: payback_months > 24

fmea.failureMode: Leaked flow >30% of compressor capacity: system pressure stability compromised — repair before load expansion
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Q_leak_m3min > compressor_power_kw / specific_power_kw_m3min * 0.3

fmea.failureMode: High utilisation: consider automated leak monitoring (ultrasonic IoT sensors) for continuous detection per DOE guidelines
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: operating_hours_yr > 7000

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Pressure Vessel Wall Thickness & MAWP — Cylindrical & Spherical Shell Design
subtitle: Pressure Equipment / Structural Integrity — ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 (UG-27, UG-99, UW-12), ASME BPVC II-D Material Tables, EN 13445-3:2021
standardsBasis: ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 (UG-27, UG-99, UW-12)

inScope: Pressure Vessel Wall Thickness & MAWP — Cylindrical & Spherical Shell Design
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Material Allowable Stress at Design Temp (S)
inputs.description: ASME BPVC II-D: SA-516-70 at 38°C → S=138 MPa; SA-240-316L → S=115 MPa

inputs.label: Weld Joint Efficiency Factor (E)
inputs.description: E=1.0: full RT; E=0.85: spot RT; E=0.70: no RT — ASME VIII UW-12

inputs.label: Mill Undertolerance (MT)
inputs.description: ASME B36.10M: 12.5% standard mill undertolerance for plate

standards.code: ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 (UG-27, UG-99, UW-12)
standards.name: Pressure Vessel Wall Thickness & MAWP — Cylindrical & Spherical Shell Design
standards.description: Pressure Vessel Wall Thickness & MAWP — Cylindrical & Spherical Shell Design — Pressure Equipment / Structural Integrity

formulas.expression: P_mpa = design_pressure_bar * 0.1; t_circ_mm = (shell_geometry === 'cylindrical') ? (P_mpa * inside_diameter_mm / 2) / (allowable_stress_mpa * weld_joint_efficiency - 0.6 * P_mpa) : (P_mpa * inside_diameter_mm / 2) / (2 * allowable_stress_mpa * weld_joint_efficiency - 0.2 * P_mpa)

t_eff_mm = t_nominal_mm * (1 - mill_undertolerance_pct/100) - corrosion_allowance_mm; MAWP_bar = (shell_geometry === 'cylindrical') ? (allowable_stress_mpa * weld_joint_efficiency * t_eff_mm) / (inside_diameter_mm/2 + 0.6*t_eff_mm) : (2 * allowable_stress_mpa * weld_joint_efficiency * t_eff_mm) / (inside_diameter_mm/2 + 0.2*t_eff_mm)

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Dt_ratio < 20
fmea.effect: D/t < 20: thick-wall condition — use Lamé equations per ASME VIII Div.1 Appendix 1-2; membrane formula is non-conservative
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: design_temp_c > 425 && allowable_stress_mpa > 138
fmea.effect: Temperature > 425°C: verify material allowable includes creep derating per ASME BPVC II-D Table 1A
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: mawp_safety_factor < 1.0
fmea.effect: MAWP < design pressure: vessel underdesigned — increase wall thickness or reduce operating pressure
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: MAWP within 5% of design pressure: no inspection margin — increase nominal thickness by minimum 10%
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: mawp_safety_factor < 1.05
fmea.control_measure: ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 (UG-27, UG-99, UW-12)

fmea.failureMode: High-pressure service with reduced joint efficiency: full radiographic testing (RT) strongly recommended — E=1.0 reduces wall thickness requirement
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: weld_joint_efficiency < 1.0 && design_pressure_bar > 100
fmea.control_measure: ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 (UG-27, UG-99, UW-12)

fmea.failureMode: Low corrosion allowance at elevated temperature: enhanced inspection frequency required per API 510
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: corrosion_allowance_mm < 1.5 && design_temp_c > 200
fmea.control_measure: ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 (UG-27, UG-99, UW-12)

fmea.failureMode: Sub-zero service: impact testing mandatory per ASME BPVC VIII UCS-66 — verify MDMT (Minimum Design Metal Temperature)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: design_temp_c < -29
fmea.control_measure: ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 (UG-27, UG-99, UW-12)

fmea.failureMode: D/t < 10: ultra-thick wall — membrane stress theory invalid; perform finite element or Div.2 Part 5 analysis
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Dt_ratio < 10
fmea.control_measure: ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 (UG-27, UG-99, UW-12)

fmea.failureMode: Large-diameter high-pressure vessel: wind/seismic loading and nozzle reinforcement analysis required per ASME UG-37
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: inside_diameter_mm > 1500 && design_pressure_bar > 50
fmea.control_measure: ASME BPVC VIII Div.1 (UG-27, UG-99, UW-12)

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Structural Steel Beam — Combined Bending, Shear & Lateral-Torsional Buckling Check
subtitle: Structural Engineering / Steel Design — AISC 360-22 Chapters F, G, EN 1993-1-1:2022 (EC3) §6.2, §6.3, ASCE 7-22 load combinations

inScope: Structural Steel Beam — Combined Bending, Shear & Lateral-Torsional Buckling Check
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Elastic Section Modulus About Strong Axis (S_x / W_el,y)
Plastic Section Modulus About Strong Axis (Z_x / W_pl,y)
Limiting Laterally Unbraced Length for Full Plasticity (L_p)
inputs.description: From section tables or AISC 360 F2-5: L_p = 1.76·r_y·√(E/F_y)

inputs.label: Limiting Laterally Unbraced Length for Inelastic LTB (L_r)
inputs.description: From section tables or AISC 360 F2-6

standards.name: Structural Steel Beam — Combined Bending, Shear & Lateral-Torsional Buckling Check
standards.description: Structural Steel Beam — Combined Bending, Shear & Lateral-Torsional Buckling Check — Structural Engineering / Steel Design

formulas.expression: DCR_moment = M_demand_knm / phi_Mn_knm; DCR_shear = V_demand_kn / phi_Vn_kn; DCR_combined = Math.max(DCR_moment, DCR_shear)

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Lr_m <= Lp_m
fmea.effect: L_r must exceed L_p — verify section table data inputs
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: DCR_moment > 1.0
fmea.effect: Bending demand exceeds capacity: increase section depth, add lateral bracing, or use higher-grade steel
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: DCR_shear > 1.0
fmea.effect: Shear demand exceeds capacity: increase web area (deeper section) or add web stiffeners
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Section overstressed: structural failure mode risk — do not proceed to fabrication without redesign
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DCR_combined > 1.0

fmea.failureMode: Unbraced length in elastic LTB zone: significant capacity reduction — add lateral restraints at midspan
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Lb_m > Lr_m

fmea.failureMode: High bending utilization with LTB active: consider intermediate bracing to restore full plastic capacity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DCR_moment > 0.85 && Lb_m > Lp_m

fmea.failureMode: High-strength steel with long unbraced length: LTB controls over yielding — bracing at L_p intervals mandatory
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Fy_mpa > 450 && Lb_m > 3

fmea.failureMode: High shape factor (> 1.20): significant plastic reserve — verify section compactness per AISC Table B4.1b
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Z_x_cm3 / S_x_cm3 > 1.20

fmea.failureMode: High combined loading: check shear-moment interaction per AISC 360-22 G2 for non-compact webs
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DCR_shear > 0.7 && DCR_moment > 0.7

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Arc Welding Heat Input, HAZ Width & Preheat Temperature Calculation
subtitle: Welding & Joining / Manufacturing — AWS D1.1-2020 §4.11, Table 3.2, EN 1011-1:2009 §7, EN 1011-2:2001 Annex C

inScope: Arc Welding Heat Input, HAZ Width & Preheat Temperature Calculation
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Process Thermal Efficiency Factor (η)
inputs.description: AWS D1.1-2020 Commentary §C-4.11 / EN 1011-1 Table B.1

inputs.label: Base Metal Carbon Equivalent (CE_IIW)
inputs.description: CE = C + Mn/6 + (Cr+Mo+V)/5 + (Ni+Cu)/15 — IIW formula

inputs.label: Diffusible Hydrogen Content of Weld Metal (H_d)
inputs.description: Per AWS A4.3 / ISO 3690 classification

standards.name: Arc Welding Heat Input, HAZ Width & Preheat Temperature Calculation
standards.description: Arc Welding Heat Input, HAZ Width & Preheat Temperature Calculation — Welding & Joining / Manufacturing

formulas.expression: CET = base_metal_CE * 0.75 + 0.25; T_preheat_c = 350 * Math.sqrt(CET - 0.1 * Math.log10(HI_kj_mm * 1000) + 0.015 * Math.log10(hydrogen_content) + 0.005 * Math.log10(plate_thickness_mm)) - 0.08 * plate_thickness_mm

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: HI_kj_mm > 3.5
fmea.effect: Heat input > 3.5 kJ/mm: excessive for most structural steels — grain coarsening and toughness loss in HAZ
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: HI_kj_mm < 0.3
fmea.effect: Heat input < 0.3 kJ/mm: very high cooling rate — hard HAZ and hydrogen cracking risk even with preheat
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: base_metal_CE > 0.65 && hydrogen_content > 5
fmea.effect: High CE + high hydrogen: severe cold cracking risk — use H5 or H2.5 consumables and increase preheat by 30°C
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Preheat > 150°C: verify interpass temperature control — max interpass typically 250°C for structural steels
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_preheat_gov_c > 150

fmea.failureMode: CE > 0.50: high cold cracking susceptibility — post-weld hydrogen release bake (200°C × 2h) strongly recommended per EN 1011-2
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: base_metal_CE > 0.50

fmea.failureMode: HAZ width > 8 mm: large heat-affected zone — CTOD toughness testing at HAZ required per BS 7448-2
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: HAZ_width_mm > 8

fmea.failureMode: t8/5 < 5 s: rapid cooling — risk of martensite formation in HAZ; verify hardness < 350 HV per ISO 15614-1
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: t85_s < 5

fmea.failureMode: GTAW at high current: consider GMAW for productivity — TIG heat input advantage diminishes above 200 A
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: thermal_efficiency === 0.60 && welding_current_a > 250

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Refrigeration System COP, Refrigerant Mass Flow & Condenser Heat Rejection
subtitle: HVAC / Refrigeration Engineering — ASHRAE 15-2022 Safety Standard, ASHRAE 34-2022 Refrigerant Designation, EN 378-1:2016

inScope: Refrigeration System COP, Refrigerant Mass Flow & Condenser Heat Rejection
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Refrigerant Enthalpy at Compressor Suction (h_1)
inputs.description: From refrigerant P-h chart or REFPROP at T_evap + superheat

inputs.label: Refrigerant Enthalpy at Expansion Valve Inlet (h_3)
inputs.description: From refrigerant P-h chart at T_cond - subcooling

inputs.label: Ideal Compressor Discharge Enthalpy (h_2s)
inputs.description: From refrigerant P-h chart: isentropic compression endpoint at P_cond

standards.name: Refrigeration System COP, Refrigerant Mass Flow & Condenser Heat Rejection
standards.description: Refrigeration System COP, Refrigerant Mass Flow & Condenser Heat Rejection — HVAC / Refrigeration Engineering

formulas.expression: COP = Q_evap_kw / W_comp_kw; Q_cond_kw = Q_evap_kw + W_comp_kw

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: T_cond_c <= T_evap_c + 10
fmea.effect: Condensing temperature must exceed evaporating temperature by at least 10 K — check cycle temperature inputs
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: q_evap_kj_kg <= 0
fmea.effect: Specific refrigerating effect ≤ 0: h_1 must exceed h_3 — verify P-h chart enthalpy values
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: COP < 1.0
fmea.effect: COP < 1.0: very low system efficiency — verify enthalpy inputs and compressor efficiency
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Lift (T_cond - T_evap) > 55 K: consider two-stage compression or economiser circuit per ASHRAE 15 §8.4
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_cond_c - T_evap_c > 55

fmea.failureMode: Isentropic efficiency < 55%: compressor in degraded condition — check valve condition, clearance volume, and refrigerant charge
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: compressor_isentropic_eff_pct < 55

fmea.failureMode: Suction superheat < 5 K: liquid slugging risk — compressor mechanical damage imminent; adjust expansion valve
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: superheat_k < 5

fmea.failureMode: Low COP for moderate evaporating temperature: check for non-condensables in system or refrigerant contamination
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: COP < 2.0 && T_evap_c > -10

fmea.failureMode: Condenser duty >80% above evaporator: high heat rejection load — verify cooling tower or condenser coil capacity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Q_cond_kw > Q_evap_kw * 1.8

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Industrial Noise Attenuation — Source SPL to Receiver Level & OSHA Dose Calculation
subtitle: Occupational Safety / Environmental Engineering — ISO 9613-2:1996, OSHA 29 CFR 1910.95, NIOSH REL (85 dB(A))

inScope: Industrial Noise Attenuation — Source SPL to Receiver Level & OSHA Dose Calculation
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Sound Power Level of Source (L_W)
inputs.description: From equipment data sheet or measured per ISO 3744/3746

inputs.label: Excess Attenuation (barriers, air absorption, ground) (A_exc)
inputs.description: Per ISO 9613-2: sum of A_atm + A_gr + A_bar; use 3 dB as conservative indoor default

options.label: 85 dB(A) — NIOSH REL / EU Action Level
80 dB(A) — EU Lower Exposure Action Value

standards.name: Industrial Noise Attenuation — Source SPL to Receiver Level & OSHA Dose Calculation
standards.description: Industrial Noise Attenuation — Source SPL to Receiver Level & OSHA Dose Calculation — Occupational Safety / Environmental Engineering

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: distance_m < 1 && Lw_source_db > 120
fmea.effect: Very close range to high-power source: near-field effects dominate — ISO 9613 far-field model not valid below 1 m
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: dose_pct > 100
fmea.effect: Noise dose > 100%: OSHA PEL exceeded — mandatory engineering controls, hearing protection, or exposure reduction
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Lp_receiver_db > 115
fmea.effect: SPL > 115 dB(A): OSHA ceiling limit — no unprotected exposure permissible regardless of duration
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Noise dose > 50%: OSHA Hearing Conservation Program mandatory — audiometric testing, training, hearing protection per 29 CFR 1910.95(c)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: dose_pct > 50

fmea.failureMode: SPL >10 dB over criterion: hearing protection alone insufficient — engineering noise control required per ISO 1996-1 hierarchy
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Lp_receiver_db > osha_criterion_db + 10

fmea.failureMode: Required attenuation > 15 dB: barrier or enclosure study needed — verify with ISO 9613-2 barrier insertion loss calculation
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: dB_reduction_needed > 15

fmea.failureMode: Multiple sources (>10): total SPL increase plateaus — adding sources beyond 10× gives <1 dB additional increment
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: num_identical_sources > 10

fmea.failureMode: Permissible exposure < 1 hour: extreme noise environment — restrict access, mandatory enclosures, and continuous monitoring
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_perm_h < 1

fmea.failureMode: Low excess attenuation at long range: re-evaluate air absorption per ISO 9613-1 — significant at >100 m above 1 kHz
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: excess_attenuation_db < 2 && distance_m > 100

name: Pipeline Wall Thickness, MAOP & Burst Pressure — Onshore Transmission Design
subtitle: Pipeline Engineering / Oil & Gas — ASME B31.8-2022 §841, ASME B31.4-2022 §403, API 5L 46th Ed. / ISO 3183:2019

inScope: Pipeline Wall Thickness, MAOP & Burst Pressure — Onshore Transmission Design
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Pipe Outside Diameter (D_o)
inputs.description: API 5L / ISO 3183 standard sizes: DN50–DN1400

inputs.label: Location Class (Population Density Zone)
inputs.description: ASME B31.8 §841.114 — design factor F per location class

standards.name: Pipeline Wall Thickness, MAOP & Burst Pressure — Onshore Transmission Design
standards.description: Pipeline Wall Thickness, MAOP & Burst Pressure — Onshore Transmission Design — Pipeline Engineering / Oil & Gas

formulas.expression: P_mpa = MAOP_bar * 0.1; t_min_mm = (P_mpa * pipe_OD_mm) / (2 * SMYS_mpa * F_factor * joint_factor_E * temperature_derating_T)
t_eff_mm = t_nominal_mm * (1 - mill_tolerance_pct/100) - corrosion_allowance_mm; P_burst_bar = (2 * SMYS_mpa * 1.4 * t_eff_mm / pipe_OD_mm) * 10

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Dt_ratio > 100
fmea.effect: D/t > 100: Barlow membrane formula loses accuracy — consult API RP 1111 for thin-wall pipes
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: safety_margin < 1.5
fmea.effect: Burst-to-MAOP margin < 1.5: insufficient structural reserve — increase wall thickness
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: t_min_mm < 3.0 && pipe_OD_mm > 300
fmea.effect: Very thin wall on large-diameter pipe: handling and installation damage risk — minimum 4 mm recommended per pipeline construction practice
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Location class mismatch: Class 3/4 requires F≤0.50 per ASME B31.8 §841.114 — re-enter correct location class
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: location_class >= 3 && F_factor === 0.72

fmea.failureMode: Burst margin < 2.0×MAOP: marginal for Class 1 service — consider corrosion management plan per NACE SP0169
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: safety_margin < 2.0

fmea.failureMode: Low CA on high-pressure pipeline: establish inline inspection (ILI) baseline within first 5 years per API RP 1163
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: corrosion_allowance_mm < 1.5 && MAOP_bar > 70

fmea.failureMode: ERW pipe above X60: verify seam weld toughness qualification per ISO 3183 PSL2 CVN requirements
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: joint_factor_E < 1.0 && SMYS_mpa > 414

fmea.failureMode: D/t < 15: heavy-wall pipe — evaluate buckle propagation arrest capacity per API RP 1111 §4.3 for offshore or steep terrain
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Dt_ratio < 15

fmea.failureMode: High MAOP in populated area: quantitative risk assessment (QRA) may be required by local authority
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: MAOP_bar > 150 && location_class >= 2

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Fatigue Life Estimation — S-N Curve, Miner's Rule Cumulative Damage & Safety Factor
subtitle: Mechanical Integrity / Fatigue Analysis — IIW Recommendations XIII-2460-13, EN 13445-3:2021 Annex B, ASME BPVC VIII Div.2 Part 5

inScope: Fatigue Life Estimation — S-N Curve, Miner's Rule Cumulative Damage & Safety Factor
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Stress Concentration Factor (K_t)
inputs.description: K_t=1 smooth bar; ~2 shoulder fillet; ~3–4 hole/notch — from Peterson's or FEA

inputs.label: Weld Detail Fatigue Class (FAT / Δσ_C at 2×10⁶ cycles)

options.label: FAT 125 — butt weld, full penetration, RT
FAT 71 — cruciform joint, fillet weld toe

inputs.label: S-N Curve Slope Exponent (m)
inputs.description: m=3 for welded details (IIW); m=5 base metal; second slope m=5 above knee per ASME

inputs.label: Total Expected Load Cycles Over Design Life (N_applied)
Mean Stress (σ_mean)
inputs.description: Positive = tensile; Goodman correction applied if σ_mean > 0

standards.name: Fatigue Life Estimation — S-N Curve, Miner's Rule Cumulative Damage & Safety Factor
standards.description: Fatigue Life Estimation — S-N Curve, Miner's Rule Cumulative Damage & Safety Factor — Mechanical Integrity / Fatigue Analysis

formulas.expression: N_ref = 2e6; N_allow = N_ref * Math.pow(fatigue_class_mpa / delta_sigma_corr_mpa, sn_slope_m)

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: delta_sigma_corr_mpa > fatigue_class_mpa * 3
fmea.effect: Applied stress range > 3× FAT class: extremely short fatigue life predicted — verify stress inputs or select higher FAT class
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: mean_stress_mpa >= material_UTS_mpa
fmea.effect: Mean stress ≥ UTS: static fracture condition — Goodman correction invalid; reduce mean stress
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: SF_actual < 1.0
fmea.effect: Fatigue life insufficient: Miner damage D > 1.0 — redesign geometry, reduce loads, or improve weld class
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: D > 0.5 on welded joint: IIW §9.5.3 recommends D_limit = 0.5 for welded structures without fracture mechanics backup
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: D_miner > 0.5 && fatigue_class_mpa <= 90

fmea.failureMode: Safety factor below target: reduce applied cycles, increase section, improve weld quality (higher FAT), or add postweld treatment
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: SF_actual < safety_factor_target

fmea.failureMode: K_t > 3.0: severe notch effect — consider redesign to smooth geometry; shot peening or HFMI treatment can increase FAT class by 1–2 steps
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: stress_concentration_Kt > 3.0

fmea.failureMode: Very high cycle regime (>10⁸): below ASTM E466 endurance limit boundary — verify S-N curve includes infinite-life region
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: applied_cycles_N > 1e8

fmea.failureMode: Mean stress >50% UTS: Goodman correction significant — consider residual stress relief (PWHT) to reduce effective mean stress
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: mean_stress_mpa > material_UTS_mpa * 0.5

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Conveyor Belt Power, Drive Tension & Sag Ratio — Bulk Material Handling Design
subtitle: Bulk Material Handling / Mechanical Engineering — ISO 5048:1989, CEMA Belt Conveyors for Bulk Materials 7th Ed., DIN 22101:2011
standardsBasis: CEMA Belt Conveyors for Bulk Materials 7th Ed.

inScope: Conveyor Belt Power, Drive Tension & Sag Ratio — Bulk Material Handling Design
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Artificial Friction Factor (f) — ISO 5048
inputs.description: ISO 5048: f=0.012 well-maintained, f=0.020 standard, f=0.030 dusty/misaligned

inputs.label: Belt Mass per Unit Length (m_belt)
inputs.description: From belt manufacturer data: EP400/3-6+3 ≈ 12 kg/m per metre width

inputs.label: Total Rotating Idler Mass per Unit Length (m_idler)
inputs.description: Carrying + return idler sets combined; depends on idler pitch and roll diameter

standards.name: Conveyor Belt Power, Drive Tension & Sag Ratio — Bulk Material Handling Design
standards.description: Conveyor Belt Power, Drive Tension & Sag Ratio — Bulk Material Handling Design — Bulk Material Handling / Mechanical Engineering

formulas.expression: mu_wrap = 0.35; wrap_rad = Math.PI; T2_kN = F_u_kN / (Math.exp(mu_wrap * wrap_rad) - 1); T1_kN = T2_kN + F_u_kN

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: sag_ratio_pct > 0.03
fmea.effect: Belt sag > 3%: increase take-up tension T2 or reduce idler pitch — material spillage and belt mistracking risk
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: belt_speed_m_s > 6 && capacity_t_h > 5000
fmea.effect: High speed on high-capacity conveyor: verify belt rating (kN/m width) and idler bearing life per ISO 281
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: lift_height_m < -100
fmea.effect: Long downhill conveyor: regenerative power possible — evaluate regenerative drives; braking tension controls design
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

name: Power Transformer Loading, No-Load/Load Losses & Economic Loading Factor

standards.name: Power Transformer Loading, No-Load/Load Losses & Economic Loading Factor
standards.description: Power Transformer Loading, No-Load/Load Losses & Economic Loading Factor — Electrical Power Systems / Energy Management

formulas.expression: P_out_w = actual_load_kva * load_power_factor * 1000; eta_transformer_pct = P_out_w / (P_out_w + P_total_w) * 100

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: beta > 1.2
fmea.effect: Loading > 120%: overload condition — IEEE C57.12 §12 limits short-time overload; check thermal withstand duration
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

name: Chemical Reactor Heat of Reaction, Adiabatic Temperature Rise & Thermal Runaway Risk
subtitle: Chemical Process Engineering / Process Safety — Stoessel 'Thermal Safety of Chemical Processes' (Wiley 2008), IEC 62675:2014, ASTM E537 / E1981

inScope: Chemical Reactor Heat of Reaction, Adiabatic Temperature Rise & Thermal Runaway Risk
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Heat of Reaction (ΔH_r) — per mole of key reactant
inputs.description: Negative = exothermic (correct sign convention); from calorimetry (DSC/RC1) or Hess's law

inputs.label: Specific Heat Capacity of Reaction Mass (c_p)
inputs.description: Aqueous systems ≈ 3.5–4.2; organic ≈ 1.5–2.5 kJ/(kg·K)

inputs.label: Onset Temperature of Decomposition (T_onset)
inputs.description: From DSC or ARC testing per ASTM E537 / IEC 62675

standards.name: Chemical Reactor Heat of Reaction, Adiabatic Temperature Rise & Thermal Runaway Risk
standards.description: Chemical Reactor Heat of Reaction, Adiabatic Temperature Rise & Thermal Runaway Risk — Chemical Process Engineering / Process Safety

formulas.expression: R = 8.314e-3; Q_rx_kw = Math.abs(delta_Hr_kj_mol) * reactant_concentration_mol_kg * batch_mass_kg * 1e6 * Math.exp(-Ea_kj_mol / (R * (T_process_c + 273.15))) / 3600
delta_T_safety = T_onset_decomp_c - MTSR_c; criticality = (MTSR_c < T_process_c + 25) ? 1 : (MTSR_c < T_onset_decomp_c) ? (delta_T_safety > 50 ? 2 : 3) : (delta_T_safety > 0 ? 4 : 5)

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: MTSR_c >= T_onset_decomp_c
fmea.effect: CRITICAL: MTSR ≥ T_onset — thermal runaway triggers decomposition: Stoessel Class 5. Do not operate without full process safety review
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: cooling_ratio < 1.0
fmea.effect: Cooling capacity insufficient to remove reaction heat at process temperature: runaway risk — increase cooling or reduce batch size
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: delta_T_ad_K > 200
fmea.effect: Adiabatic temperature rise > 200 K: high-energy reaction — consider semi-batch dosing to limit accumulation
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Stoessel Class 4/5: MTSR approaches or exceeds T_onset — emergency relief sizing per AIChE DIERS mandatory before scale-up
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: criticality_class >= 4

fmea.failureMode: Temperature safety margin < 50 K: insufficient buffer — consider lower process temperature, controlled dosing, or additional cooling per Stoessel §4.4
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_safety_margin_K < 50

fmea.failureMode: Cooling ratio 1–2: marginal safety — any cooling failure leads to thermal runaway; install high-temperature interlock per IEC 62675
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: cooling_ratio < 2.0 && cooling_ratio >= 1.0

fmea.failureMode: High ΔT_ad at high concentration: implement semi-batch (fed-batch) operation to limit reactant accumulation per Stoessel §7
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: delta_T_ad_K > 100 && reactant_concentration_mol_kg > 5

fmea.failureMode: Large-scale Class 3+ reaction: HAZOP and Layer of Protection Analysis (LOPA) required per IEC 61511 before production
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: batch_mass_kg > 10000 && criticality_class >= 3

name: Cylindrical Gear Pair — Bending & Pitting (Contact) Stress Rating per ISO 6336
subtitle: Mechanical Power Transmission / Machine Design — ISO 6336-1:2019, ISO 6336-2:2019, ISO 6336-3:2019

inScope: Cylindrical Gear Pair — Bending & Pitting (Contact) Stress Rating per ISO 6336
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Normal Module (m_n)
inputs.description: ISO 54 preferred module series

inputs.label: Allowable Contact Stress Number (σ_Hlim) — Pinion Material
inputs.description: ISO 6336-5: case-hardened 20MnCr5 → σ_Hlim=1500 MPa; through-hardened 42CrMo4 → σ_Hlim=700 MPa

inputs.label: Allowable Bending Stress Number (σ_Flim) — Pinion Material
inputs.description: ISO 6336-5: case-hardened → σ_Flim=430 MPa; through-hardened → σ_Flim=275 MPa

inputs.label: Application Factor (K_A)
inputs.description: ISO 6336-1 Table A.1

standards.name: Cylindrical Gear Pair — Bending & Pitting (Contact) Stress Rating per ISO 6336
standards.description: Cylindrical Gear Pair — Bending & Pitting (Contact) Stress Rating per ISO 6336 — Mechanical Power Transmission / Machine Design

formulas.expression: d1_mm = module_mm * z1_teeth; T1_Nm = P_kw * 9549.3 / n1_rpm; Ft_N = 2000 * T1_Nm / d1_mm
v_m_s = Math.PI * d1_mm * n1_rpm / 60000; K_v = 1 + (0.0125 * v_m_s * Math.pow(z1_teeth * gear_ratio_u, 0.5)) / (1 + 0.0125 * v_m_s)
Z_E = 189.8; Z_H = 2.5; u = gear_ratio_u; sigma_H_mpa = Z_E * Z_H * Math.sqrt(Ft_N * KA_app_factor * K_v * (u+1) / (facewidth_mm * d1_mm * u))
Y_F = 2.7; Y_S = 1.6; sigma_F_mpa = Ft_N * KA_app_factor * K_v * Y_F * Y_S / (facewidth_mm * module_mm)

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: S_H < 1.0
fmea.effect: Contact safety factor S_H < 1.0: pitting failure at operating load — increase facewidth, module, or use higher σ_Hlim material
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: S_F < 1.4
fmea.effect: Bending safety factor S_F < 1.4: ISO 6336 minimum for industrial drives — increase module or facewidth
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: facewidth_mm > 12 * module_mm * z1_teeth
fmea.effect: Facewidth exceeds 12×module×z_1: excessive width relative to pinion diameter — shaft deflection and load distribution factor K_Hβ will be critical
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: S_H < 1.1: marginal contact safety — case-harden to σ_Hlim ≥ 1400 MPa or apply profile modification (tip relief) per ISO 6336-1 §7
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: S_H < 1.1

fmea.failureMode: Pitch-line velocity > 25 m/s: high dynamic loads — verify gear quality grade ≤ 5 per ISO 1328-1 and check lubrication viscosity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: v_m_s > 25

fmea.failureMode: High ratio with few pinion teeth: undercut risk — verify minimum tooth count for standard addendum without profile shift
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: gear_ratio_u > 6 && z1_teeth < 17

fmea.failureMode: Dynamic factor K_v > 1.5: significant dynamic load amplification — reduce speed, improve gear quality, or add mass damping
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: K_v > 1.5

fmea.failureMode: Low bending safety under heavy shock loading: risk of tooth fracture at peak transients — consider shot-peened root fillet per ISO 6336-5 §5.4
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: S_F < 2.0 && KA_app_factor >= 1.5

name: Anchor Bolt Group — Tension, Shear & Combined Interaction for Column Baseplates
subtitle: Structural Engineering / Foundation Design — ACI 318-19 Chapter 17, AISC Design Guide 1 3rd Ed., EN 1992-4:2018

inScope: Anchor Bolt Group — Tension, Shear & Combined Interaction for Column Baseplates
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Number of Anchor Bolts in Group (n)
Factored Tension Demand on Bolt Group (N_u / N_Ed)
Factored Shear Demand on Bolt Group (V_u / V_Ed)
Minimum Edge Distance to Any Bolt (c_a1)

standards.name: Anchor Bolt Group — Tension, Shear & Combined Interaction for Column Baseplates
standards.description: Anchor Bolt Group — Tension, Shear & Combined Interaction for Column Baseplates — Structural Engineering / Foundation Design

formulas.expression: Nb_kn = 16 * Math.sqrt(fc_prime_mpa) * Math.pow(embed_depth_mm, 1.5) / 1000; AN = Math.pow(3 * embed_depth_mm, 2); AN0 = 9 * embed_depth_mm^2; phi_Ncb_kn = 0.70 * (AN/AN0) * Nb_kn
DCR_tension = Nu_kn / Math.min(phi_Nsa_kn, phi_Ncb_kn); DCR_shear = Vu_kn / Math.min(phi_Vsa_kn, phi_Vcp_kn); interaction = Math.pow(DCR_tension, 5/3) + Math.pow(DCR_shear, 5/3)

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: interaction > 1.2
fmea.effect: Combined tension-shear interaction > 1.2: ACI 318 §17.8.3 exceeded — increase bolt diameter, embedment, or bolt count
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: edge_dist_min_mm < 6 * bolt_dia_mm
fmea.effect: Edge distance < 6×d_a: concrete edge breakout controls — check ACI §17.7.2; may require edge reinforcement
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: embed_depth_mm < 8 * bolt_dia_mm
fmea.effect: Embedment < 8×d_a: shallow anchor — concrete breakout likely governs; increase h_ef to minimum 8×d_a per AISC DG1
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Tension DCR > 0.8: low reserve — verify load combinations include seismic overstrength per ASCE 7-22 §12.4.3 if applicable
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DCR_tension > 0.8

fmea.failureMode: Concrete breakout capacity <70% of steel capacity: brittle failure mode governs — increase embedment or add supplemental reinforcement per ACI §17.3.3
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: phi_Ncb_kn < phi_Nsa_kn * 0.7

fmea.failureMode: Low-strength concrete with high tension: consider post-installed adhesive anchors (ETAG 001) for higher concrete capacity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fc_prime_mpa < 25 && Nu_kn > 100

fmea.failureMode: Less than 4 bolts under combined loading: all bolts in tension and shear simultaneously — verify baseplate rotational stiffness per AISC DG1

name: Wet Scrubber / Packed Column Sizing — Mass Transfer NTU, HTU & Pressure Drop
subtitle: Chemical Process Engineering / Air Quality — GPSA Engineering Data Book 14th Ed. §9, AIChE Distillation Design Manual, VDI 2440:2000

inScope: Wet Scrubber / Packed Column Sizing — Mass Transfer NTU, HTU & Pressure Drop
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Henry's Law Constant (H) — dimensionless (c_gas / c_liquid)
inputs.description: H = p / (H_cc × RT); SO₂/water at 25°C ≈ 0.008; NH₃/water ≈ 0.0006; HCl/water ≈ 0.0001

inputs.label: Liquid-to-Gas Ratio (L/G) — volumetric
inputs.description: Typical: acid gas scrubbing 1–3 L/m³; particulate 0.5–1.5 L/m³

inputs.label: Overall Gas-Phase Mass Transfer Coefficient × Packing Area (K_ya)
inputs.description: Depends on packing type and gas velocity; 25 mm Raschig rings ≈ 0.05–0.15; structured packing ≈ 0.15–0.5

inputs.label: Superficial Gas Velocity in Column (u_s)
inputs.description: Typically 60–80% of flood velocity; GPSA recommends ≤75%

inputs.label: Packing Pressure Drop per Unit Height (ΔP/Z)
inputs.description: GPSA Fig. 9-2: random packing at operating velocity; structured packing 50–150 Pa/m

standards.name: Wet Scrubber / Packed Column Sizing — Mass Transfer NTU, HTU & Pressure Drop
standards.description: Wet Scrubber / Packed Column Sizing — Mass Transfer NTU, HTU & Pressure Drop — Chemical Process Engineering / Air Quality

formulas.expression: eta_pct = (inlet_conc_ppm - outlet_conc_ppm) / inlet_conc_ppm * 100; R = inlet_conc_ppm / outlet_conc_ppm
gas_vel_m_s = column_velocity_m_s; HTU_m = gas_vel_m_s / Kya_1_m; Z_m = NTU * HTU_m
Q_gas_m3_s = gas_flow_am3_h / 3600; A_col_m2 = Q_gas_m3_s / column_velocity_m_s; D_col_m = 2 * Math.sqrt(A_col_m2 / Math.PI)

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: outlet_conc_ppm >= inlet_conc_ppm
fmea.effect: Outlet concentration must be less than inlet — check concentration inputs
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Absorption factor < 1.0: L/G ratio insufficient for equilibrium — infinite packing height required; increase L/G or reduce H by cooling liquid
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: dP_total_pa > 2500
fmea.effect: Column pressure drop > 2500 Pa: high fan/blower energy penalty — consider lower-ΔP structured packing or reduce packing height
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Absorption factor < 1.2: marginal driving force — increase L/G by 50% to improve reliability and handle inlet concentration spikes
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: A_factor < 1.2

fmea.failureMode: NTU > 10: very high packing height required — verify Kya with pilot test; consider two-stage scrubbing
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: NTU > 10

fmea.failureMode: Large column (D>3 m, Z>6 m): consider multiple smaller columns in parallel — liquid distribution and wall effects critical above 3 m diameter
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: D_col_m > 3 && Z_m > 6

fmea.failureMode: Removal efficiency > 99.9%: verify NTU calculation at very high R values — small errors in inlet/outlet measurement dominate uncertainty
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: eta_pct > 99.9

fmea.failureMode: High Henry's constant (H > 1): sparingly soluble contaminant — scrubbing may be ineffective; consider activated carbon or catalytic destruction instead

fmea.effect: κ < 0.4: severe mixed lubrication regime — catastrophic wear within weeks; increase oil viscosity or reduce operating temperature
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Modified life L_nm < 20,000 h: below typical industrial minimum — improve lubrication (κ), reduce contamination (e_C), or upsize bearing
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: L_nm_h < 20000

fmea.failureMode: κ < 1.0: insufficient lubricant film — metal-to-metal contact risk; switch to higher viscosity grade or add EP additive
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: viscosity_ratio_kappa < 1.0

fmea.failureMode: High L10h but severe contamination: actual life will be far shorter than calculated — implement effective sealing and re-lubrication programme
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: contamination_factor_eC < 0.4 && L10h > 50000

fmea.failureMode: Ball bearing S_0 < 1.5: insufficient static reserve for machinery with vibration or shock loading per ISO 76 §4.2
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: S0 < 1.5 && bearing_type === 3

fmea.failureMode: High speed × load factor: verify bearing speed limit (n·dm) from catalogue — thermal and cage stability may limit life independently of fatigue
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: n_rpm * bearing_type > 500000

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Substation Earthing Grid — Ground Resistance, Touch & Step Voltage Safety Check
subtitle: Electrical Safety / Grounding Systems — IEEE Std 80-2013, IEEE Std 81-2012 (soil resistivity measurement), IEC 60364-5-54:2011

inScope: Substation Earthing Grid — Ground Resistance, Touch & Step Voltage Safety Check
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Soil Resistivity (ρ)
inputs.description: Field measurement per IEEE Std 81 (Wenner method); sandy/dry ≈ 1000 Ω·m; clay wet ≈ 20 Ω·m

inputs.label: Fault Clearing Time (t_f)
inputs.description: Protection relay clearing time; IEEE 80 uses t_f for shock duration

inputs.label: Surface Crushed Rock / Gravel Layer Resistivity (ρ_s)
inputs.description: Dry crushed granite ≈ 3000–10000 Ω·m; wet gravel ≈ 500–2000 Ω·m

standards.name: Substation Earthing Grid — Ground Resistance, Touch & Step Voltage Safety Check
standards.description: Substation Earthing Grid — Ground Resistance, Touch & Step Voltage Safety Check — Electrical Safety / Grounding Systems

formulas.expression: Cs = 1 - 0.09*(1 - soil_resistivity_ohm_m/surface_layer_resistivity) / (2*surface_layer_depth_m + 0.09); Rfoot_ohm = 1.5 * Cs * surface_layer_resistivity; E_touch_v = IB_mA/1000 * (1000 + 2*Rfoot_ohm)

D_m = Math.sqrt(grid_area_m2 / Math.round(grid_conductor_length_m / Math.sqrt(grid_area_m2))); Km = 1/(2*Math.PI) * (Math.log(D_m^2/(16*0.005*burial_depth_m)) + Math.log(D_m/(3.84*burial_depth_m))); Ki = 0.644 + 0.148*4; E_mesh_v = soil_resistivity_ohm_m * fault_current_kA * 1000 * Km * Ki / grid_conductor_length_m

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: E_mesh_v > E_touch_v
fmea.effect: Mesh voltage exceeds tolerable touch voltage: increase conductor density (reduce mesh spacing), add conductors, or increase burial depth
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: GPR_v > 5000 && Rg_ohm > 1.0
fmea.effect: GPR > 5000 V with R_g > 1 Ω: high-voltage hazard zone — telecommunications and fence isolation required per IEEE 80 §17
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: grid_conductor_length_m / grid_area_m2 < 0.5
fmea.effect: Conductor density < 0.5 m/m²: sparse grid — mesh voltage will be high; add parallel conductors to achieve minimum 1 m/m² density
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Mesh voltage within 20% of tolerable limit: marginal safety margin — add equalising conductors or equipotential mats at operator locations
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: E_mesh_v > E_touch_v * 0.8

fmea.failureMode: High soil resistivity (> 1000 Ω·m): achieving low grid resistance requires extensive grid or deep-driven rods — consider chemical ground enhancement
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: soil_resistivity_ohm_m > 1000

fmea.failureMode: R_g > 1 Ω at high fault current: GPR will be dangerously high — verify grid extends beyond substation fence; add remote earth electrodes
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Rg_ohm > 1.0 && fault_current_kA > 10

fmea.failureMode: Surface layer resistivity < 500 Ω·m: low benefit from surface treatment — increase layer thickness or use higher-resistivity dry crushed stone
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: surface_layer_resistivity < 500

fmea.failureMode: Fault clearing time > 1.0 s: tolerable voltages reduce significantly — upgrade protection relay speed or re-evaluate system protection coordination
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fault_duration_s > 1.0

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Fired Heater Thermal Efficiency — Stack Loss, Excess Air Optimisation & Process Duty
subtitle: Oil & Gas Processing / Energy Management — API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery), ISO 13705:2006, ASME PTC 4-2013
standardsBasis: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)

inScope: Fired Heater Thermal Efficiency — Stack Loss, Excess Air Optimisation & Process Duty
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Fuel Lower Heating Value (LHV)
inputs.description: Natural gas ≈ 47500; refinery fuel gas ≈ 42000–46000; fuel oil ≈ 40000–42000 kJ/kg

inputs.label: Flue Gas O₂ Content at Stack (dry basis)
inputs.description: Measured by portable O₂ analyser at stack; typical target 2–3% for gas fuel

inputs.label: Fuel Carbon Content (mass fraction)
inputs.description: Natural gas ≈ 75%; fuel oil ≈ 86%; coal ≈ 70–80%

inputs.label: Radiation & Convection Losses (% of fired duty)
inputs.description: API 560: typically 1–2% for well-insulated heaters; up to 3–5% for small units

standards.code: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)
standards.name: Fired Heater Thermal Efficiency — Stack Loss, Excess Air Optimisation & Process Duty
standards.description: Fired Heater Thermal Efficiency — Stack Loss, Excess Air Optimisation & Process Duty — Oil & Gas Processing / Energy Management

formulas.expression: m_air_stoich = 11.5 * fuel_carbon_content_pct / 100; m_fg_kg_kg = m_air_stoich * (1 + XS_air_pct/100) + 1; Q_stack_pct = m_fg_kg_kg * cp_fg_kj_kg_k * (stack_temp_c - ambient_temp_c) / fuel_LHV_kj_kg * 100
Q_fired_kw = fuel_flow_kg_h / 3600 * fuel_LHV_kj_kg; fuel_utilisation_pct = process_fluid_duty_kw / Q_fired_kw * 100

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: O2_stack_pct < 1.0
fmea.effect: Stack O₂ < 1%: risk of sub-stoichiometric combustion — CO formation, afterburning, tube coking; increase air supply immediately
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: stack_temp_c > 600
fmea.effect: Stack temperature > 600°C: severe stack heat loss — verify convection section fouling; consider air preheater installation
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: fuel_utilisation_pct < eta_th_pct - 5
fmea.effect: Process duty significantly below thermal efficiency × fired duty: check process-side fouling, flow maldistribution, or tube scaling
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Excess air > 25%: significant stack loss penalty — each 10% excess air at 300°C stack increases stack loss ≈ 0.5%; install O₂ trim controller
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: XS_air_pct > 25
fmea.control_measure: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)

fmea.failureMode: Thermal efficiency < 85%: below API 560 design target for natural gas fuel — assess convection section tube cleaning and air preheater condition
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: eta_th_pct < 85
fmea.control_measure: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)

fmea.failureMode: Stack ΔT > 350 K: air preheater economiser has strong ROI — each 20°C stack temp reduction raises efficiency ≈ 1%
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: stack_temp_c - ambient_temp_c > 350
fmea.control_measure: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)

fmea.failureMode: High O₂ (>6%) on large heater: major energy waste — reduce excess air to 2–3% target; NOₓ and stack loss both decrease
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: O2_stack_pct > 6 && fuel_flow_kg_h > 1000
fmea.control_measure: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)

fmea.failureMode: High radiation loss (>3%): check casing and refractory integrity — hot spots and casing leaks waste energy and create safety hazard
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: radiation_loss_pct > 3
fmea.control_measure: API 560 5th Ed. (Fired Heaters for General Refinery)

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Storm Water Peak Runoff — Rational Method, Time of Concentration & Culvert Capacity
subtitle: Civil / Hydraulic Engineering — ASCE 7-22, FHWA HEC-22 3rd Ed., EN 752:2017

inScope: Storm Water Peak Runoff — Rational Method, Time of Concentration & Culvert Capacity
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Weighted Runoff Coefficient (C)
inputs.description: ASCE Manual 36: impervious pavement C=0.9; lawns C=0.25–0.35; mixed urban C=0.55–0.75

inputs.label: Design Rainfall Intensity (i) for Return Period
inputs.description: From IDF (Intensity-Duration-Frequency) curve at T_c for selected return period; NOAA Atlas 14 (US)

inputs.label: Manning's Roughness Coefficient (n)
inputs.description: Concrete pipe n=0.013; corrugated metal n=0.024; smooth plastic n=0.010

standards.name: Storm Water Peak Runoff — Rational Method, Time of Concentration & Culvert Capacity
standards.description: Storm Water Peak Runoff — Rational Method, Time of Concentration & Culvert Capacity — Civil / Hydraulic Engineering

formulas.expression: A_pipe_m2 = Math.PI/4 * Math.pow(culvert_diameter_mm/1000, 2); R_h_m = culvert_diameter_mm/1000/4; Q_pipe_m3s = (1/manning_n) * A_pipe_m2 * Math.pow(R_h_m, 2/3) * Math.pow(pipe_slope_pct/100, 0.5)

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: catchment_area_ha > 80 && runoff_coeff_C > 0.5
fmea.effect: Catchment > 80 ha: Rational Method loses accuracy for large areas — use TR-55 or SCS unit hydrograph method per USDA NEH-4
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: cap_ratio < 1.0
fmea.effect: Pipe capacity insufficient for design storm: increase diameter or slope — surface flooding will occur at design return period
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: v_pipe_m_s < 0.7
fmea.effect: Flow velocity < 0.7 m/s: below self-cleansing threshold — sediment deposition and blockage; increase slope or reduce diameter
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Capacity ratio < 1.2: insufficient blockage margin per EN 752 §7.3 — upsize by one standard diameter
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: cap_ratio < 1.2

fmea.failureMode: Velocity > 4 m/s: concrete pipe erosion risk — consider energy dissipator at outlet or use abrasion-resistant liner
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: v_pipe_m_s > 4.0

fmea.failureMode: T_c < 5 min: very short time of concentration — peak rainfall intensity assumption very sensitive; verify IDF curve resolution
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Tc_min < 5

fmea.failureMode: Long return period with low C: verify C accounts for soil saturation at extended storms — effective C increases with event duration
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: return_period_yr >= 50 && runoff_coeff_C < 0.5

fmea.failureMode: Pipe slope < 0.3%: hydraulic gradient very flat — air entrainment, surcharge, and odour release risk; maintain minimum 0.5% per EN 752 §7
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: pipe_slope_pct < 0.3

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Counterbalance Forklift Stability — Load Centre Shift, Residual Capacity & Tipping Moment
subtitle: Material Handling / Occupational Safety — ISO 22915-1:2016, EN 16307-1:2019, OSHA 29 CFR 1910.178

inScope: Counterbalance Forklift Stability — Load Centre Shift, Residual Capacity & Tipping Moment
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Forklift Rated Capacity at Standard Load Centre (Q_rated)
inputs.description: Per nameplate at standard load centre (typically 500 mm for IC; 600 mm for large trucks)

options.label: 500 mm — standard IC up to 5 t

inputs.label: Actual Load Centre of Specific Payload (D_act)
inputs.description: Distance from fork face to load centre of gravity; non-standard pallet or long load will shift this

inputs.label: Attachment Centre of Gravity from Fork Face (D_att)

standards.name: Counterbalance Forklift Stability — Load Centre Shift, Residual Capacity & Tipping Moment
standards.description: Counterbalance Forklift Stability — Load Centre Shift, Residual Capacity & Tipping Moment — Material Handling / Occupational Safety

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: util_ratio > 1.0
fmea.effect: OVERLOAD: payload exceeds residual capacity at actual load centre — operation prohibited per OSHA 1910.178(o)(2) and ISO 22915-1
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: actual_load_centre_mm > std_load_centre_mm * 1.5 && payload_kg > rated_capacity_kg * 0.5
fmea.effect: Load centre >150% of standard with significant payload: verify mast and carriage deflection with OEM — structural rating may limit further
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: lift_height_mm > 6000 && util_ratio > 0.85
fmea.effect: High lift (>6 m) at >85% capacity: dynamic longitudinal stability severely reduced — reduce lift height or reduce load
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Tipping margin < 10%: dangerously close to longitudinal tip-over — reduce payload or move load CG closer to fork face; operator training mandatory per OSHA 1910.178(l)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: tip_margin_pct < 10

fmea.failureMode: Utilisation > 90% of residual capacity: marginal stability — prohibit operation on ramps, uneven surfaces, or with mast fully tilted forward
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: util_ratio > 0.9

fmea.failureMode: Attachment mass >15% of rated capacity: significant derating effect — obtain attachment data plate per ISO 22915-1 §4.3 mandatory
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: attachment_weight_kg > rated_capacity_kg * 0.15

fmea.failureMode: Forward tilt >5° at elevated height: pendulum effect amplifies tip-over moment — restrict forward tilt to minimum when elevated
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: mast_forward_tilt_deg > 5 && lift_height_mm > 2000

fmea.failureMode: Load centre > 800 mm: long or oversized load — reposition forks, use extended carriage, or engage slinging — standard stability assumptions invalid
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: actual_load_centre_mm > 800

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Cooling Tower Performance — Thermal Range, Approach, NTU & Makeup Water Rate
subtitle: Utilities / Heat Rejection / HVAC — CTI STD-201 RS-78, ASHRAE 90.1-2022 §6.5.2, ASHRAE Fundamentals Ch.40

inScope: Cooling Tower Performance — Thermal Range, Approach, NTU & Makeup Water Rate
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Ambient Wet-Bulb Temperature (T_wb)
inputs.description: Design wet-bulb from ASHRAE 0.4% cooling design data for location

inputs.label: Cycles of Concentration (COC)
inputs.description: COC = TDS_blowdown / TDS_makeup; typical 3–6; higher COC = less water use but more scaling risk

inputs.label: Drift Loss Rate (% of circulating water flow)
inputs.description: Modern fills with drift eliminators: 0.001–0.003%; CTI STD-201 design default

standards.name: Cooling Tower Performance — Thermal Range, Approach, NTU & Makeup Water Rate
standards.description: Cooling Tower Performance — Thermal Range, Approach, NTU & Makeup Water Rate — Utilities / Heat Rejection / HVAC

formulas.expression: range_k = T_hot_in_c - T_cold_out_c; approach_k = T_cold_out_c - T_wb_c
drift_kg_s = drift_loss_pct / 100 * m_dot_cw_kg_s; blowdown_kg_s = E_loss_kg_s / (cycles_of_concentration - 1); MU_kg_s = E_loss_kg_s + blowdown_kg_s + drift_kg_s
RT_capacity = Q_rejected_kw / 3.517; kW_per_RT = fan_power_kw / RT_capacity; annual_water_m3 = MU_kg_s * 3600 * 8760 / 1000

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: approach_k < 3
fmea.effect: Approach < 3 K: near theoretical minimum — tower will be very large and sensitive to wet-bulb fluctuation; verify CTI certification data
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: T_cold_out_c <= T_wb_c
fmea.effect: Cold water outlet ≤ wet-bulb temperature: thermodynamically impossible for evaporative cooling — increase T_CW,out above T_wb
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: cycles_of_concentration > 7 && Q_rejected_kw > 1000
fmea.effect: COC > 7: elevated scaling and corrosion risk — verify water treatment programme and Langelier Saturation Index per ASHRAE
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Approach < 5 K: performance highly sensitive to wet-bulb exceedances — model with 99.6% wet-bulb value, not just design point
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: approach_k < 5

fmea.failureMode: Fan power > 0.075 kW/RT: above ASHRAE 90.1-2022 fan power limit for centrifugal towers — evaluate VFD on fans for part-load savings
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: kW_per_RT > 0.075

fmea.failureMode: Annual makeup water > 100,000 m³: significant water cost — evaluate side-stream filtration, higher COC, or hybrid dry/wet tower
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: annual_water_m3 > 100000

fmea.failureMode: COC < 3: excessive blowdown — water and chemical waste; increase COC to 4–5 with proper chemical treatment to reduce makeup by 20–30%
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: cycles_of_concentration < 3

fmea.failureMode: Thermal range < 4 K: low-range system requires high water flow — verify pump sizing; consider larger ΔT to reduce circulating flow and pump energy
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: range_k < 4

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Reciprocating Compressor Cylinder Sizing — Displacement, Volumetric Efficiency & Rod Load
subtitle: Rotating Equipment / Gas Compression — API 618 5th Ed. (2007), ISO 1217:2009 Annex C, ASME PTC 9-1970 (reaffirmed 2003)

inScope: Reciprocating Compressor Cylinder Sizing — Displacement, Volumetric Efficiency & Rod Load
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Required Actual Volume Flow at Suction (Q_act)
Gas Isentropic Exponent (k = c_p / c_v)
inputs.description: Natural gas ≈ 1.27–1.31; air = 1.40; H₂ = 1.41; CO₂ = 1.28; propane = 1.13

inputs.label: Crankshaft Speed (n)
inputs.description: Slow-speed integral: 200–360 rpm; high-speed separable: 900–1800 rpm — API 618 Table 1

inputs.label: Cylinder Clearance Volume (c)
inputs.description: Fixed clearance typical range 8–20%; adjustable clearance pockets can reach 30–35%

standards.name: Reciprocating Compressor Cylinder Sizing — Displacement, Volumetric Efficiency & Rod Load
standards.description: Reciprocating Compressor Cylinder Sizing — Displacement, Volumetric Efficiency & Rod Load — Rotating Equipment / Gas Compression

formulas.expression: A_bore_m2 = Math.PI/4 * Math.pow(bore_mm/1000, 2); rod_load_comp_kn = (P_discharge_bar - P_suction_bar) * 1e5 * A_bore_m2 / 1000; rod_load_tension_kn = rod_load_comp_kn * 0.85

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: r_p > 4.5
fmea.effect: Compression ratio > 4.5: API 618 §3.1 recommends single-stage r_p ≤ 4.5 — consider two-stage compression with inter-cooling
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: eta_v < 0.55
fmea.effect: Volumetric efficiency < 55%: cylinder re-expansion dominates — clearance volume excessive or compression ratio too high; add unloader pockets
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Q_cyl_m3h < Q_actual_m3h * 0.9
fmea.effect: Single cylinder capacity insufficient for required flow — add cylinders in parallel or increase bore/speed
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: r_p > 6: very high ratio — discharge temperature will exceed 150°C for most gases; inter-stage cooling mandatory per API 618 §3.1.2
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: r_p > 6

fmea.failureMode: Rod load > 450 kN: check against frame rated rod load in API 618 datasheet — exceeded frame load causes crankshaft and crosshead failure
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: rod_load_comp_kn > 450

fmea.failureMode: High speed on large bore: piston velocity > 4 m/s — verify ring and packing wear rates; API 618 Table 1 speed limit
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: speed_rpm > 600 && bore_mm > 400

fmea.failureMode: High clearance (>25%): volumetric efficiency severely reduced — verify unloader pocket sizing and gas passage flow velocity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: clearance_pct > 25

fmea.failureMode: Discharge pressure > 100 bar: high-pressure classification — ASME B31.3 Class 600 piping, material qualification, and pressure testing per API 618 §7 required
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: P_discharge_bar > 100

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: RC Column Axial-Bending Interaction — Capacity Check, Eccentricity & Confinement
subtitle: Structural Engineering / Concrete Design — ACI 318-19 §10, §22.4, §6.2.5, EN 1992-1-1:2004 §5.8 (EC2 slenderness), AS 3600-2018 §10

inScope: RC Column Axial-Bending Interaction — Capacity Check, Eccentricity & Confinement
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

options.label: 420 MPa — ASTM A615 Gr60 / ASTM A706

inputs.label: Longitudinal Reinforcement Ratio (ρ_g)
inputs.description: ACI 318-19 §10.6.1.1: 1% ≤ ρ_g ≤ 8%; practical max 4–5% for constructability

standards.name: RC Column Axial-Bending Interaction — Capacity Check, Eccentricity & Confinement
standards.description: RC Column Axial-Bending Interaction — Capacity Check, Eccentricity & Confinement — Structural Engineering / Concrete Design

formulas.expression: Ag_mm2 = b_mm * h_mm; Ast_mm2 = rho_g_pct / 100 * Ag_mm2

d_mm = h_mm - cover_mm - 8 - 16; cb_mm = 0.003/(0.003 + fy_mpa/200000) * d_mm; ab_mm = 0.85 * cb_mm; Pb_kn = 0.65 * (0.85 * fc_prime_mpa * b_mm * ab_mm - Ast_mm2/2 * fy_mpa + Ast_mm2/2 * fy_mpa) / 1000
r_gyration = 0.3 * h_mm; kL_r = 1.0 * column_height_m * 1000 / r_gyration; slender = (kL_r > 22) ? 'SLENDER — moment magnification required per ACI §6.6.4' : 'SHORT — slenderness effects negligible'

DCR_axial = Pu_kn / phi_Pn_max_kn; phi_Mn_knm = 0.65 * Ast_mm2/2 * fy_mpa * (h_mm - 2*cover_mm - 16) / 1e6; DCR_moment = Mu_knm / phi_Mn_knm; interaction_ACI = (Pu_kn >= phi_Pn_max_kn * 0.2) ? DCR_axial + 8/9 * DCR_moment : DCR_axial/2 + DCR_moment

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: interaction_ACI > 1.0
fmea.effect: Column overstressed: increase cross-section dimensions, concrete strength, or reinforcement ratio — full P-M interaction diagram required
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: rho_g_pct > 6
fmea.effect: ρ_g > 6%: congestion risk — lap splice and concrete consolidation problems; split into two columns or increase section size
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: kL_r > 100
fmea.effect: kL/r > 100: highly slender column — second-order effects dominant; P-delta analysis and moment magnification required per ACI §6.6.4.5
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Interaction ratio > 0.9: near capacity — seismic or wind load combination check with overstrength factor per ACI §18.7
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: interaction_ACI > 0.9

fmea.failureMode: Slender column under low eccentricity: buckling controls over material failure — verify EI_eff and compute moment magnifier δ_ns per ACI §6.6.4.4
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: kL_r > 22 && e_ratio < 0.1

fmea.failureMode: Low-strength concrete at high axial load: creep and shrinkage deformations significant — long-term P-delta effects must be included per ACI §6.6.4.4
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fc_prime_mpa < 25 && Pu_kn > phi_Pn_max_kn * 0.7

fmea.failureMode: Lightly reinforced column at high axial demand: minimum 1.5% steel recommended for ductility and construction tolerance per ACI §10.6.1
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: rho_g_pct < 1.5 && DCR_axial > 0.7

fmea.failureMode: Low cover in moderate-strength concrete: consider 50 mm cover for exposed or marine environments per ACI §20.6.1.3
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: cover_mm < 30 && fc_prime_mpa < 30

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Industrial Ventilation Duct Sizing — Air Volume, Velocity, Friction Loss & Fan Selection
subtitle: HVAC / Industrial Ventilation — ASHRAE 62.1-2022, ASHRAE Fundamentals 2021 Ch.21, SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction 3rd Ed.

inScope: Industrial Ventilation Duct Sizing — Air Volume, Velocity, Friction Loss & Fan Selection
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Duct Height b (rectangular only; enter 0 for circular)
Sum of Local Loss Coefficients (ΣC_local) — Fittings, Bends, Diffusers
inputs.description: ASHRAE Fundamentals Ch.21 fitting coefficients: 90° elbow R/D=1.5 → C≈0.22; branch tee → C≈0.5–1.0

inputs.label: Air Density at Operating Conditions (ρ_air)
inputs.description: Standard air at 20°C, sea level: 1.204 kg/m³; hot process air reduces density

standards.name: Industrial Ventilation Duct Sizing — Air Volume, Velocity, Friction Loss & Fan Selection
standards.description: Industrial Ventilation Duct Sizing — Air Volume, Velocity, Friction Loss & Fan Selection — HVAC / Industrial Ventilation

formulas.expression: A_duct_m2 = (duct_shape === 'circular') ? Math.PI/4 * Math.pow(duct_dim_1_mm/1000, 2) : (duct_dim_1_mm * duct_dim_2_mm) / 1e6; v_m_s = Q_air_m3h / 3600 / A_duct_m2
Re = air_density_kg_m3 * v_m_s * D_h_mm/1000 / 1.81e-5; f = 0.02; for(let i=0;i<50;i++){f=1/Math.pow(-2*Math.log10(roughness_mm/(3.7*D_h_mm)+2.51/(Re*Math.sqrt(f))),2)}
dP_total_pa = dP_f_pa + dP_local_pa; dP_per_m_pa = dP_f_pa / duct_length_m

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: v_m_s > 12 && duct_shape === 'rectangular'
fmea.effect: Rectangular duct velocity > 12 m/s: high noise generation (NC > 45) and structural stress — reduce velocity or use circular duct
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: dP_per_m_pa > 3.0
fmea.effect: Friction rate > 3.0 Pa/m: excessive pressure drop — increase duct size; energy cost will be significant
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: v_m_s < 3.0 && Q_air_m3h > 500
fmea.effect: Supply air velocity < 3 m/s: oversized duct — risk of dust settlement in horizontal runs; minimum 5 m/s for dust-laden air streams
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Circular duct velocity > 10 m/s: exceeds SMACNA medium-pressure classification — verify duct pressure class and seam/joint rating
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: v_m_s > 10 && duct_shape === 'circular'

fmea.failureMode: Friction rate > 2 Pa/m: increase duct diameter by one standard size — velocity reduces by ~20%, friction drops ~36%
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: dP_per_m_pa > 2.0

fmea.failureMode: Fitting losses dominate: local ΔP > 50% of friction ΔP — optimise fitting selection (long-radius elbows, conical transitions) to reduce system resistance
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: local_loss_coeff_sum > duct_length_m * 0.5

fmea.failureMode: High roughness at elevated velocity: flexible duct in high-velocity application significantly increases fan energy — replace with rigid duct
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: roughness_mm > 0.5 && v_m_s > 7

fmea.failureMode: Low air density (high temp or altitude): fan performance curves must be corrected for density — rated pressure and flow will be reduced
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: air_density_kg_m3 < 1.0
