inScope: Compressed Air Receiver Volume Sizing — Peak Demand Buffer, Pressure Drop & ASME Certification
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Compressor Free Air Delivery (C_fad)
inputs.description: FAD at reference conditions: ISO 1217 — 1 bar(a), 20°C, 0% RH

inputs.label: Minimum Acceptable System Pressure — Cut-In / Process Minimum (P_min)
Duration of Peak Demand Event (t_peak)
inputs.description: Pneumatic tool burst: 5–15 s; production line demand spike: 30–120 s

inputs.label: Safety Factor for ASME / PSSR Receiver Rating

standards.name: Compressed Air Receiver Volume Sizing — Peak Demand Buffer, Pressure Drop & ASME Certification
standards.description: Compressed Air Receiver Volume Sizing — Peak Demand Buffer, Pressure Drop & ASME Certification — Utilities / Compressed Air Systems

formulas.expression: D_vessel_mm = Math.pow(V_design_m3 * 6 / Math.PI, 1/3) * 1000; P_design_bar = P_max_bar * 1.1; S_allow_mpa = 138; E_joint = 0.85; t_wall_mm = P_design_bar * 0.1 * D_vessel_mm / (2 * S_allow_mpa * E_joint - 1.2 * P_design_bar * 0.1)

T_on_min = V_design_m3 * (P_max_bar - P_min_bar) / (1.013 * compressor_capacity_m3min); T_cycle_min = V_design_m3 * (P_max_bar - P_min_bar) / (1.013 * (compressor_capacity_m3min - peak_demand_m3min / 2))

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: P_min_bar >= P_max_bar
fmea.effect: P_min must be less than P_max — check pressure band inputs
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: T_on_min < 3 && compressor_capacity_m3min < 10
fmea.effect: Compressor on-time < 3 minutes: motor short-cycling risk — increase receiver volume or reduce pressure band
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: V_design_m3 > 1 && P_max_bar > 10
fmea.effect: Receiver > 1 m³ above 10 bar: PSSR 2000 / PED 2014/68/EU category III vessel — notified body inspection and written scheme of examination mandatory
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Peak demand > 150% of compressor FAD: receiver will empty rapidly — evaluate additional compressor or demand-side management; verify peak event frequency
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: peak_demand_m3min > compressor_capacity_m3min * 1.5

fmea.failureMode: Pressure band < 1 bar: very small storage window — tight band requires frequent compressor cycling; widen band to minimum 1.5 bar or add receiver volume
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: P_max_bar - P_min_bar < 1.0

fmea.failureMode: Large high-pressure receiver: stored energy exceeds 250 bar·L — perform consequence analysis per API 521; locate receiver in blast-resistant area
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: V_design_m3 > 5 && P_max_bar > 50

fmea.failureMode: Calculated wall thickness < 4 mm: minimum practical plate thickness for receiver fabrication is 4 mm per ASME BPVC; use 4 mm minimum
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: t_wall_mm < 4

fmea.failureMode: Compressor capacity meets peak demand with no deficit: receiver sized by rule-of-thumb only — primarily for pressure stability and wet air separation, not storage
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Q_deficit_m3 === 0 && peak_demand_m3min <= compressor_capacity_m3min

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Crane Runway Beam — Maximum Wheel Load, Vertical & Lateral Bending, Fatigue Check
subtitle: Structural Engineering / Material Handling — AISC Design Guide 7 (Crane Buildings) 2nd Ed., CMAA Specification 70 (Top-Running) & 74 (Underhung), EN 1993-6:2007 (EC3 crane runways)

inScope: Crane Runway Beam — Maximum Wheel Load, Vertical & Lateral Bending, Fatigue Check
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Crane Bridge (Girder) Self-Weight (W_bridge)
inputs.description: From crane manufacturer data sheet; rough estimate: W_bridge ≈ 0.3–0.5 × W_rated for standard EOT cranes

options.label: CMAA Class C — Light service (2–5 lifts/hr)
CMAA Class F — Severe continuous duty

inputs.label: Runway Beam Section Modulus — Vertical (I_x)
inputs.description: From standard beam tables: W24×55 → I_x = 1350 cm⁴; W36×135 → I_x = 7800 cm⁴

standards.name: Crane Runway Beam — Maximum Wheel Load, Vertical & Lateral Bending, Fatigue Check
standards.description: Crane Runway Beam — Maximum Wheel Load, Vertical & Lateral Bending, Fatigue Check — Structural Engineering / Material Handling

formulas.expression: phi_v = {A:1.10, C:1.15, D:1.20, E:1.25, F:1.30}[crane_class]; P_factored_kn = P_static_kn * phi_v
beam_self_wt_kn_m = 0.8; M_max_knm = P_factored_kn * runway_beam_span_m / 4 + beam_self_wt_kn_m * Math.pow(runway_beam_span_m, 2) / 8
fatigue_cat = {A:'E (120 MPa)', C:'E (110 MPa)', D:'D (110 MPa)', E:'C (165 MPa, check)', F:'B (165 MPa, check)'}[crane_class]; stress_range_mpa = sigma_bend_mpa * 0.6

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: sigma_bend_mpa > 165
fmea.effect: Bending stress > 165 MPa: exceeds allowable for A36/S275 steel (0.66Fy) — upsize beam section or reduce span
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: runway_beam_span_m / beam_depth_mm * 1000 > 25
fmea.effect: L/d ratio > 25: shallow beam relative to span — deflection likely excessive; AISC DG7 recommends L/1000 vertical limit; check I_x
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: stress_range_mpa > 110 && crane_class === 'D'
fmea.effect: Fatigue stress range exceeds AISC Category D allowable (110 MPa) — weld details and connection design critical; consider Category C details
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Class E/F crane: heavy fatigue regime — runway beam fatigue life governs over static strength; use AISC Category B/C weld details and specify weld quality per AWS D1.1
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: crane_class === 'E' || crane_class === 'F'

fmea.failureMode: High lateral thrust: verify top flange bracing and rail clip design — AISC DG7 §4 requires continuous top flange bracing for heavy crane classes
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: H_lat_kn > crane_capacity_kn * 0.25

fmea.failureMode: Long bridge span + moderate duty: trolley skewing forces become significant — verify bumper and end stop design per CMAA 70 §3.6
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: bridge_span_m > 20 && crane_class === 'D'

fmea.failureMode: High bending stress: verify lateral-torsional buckling of top compression flange per AISC 360-22 F2 — crane beams often unbraced between columns
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: sigma_bend_mpa > 140

fmea.failureMode: Wheel load > 500 kN: heavy crane — verify column base plate, anchor bolts, and foundation design for combined gravity and lateral crane thrust per AISC DG1
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: P_factored_kn > 500

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Industrial Evaporator Design — Steam Economy, Boiling Point Rise & Heat Transfer Area
subtitle: Chemical Process Engineering / Evaporation — Perry's Chemical Engineers' Handbook 9th Ed. §11.12, TEMA Standards 10th Ed., AIChE Evaporation Design Guide
standardsBasis: Alfa Laval / GEA Evaporator Engineering Practice

inScope: Industrial Evaporator Design — Steam Economy, Boiling Point Rise & Heat Transfer Area
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Heating Steam Pressure (P_steam)
inputs.description: Steam saturation temp at 3 bar(a) = 133°C; 1.5 bar(a) = 112°C

inputs.label: Evaporator Operating Pressure (P_evap)
inputs.description: Vacuum evaporation at 0.3 bar(a) → T_sat = 69°C; reduces thermal degradation

inputs.label: Boiling Point Rise of Solution (BPR)
inputs.description: At product concentration: NaCl at 20% → BPR ≈ 2.6 K; sugar at 60% → BPR ≈ 5 K; caustic at 40% → BPR ≈ 20 K

inputs.label: Overall Heat Transfer Coefficient (U)
inputs.description: Falling film (water) ≈ 3000–5000; forced circulation (viscous) ≈ 1000–2500; natural circ. ≈ 1500–3000

standards.name: Industrial Evaporator Design — Steam Economy, Boiling Point Rise & Heat Transfer Area
standards.description: Industrial Evaporator Design — Steam Economy, Boiling Point Rise & Heat Transfer Area — Chemical Process Engineering / Evaporation

formulas.expression: V_kg_h = feed_flow_kg_h * (1 - feed_conc_pct / product_conc_pct); P_kg_h = feed_flow_kg_h - V_kg_h

T_steam_c = 100 * Math.pow(steam_pressure_bar, 0.25) + 33 * Math.pow(steam_pressure_bar, 0.05) - 33; T_evap_sat_c = 100 * Math.pow(evap_pressure_bar, 0.25) + 33 * Math.pow(evap_pressure_bar, 0.05) - 33; T_boil_c = T_evap_sat_c + BPR_k

lambda_steam_kj_kg = 2676 - 2.09 * T_steam_c; cp_feed_kj_kgk = 4.18 * (1 - feed_conc_pct/100) + 1.5 * feed_conc_pct/100; lambda_evap_kj_kg = 2676 - 2.09 * T_boil_c; Q_preheat_kw = feed_flow_kg_h / 3600 * cp_feed_kj_kgk * (T_boil_c - feed_temp_c); Q_evap_kw = V_kg_h / 3600 * lambda_evap_kj_kg; Q_steam_kw = Q_preheat_kw + Q_evap_kw
S_kg_h = Q_steam_kw * 3600 / (2676 - 2.09 * T_steam_c); steam_economy = V_kg_h / S_kg_h

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: product_conc_pct <= feed_conc_pct
fmea.effect: Product concentration must exceed feed concentration — check x_F and x_P inputs
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: dT_eff < 5
fmea.effect: Effective ΔT < 5 K: very small driving force — heat transfer area will be enormous; increase steam pressure or reduce evaporator pressure (deeper vacuum)
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: steam_economy < 0.7
fmea.effect: Steam economy < 0.7: below single-effect minimum — heat losses may be underestimated; verify feed preheat duty and BPR value
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: ΔT_eff < 10 K: economically marginal single-effect operation — evaluate multiple-effect evaporation (economy improves linearly with number of effects)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: dT_eff < 10

fmea.failureMode: BPR > 15 K: large boiling point rise severely reduces ΔT and increases steam consumption — evaluate mechanical vapour recompression (MVR) for high-BPR solutions
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: BPR_k > 15

fmea.failureMode: Large heat transfer area (> 1000 m²): multiple evaporator bodies likely required — evaluate falling film with large-diameter tubes for reduced fouling
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: A_evap_m2 > 1000

fmea.failureMode: High boiling temperature at elevated concentration: thermal degradation risk for heat-sensitive products (food, pharma) — deepen vacuum to reduce T_boil
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_boil_c > 100 && product_conc_pct > 50

fmea.failureMode: Low economy at large evaporation rate: high operating cost — each additional effect saves ~35–40% steam; payback on triple-effect typically < 2 years
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: steam_economy < 1.0 && V_kg_h > 10000

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Metallic Expansion Joint Sizing — Axial Displacement, Spring Rate & Fatigue Cycle Life
subtitle: Piping Systems / Thermal Engineering — EJMA Standards 10th Ed., EN 14917:2012, ASME B31.3-2022 §319.4

inScope: Metallic Expansion Joint Sizing — Axial Displacement, Spring Rate & Fatigue Cycle Life
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Pipe Run Length Between Fixed Anchors (L_pipe)
Number of Convolutions (Bellows Corrugations)
inputs.description: More convolutions → lower spring rate, higher cycle life, lower stress per cycle

inputs.label: Bellows Material Yield Strength at Temperature (S_y)
inputs.description: SS316L at 20°C → 205 MPa; at 400°C → 140 MPa; Inconel 625 → 415 MPa

standards.name: Metallic Expansion Joint Sizing — Axial Displacement, Spring Rate & Fatigue Cycle Life
standards.description: Metallic Expansion Joint Sizing — Axial Displacement, Spring Rate & Fatigue Cycle Life — Piping Systems / Thermal Engineering

formulas.expression: delta_T = T_operating_c - T_install_c; delta_axial_mm = pipe_material * 1e-6 * delta_T * pipe_length_m * 1000
D_m_mm = pipe_OD_mm + 30; t_bellow_mm = 0.8; f_ax_n_mm = 1.7 * Math.pow(D_m_mm, 0.5) * bellows_material_yield_mpa * Math.pow(t_bellow_mm, 3) / (Math.pow(30, 3) * num_convolutions)
F_pressure_kn = design_pressure_bar * 1e5 * Math.PI/4 * Math.pow(pipe_OD_mm/1000, 2) / 1000; F_spring_kn = f_ax_n_mm * delta_axial_mm / 1000; F_anchor_kn = F_pressure_kn + F_spring_kn

stress_ratio = sigma_m_mpa / bellows_material_yield_mpa; N_life = (stress_ratio < 0.3) ? 100000 : (stress_ratio < 0.5) ? 50000 / stress_ratio : (stress_ratio < 0.8) ? 10000 / Math.pow(stress_ratio, 3) : 1000 / Math.pow(stress_ratio, 5)

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: delta_req_mm > num_convolutions * 15
fmea.effect: Required displacement exceeds convolution capacity (15 mm/conv limit): add convolutions, use two expansion joints, or reduce anchor spacing
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: sigma_m_mpa > bellows_material_yield_mpa * 0.8
fmea.effect: Meridional stress > 80% yield: very short fatigue life — increase convolution count or specify lower spring rate bellows with larger crown height
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: N_life < 1000 && design_pressure_bar > 0
fmea.effect: Fatigue life < 1000 cycles: bellows will fail rapidly — review displacement calculation; consider tied or hinged joint if lateral movement is present
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Anchor load > 500 kN: large main anchors required — pressure thrust on unrestrained bellows is major structural load; evaluate tied universal expansion joint to eliminate thrust
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: F_anchor_kn > 500

fmea.failureMode: Carbon steel above 450°C: creep domain — ASME B31.3 requires time-dependent allowable stress; verify bellows material rating at temperature
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_operating_c > 450 && pipe_material === 11.7

fmea.failureMode: High pressure with few convolutions: instability risk — EJMA §7 squirm (in-plane and column) must be checked; add internal sleeve for pressure > 40 bar
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: design_pressure_bar > 40 && num_convolutions < 3

fmea.failureMode: Large displacement with few convolutions: excessive stress per convolution — minimum 6 convolutions for δ > 50 mm per EJMA design practice
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: delta_req_mm > 50 && num_convolutions < 6

fmea.failureMode: Fatigue life < 10,000 cycles: limited life joint — document replacement interval in maintenance plan; N_life cycles at operating temperature ΔT per EJMA C
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: N_life < 10000

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Inline Static Mixer — Pressure Drop, Mixing Length & Coefficient of Variation
subtitle: Chemical Process Engineering / Mixing — Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide, Chemineer Design Manual, Perry's Chemical Engineers' Handbook 9th Ed. §18.5
standardsBasis: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide

inScope: Inline Static Mixer — Pressure Drop, Mixing Length & Coefficient of Variation
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Process Fluid Dynamic Viscosity (μ)
inputs.description: Water at 20°C = 1.0 mPa·s; light oil ≈ 10; heavy oil ≈ 500–5000 mPa·s

inputs.label: Static Mixer Element Type
inputs.description: K_p = pressure drop factor: ΔP_mixer = K_p × ΔP_empty_pipe; from manufacturer data

inputs.label: Number of Mixer Elements (N_e)
inputs.description: Kenics: 6–12 for turbulent; 20–30 for laminar. Sulzer SMX: 5–8 elements typically

inputs.label: Required Coefficient of Variation at Mixer Exit (CoV_req)

standards.code: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide
standards.name: Inline Static Mixer — Pressure Drop, Mixing Length & Coefficient of Variation
standards.description: Inline Static Mixer — Pressure Drop, Mixing Length & Coefficient of Variation — Chemical Process Engineering / Mixing

formulas.expression: A_pipe_m2 = Math.PI / 4 * Math.pow(pipe_ID_mm / 1000, 2); v_m_s = flow_rate_m3h / 3600 / A_pipe_m2; Re = fluid_density_kg_m3 * v_m_s * pipe_ID_mm / 1000 / (fluid_viscosity_mPas / 1000)

f_fanning = (Re < 2300) ? 16/Re : 0.0791 * Math.pow(Re, -0.25); L_mixer_m = num_elements * pipe_ID_mm / 1000 * 1.5; dP_pipe_pa = 4 * f_fanning * (L_mixer_m / (pipe_ID_mm/1000)) * fluid_density_kg_m3 * Math.pow(v_m_s, 2) / 2
CoV_0 = 1.0; CoV_actual = (Re > 10000) ? CoV_0 * Math.pow(0.316, num_elements) : CoV_0 * Math.pow(0.5, num_elements * 0.7)
L_total_m = num_elements * pipe_ID_mm / 1000 * 1.5; epsilon_w_kg = dP_mixer_pa * v_m_s / fluid_density_kg_m3

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: CoV_actual > CoV_required
fmea.effect: Actual CoV exceeds required mixing quality: add more elements (N_req) or switch to higher-performance mixer type (e.g. SMX from Kenics)
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Re < 100 && mixer_type <= 8
fmea.effect: Very low Reynolds number (Re < 100): laminar segregation — Kenics HEV/SMXL insufficient for viscous mixing; use Sulzer SMX designed for Re < 10
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: dP_mixer_pa > 500000
fmea.effect: Mixer pressure drop > 5 bar: very high ΔP — consider low-pressure SMXL elements, larger pipe diameter, or reduce flow velocity
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Laminar flow with Kenics HEV: designed for turbulent use — switch to Sulzer SMX (K_p=37) optimised for viscous laminar mixing
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Re < 2300 && mixer_type === 6.3
fmea.control_measure: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide

fmea.failureMode: Element count below minimum for target CoV — mixing quality not achieved; add N_req − N_e elements or accept higher CoV
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: num_elements < N_req
fmea.control_measure: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide

fmea.failureMode: High velocity in dense fluid: erosion risk on mixer elements — verify element material hardness; consider ceramic-coated elements for slurries
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: v_m_s > 5 && fluid_density_kg_m3 > 800
fmea.control_measure: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide

fmea.failureMode: Low specific energy dissipation (<0.1 W/kg): mixing is gentle — suitable for shear-sensitive products (emulsions, cells); otherwise increase velocity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: epsilon_w_kg < 0.1 && Re > 10000
fmea.control_measure: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide

fmea.failureMode: High viscosity fluid (>10 Pa·s): static mixer will have very high ΔP — evaluate dynamic (agitated) mixer or reduce viscosity by heating before mixing
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fluid_viscosity_mPas > 10000
fmea.control_measure: Sulzer Chemtech Static Mixer Engineering Guide

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Industrial Heat Pump COP, Annual Energy Saving & Carbon Payback vs Steam Boiler
subtitle: Energy Management / Decarbonisation — EN 14511:2018 (heat pump rating), VDI 4650:2009 (COP approximation), ISO 13600:1997 (rational energy use)

inScope: Industrial Heat Pump COP, Annual Energy Saving & Carbon Payback vs Steam Boiler
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Heat Source Temperature (T_source) — Waste Heat / Ground
inputs.description: Waste cooling water ≈ 25–40°C; ground loop ≈ 8–12°C; exhaust air ≈ 20–35°C

inputs.label: Process Heat Delivery Temperature (T_delivery)
inputs.description: Low-temp drying ≈ 60–80°C; pasteurisation ≈ 85–95°C; HTHP steam ≈ 120–160°C

inputs.label: Heat Pump Carnot Efficiency Factor (η_Carnot)
inputs.description: Industrial heat pumps: η_Carnot ≈ 0.40–0.55; well-optimised: up to 0.60; VDI 4650 §4

inputs.label: Displaced Natural Gas Boiler Efficiency (η_boiler)
inputs.description: Condensing boiler ≈ 93–95%; conventional ≈ 82–88%; old steam boiler ≈ 75–82%

inputs.label: Natural Gas Carbon Emission Factor
inputs.description: UK BEIS 2024: 0.202 kgCO₂/kWh gas; EU average ≈ 0.198; US EIA ≈ 0.181 kgCO₂/kWh

inputs.label: Grid Electricity Carbon Emission Factor
inputs.description: UK grid 2024: 0.233 kgCO₂e/kWh; EU avg ≈ 0.275; US avg ≈ 0.386; decarbonised grid → < 0.10

standards.name: Industrial Heat Pump COP, Annual Energy Saving & Carbon Payback vs Steam Boiler
standards.description: Industrial Heat Pump COP, Annual Energy Saving & Carbon Payback vs Steam Boiler — Energy Management / Decarbonisation

formulas.expression: T_H_K = T_delivery_c + 273.15; T_L_K = T_source_c + 273.15; COP_Carnot = T_H_K / (T_H_K - T_L_K)
W_elec_kw = Q_delivery_kw / COP_HP; Q_source_kw = Q_delivery_kw - W_elec_kw
E_elec_kwh_yr = W_elec_kw * operating_hours_yr; Q_heat_kwh_yr = Q_delivery_kw * operating_hours_yr; E_gas_saved_kwh_yr = Q_heat_kwh_yr / (boiler_efficiency_pct / 100)

CO2_gas_saved_t = E_gas_saved_kwh_yr * gas_emission_factor_kg_kwh / 1000; CO2_elec_consumed_t = E_elec_kwh_yr * grid_emission_factor_kg_kwh / 1000; CO2_net_saving_t = CO2_gas_saved_t - CO2_elec_consumed_t

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: T_delivery_c <= T_source_c + 5
fmea.effect: Delivery temperature ≤ source temperature + 5 K: heat pump provides no thermodynamic lift — direct heat exchange is appropriate instead
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: COP_HP < 2.0
fmea.effect: COP < 2.0: marginal heat pump benefit — evaluate if Carnot efficiency factor is correct; large temperature lift with low η may not justify capital investment
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: CO2_net_saving_t < 0
fmea.effect: Net carbon saving is negative: current grid carbon intensity is too high — heat pump increases CO₂ vs gas boiler; revisit when grid decarbonises below CI_breakeven
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Delivery > 120°C: high-temperature heat pump (HTHP) territory — limited commercial units available; verify refrigerant rating (R245fa, R1234ze(Z)) per ASHRAE 34
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_delivery_c > 120

fmea.failureMode: Low COP on high-carbon grid: net carbon reduction minimal — prioritise grid decarbonisation timeline in investment decision
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: COP_HP < 3 && grid_emission_factor_kg_kwh > 0.35

fmea.failureMode: Very high Carnot COP: small temperature lift — confirm source availability is reliable year-round; COP may fall significantly in winter if source temperature drops
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: COP_Carnot > 15

fmea.failureMode: Large heat source extraction (>70% of delivery): verify source stream capacity and minimum outlet temperature — excessive extraction degrades source quality
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Q_source_kw > Q_delivery_kw * 0.7

fmea.failureMode: Break-even grid intensity > 0.5 kgCO₂/kWh: project is carbon-positive now and in most future grid scenarios — strong decarbonisation case; proceed
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: CI_breakeven_kg_kwh > 0.5

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Atmospheric Storage Tank Venting — Normal Breathing, Liquid Movement & Emergency Fire Relief
subtitle: Oil & Gas Storage / Process Safety — API 2000 7th Ed. 2014, EN 14015:2004 §15, ISO 28300:2008 (identical to API 2000)

inScope: Atmospheric Storage Tank Venting — Normal Breathing, Liquid Movement & Emergency Fire Relief
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Product Flash Point (T_flash)
inputs.description: Gasoline < 0°C; diesel ≈ 55°C; heavy fuel oil ≈ 70–120°C; crude oil varies

inputs.label: Product Reid Vapour Pressure (RVP)
inputs.description: Gasoline ≈ 55–90 kPa; crude ≈ 40–100 kPa; diesel < 10 kPa; water ≈ 2.3 kPa at 20°C

standards.name: Atmospheric Storage Tank Venting — Normal Breathing, Liquid Movement & Emergency Fire Relief
standards.description: Atmospheric Storage Tank Venting — Normal Breathing, Liquid Movement & Emergency Fire Relief — Oil & Gas Storage / Process Safety

formulas.expression: V_total_out_Nm3h = V_thermal_outbreath_Nm3h + V_fill_vent_Nm3h; V_total_in_Nm3h = V_thermal_inbreath_Nm3h + V_draw_vent_Nm3h

A_wetted_m2 = Math.PI * tank_diameter_m * Math.min(9.14, Math.sqrt(tank_capacity_m3 / (Math.PI/4 * Math.pow(tank_diameter_m, 2)))); Q_fire_kw = 43200 * Math.pow(A_wetted_m2, 0.82) / 3600; lambda_evap_kj_kg = 270; rho_vapour = 3.0; V_fire_Nm3h = Q_fire_kw * 3600 / (lambda_evap_kj_kg * rho_vapour)
V_governing_out = Math.max(V_total_out_Nm3h, V_fire_Nm3h); V_governing_in = V_total_in_Nm3h; is_emergency_governing = V_fire_Nm3h > V_total_out_Nm3h

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: product_flash_c < 23 && design_temp_c > product_flash_c
fmea.effect: Flammable vapour at ambient: tank vapour space above flash point — Class I flammable liquid requires NFPA 30 §22.6 emergency vent; flame arrester mandatory
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: fill_rate_m3h > tank_capacity_m3 * 0.5
fmea.effect: Fill rate > 50% of capacity per hour: very high fill velocity — verify anti-static precautions and bottom fill pipe velocity ≤ 1 m/s per API 2003
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: product_RVP_kpa > 110 && design_temp_c > 35
fmea.effect: High RVP product at elevated temperature: significant vapour generation — evaluate floating roof tank or nitrogen blanketing to reduce VOC emissions and fire risk
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Emergency fire venting governs: install dedicated emergency pressure-relief device per API 2000 §5.4 — PV valve alone may not provide sufficient fire relief capacity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: is_emergency_governing

fmea.failureMode: Large vent flow (>5000 Nm³/h): multiple PV valves or large conservation vent required — coordinate with atmospheric dispersion model for vapour cloud assessment
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: V_governing_out > 5000

fmea.failureMode: Large flammable product tank: Category I tank per NFPA 30 — secondary containment bunding, foam deluge system, and thermal radiation analysis required
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: product_flash_c < 60 && tank_capacity_m3 > 10000

fmea.failureMode: High temperature + high RVP: thermal out-breathing significantly elevated — verify vent device set pressure and flow rating at T_design per EN 14015 §15.4
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: design_temp_c > 50 && product_RVP_kpa > 60

fmea.failureMode: No liquid movement: vent sized for thermal breathing only — verify fill/draw rates and confirm tank is not a standby tank with infrequent but fast filling
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fill_rate_m3h === 0 && draw_rate_m3h === 0

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Activated Sludge Aeration Design — Oxygen Demand, Tank Volume, MLSS & Blower Power
subtitle: Water & Wastewater Treatment / Environmental Engineering — ATV-DVWK A-131 (2000), EN 12255-7:2001 (aeration), WEF MOP 8 6th Ed.

inScope: Activated Sludge Aeration Design — Oxygen Demand, Tank Volume, MLSS & Blower Power
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Influent Total Kjeldahl Nitrogen (TKN)
inputs.description: Domestic WW: TKN ≈ 30–60 mg/L; industrial can be higher

inputs.label: Design Sludge Retention Time (SRT / θ_c)
inputs.description: Nitrification SRT > 8–12 days at 15°C; ATV-DVWK A-131 §4.2

inputs.label: Design Mixed Liquor Suspended Solids (MLSS)
inputs.description: Conventional AS: 2000–4000 mg/L; MBR: 8000–12000 mg/L

inputs.label: Aeration System Transfer Efficiency (AE)
inputs.description: Fine bubble diffuser (submerged 4m) ≈ 1.8–2.5 kgO₂/kWh; surface aerator ≈ 1.2–1.8; coarse bubble ≈ 0.8–1.2

standards.name: Activated Sludge Aeration Design — Oxygen Demand, Tank Volume, MLSS & Blower Power
standards.description: Activated Sludge Aeration Design — Oxygen Demand, Tank Volume, MLSS & Blower Power — Water & Wastewater Treatment / Environmental Engineering

formulas.expression: BOD_removed_mg_l = BOD5_influent_mg_l - BOD5_effluent_mg_l; Y_obs = 0.5 / (1 + 0.06 * SRT_days); P_x_kg_d = Q_flow_m3d * BOD_removed_mg_l * Y_obs / 1000; OD_carb_kg_d = Q_flow_m3d * BOD_removed_mg_l / 1000 - 1.42 * P_x_kg_d
VSS_fraction = 0.75; X_VSS = MLSS_mg_l * VSS_fraction; V_tank_m3 = SRT_days * P_x_kg_d * 1000 / X_VSS

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: SRT_days < 8 && TKN_influent_mg_l > 20
fmea.effect: SRT < 8 days with significant TKN: nitrification will be incomplete at 15°C — increase SRT to minimum 10–12 days for reliable nitrification per ATV-DVWK A-131
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: HRT_h < 4
fmea.effect: HRT < 4 hours: insufficient contact time for BOD removal — increase tank volume or reduce influent flow; minimum 4 h for carbonaceous removal
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: MLSS_mg_l > 6000 && aeration_efficiency_kg_kwh > 2.5
fmea.effect: High MLSS with high AE assumption: alpha factor for fine bubble diffusers drops sharply above 5000 mg/L MLSS — use α ≤ 0.45 correction in actual SOTE calculation
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Peak-to-average ratio > 2.5: highly variable load — consider flow equalisation basin to reduce blower oversizing and energy cost
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: OD_peak_kg_h / OD_total_kg_d * 24 > 2.5

fmea.failureMode: Blower power > 500 kW: aeration is major energy consumer — evaluate turbo blowers over lobe type (15–20% energy saving) and DO control with AMP §4.3
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: P_blower_kw > 500

fmea.failureMode: HRT > 20 hours: over-designed for carbonaceous removal — evaluate extended aeration (no primary clarifier) or reduce SRT to match required effluent quality
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: HRT_h > 20

fmea.failureMode: Low aeration efficiency (<1.2 kgO₂/kWh): coarse bubble or surface aerator — fine bubble diffuser upgrade typically saves 30–40% energy; evaluate capital vs operating cost
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: aeration_efficiency_kg_kwh < 1.2

fmea.failureMode: Stringent effluent BOD target with short SRT: risk of effluent quality exceedance during peak loads — increase SRT to 12–15 days to ensure robust nitrification and BOD polishing
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: BOD5_effluent_mg_l < 10 && SRT_days < 12

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Helical Torsion Spring Design — Bending Stress, Angular Deflection & Fatigue Life
subtitle: Mechanical Design / Spring Engineering — DIN 2088:2009, ASTM A228/A227/A313, SAE HS-795 Spring Design Manual

inScope: Helical Torsion Spring Design — Bending Stress, Angular Deflection & Fatigue Life
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

options.label: Music wire ASTM A228 — E=206 GPa, Su=2060/d^0.163 MPa
Hard-drawn MB ASTM A227 — E=196 GPa, Su=1510/d^0.201 MPa
Chrome-silicon SAE 9254 — E=203 GPa, Su=1960/d^0.108 MPa
Stainless 302/304 ASTM A313 — E=193 GPa, Su=1867/d^0.146 MPa

standards.name: Helical Torsion Spring Design — Bending Stress, Angular Deflection & Fatigue Life
standards.description: Helical Torsion Spring Design — Bending Stress, Angular Deflection & Fatigue Life — Mechanical Design / Spring Engineering

formulas.expression: const SU_params = {A228:[2060,0.163], A227:[1510,0.201], '9254':[1960,0.108], A313:[1867,0.146]}; const p = SU_params[wire_material]; Su_mpa = p[0] / Math.pow(wire_diameter_mm, p[1])

const E_map = {A228:206000, A227:196000, '9254':203000, A313:193000}; E_mpa = E_map[wire_material]; R_T_Nm_deg = E_mpa * Math.PI * Math.pow(wire_diameter_mm, 4) / (3667 * mean_coil_diameter_mm * num_active_coils); T_calc_Nm = R_T_Nm_deg * angular_deflection_deg
Se_mpa = (fatigue_cycles >= 1e7) ? 0.45 * Su_mpa : 0.45 * Su_mpa * Math.pow(fatigue_cycles / 1e7, -0.085); SF_f = Se_mpa / sigma_b_mpa

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: C < 3 || C > 16
fmea.effect: Spring index C outside 3–16: C < 3 causes severe coiling stress; C > 16 causes tangling — redesign with D_m/d ratio in 4–12 range
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: SF_s < 1.0
fmea.effect: Static safety factor < 1.0: wire will yield at applied torque — increase wire diameter, reduce torque, or use higher-strength material
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Math.abs(T_calc_Nm - torque_Nm) / torque_Nm > 0.20
fmea.effect: Torque mismatch > 20%: spring geometry inconsistent with required torque at given deflection — verify N_a, θ, and T inputs
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Fatigue safety factor < 1.2: spring fatigue failure likely before target life — increase wire diameter, reduce stress ratio, or apply shot peening (+20% endurance)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: SF_f < 1.2

fmea.failureMode: Static SF < 1.5: marginal — spring may take permanent set under peak torque; consider SF ≥ 2.0 for critical applications per SAE HS-795 §7.6
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: SF_s < 1.5

fmea.failureMode: Bending stress > 90% of UTS: spring operating near material limit — significant risk of brittle fracture; redesign with larger wire or lower C
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: sigma_b_mpa > 0.9 * Su_mpa

fmea.failureMode: SS wire at high stress: austenitic stainless has lower fatigue limit than music wire — consider Elgiloy or 17-7PH for high-cycle SS applications
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: wire_material === 'A313' && sigma_b_mpa > 700

fmea.failureMode: Deflection approaching coil binding: spring may bind before reaching full deflection — verify body length and free-length tolerance
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: angular_deflection_deg > 360 * num_active_coils * 0.8

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Dust Explosion Severity — Kst Classification, Pmax & Vent Area per EN 14491 / NFPA 68
subtitle: Process Safety / Fire & Explosion Protection — EN 14491:2012 (dust explosion venting), NFPA 68-2018 (explosion protection by deflagration venting), VDI 2263 Part 1

inScope: Dust Explosion Severity — Kst Classification, Pmax & Vent Area per EN 14491 / NFPA 68
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Dust Explosion Constant (K_st)
inputs.description: From ISO 6184-1 20-L sphere test; St1: 1–200; St2: 201–300; St3: >300 bar·m/s

inputs.label: Maximum Explosion Pressure (P_max)
inputs.description: From 20-L sphere test; most organic dusts: P_max = 7–10 bar(g)

inputs.label: Maximum Reduced Explosion Pressure (P_red)
inputs.description: Structural strength of enclosure; thin sheet metal ≈ 0.05–0.1 bar(g); reinforced ≈ 0.5–2.0 bar(g)

inputs.label: Vent Closure Opening Pressure (P_stat)
inputs.description: Bursting disc or vent panel opening pressure; must be < P_red; typical 0.02–0.1 bar(g)

inputs.label: Enclosure Length-to-Diameter Ratio (L/D)
inputs.description: Silos/bins with L/D > 5: turbulence correction required; EN 14491 §5.2.3

standards.name: Dust Explosion Severity — Kst Classification, Pmax & Vent Area per EN 14491 / NFPA 68
standards.description: Dust Explosion Severity — Kst Classification, Pmax & Vent Area per EN 14491 / NFPA 68 — Process Safety / Fire & Explosion Protection

formulas.expression: Av_V_ratio = A_v_design_m2 / vessel_volume_m3; governing = (A_v_design_m2 >= A_nfpa_m2) ? 'EN 14491 governs' : 'NFPA 68 governs'

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Pstat_bar >= Pred_bar
fmea.effect: P_stat ≥ P_red: vent will not open before structural failure — P_stat must be ≤ 0.5 × P_red per EN 14491 §4.2
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Pred_bar >= Pmax_bar
fmea.effect: P_red ≥ P_max: structurally the enclosure withstands full explosion — venting not required for protection; verify intent
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: dust_class === 'St3' && L_D_ratio > 5
fmea.effect: St3 dust in elongated vessel: EN 14491 nomograph validity limit exceeded — perform CFD or consult VDI 2263 Part 7 for St3 + L/D > 5 combinations
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: St3 dust: highly energetic explosion — venting alone may be insufficient; evaluate suppression system, inherently safe design, or inerting as primary protection per NFPA 69
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Kst_bar_m_s > 300

fmea.failureMode: Vent area > 50% of vessel volume (proxy): very large vent relative to enclosure — verify structural integrity of remaining panels; consider suppression as alternative
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: A_v_design_m2 > vessel_volume_m3 * 0.5

fmea.failureMode: EN and NFPA results differ by > 30%: at boundary conditions or outside nomograph validity — seek qualified dust explosion specialist review
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Math.abs(A_v_design_m2 - A_nfpa_m2) / A_v_design_m2 > 0.3

fmea.failureMode: Very low P_red on large volume: extremely large vent area required — light-duty enclosures (P_red < 0.07 bar) unsuitable for large dust collectors; reinforce or use suppression
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Pred_bar < 0.07 && vessel_volume_m3 > 100

fmea.failureMode: High-end St1 approaching St2 boundary: ensure test conditions (concentration, moisture, particle size) represent worst-case — conservative K_st selection critical for vent sizing
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: dust_class === 'St1' && Kst_bar_m_s > 150

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Roof Truss Member Force & Joint Reaction — Simple Warren / Pratt Configuration
subtitle: Structural Engineering / Steel Design — AISC 360-22 Chapters D, E, F, ASCE 7-22 §2.3, §7, §27, EN 1993-1-1:2022 §6.2

inScope: Roof Truss Member Force & Joint Reaction — Simple Warren / Pratt Configuration
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Dead Load — Roof Cladding + Self-Weight (g_k)
inputs.description: Metal deck + insulation ≈ 0.4–0.7 kN/m²; concrete slab ≈ 3–5 kN/m²

inputs.label: Imposed / Snow Load (q_k)
inputs.description: EN 1991-1-3 snow or ASCE 7-22 §7 — regional ground snow × shape factor

inputs.label: Design Wind Uplift Pressure (w_u)
inputs.description: Negative (uplift) wind pressure; ASCE 7-22 §27 or EN 1991-1-4 Cp,e

standards.name: Roof Truss Member Force & Joint Reaction — Simple Warren / Pratt Configuration
standards.description: Roof Truss Member Force & Joint Reaction — Simple Warren / Pratt Configuration — Structural Engineering / Steel Design

formulas.expression: w_dead_kn_m = dead_load_kn_m2 * tributary_width_m; w_live_kn_m = live_load_kn_m2 * tributary_width_m; w_factored_kn_m = 1.2 * w_dead_kn_m + 1.6 * w_live_kn_m; panel_length_m = truss_span_m / num_panels; P_u_kn = w_factored_kn_m * panel_length_m
M_max_knm = R_kn * truss_span_m / 2 - P_u_kn * panel_length_m * num_panels / 4; F_BChord_kn = M_max_knm / truss_height_m
panel_diagonal_m = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(panel_length_m, 2) + Math.pow(truss_height_m, 2)); theta_rad = Math.atan(truss_height_m / panel_length_m); F_TChord_kn = F_BChord_kn / Math.cos(theta_rad) * (-1)
V_max_kn = R_kn - P_u_kn; F_diag_kn = V_max_kn / Math.sin(theta_rad)
phi_t = 0.90; A_req_mm2 = F_BChord_kn * 1000 / (phi_t * steel_grade_fy_mpa); wind_uplift_factor = wind_uplift_kn_m2 * tributary_width_m * panel_length_m

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: truss_height_m / truss_span_m < 0.05
fmea.effect: Truss depth-to-span ratio < 1:20: very shallow truss — deflection and chord force will be excessive; minimum h/L = 1/10 recommended per AISC DG11
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: wind_uplift_kn_m2 > dead_load_kn_m2 * 0.9
fmea.effect: Wind uplift > 90% of dead load: net uplift possible under 0.9D + 1.0W — bottom chord becomes compression; verify buckling capacity and purlin connections
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: num_panels < 4
fmea.effect: Less than 4 panels: very steep diagonal angle — excessive web member forces; use minimum 6 panels for efficient truss geometry
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Long shallow truss: chord forces very large — chord section and connection design critical; camber ≥ L/500 required per AISC Code of Standard Practice
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: truss_span_m > 40 && truss_height_m / truss_span_m < 0.08

fmea.failureMode: Top chord compression > 2000 kN: heavy member required — verify lateral buckling between purlin points per AISC 360-22 Chapter E; ensure continuous lateral restraint
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: F_TChord_kn > 2000

fmea.failureMode: High-strength steel on long span: deflection (not strength) may govern — S460 reduces weight but deflection limit L/360 still applies; verify serviceability
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: steel_grade_fy_mpa > 355 && truss_span_m > 30

fmea.failureMode: Light dead load with heavy imposed load: dynamic amplification possible for crowds or machinery — check vibration criteria per SCI P354 or AISC DG11
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: dead_load_kn_m2 < 0.5 && live_load_kn_m2 > 1.5

fmea.failureMode: Required bottom chord area > 15,000 mm²: very large section — consider HSS, built-up section, or reduce span with intermediate column; verify connection capacity at joints
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: A_req_mm2 > 15000

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Orifice Plate Flow Meter Sizing — Beta Ratio, Discharge Coefficient & Differential Pressure
subtitle: Instrumentation / Flow Measurement — ISO 5167-1:2022 §6, ISO 5167-2:2022 (orifice plates), ASME MFC-3M-2004

inScope: Orifice Plate Flow Meter Sizing — Beta Ratio, Discharge Coefficient & Differential Pressure
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Design (Maximum) Volume Flow Rate at Operating Conditions (Q_op)
Fluid Density at Operating Conditions (ρ)
inputs.description: Gas: use operating density ρ = PM/(ZRT); liquid: use process temperature density

inputs.label: Fluid Dynamic Viscosity at Operating Conditions (μ)
Maximum Allowable Differential Pressure (ΔP_max)
inputs.description: Transmitter range; higher ΔP → better accuracy; lower ΔP → lower permanent pressure loss

options.label: Corner taps — ISO 5167 standard, most common
Flange taps (1 inch from faces) — ASME / AGA 3

inputs.label: Gas Isentropic Exponent (κ) — Set 1.0 for liquid
inputs.description: Liquid κ = 1.0 (no expansibility correction); air κ = 1.4; steam ≈ 1.3; natural gas ≈ 1.27

standards.name: Orifice Plate Flow Meter Sizing — Beta Ratio, Discharge Coefficient & Differential Pressure
standards.description: Orifice Plate Flow Meter Sizing — Beta Ratio, Discharge Coefficient & Differential Pressure — Instrumentation / Flow Measurement

formulas.expression: A_pipe_m2 = Math.PI / 4 * Math.pow(pipe_ID_mm / 1000, 2); v_pipe_m_s = design_flow_m3h / 3600 / A_pipe_m2; Re_D = fluid_density_kg_m3 * v_pipe_m_s * pipe_ID_mm / 1000 / (fluid_viscosity_mPas / 1000)

Cd_est = 0.6; epsilon = (isentropic_exponent > 1.0) ? 1 - 0.41 * Math.pow(0.5, 1/isentropic_exponent) : 1.0; beta = Math.pow(1 / (1 + Math.pow(Cd_est * epsilon * A_pipe_m2 * Math.sqrt(2 * max_dP_kpa * 1000 / fluid_density_kg_m3) / (design_flow_m3h / 3600), 2)), 0.25)

b4 = Math.pow(beta, 4); A_coef = (19000 * beta / Re_D) < 1 ? 0 : 0.0188 + 0.0063 * Math.pow(19000 * beta / Re_D, 0.8); Cd = 0.5961 + 0.0261 * beta * beta - 0.216 * Math.pow(beta, 8) + 0.000521 * Math.pow(1e6 * beta / Re_D, 0.7) + (0.0188 + 0.0063 * A_coef) * Math.pow(beta, 3.5) * Math.pow(1e6 / Re_D, 0.3)

epsilon_act = (isentropic_exponent > 1.0) ? 1 - (0.351 + 0.256 * Math.pow(beta, 4) + 0.93 * Math.pow(beta, 8)) * (1 - Math.pow(1 - max_dP_kpa * 1000 / (fluid_density_kg_m3 * v_pipe_m_s * v_pipe_m_s / 2 * 100), 1/isentropic_exponent)) : 1.0; dP_actual_kpa = Math.pow(design_flow_m3h / 3600 / (Cd * epsilon_act * A_pipe_m2 * Math.pow(beta, 2) / Math.sqrt(1 - Math.pow(beta, 4))), 2) * fluid_density_kg_m3 / 2 / 1000
E_factor = 1 / Math.sqrt(1 - Math.pow(beta, 4)); dP_perm_kpa = dP_actual_kpa * (1 - Math.pow(beta, 2)) * (1 - Cd * Math.pow(beta, 2) * E_factor)

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: beta < 0.20 || beta > 0.75
fmea.effect: Beta ratio outside 0.20–0.75: ISO 5167-2 validity range exceeded — accuracy degrades sharply; select ΔP range to keep β in 0.30–0.65 for optimal accuracy
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Re_D < 5000
fmea.effect: Pipe Reynolds number < 5000: ISO 5167 RHG equation loses accuracy below Re = 5000; consider Coriolis or magnetic flowmeter for low-Re applications
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: dP_perm_kpa > max_dP_kpa * 0.8
fmea.effect: Permanent pressure loss > 80% of ΔP_max: high energy penalty — consider low-loss flow element (Venturi, V-cone, or Annubar) to reduce permanent loss
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: β > 0.65: high beta ratio — discharge coefficient uncertainty increases; upstream straight pipe requirement extends to 44D (vs 10D at β=0.3); verify straight run length
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: beta > 0.65

fmea.failureMode: High gas velocity (>30 m/s): compressibility and expansibility correction significant — verify ε factor is applied; choked flow check required if ΔP/P > 0.03
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: v_pipe_m_s > 30 && fluid_density_kg_m3 < 5

fmea.failureMode: Actual ΔP exceeds transmitter range: flow measurement will saturate — increase beta ratio or specify higher-range transmitter
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: dP_actual_kpa > max_dP_kpa * 1.1

fmea.failureMode: High viscosity fluid (>50 mPa·s): orifice plate accuracy degrades for viscous fluids — consider Coriolis or gear meter for μ > 100 mPa·s
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fluid_viscosity_mPas > 50

fmea.failureMode: Low Re with high β: Cd correction for low-Re is significant — apply RHG equation iteratively; 3–5 iterations typically required for convergence to ±0.1%
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Re_D < 20000 && beta > 0.5

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Electroplating Bath — Faraday Deposition Rate, Plating Time & Rectifier Power
subtitle: Surface Engineering / Manufacturing — ISO 2081:2018 (zinc electroplating), ASTM B633-19 (zinc on iron/steel), EN ISO 4527:2003 (autocatalytic nickel)

inScope: Electroplating Bath — Faraday Deposition Rate, Plating Time & Rectifier Power
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Total Part Surface Area to be Plated (A_part)
inputs.description: 1 dm² = 100 cm² = 0.01 m²; measure exposed area only

inputs.label: Required Plating Thickness (δ)
inputs.description: ISO 2081 Zn: Fe/Zn 8c = 8 µm; Fe/Zn 25c = 25 µm; EN ISO 4527 Ni: min 25 µm

inputs.label: Applied Current Density (i)
inputs.description: Zinc acid bath: 2–6 A/dm²; decorative Cr: 15–25 A/dm²; hard Cr: 25–60 A/dm²; Au (connector): 0.1–0.5 A/dm²

inputs.label: Cathode Current Efficiency (η_c)
inputs.description: Zn acid: 95–99%; Ni sulfamate: 95–98%; Cr (hexavalent): 10–25%; Cu sulfate: 95–100%

inputs.label: Cell (Bath) Voltage (U_cell)
inputs.description: Zn ≈ 4–8 V; Ni ≈ 5–8 V; decorative Cr ≈ 6–12 V; hard Cr ≈ 12–24 V

standards.name: Electroplating Bath — Faraday Deposition Rate, Plating Time & Rectifier Power
standards.description: Electroplating Bath — Faraday Deposition Rate, Plating Time & Rectifier Power — Surface Engineering / Manufacturing

formulas.expression: const m2 = METALS[metal_deposited]; r_dep_um_min = E_eq_g_Ah * current_density_a_dm2 * cathode_efficiency_pct / 100 * 10000 / (m2.rho * 60)
P_rect_kw = I_rect_a * bath_voltage_v / 1000; E_spec_kw_dm2 = P_rect_kw / part_surface_area_dm2
const m3 = METALS[metal_deposited]; W_deposit_g = E_eq_g_Ah * I_rect_a * t_plate_min / 60 * cathode_efficiency_pct / 100; W_anode_g = W_deposit_g / (cathode_efficiency_pct / 100) * 1.05

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: cathode_efficiency_pct < 20 && metal_deposited !== 'Cr'
fmea.effect: Cathode efficiency < 20% for non-chromium process: abnormally low — check bath chemistry pH, temperature, and additive concentrations
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: current_density_a_dm2 > 60 && metal_deposited === 'Cr'
fmea.effect: Current density > 60 A/dm² for Cr: hard chrome territory — bath temperature control critical (50–60°C); burning and pitting risk above CD limit
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: t_plate_min > 120
fmea.effect: Plating time > 2 hours: very long cycle — evaluate higher current density, tank agitation, or split into two baths; bath conductivity and thermal management critical for extended runs
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Chromium cathode efficiency > 30%: unusually high for hexavalent Cr bath — verify input; trivalent Cr (Cr³⁺) baths achieve 80–95% efficiency but require different parameters
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: metal_deposited === 'Cr' && cathode_efficiency_pct > 30

fmea.failureMode: Rectifier current > 10,000 A: industrial-scale rectifier — specify thyristor or IGBT rectifier with ±1% voltage regulation; busbar cross-section critical for voltage uniformity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: I_rect_a > 10000

fmea.failureMode: High power input at elevated bath temperature: Joule heating will raise bath temperature — specify bath cooling system capacity as Q_cool ≈ P_rect × 0.6 kW
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: P_rect_kw > 100 && bath_temperature_c > 50

fmea.failureMode: Gold plating > 5 µm: very high material cost — verify specification requires full gold; consider gold flash (0.5 µm) over nickel undercoat for connector applications per MIL-G-45204
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: metal_deposited === 'Au' && target_thickness_um > 5

fmea.failureMode: Low CD on large area: very long plating time — evaluate rack density, multiple simultaneous racks, or continuous plating line for high-volume parts
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: current_density_a_dm2 < 1 && part_surface_area_dm2 > 1000

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Chiller Plant Free-Cooling Economiser — Switchover Temperature, Annual Hours & Energy Saving
subtitle: HVAC / Energy Management — ASHRAE 90.1-2022 §6.5.1.1, CIBSE TM21:2016 (Minimising fan energy), EN 14511:2018

inScope: Chiller Plant Free-Cooling Economiser — Switchover Temperature, Annual Hours & Energy Saving
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Chiller Full-Load COP (rated condition)
inputs.description: AHRI 551/591 rating at 7/12°C CHWS/R, 30/35°C CWS/R

inputs.label: Cooling Tower / Dry Cooler Approach Temperature (ΔT_approach)
inputs.description: Wet cooling tower: approach = T_CW,out − T_wb; dry cooler: approach = T_fluid,out − T_db

inputs.label: Plate Heat Exchanger Temperature Approach (ΔT_HX)
inputs.description: Brazed plate HX: ΔT_HX = 1.5–3 K; gasketed frame: 2–5 K

inputs.label: Site Climate Zone — Bin Hours Below Switchover

standards.name: Chiller Plant Free-Cooling Economiser — Switchover Temperature, Annual Hours & Energy Saving
standards.description: Chiller Plant Free-Cooling Economiser — Switchover Temperature, Annual Hours & Energy Saving — HVAC / Energy Management

formulas.expression: pump_fan_parasitic_kw = design_cooling_load_kw * 0.03; E_saving_kwh = (design_cooling_load_kw / chiller_COP - pump_fan_parasitic_kw) * h_FC
cost_saving_usd = E_saving_kwh * electricity_rate_usd_kwh; HX_cost_est_usd = design_cooling_load_kw * 40; payback_yr = HX_cost_est_usd / cost_saving_usd

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: T_wb_sw_c < 0 && site_climate_zone < 1000
fmea.effect: Switchover WB temperature below 0°C in warm climate: free-cooling hours will be negligible — evaluate dry cooler or adiabatic pre-cooling to raise T_wb,sw

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: chilled_water_supply_c < 6 && heat_exchanger_dT_k < 1.5
fmea.effect: Very low CHWS temp with tight HX approach: fouling will increase ΔT_HX rapidly — specify minimum HX surface oversize of 20% per TEMA C

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: F_FC > 0.8
fmea.effect: Free-cooling fraction > 80%: chiller operates less than 20% of time — evaluate chiller downsizing or VFD for part-load optimisation; chiller may fall below minimum unload point

fmea.failureMode: Free-cooling payback < 2 years: strong ROI — prioritise HX installation in next capital cycle; consider controls upgrade for automatic switchover per ASHRAE 90.1 §6.5.1
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: payback_yr < 2

fmea.failureMode: Warm climate with few free-cooling hours: marginal economic case — evaluate night-time free-cooling for data centres where T_wb is lower overnight
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: site_climate_zone < 500

fmea.failureMode: High cooling tower approach (>6 K): tower fouled or undersized — cleaning or replacement can recover 2–3 K and extend free-cooling season significantly
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: approach_temp_k > 6

fmea.failureMode: Free-cooling saves >50% of baseline chiller energy: very favourable climate — evaluate tri-mode (full/partial/zero mechanical) controls for transitional hours
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: E_saving_kwh > E_chiller_base_kwh * 0.5

fmea.failureMode: Low-efficiency chiller in cold climate: both free-cooling AND chiller replacement are warranted — combined ROI should be modelled jointly
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: chiller_COP < 4 && site_climate_zone > 2000

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Cathodic Protection Design — Sacrificial Anode Current Demand, Mass & Service Life
subtitle: Corrosion Engineering / Asset Integrity — DNV-RP-B401:2021, ISO 15589-2:2012, NACE SP0169-2013

inScope: Cathodic Protection Design — Sacrificial Anode Current Demand, Mass & Service Life
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Coating Breakdown Factor at End of Life (f_c)
inputs.description: DNV-RP-B401 Table 10-1: well-coated new: 3–5%; aged coating: 10–25%; bare/uncoated: 100%

inputs.label: Design Current Density (i_c) — Mean Value
inputs.description: DNV-RP-B401 Table 10-3: North Sea seabed ≈ 20–25 mA/m²; tropical shallow water ≈ 50–80 mA/m²; soil ≈ 10–30 mA/m²

options.label: Zinc (seawater) — ε=780 Ah/kg, E=−1.05 V vs Ag/AgCl

inputs.label: Anode Utilisation Factor (u)
inputs.description: DNV-RP-B401 §6.4: bracelet anodes u=0.80; flush-mounted u=0.90; stand-off u=0.85

options.label: −900 mV — Elevated temperature or H₂S service

standards.name: Cathodic Protection Design — Sacrificial Anode Current Demand, Mass & Service Life
standards.description: Cathodic Protection Design — Sacrificial Anode Current Demand, Mass & Service Life — Corrosion Engineering / Asset Integrity

formulas.expression: const ECA = {Zn:780, AlIn:2000, Mg:1230}[anode_material]; Ma_kg = Q_T_Ah / (ECA * anode_utilisation_pct / 100)

const E_anode = {Zn:-1050, AlIn:-1050, Mg:-1550}[anode_material]; dE_mv = E_anode - protection_potential_mv; resistance_est_ohm = 0.315 * Math.sqrt(A_eff_m2 / N_a) / 10; I_output_A = dE_mv / 1000 / resistance_est_ohm

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: coating_breakdown_pct === 100 && current_density_mA_m2 < 30
fmea.effect: Bare steel with low current density: verify environment — bare steel in stagnant seawater may require 50–100 mA/m² per DNV Table 10-3

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: anode_material === 'Zn' && current_density_mA_m2 > 50
fmea.effect: Zinc anodes at high current density: driving voltage insufficient for tropical warm water — switch to Al-In alloy with higher electrochemical efficiency

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: Ma_kg > 100000
fmea.effect: Total anode mass > 100 tonnes: very large CP system — evaluate impressed current cathodic protection (ICCP) for structures > 20,000 m²; lower OPEX despite higher CAPEX

fmea.failureMode: Zinc anodes for 20+ year design life: mass penalty is severe — Al-In provides 2.6× more charge per kg at same potential; mandatory for deepwater and long-life structures per DNV §5.2
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: design_life_yr > 20 && anode_material === 'Zn'

fmea.failureMode: Driving voltage < 150 mV: anode current output will be marginal — verify polarisation resistance of structure; may need to increase anode count by 40% for adequate protection
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: dE_mv < 150

fmea.failureMode: Over-protection risk with Mg anodes (E < −900 mV): hydrogen embrittlement possible on high-strength steels (Fy > 550 MPa) — limit to −950 mV per NACE SP0169 §6.2.2
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: protection_potential_mv < -900 && anode_material === 'Mg'

fmea.failureMode: Large exposed area with few anodes: current distribution will be uneven — increase anode count and space evenly; maximum spacing = 3 m for bare surfaces per DNV §10.3
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: A_eff_m2 > 5000 && N_a < 20

fmea.failureMode: Low coating breakdown (<3%): CP demand is minimal — verify coating integrity with holiday detection per NACE SP0188; CP as backup protection only
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: coating_breakdown_pct < 3

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Steam Boiler Thermal Efficiency — Indirect Loss Method, Flue Gas Dew Point & Stack Heat Loss
subtitle: Energy Management / Steam Systems — ASME PTC 4-2013 (Fired Steam Generators), EN 12952-15:2016, EN 303-5:2021 (CO limits)

inScope: Steam Boiler Thermal Efficiency — Indirect Loss Method, Flue Gas Dew Point & Stack Heat Loss
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

options.label: Natural gas — LHV=47500 kJ/kg, C=75%, H=24%, S=0%
Fuel oil No.6 HFO — LHV=40600 kJ/kg, C=86%, H=11%, S=2.5%
LPG Propane — LHV=46350 kJ/kg, C=82%, H=18%, S=0%
Coal (bituminous) — LHV=26000 kJ/kg, C=73%, H=5%, S=1.5%

inputs.label: Flue Gas O₂ Concentration (dry basis)
inputs.description: Measured by portable O₂ analyser; natural gas target: 2–3% O₂

inputs.label: Flue Gas CO Concentration (dry basis)
inputs.description: < 100 ppm: good combustion; > 400 ppm: incomplete combustion — EN 303-5 limit: 250 ppm

inputs.label: Surface Radiation & Convection Loss (% of fired duty)
inputs.description: ASME PTC 4 Appendix B: well-insulated gas boiler ≈ 0.3–0.8%; older/larger ≈ 0.5–1.5%

standards.name: Steam Boiler Thermal Efficiency — Indirect Loss Method, Flue Gas Dew Point & Stack Heat Loss
standards.description: Steam Boiler Thermal Efficiency — Indirect Loss Method, Flue Gas Dew Point & Stack Heat Loss — Energy Management / Steam Systems

formulas.expression: const FC = {NG:{LHV:47500,C:0.75,H:0.24,S:0}, HFO:{LHV:40600,C:0.86,H:0.11,S:0.025}, LPG:{LHV:46350,C:0.82,H:0.18,S:0}, Coal:{LHV:26000,C:0.73,H:0.05,S:0.015}}; const fp = FC[fuel_type]; LHV_kj_kg=fp.LHV; C_frac=fp.C; H_frac=fp.H; S_frac=fp.S

m_air_stoich = 11.5 * C_frac + 34.5 * H_frac + 4.3 * S_frac; m_fg_kg_kg = m_air_stoich * (1 + XS_air_pct/100) + 1; cp_fg = 1.004 + 0.000254 * stack_temp_c; L_fg_pct = m_fg_kg_kg * cp_fg * (stack_temp_c - ambient_temp_c) / LHV_kj_kg * 100

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: eta_th_pct < 75
fmea.effect: Boiler efficiency < 75%: critically low — stack temperature and/or excess air are excessive; immediate combustion optimisation and flue gas audit required

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: CO_stack_ppm > 400
fmea.effect: CO > 400 ppm: incomplete combustion — adjust air-to-fuel ratio; CO > 1000 ppm indicates safety risk of CO accumulation in boiler room

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: stack_temp_c < T_dew_c + 10
fmea.effect: Stack temperature within 10°C of acid dew point: sulphuric acid condensation risk — economiser and stack lining corrosion; maintain stack temperature ≥ T_dew + 20°C

fmea.failureMode: Excess air > 30%: major energy waste — each 10% excess air at 250°C stack raises stack loss ≈ 0.6%; install O₂ trim control for continuous optimisation
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: XS_air_pct > 30

fmea.failureMode: Stack temperature > 250°C on gas-fired boiler: economiser opportunity — recovering heat to 130°C typically saves 4–8% fuel; verify dew point before sizing
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: stack_temp_c > 250 && fuel_type === 'NG'

fmea.failureMode: Natural gas boiler below 88% efficiency: condensing technology available — condensing boilers achieve 92–97% (NCV basis) by recovering latent heat of flue gas water vapour
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: eta_th_pct < 88 && fuel_type === 'NG'

fmea.failureMode: Low O₂ + high CO: sub-stoichiometric combustion — immediate safety risk of CO poisoning and explosion; increase combustion air immediately and check burner condition
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: flue_gas_O2_pct < 1.5 && CO_stack_ppm > 200

fmea.failureMode: Stack loss > 15%: dominant efficiency penalty — target: natural gas boiler stack loss ≤ 10% at 230°C stack; prioritise air preheating or economiser installation
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: L_fg_pct > 15

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Cantilever Retaining Wall — Overturning, Sliding, Bearing & Global Stability Check
subtitle: Geotechnical Engineering / Civil Structures — EN 1997-1:2004 (EC7) §6, §9, ASCE 7-22 §11 (seismic), NAVFAC DM-7.2 §5

inScope: Cantilever Retaining Wall — Overturning, Sliding, Bearing & Global Stability Check
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Backfill Friction Angle (φ_b)
inputs.description: Compacted granular fill ≈ 32–38°; gravel ≈ 35–42°; sandy silt ≈ 28–32°

inputs.label: Uniform Surcharge on Retained Surface (q_s)
inputs.description: Highway: 10–20 kPa; railway: 20–50 kPa; construction traffic: 12 kPa per AASHTO

inputs.label: Base-Soil Friction Angle (φ_base)
inputs.description: Concrete on soil: δ = 2/3·φ_soil; rough concrete on gravel: δ ≈ φ_soil

inputs.label: Allowable Bearing Pressure of Foundation Soil (q_allow)
inputs.description: From geotechnical investigation; sand ≈ 150–300 kPa; gravel ≈ 300–600 kPa; clay varies widely

standards.name: Cantilever Retaining Wall — Overturning, Sliding, Bearing & Global Stability Check
standards.description: Cantilever Retaining Wall — Overturning, Sliding, Bearing & Global Stability Check — Geotechnical Engineering / Civil Structures

formulas.expression: phi_rad = phi_backfill_deg * Math.PI / 180; Ka = Math.pow(Math.tan(Math.PI/4 - phi_rad/2), 2)
Pa_soil_kn_m = 0.5 * Ka * gamma_backfill_kn_m3 * Math.pow(wall_height_m, 2); Pa_surcharge_kn_m = Ka * surcharge_kpa * wall_height_m; Pa_total_kn_m = Pa_soil_kn_m + Pa_surcharge_kn_m

M_OT = Pa_soil_kn_m * wall_height_m/3 + Pa_surcharge_kn_m * wall_height_m/2; W_wall_kn_m = wall_concrete_kn_m3 * base_width_m * 0.3; W_soil_kn_m = gamma_backfill_kn_m3 * wall_height_m * (base_width_m * 0.6); M_R = (W_wall_kn_m + W_soil_kn_m) * base_width_m / 2
phi_base_rad = phi_base_deg * Math.PI / 180; W_total = W_wall_kn_m + W_soil_kn_m; F_resist = W_total * Math.tan(phi_base_rad); SSF = F_resist / Pa_total_kn_m
W_total_kn_m = W_wall_kn_m + W_soil_kn_m; e = base_width_m/2 - (M_R - M_OT)/W_total_kn_m; q_max_kpa = W_total_kn_m/base_width_m * (1 + 6*e/base_width_m)

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: OTF < 1.5
fmea.effect: Overturning safety factor < 1.5: wall will overturn — increase base width, add heel extension, or install ground anchor
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: SSF < 1.2
fmea.effect: Sliding safety factor < 1.2: wall will slide — add base key, increase base width, or install batter piles
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: q_max_kpa > q_allow_kpa
fmea.effect: Maximum bearing pressure exceeds allowable: foundation failure risk — widen base, reduce eccentricity, or improve ground by compaction or piling

fmea.failureMode: OTF < 2.0: EN 1997-1 DA1 requirement not met for permanent structures — increase base width to B ≥ 0.6H for typical granular backfill
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: OTF < 2.0

fmea.failureMode: Sliding SF < 1.5: marginal — NAVFAC DM-7.2 requires SSF ≥ 1.5; add shear key 300 mm deep at front third of base for significant resistance improvement
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: SSF < 1.5

fmea.failureMode: High surcharge on weak backfill: active pressure substantially elevated — verify drainage behind wall; waterlogged granular fill can reach K_0 = 1.0
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: surcharge_kpa > 20 && phi_backfill_deg < 28

fmea.failureMode: High wall with marginal OTF: seismic loading will reduce OTF further — ASCE 7 §11 seismic lateral earth pressure adds 50–100% to static thrust; perform seismic analysis
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: wall_height_m > 6 && OTF < 2.5

fmea.failureMode: Bearing pressure > 80% of allowable: settlement risk under sustained surcharge — monitor differential settlement; consider ground improvement if soft soil detected below
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: q_max_kpa > q_allow_kpa * 0.8

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Biomass Combustion — Moisture-Corrected NCV, Combustion Air & Boiler Rating
subtitle: Renewable Energy / Biomass / Energy Management — EN ISO 18134:2017 (moisture), EN ISO 18125:2017 (calorific value), EN ISO 17225:2021 (fuel specs)

inScope: Biomass Combustion — Moisture-Corrected NCV, Combustion Air & Boiler Rating
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

options.label: Wood pellets EN ISO 17225-2 — GCV_dry=19800 kJ/kg

inputs.label: Fuel Moisture Content (M) — as-received basis
inputs.description: EN ISO 18134-3 gravimetric method; fresh wood: 40–55%; air-dried: 20–30%; pellets: 8–12%

inputs.label: Hydrogen Content of Dry Fuel (H_dry)
inputs.description: From ultimate analysis EN ISO 16948; woody biomass ≈ 5.8–6.2%; straw ≈ 5.5–6.0%

inputs.label: Boiler Thermal Efficiency (η_boiler)
inputs.description: EN 303-5 Class 5 (pellets): ≥ 88%; wood chips direct: 75–85%; stoker-fired straw: 78–86%

inputs.label: Oxygen Content in Flue Gas (O₂, dry)
inputs.description: Biomass requires higher excess air than gas (6–12% O₂); grate combustion: 7–10%; fluidised bed: 3–6%

inputs.label: Fuel Bulk Density (ρ_bulk)
inputs.description: Wood chips G30 ≈ 200–300 kg/m³; pellets 6 mm ≈ 600–700 kg/m³; straw bales ≈ 130–180 kg/m³

standards.name: Biomass Combustion — Moisture-Corrected NCV, Combustion Air & Boiler Rating
standards.description: Biomass Combustion — Moisture-Corrected NCV, Combustion Air & Boiler Rating — Renewable Energy / Biomass / Energy Management

formulas.expression: const GCV_map = {wood_chips:19500, pellets:19800, straw:17500, miscanthus:18000, sawdust:19000}; GCV_dry_kj_kg = GCV_map[biomass_type]
M_frac = moisture_content_pct / 100; NCV_ar_kj_kg = GCV_dry_kj_kg * (1 - M_frac) - 2440 * (M_frac + 0.09 * H_content_dry_pct/100 * (1 - M_frac))
XS_air_pct = O2_flue_pct * 100 / (21 - O2_flue_pct); m_air_stoich_kg_kg = 8.89 * (1 - moisture_content_pct/100); m_air_actual_kg_kg = m_air_stoich_kg_kg * (1 + XS_air_pct / 100)

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: NCV_ar_kj_kg < 5000
fmea.effect: NCV_ar < 5000 kJ/kg: very wet fuel — combustion may not self-sustain below 35% moisture without auxiliary burner support; dry fuel below 25% moisture for reliable combustion

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: moisture_content_pct > 50 && biomass_type === 'pellets'
fmea.effect: Pellet moisture cannot exceed 50% — EN ISO 17225-2 Class A1 pellets: M ≤ 10%; M > 50% physically impossible for pellets; check input
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: O2_flue_pct < 4 && biomass_type === 'straw'
fmea.effect: O₂ < 4% for straw combustion: high CO and unburned carbon risk — straw requires O₂ ≥ 6% due to irregular particle shape; increase secondary air

fmea.failureMode: Moisture > 35%: NCV drops sharply and boiler efficiency falls significantly — each 10% moisture increase reduces NCV by ~15%; pre-dry fuel or select lower-moisture grade per EN ISO 17225
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: moisture_content_pct > 35

fmea.failureMode: NCV_ar < 10 MJ/kg: low energy density — fuel consumption and storage volume approximately double vs dry fuel; evaluate chip drying or switch to pellets
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: NCV_ar_kj_kg < 10000

fmea.failureMode: Excess air > 80%: very high for biomass — stack losses will be excessive; target 40–60% excess air (O₂ 7–9%) for grate-fired systems per EN 303-5
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: XS_air_pct > 80

fmea.failureMode: Daily fuel volume > 50 m³: large biomass logistics requirement — size vehicle access, unloading equipment, and fire compartmentation per NFPA 664
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: V_fuel_daily_m3 > 50

fmea.failureMode: Pellet boiler efficiency < 80%: below EN 303-5 Class 4 minimum for pellet appliances — verify combustion tuning, grate design, and heat exchanger fouling
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: boiler_efficiency_pct < 80 && biomass_type === 'pellets'

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Fillet Weld Sizing — Effective Throat, Shear Capacity & Minimum Leg Size per AWS D1.1 / EC3
subtitle: Steel Fabrication / Welding Engineering — AWS D1.1-2020 §2.4, Table 8.8, EN 1993-1-8:2005 §4.5.3, ISO 2553:2019

inScope: Fillet Weld Sizing — Effective Throat, Shear Capacity & Minimum Leg Size per AWS D1.1 / EC3
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Applied Shear Force on Weld Group (V_u / V_Ed)
Applied Normal (Tension/Compression) Force on Weld (N_u / N_Ed)
Effective Weld Length (L_eff) — excluding craters
inputs.description: AWS D1.1 §2.5: L_eff = L_total − 2×leg size (end craters); minimum L_eff ≥ 4×leg size

inputs.label: Thickness of Thicker Connected Part (t_max)
inputs.description: Controls minimum leg size per AWS D1.1 Table 8.8 and EN 1993-1-8 §4.5.3

standards.name: Fillet Weld Sizing — Effective Throat, Shear Capacity & Minimum Leg Size per AWS D1.1 / EC3
standards.description: Fillet Weld Sizing — Effective Throat, Shear Capacity & Minimum Leg Size per AWS D1.1 / EC3 — Steel Fabrication / Welding Engineering

formulas.expression: a_min_aws_mm = (thicker_part_mm <= 6) ? 3 : (thicker_part_mm <= 12) ? 5 : (thicker_part_mm <= 19) ? 6 : (thicker_part_mm <= 38) ? 8 : 10; a_min_ec3_mm = Math.max(Math.sqrt(thicker_part_mm) - 0.5, 3); a_min_mm = (weld_standard === 'AWS') ? a_min_aws_mm : a_min_ec3_mm

F_w_mpa = (weld_standard === 'AWS') ? 0.6 * filler_metal_fexx_mpa * 0.707 : (filler_metal_fexx_mpa / Math.sqrt(3)) / 1.25; demand_kn_mm = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(V_demand_kn, 2) + Math.pow(N_demand_kn, 2)) / weld_length_mm; a_req_mm = demand_kn_mm * 1000 / F_w_mpa
F_w_mpa = (weld_standard === 'AWS') ? 0.75 * 0.6 * filler_metal_fexx_mpa : filler_metal_fexx_mpa / (Math.sqrt(3) * 1.25); phi_Rn_kn = F_w_mpa * A_w_mm2 / 1000
V_resultant_kn = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(V_demand_kn, 2) + Math.pow(N_demand_kn, 2)); DCR_weld = V_resultant_kn / phi_Rn_kn

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: DCR_weld > 1.0
fmea.effect: Weld group overstressed — increase leg size, add weld length, or select higher-strength filler metal
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: a_design_mm < a_min_mm
fmea.effect: Design leg size below code minimum for plate thickness — minimum leg size per AWS Table 8.8 / EC3 §4.5.3 must be met regardless of calculated demand
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: weld_length_mm < 4 * a_design_mm
fmea.effect: Weld length < 4× leg size: weld too short relative to size — AWS D1.1 §2.5 minimum effective length requirement violated; increase length or reduce leg size

fmea.failureMode: Weld leg > 75% of plate thickness: excessive heat input risk — risk of burn-through and distortion; maximum weld leg = t − 1.5 mm for plates < 12 mm per AWS §2.5.2
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: a_design_mm > thicker_part_mm * 0.75

fmea.failureMode: Weld utilisation > 85%: limited reserve for dynamic or fatigue loading — increase leg size by minimum 2 mm or add return welds at ends
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DCR_weld > 0.85

fmea.failureMode: Undermatching filler metal: F_EXX < 1.2×F_y of base metal — acceptable for ductility in some cases, but verify per AWS D1.1 Annex I for high-strength steel
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: filler_metal_fexx_mpa < base_metal_Fy_mpa * 1.2

fmea.failureMode: Small weld leg under high load: verify welder qualification — leg size < 5 mm requires special quality control per ISO 3834-2
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: a_design_mm < 5 && V_demand_kn > 50

fmea.failureMode: Significant tension component: combined stress state — verify weld orientation; transverse welds to applied tension are 50% stronger than longitudinal per AWS Annex B
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: N_demand_kn > V_demand_kn * 0.5
