subtitle: CNC Machining / Threading Operations — ISO 68-1:1998, ISO 965-1:1998, DIN 13-1:1999

inScope: CNC Thread Milling & Tapping — Tap Drill Diameter, Helix Angle, Lead & Cutting Parameters
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Required Thread Engagement Depth (L_eng)
inputs.description: ISO 898-1: minimum engagement = 1.0D for steel-in-steel; 1.5D for bolt into aluminium

inputs.label: Cutting Speed for Tapping / Thread Milling (V_c)

standards.name: CNC Thread Milling & Tapping — Tap Drill Diameter, Helix Angle, Lead & Cutting Parameters
standards.description: CNC Thread Milling & Tapping — Tap Drill Diameter, Helix Angle, Lead & Cutting Parameters — CNC Machining / Threading Operations

formulas.expression: Vc_factor = {flood:1.0, MQL:0.80, dry:0.60}[coolant_type]; Vc_corrected = Vc_m_min * Vc_factor
const Vc_mult = {P:1.0, M:1.8, K:0.7, N:0.4, S:2.5}[workpiece_material_group]; Mc_Nm = 0.00022 * Vc_mult * Math.pow(nominal_D, 2.6) * pitch_mm

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: tap_drill_mm > nominal_D * 0.95
fmea.effect: Tap drill diameter > 95% of nominal: insufficient thread material — tap drill calculation error; verify thread pitch
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: thread_depth_mm < nominal_D * 0.8 && workpiece_material_group !== 'P'
fmea.effect: Thread engagement < 0.8D in non-steel material: strip-out risk — increase engagement to minimum 1.5D for aluminium, 2.0D for plastics

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: n_rpm > 10000 && nominal_D > 10
fmea.effect: High speed on large tap (D > 10 mm): cutting speed is aggressive — verify machine spindle torque rating at this RPM; tap breakage risk increases significantly

fmea.failureMode: Dry tapping in stainless steel: tap seizure imminent — stainless work-hardens rapidly; mandatory MQL or flood coolant; reduce Vc to ≤ 8 m/min
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: workpiece_material_group === 'M' && coolant_type === 'dry'

fmea.failureMode: Cutting speed too high for superalloy threading: V_c > 6 m/min in Ni/Ti alloys causes rapid BUE and tap fracture — reduce to 3–5 m/min and use HSSE-PM or solid carbide tap
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: workpiece_material_group === 'S' && Vc_m_min > 6

fmea.failureMode: Very small helix angle (< 2°): fine pitch thread — chip evacuation critical; use spiral flute tap (right-hand helix) for blind holes to eject chips upward
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: alpha_deg < 2

fmea.failureMode: High feed rate on small tap: synchronisation error of ±1 rpm causes proportionally large pitch error — verify CNC rigid tapping encoder resolution; use compensation chuck below M8
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Vf_mm_min > 500 && nominal_D < 8

fmea.failureMode: Deep blind hole threading: chip accumulation risk — use spiral flute tap and retract every 5 mm for chip evacuation; flood coolant at maximum pressure mandatory
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: thread_depth_mm > nominal_D * 2.5

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Steel Beam Deflection Under Service Load — Elastic Deflection, Camber & L/d Efficiency
subtitle: Structural Steel / Construction Engineering — EN 1993-1-1:2022 §7.2, AISC 360-22 Appendix 2, SCI P355:2011

inScope: Steel Beam Deflection Under Service Load — Elastic Deflection, Camber & L/d Efficiency
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Second Moment of Area About Strong Axis (I_x)
inputs.description: From section tables: UB 406×178×67 → I_x = 24330 cm⁴; IPE 360 → I_x = 16270 cm⁴

standards.name: Steel Beam Deflection Under Service Load — Elastic Deflection, Camber & L/d Efficiency
standards.description: Steel Beam Deflection Under Service Load — Elastic Deflection, Camber & L/d Efficiency — Structural Steel / Construction Engineering

formulas.expression: Cd_map = {SS: 5/384, FF: 1/384, FP: 1/185, cant: 1/8}; Cd = Cd_map[end_condition]
E_steel = 210000; I_m4 = Ix_cm4 * 1e-8; L_m = span_m; delta_DL_mm = Cd * w_dead_kn_m * 1000 * Math.pow(L_m, 4) / (E_steel * 1e6 * I_m4) * 1000

delta_LL_mm = Cd * w_live_kn_m * 1000 * Math.pow(span_m, 4) / (210000e6 * Ix_cm4 * 1e-8) * 1000 + (P_point_kn > 0 ? 1/48 * P_point_kn * 1000 * Math.pow(span_m, 3) / (210000e6 * Ix_cm4 * 1e-8) * 1000 : 0)
delta_total_mm = delta_DL_mm + delta_LL_mm; delta_allow_mm = span_m * 1000 / deflection_limit_ratio

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: deflection_util > 1.0
fmea.effect: Live load deflection exceeds allowable limit — increase section size (I_x), reduce span with intermediate support, or accept lower deflection limit

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: delta_total_mm > span_m * 10
fmea.effect: Total deflection exceeds L/100: extreme deflection — verify input values; beam is severely undersized or span is unrealistic for this load

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: Ld_ratio > 30 && w_live_kn_m > 5
fmea.effect: L/d > 30 with significant live load: shallow beam will deflect excessively — consider L/d ≤ 20 as starting point for preliminary sizing under office / industrial loads

fmea.failureMode: Camber specified on non-simply-supported beam: verify that end connection allows free rotation and that camber direction is consistent with actual dead load deflection shape
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: camber_mm > 0 && end_condition !== 'SS'

fmea.failureMode: Deflection utilisation > 85%: marginal — consider one section deeper; increasing depth by 50 mm typically reduces δ by 15–20% for the same weight
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: deflection_util > 0.85

fmea.failureMode: Dead load deflection > L/400: significant long-term creep and ponding risk on flat roofs — EC3 recommends δ_DL + creep < L/300 for roofs per §7.2.1
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: delta_DL_mm > span_m * 1000 / 400

fmea.failureMode: Precision deflection limit on shallow beam: floor vibration may govern before deflection limit — check natural frequency per SCI P354 (fn > 4 Hz for office floors)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: deflection_limit_ratio >= 500 && Ld_ratio > 20

fmea.failureMode: Long cantilever (> 3 m): deflection tip grows as L³; very sensitive to load position — verify back-span anchorage and connection rotation capacity; dynamic check mandatory per SCI P076
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: end_condition === 'cant' && span_m > 3

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Surface Roughness Conversion — Ra, Rz, Rq, Rt & Machining Process Capability
subtitle: Quality Engineering / Metrology / CNC Machining — ISO 4287:1997, ISO 4288:1996, ISO 1302:2002

inScope: Surface Roughness Conversion — Ra, Rz, Rq, Rt & Machining Process Capability
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Arithmetic Mean Roughness (Ra)
inputs.description: ISO 4287 §4.2.1 — Ra: most universal specification parameter; measured by profilometer

inputs.label: Evaluation Cut-off Length (λ_c)
inputs.description: ISO 4288:1996 Table 1 — standard cut-off selection based on expected Ra range

inputs.label: Measurement Traverse Length (L_t)
inputs.description: ISO 4288: L_t = 5 × λ_c for standard measurement (5 sampling lengths)

standards.name: Surface Roughness Conversion — Ra, Rz, Rq, Rt & Machining Process Capability
standards.description: Surface Roughness Conversion — Ra, Rz, Rq, Rt & Machining Process Capability — Quality Engineering / Metrology / CNC Machining

formulas.expression: const PR = {grinding:[0.1,1.6], turning_finish:[0.8,3.2], turning_rough:[3.2,12.5], milling_finish:[0.8,3.2], milling_rough:[3.2,12.5], honing:[0.05,0.4], lapping:[0.01,0.2], edm:[0.4,6.3], shot_blast:[6.3,25]}; const r=PR[machining_process]; Ra_min_proc=r[0]; Ra_max_proc=r[1]; in_range = Ra_um >= Ra_min_proc && Ra_um <= Ra_max_proc

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: !in_range
fmea.effect: Specified Ra is outside the normal capability of the selected machining process — verify process selection or accept that special tooling, passes, and conditions are required

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: Ra_um < 0.05 && machining_process !== 'lapping' && machining_process !== 'honing'
fmea.effect: Ra < 0.05 µm with standard machining: only achievable by lapping, superfinishing, or polishing — specify correct process

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: measurement_traverse_mm < 5 * cutoff_lambda_c_mm
fmea.effect: Traverse length < 5 × cut-off length: ISO 4288 requires minimum 5 sampling lengths for reliable Ra measurement — increase traverse length

fmea.failureMode: Ra < 0.4 µm from EDM: requires mirror EDM with very low energy settings — expect 5–10× longer machining time vs standard EDM at Ra 1.6 µm
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Ra_um < 0.4 && machining_process === 'edm'

fmea.failureMode: Ra inconsistency: specified Ra > 3.2 µm but N-value requests ≤ 1.6 µm — review which parameter is the actual requirement; Ra from profilometer takes precedence
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Ra_um > 3.2 && N_value <= 7

fmea.failureMode: High Rz on finish turning: check insert nose condition and cutting parameters — Rz > 25 µm suggests chatter or built-up edge; increase Vc or reduce feed
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Rz_um > 25 && machining_process === 'turning_finish'

fmea.failureMode: Very fine surface with long cut-off: ISO 4288 requires λ_c = 0.25 mm for Ra < 0.1 µm — using 0.8 mm cut-off will include waviness in Ra and give falsely high reading
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Ra_um < 0.2 && cutoff_lambda_c_mm > 0.8

fmea.failureMode: N-number and Ra values are inconsistent by more than 2×: specify one parameter and derive the other — do not independently specify both to avoid conflicting drawing requirements
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Ra_from_N_um > Ra_um * 2 || Ra_from_N_um < Ra_um * 0.5

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Rebar Development & Lap Splice Length — Tension, Compression & Seismic Requirements
subtitle: Concrete Construction / Reinforcement Engineering — ACI 318-19 §25.4, §25.5, EN 1992-1-1:2004 §8.4, §8.7, ACI 318-19 §18.8 (seismic)

inScope: Rebar Development & Lap Splice Length — Tension, Compression & Seismic Requirements
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Concrete Cover to Bar Surface (c)
inputs.description: ACI 318 §20.6.1: columns/beams min 40 mm; slabs min 20 mm; exposure class XS: min 50 mm

inputs.label: Clear Spacing Between Bars (c_s)
inputs.description: ACI §25.2.1: minimum clear spacing ≥ d_b, 25 mm, or 4/3 × aggregate size

options.label: Tension — seismic (Class SDC D/E/F or DCM/DCH)
Ties/stirrups present meeting ACI §25.5.2 or EC2 §8.7

standards.name: Rebar Development & Lap Splice Length — Tension, Compression & Seismic Requirements
standards.description: Rebar Development & Lap Splice Length — Tension, Compression & Seismic Requirements — Concrete Construction / Reinforcement Engineering

formulas.expression: lambda = 1.0; psi_t = 1.0; psi_e = 1.0; psi_s = (bar_diameter_mm <= 19) ? 0.8 : 1.0; cb = Math.min(cover_mm + bar_diameter_mm/2, bar_spacing_mm/2 + bar_diameter_mm/2); Ktr = (transverse_reinforcement === 'ties') ? 40 * 0.00129 * bar_diameter_mm / bar_diameter_mm * 10 : 0; cb_plus_Ktr_db = Math.min((cb + Ktr) / bar_diameter_mm, 2.5); ld_ACI_mm = (3 * fy_mpa * psi_t * psi_e * psi_s * lambda / (40 * lambda * Math.sqrt(fc_prime_mpa) * cb_plus_Ktr_db)) * bar_diameter_mm

fbd_mpa = 2.25 * 1.0 * 1.0 * 0.21 * Math.pow(fc_prime_mpa, 2/3); lb_req_mm = (bar_diameter_mm / 4) * (fy_mpa / (1.15 * fbd_mpa)); alpha1=1.0; alpha2=Math.max(1 - 0.15*(cover_mm - bar_diameter_mm)/bar_diameter_mm, 0.7); lbd_EC2_mm = Math.max(alpha1 * alpha2 * lb_req_mm, Math.max(0.3 * lb_req_mm, 10 * bar_diameter_mm, 100))
splice_factor = (load_type === 'seismic') ? 1.7 : (transverse_reinforcement === 'none') ? 1.3 : 1.3; ld_governing = Math.max(ld_ACI_mm, lbd_EC2_mm); ls_mm = ld_governing * splice_factor

ldc_ACI_mm = (fy_mpa <= 420) ? Math.max(0.24 * fy_mpa / Math.sqrt(fc_prime_mpa) * bar_diameter_mm, 0.043 * fy_mpa * bar_diameter_mm) : Math.max((0.25 * fy_mpa - 24) / Math.sqrt(fc_prime_mpa) * bar_diameter_mm, 0.043 * fy_mpa * bar_diameter_mm); ldc_mm = Math.max(ldc_ACI_mm, 200)
l_governing_mm = (load_type === 'compression') ? ldc_mm : ls_mm; l_bars = Math.ceil(l_governing_mm / bar_diameter_mm)

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: cover_mm < bar_diameter_mm
fmea.effect: Concrete cover less than bar diameter: ACI §20.6.1 minimum cover requirement violated — fire resistance, corrosion protection, and bond transfer all inadequate
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: bar_spacing_mm < bar_diameter_mm
fmea.effect: Clear bar spacing less than bar diameter: ACI §25.2.1 minimum spacing violated — concrete cannot flow between bars; splitting failure and poor consolidation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: load_type === 'seismic' && fc_prime_mpa < 25
fmea.effect: Seismic splices in low-strength concrete: ACI 318 §18.12.2 recommends f'_c ≥ 25 MPa in special moment frames; low concrete strength requires significantly longer lap lengths

fmea.failureMode: Lap splice > 60 bar diameters: very long splice — consider mechanical couplers (ISO 15835-1) or welded splices to reduce congestion; check if staggering splices is feasible
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: l_governing_mm > 60 * bar_diameter_mm

fmea.failureMode: Large bar (d_b > 28 mm) in seismic zone: ACI 318 §18.8.2.1 prohibits lap splices within beam-column joints and in potential plastic hinge zones — use mechanical couplers
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: load_type === 'seismic' && bar_diameter_mm > 28

fmea.failureMode: No transverse reinforcement on tension splice: splitting failure risk — provide minimum ties per ACI §25.5.2; minimum 2 ties within splice length at third-points
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: transverse_reinforcement === 'none' && load_type === 'tension'

fmea.failureMode: Concrete strength below recommended for large bars: development length will be excessive and bond stress may cause splitting — upgrade to recommended f'_c
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: !fc_adequate && bar_diameter_mm >= 25

fmea.failureMode: EC2 development length > 1.5× ACI value: significant code disparity — verify which standard governs for the project; EC2 tends to be more conservative for poor cover conditions
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: lbd_EC2_mm > ld_ACI_mm * 1.5

name: Hollow Section (RHS/SHS/CHS) Beam-Column — Combined Axial + Bending Interaction Check
subtitle: Structural Steel / Steel Fabrication — EN 1993-1-1:2022 §6.2.9, §6.3.1, §6.3.3, CIDECT Design Guide 2 (2nd Ed.), AISC 360-22 Chapter H

inScope: Hollow Section (RHS/SHS/CHS) Beam-Column — Combined Axial + Bending Interaction Check
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Factored Axial Load (N_Ed / P_u)
inputs.description: Positive = compression; negative = tension

inputs.label: Cross-Sectional Area (A)
inputs.description: From CIDECT/manufacturer tables: RHS 200×100×8 → A=4448 mm²; SHS 150×150×8 → A=4448 mm²

inputs.label: Plastic Section Modulus About Bending Axis (W_pl,y)
Second Moment of Area About Bending Axis (I_y)
Radius of Gyration About Bending Axis (i_y)

standards.name: Hollow Section (RHS/SHS/CHS) Beam-Column — Combined Axial + Bending Interaction Check
standards.description: Hollow Section (RHS/SHS/CHS) Beam-Column — Combined Axial + Bending Interaction Check — Structural Steel / Steel Fabrication

formulas.expression: lambda_bar = (L_eff_m * 1000 / i_y_mm) / (Math.PI * Math.sqrt(210000 / fy_mpa)); phi_buck = 0.5 * (1 + 0.21 * (lambda_bar - 0.2) + Math.pow(lambda_bar, 2)); chi = Math.min(1.0, 1 / (phi_buck + Math.sqrt(Math.pow(phi_buck, 2) - Math.pow(lambda_bar, 2))))
k_yy = 1 + (lambda_bar - 0.2) * N_Ed_kn / NbRd_kn; k_yy = Math.min(k_yy, 1.0 + 0.8 * N_Ed_kn / NbRd_kn); UC_6_61 = N_Ed_kn / NbRd_kn + k_yy * M_Ed_knm / MRd_knm

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: UC_6_61 > 1.0
fmea.effect: Beam-column interaction check failed (EC3 Eq.6.61 > 1.0) — increase section size, reduce effective length, or reduce applied loads
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: UC_cross > 1.0
fmea.effect: Cross-section combined loading check failed — section yields locally; use plastic moment capacity only if section is Class 1
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: lambda_bar > 2.0
fmea.effect: Non-dimensional slenderness > 2.0: highly slender — buckling governs heavily; N_b,Rd < 0.25·N_Rd; intermediate bracing strongly recommended

fmea.failureMode: Utilisation ratio > 90%: minimal safety reserve — verify load combinations include all accidental and wind/seismic actions; do not proceed without structural engineer sign-off
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: UC_6_61 > 0.90 || UC_cross > 0.90

fmea.failureMode: High axial + moderate moment: k_yy amplification significant — consider moment connection detail to reduce effective eccentricity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: N_Ed_kn > NbRd_kn * 0.7 && M_Ed_knm > MRd_knm * 0.3

fmea.failureMode: Slender CHS column: LTB does not apply for CHS — only flexural buckling governs; CHS advantage over open sections is fully realised here
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: lambda_bar > 1.5 && section_type === 'CHS'

fmea.failureMode: Long column in S235: consider S355 to reduce section size — same χ at lower weight for slenderness-governed design
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: L_eff_m > 8 && fy_mpa === 235

fmea.failureMode: Buckling factor χ < 0.5: more than 50% of section capacity lost to buckling — verify effective length assumptions; pinned connections often provide partial restraint; consider intermediate bracing
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: chi < 0.5

name: Precision Boring & Reaming — ISO Fit Selection, Tolerance Band & Achievable Surface Roughness
subtitle: CNC Machining / Precision Engineering / Metrology — ISO 286-1:2010, ISO 286-2:2010 (preferred fits), ISO 2768-1:1989

inScope: Precision Boring & Reaming — ISO Fit Selection, Tolerance Band & Achievable Surface Roughness
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

options.label: IT5 — Precision bearings, gauge bores (Ra ≤ 0.4 µm)
IT6 — General precision, ball bearings (Ra ≤ 0.8 µm)
IT8 — Medium precision, loose fits (Ra ≤ 3.2 µm)
IT9 — Coarse press fits, rough bores (Ra ≤ 6.3 µm)
H — Zero lower deviation (standard hole basis)

inputs.label: Mating Shaft Designation (e.g. h6, f7, k6)

options.label: Honing after boring — IT5–IT6, Ra 0.05–0.4 µm

inputs.label: Cutting Speed (V_c)
inputs.description: Reaming steel: 8–15 m/min; boring steel: 60–180 m/min; boring aluminium: 200–500 m/min

inputs.label: Feed per Revolution (f_r)
inputs.description: Reaming: 0.1–0.5 mm/rev; fine boring: 0.03–0.15 mm/rev; rough boring: 0.2–1.0 mm/rev

standards.name: Precision Boring & Reaming — ISO Fit Selection, Tolerance Band & Achievable Surface Roughness
standards.description: Precision Boring & Reaming — ISO Fit Selection, Tolerance Band & Achievable Surface Roughness — CNC Machining / Precision Engineering / Metrology

formulas.expression: D_mean = Math.sqrt(Math.max(1, nominal_diameter_mm - 3) * nominal_diameter_mm); i_micron = 0.45 * Math.pow(D_mean, 1/3) + 0.001 * D_mean; const IT_factor = {5:7, 6:10, 7:16, 8:25, 9:40}; IT_um = IT_factor[ISO_hole_tolerance] * i_micron
EI_um = 0; ES_um = IT_um; bore_min_mm = nominal_diameter_mm + EI_um/1000; bore_max_mm = nominal_diameter_mm + ES_um/1000

const shaft_offsets = {h6:{ei:-IT_um*0.4, es:0}, h7:{ei:-IT_um*0.6, es:0}, f7:{ei:-IT_um*1.6, es:-IT_um}, g6:{ei:-IT_um*0.7, es:-IT_um*0.3}, k6:{ei:0, es:IT_um*0.5}, p6:{ei:IT_um*0.5, es:IT_um*1.2}, s6:{ei:IT_um*1.2, es:IT_um*2.0}}; const sf = shaft_offsets[shaft_designation]; shaft_min_mm = nominal_diameter_mm + sf.ei/1000; shaft_max_mm = nominal_diameter_mm + sf.es/1000
n_rpm = Vc_m_min * 1000 / (Math.PI * nominal_diameter_mm); Vf_mm_min = n_rpm * feed_per_rev_mm

const Ra_cap = {fine_boring:[0.2,0.8], reaming:[0.4,1.6], rough_boring:[3.2,12.5], honing:[0.05,0.4]}; const rc = Ra_cap[boring_tool]; Ra_achievable_min=rc[0]; Ra_achievable_max=rc[1]; const IT_Ra_map = {5:0.4, 6:0.8, 7:1.6, 8:3.2, 9:6.3}; Ra_req_max = IT_Ra_map[ISO_hole_tolerance]; process_capable = Ra_achievable_max <= Ra_req_max

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: !process_capable
fmea.effect: Selected boring/reaming process cannot achieve required Ra for specified IT grade — upgrade to finer process (e.g., add honing after boring for IT5/IT6)

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: min_clearance_um > 200 && shaft_designation !== 'f7'
fmea.effect: Minimum clearance > 200 µm: very loose fit — verify shaft designation is correct; this may cause bearing misalignment or excessive play

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: Vf_mm_min > 500 && boring_tool === 'reaming'
fmea.effect: Table feed > 500 mm/min for reaming: excessive — maximum feed for reaming is typically 150 mm/min; higher feeds cause chatter marks and dimensional errors

fmea.failureMode: Unexpected interference fit: verify shaft designation — transition fits (k6) can have interference but assembly may require press or thermal expansion method
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: min_clearance_um < -50 && shaft_designation !== 'p6' && shaft_designation !== 's6'

fmea.failureMode: IT tolerance < 10 µm required but rough boring selected: this process cannot hold ≤ IT5 — mandatory fine boring or honing; tool setup and thermal stabilisation critical
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: IT_um < 10 && boring_tool === 'rough_boring'

fmea.failureMode: Large bore (D > 500 mm) with tight IT6 tolerance: thermal expansion alone can exceed IT value — workpiece must be measured at 20°C ±1°C per ISO 1 temperature standard
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: nominal_diameter_mm > 500 && ISO_hole_tolerance <= 6

fmea.failureMode: Reaming speed > 150 m/min: built-up edge and chatter risk — reaming is inherently a low-speed finishing process; reduce to 8–15 m/min for steel, 30–50 m/min for aluminium
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Vc_m_min > 150 && boring_tool === 'reaming'

fmea.failureMode: Wide clearance band relative to IT: verify mating shaft tolerance is correctly specified — total system clearance variation may affect running accuracy or press-fit security
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: max_clearance_um - min_clearance_um > IT_um * 3

inputs.description: Bracket with standoff distance: e = horizontal distance from load to weld group centroid

standards.name: Welded T-Joint & Angle Bracket — Weld Group Moment, Shear & Torsion Capacity
standards.description: Welded T-Joint & Angle Bracket — Weld Group Moment, Shear & Torsion Capacity — Steel Fabrication / Structural Connections

formulas.expression: a_throat = weld_leg_mm * 0.707; const patterns = {three_side: {A: (2*plate_height_mm + plate_width_mm)*a_throat, Iy: (2*Math.pow(plate_height_mm,3)/12 + plate_width_mm*Math.pow(plate_height_mm/2,2))*a_throat/1e6}, four_side: {A: 2*(plate_width_mm+plate_height_mm)*a_throat, Iy: (2*plate_width_mm*Math.pow(plate_height_mm/2,2) + 2*Math.pow(plate_height_mm,3)/12)*a_throat/1e6}}; const wp = patterns['four_side']; Aw_mm2 = 2*(plate_width_mm + plate_height_mm) * a_throat; Iy_mm4 = (2*plate_width_mm*Math.pow(plate_height_mm,2)/4 + 2*Math.pow(plate_height_mm,3)/12) * a_throat
M_total_Nmm = (M_Ed_knm + V_Ed_kn * eccentricity_mm / 1000) * 1e6; fb_mpa = M_total_Nmm * (plate_height_mm / 2) / Iy_mm4
f_resultant_mpa = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(fv_mpa, 2) + Math.pow(fb_mpa, 2)); UC_weld = f_resultant_mpa / f_w_allow_mpa

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: UC_weld > 1.0
fmea.effect: Weld group overstressed — increase weld leg size, extend weld pattern to 4-sided, or reduce applied loads
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: weld_leg_mm < 5 && M_Ed_knm > 5
fmea.effect: Small weld leg under significant moment: moment arm is short — minimum 6 mm leg recommended for moment connections; quality control of small welds is difficult

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: eccentricity_mm > plate_height_mm && V_Ed_kn > 20
fmea.effect: Large eccentricity relative to plate height: torsion-like loading — verify weld group polar moment of inertia; instantaneous centre of rotation method may be required for high eccentricity

fmea.failureMode: Weld utilisation > 85%: increase leg by 2 mm increments — note that a 25% increase in leg size (e.g., 8 → 10 mm) increases capacity by 25% for the same pattern
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: UC_weld > 0.85

fmea.failureMode: Bending stress dominates (>3× shear): verify that weld configuration is optimised — adding weld at extreme fibres (top/bottom) is most efficient for moment resistance
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fb_mpa > fv_mpa * 3

fmea.failureMode: Two vertical welds under moment: very low horizontal polar moment — moment creates high horizontal force with no top/bottom horizontal weld to resist it; switch to 4-sided weld pattern
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: weld_pattern === '2_vert' && M_Ed_knm > 0

fmea.failureMode: Large bracket eccentricity (> 200 mm) with high shear: significant eccentric moment — verify weld group using instantaneous centre method per AISC Manual Table 8-4 for accurate capacity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: eccentricity_mm > 200 && V_Ed_kn > 50

fmea.failureMode: Required weld leg exceeds half the plate thickness: weld will be as thick as the plate — verify plate adequate strength and consider full-penetration butt weld instead
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: a_req_mm > plate_width_mm * 0.5 / 0.707

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Masonry Load-Bearing Wall — Axial Capacity, Slenderness Reduction & Eccentricity Check
subtitle: Construction / Masonry Structures — EN 1996-1-1:2005 (EC6) §5.5, §6.1, ACI 530-13 §2.3, BS 5628-1:2005 §28

inScope: Masonry Load-Bearing Wall — Axial Capacity, Slenderness Reduction & Eccentricity Check
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Wall Thickness (t)
inputs.description: Standard: 100 mm block, 140 mm, 190 mm, 200 mm, 215 mm (brick), 250 mm, 300 mm

inputs.label: Masonry Characteristic Compressive Strength (f_k)
inputs.description: EN 1996 Table NA.1: 100 mm dense block + M4 mortar → f_k ≈ 3.5–6 MPa; engineering brick + M12 → 8–15 MPa

options.label: γ_M = 1.5 — Category I units, controlled site
γ_M = 2.0 — Category II units, standard site

inputs.label: Design Vertical Load per Unit Length of Wall (N_Ed)
Load Eccentricity at Mid-Height (e_mid)
inputs.description: EN 1996-1-1 §6.1.2.2: e_mid = e_i + e_hi; e_i = accidental eccentricity = h_eff/450; e_hi from horizontal loads

standards.name: Masonry Load-Bearing Wall — Axial Capacity, Slenderness Reduction & Eccentricity Check
standards.description: Masonry Load-Bearing Wall — Axial Capacity, Slenderness Reduction & Eccentricity Check — Construction / Masonry Structures

formulas.expression: h_eff_m = wall_height_m * end_condition; lambda = h_eff_m * 1000 / wall_thickness_mm
e_rel = eccentricity_mm / wall_thickness_mm; A_phi = 1 - 2 * e_rel; Phi_m = Math.max(A_phi * (1 - (lambda/140)^2), 0.0)

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: DCR > 1.0
fmea.effect: Wall load capacity exceeded — increase thickness, use higher-strength masonry units, or reduce applied load
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: lambda > 27
fmea.effect: Slenderness ratio > 27: EC6 §6.1.2.2 maximum limit — wall is too slender; add pilasters, returns, or intermediate lateral supports
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: eccentricity_mm > wall_thickness_mm / 3
fmea.effect: Eccentricity exceeds t/3: EC6 §6.1.2.2 limit — Φ_m approaches zero; review structural layout to reduce eccentricity; consider larger wall thickness

fmea.failureMode: High slenderness with pinned ends: Φ_m strongly reduced — adding simple floor/roof tie at top converts to fixed-pin (ρ=0.75), increasing N_Rd by ~25%
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: lambda > 20 && end_condition === 1.0

fmea.failureMode: Low-strength masonry under significant load: specify minimum M4 mortar and Category I units with testing — avoid using generic f_k without material testing on site
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fk_mpa < 3.5 && N_Ed_kn_m > 100

fmea.failureMode: Utilisation > 80%: marginal capacity for concentrated loads (beam bearings) — verify bearing stress at load introduction point per EC6 §6.1.3
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DCR > 0.80

fmea.failureMode: Thin wall (< 100 mm) under significant vertical load: structural masonry requires minimum 100 mm; check if wall is load-bearing or partition — reclassify if non-structural
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: wall_thickness_mm < 100 && N_Ed_kn_m > 50

fmea.failureMode: Combined high eccentricity + slenderness: double penalty on Φ_m — capacity is severely reduced; detailed second-order analysis required per EC6 Annex G
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: eccentricity_mm > wall_thickness_mm * 0.25 && lambda > 18

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: CNC Cylindrical Grinding — Wheel Speed, Q-Prime MRR, G-Ratio & Grinding Burn Prevention
subtitle: CNC Machining / Precision Grinding — ANSI B7.1-2017 (Safety of Abrasive Wheels), EN 13236:2012, ISO 525:2013 (bonded abrasives)

inScope: CNC Cylindrical Grinding — Wheel Speed, Q-Prime MRR, G-Ratio & Grinding Burn Prevention
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Grinding Wheel Outside Diameter (D_s)
inputs.description: Standard sizes: 200, 250, 300, 350, 400, 450, 500 mm for cylindrical OD grinding

inputs.label: Wheel Rotational Speed (n_s)
inputs.description: Never exceed wheel's maximum RPM stamped on blotter — ANSI B7.1 §6.3 mandatory check

inputs.label: Workpiece Rotational Speed (n_w)
inputs.description: Typical OD grinding: 100–400 rpm; higher speeds increase temperature and heat generation

inputs.label: Radial Infeed per Pass (a_e)
inputs.description: Roughing: 0.02–0.05 mm/pass; semi-finish: 0.005–0.02; finish: 0.001–0.005; sparking-out: 0

inputs.label: Axial Traverse Feed per Workpiece Revolution (f_a)
inputs.description: f_a = fraction of wheel width B: roughing ≈ 0.5–0.8B; finishing ≈ 0.1–0.3B

options.label: F/G — Soft (vitrified, soft workpieces, heavy stock)
Q/R/S — Hard (fine finish, very hard materials)

standards.name: CNC Cylindrical Grinding — Wheel Speed, Q-Prime MRR, G-Ratio & Grinding Burn Prevention
standards.description: CNC Cylindrical Grinding — Wheel Speed, Q-Prime MRR, G-Ratio & Grinding Burn Prevention — CNC Machining / Precision Grinding

formulas.expression: const G_base = {soft:20, medium_soft:30, medium:50, medium_hard:80, hard:120}; G_est = G_base[wheel_grade_hardness] * (Vs_m_s / 35)

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Vs_m_s > 45
fmea.effect: Wheel peripheral speed > 45 m/s: exceeds standard vitrified wheel limit — verify wheel is rated for high-speed grinding (HSG mark); operator safety risk of wheel burst per ANSI B7.1
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: q_ratio < 20
fmea.effect: Speed ratio q < 20: conventional grinding range too low — wheel may load and glaze; increase V_s or reduce V_w; target q = 60–150 for conventional grinding

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: temp_index > 0.05
fmea.effect: Thermal index > 0.05: grinding burn risk on steel — reduce Q'_w by decreasing infeed or workpiece speed; verify coolant flow rate ≥ 20 L/min at wheel contact zone

fmea.failureMode: High wheel speed with soft grade: wheel structure opens rapidly — soft grades lose bonds faster at high speed; upgrade to medium-soft for V_s > 35 m/s applications
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Vs_m_s > 35 && wheel_grade_hardness === 'soft'

fmea.failureMode: Large infeed on small workpiece: Q'_w very high relative to workpiece thermal mass — grinding burn and workpiece deflection imminent; reduce infeed to < 0.01 mm for D_w < 20 mm
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: infeed_mm_pass > 0.03 && workpiece_diameter_mm < 20

fmea.failureMode: Wide traverse pitch on fine infeed: likely chatter pattern will appear — finish pass traverse should be ≤ 1/3 of wheel width B for uniform surface without helical marks
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: traverse_feed_mm_rev > 5 && infeed_mm_pass < 0.005

fmea.failureMode: Low G-ratio estimate (< 15): wheel wears rapidly — check if wheel grade is too soft for workpiece hardness; consider harder grade or CBN/diamond wheel for hardened steels (HRC > 55)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: G_est < 15

fmea.failureMode: High workpiece speed on small diameter: dynamic balance and bearing precision critical — workpiece runout at high rpm causes chatter; verify workpiece mounting and centre alignment to < 0.002 mm TIR
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Vw_m_min > 50 && workpiece_diameter_mm < 30

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: High-Strength Bolt Group — Shear Capacity, Bearing Resistance & Slip Check per EC3 / AISC
subtitle: Steel Fabrication / Structural Connections — EN 1993-1-8:2005 §3.6, §3.7, §3.9, ISO 898-1:2013, AISC 360-22 §J3

inScope: High-Strength Bolt Group — Shear Capacity, Bearing Resistance & Slip Check per EC3 / AISC
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Factored Shear Force on Bolt Group (V_Ed)
End Distance in Load Direction (e_1)
inputs.description: EC3 §3.5: minimum e_1 ≥ 1.2 × d_0 (hole diameter = d + 2 mm)

inputs.label: Bolt Pitch in Load Direction (p_1)
inputs.description: EC3 §3.5: minimum p_1 ≥ 2.2 × d_0; standard: 3d to 5d

standards.name: High-Strength Bolt Group — Shear Capacity, Bearing Resistance & Slip Check per EC3 / AISC
standards.description: High-Strength Bolt Group — Shear Capacity, Bearing Resistance & Slip Check per EC3 / AISC — Steel Fabrication / Structural Connections

formulas.expression: const As_map = {12:84.3, 16:157, 20:245, 24:353, 27:459, 30:561}; As_mm2 = As_map[bolt_diameter_mm]; const fub_map = {'8.8':800, '10.9':1040, '12.9':1220}; fub_mpa = fub_map[property_class]
gamma_M2 = 1.25; alpha_v = 0.6; Fv_Rd_kn = alpha_v * fub_mpa * As_mm2 * n_shear_planes / (gamma_M2 * 1000)

d0_mm = bolt_diameter_mm + 2; alpha_b = Math.min(e1_d0 = edge_dist_e1_mm / d0_mm / 3, p1_d0 = pitch_p1_mm / d0_mm / 3 - 0.25, fub_mpa / (plate_fy_mpa * 1.3), 1.0); k1 = Math.min(2.8 * 30 / d0_mm - 1.7, 2.5); Fb_Rd_kn = k1 * alpha_b * plate_fy_mpa * 1.3 * bolt_diameter_mm * connected_plate_t_mm / (gamma_M2 * 1000)
Fgov_single_kn = Math.min(Fv_Rd_kn, Fb_Rd_kn); phi_Rn_group_kn = n_bolts * Fgov_single_kn
n_req = Math.ceil(V_Ed_kn / Fgov_single_kn); slip_check_kn = 0.7 * fub_mpa * As_mm2 * 0.5 / (gamma_M2 * 1000) * n_bolts

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: DCR > 1.0
fmea.effect: Bolt group shear/bearing capacity exceeded — increase bolt count, diameter, or property class
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: edge_dist_e1_mm < 1.2 * (bolt_diameter_mm + 2)
fmea.effect: End distance e_1 < 1.2 × d_0: EC3 §3.5 minimum violated — bolt will tear out of plate
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: pitch_p1_mm < 2.2 * (bolt_diameter_mm + 2)
fmea.effect: Bolt pitch p_1 < 2.2 × d_0: below EC3 minimum spacing — risk of plate splitting between holes

fmea.failureMode: Bearing governs at <60% of shear capacity: plate is thin or end/edge distance is short — increase plate thickness or end distance e_1 to balance resistances
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Fb_Rd_kn < Fv_Rd_kn * 0.6

fmea.failureMode: Large bolt group in single shear (n > 8): EC3 §3.8 long joint reduction factor β_Lj applies when Lj > 15d — verify connection length does not trigger reduction
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: n_bolts > 8 && n_shear_planes === 1

fmea.failureMode: 12.9 class bolt with thin plate: bearing governs severely — 12.9 bolts rarely needed in standard structural connections; verify whether 10.9 suffices
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: property_class === '12.9' && connected_plate_t_mm < 15

fmea.failureMode: Utilisation > 85%: marginal for fatigue or seismic load — add one bolt or increase diameter; fatigue class detail per EC3-1-9 is independent of static capacity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: DCR > 0.85

fmea.failureMode: Slip resistance insufficient for Category B (serviceability): connection will slip under service load — specify pretensioned bolts and improve faying surface to Class A (sand-blasted) μ=0.5
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: slip_check_kn < V_Ed_kn && n_shear_planes === 1

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Orthogonal Cutting Mechanics — Chip Thickness Ratio, Shear Angle, Cutting Forces & Tool Life
subtitle: CNC Machining / Metal Cutting Science — ISO 3685:1993 (tool life testing), Sandvik C-2920 §4, Merchant (1945) ASME Trans.

inScope: Orthogonal Cutting Mechanics — Chip Thickness Ratio, Shear Angle, Cutting Forces & Tool Life
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Uncut Chip Thickness (t_1 = feed per rev for orthogonal cut)
inputs.description: For orthogonal turning: t_1 = f (feed). Defines pre-cut chip geometry.

inputs.label: Measured Chip Thickness After Cutting (t_2)
inputs.description: Measure deformed chip with micrometer; always t_2 > t_1 for normal cutting

inputs.label: Tool Rake Angle (γ)
inputs.description: Positive rake = sharper, lower force; negative rake = stronger edge, higher force; carbide inserts: -7° to +15°

inputs.label: Tool-Chip Interface Friction Coefficient (μ)
inputs.description: Dry cutting steel on carbide: μ≈0.5–0.8; with flood coolant: μ≈0.3–0.5; can be measured via F_t/F_c ratio

inputs.label: Shear Yield Stress of Workpiece at Shear Plane (k_s)
inputs.description: k_s ≈ 0.6 × Rm (UTS); steel 0.45C: k_s≈350 MPa; Ti6Al4V: k_s≈550 MPa; Al7075: k_s≈200 MPa

inputs.label: Width of Cut (b = depth of cut for orthogonal)
Taylor Constant C at Reference Conditions
inputs.description: From manufacturer or ISO 3685 tool life test; C = speed for 1-minute tool life

standards.name: Orthogonal Cutting Mechanics — Chip Thickness Ratio, Shear Angle, Cutting Forces & Tool Life
standards.description: Orthogonal Cutting Mechanics — Chip Thickness Ratio, Shear Angle, Cutting Forces & Tool Life — CNC Machining / Metal Cutting Science

formulas.expression: r_c = uncut_chip_thickness_mm / chip_thickness_mm; gamma_rad = rake_angle_deg * Math.PI / 180; phi_rad = Math.atan(r_c * Math.cos(gamma_rad) / (1 - r_c * Math.sin(gamma_rad))); phi_deg = phi_rad * 180 / Math.PI
shear_area_mm2 = uncut_chip_thickness_mm * width_of_cut_mm / Math.sin(phi_rad); Fs_N = shear_stress_mpa * shear_area_mm2; Fn_N = Fs_N * Math.tan(phi_rad + beta_rad - gamma_rad)
Fc_N = Fs_N * Math.cos(beta_rad - gamma_rad) / Math.cos(phi_rad + beta_rad - gamma_rad); Ft_N = Fs_N * Math.sin(beta_rad - gamma_rad) / Math.cos(phi_rad + beta_rad - gamma_rad)
Pc_kw = Fc_N * Vc_m_min / 60000; u_specific_J_mm3 = Fc_N / (uncut_chip_thickness_mm * width_of_cut_mm * Vc_m_min / 60 * 1000) * Vc_m_min / 60
T_life_min = Math.pow(Taylor_C_ref / Vc_m_min, 1 / Taylor_n_exp); T_eco_min = Taylor_n_exp / (1 - Taylor_n_exp) * (2 + 0); V_eco_m_min = Taylor_C_ref * Math.pow(T_eco_min, -Taylor_n_exp)

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: chip_thickness_mm < uncut_chip_thickness_mm
fmea.effect: Chip thickness t_2 must exceed uncut thickness t_1: chip always thickens during cutting (r_c < 1). Verify measurement — thinner chip indicates measurement error or built-up edge
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: phi_deg < 5
fmea.effect: Shear angle < 5°: extreme chip compression — very high cutting forces and temperature; check if friction is excessive or rake angle too negative

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: T_life_min < 5
fmea.effect: Tool life < 5 minutes: uneconomical — reduce V_c by 15–20%; target T = 15–25 min for economic insert change interval per ISO 3685

fmea.failureMode: Chip thickness ratio < 0.2: severe chip compression — chip is 5× thicker than uncut; heat generation and tool wear will be extreme; increase rake angle or apply flood coolant
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: r_c < 0.2

fmea.failureMode: Shear angle > 50°: unusually large — verify chip thickness measurement accuracy; r_c > 0.8 may indicate built-up edge depositing on tool face
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: phi_deg > 50

fmea.failureMode: Cutting power > 50 kW: verify machine spindle rating; thermal loading at this level causes workpiece distortion; verify fixturing stiffness and coolant supply
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Pc_kw > 50

fmea.failureMode: Thrust force > 50% of cutting force: high passive/radial load — deflects slender workpieces; L/D > 4 requires steady rest; verify surface finish will be acceptable
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Ft_N > Fc_N * 0.5

fmea.failureMode: Operating below economic tool life: running too fast — at current V_c, tool change cost dominates; reduce to V_eco for minimum cost per part
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_life_min < T_eco_min * 0.5

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Single Pile Axial Capacity — Shaft Friction, Base Resistance & EC7 Design Verification
subtitle: Geotechnical Engineering / Foundation Design — EN 1997-1:2004 (EC7) §7.6, API RP 2GEO:2014 §6.4, FHWA NHI-16-064 §3

inScope: Single Pile Axial Capacity — Shaft Friction, Base Resistance & EC7 Design Verification
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Pile Shaft Diameter (D)
inputs.description: Standard CFA: 300–900 mm; driven steel H: 250–360 mm; bored cast-in-situ: 300–1500 mm

options.label: Driven steel H-pile — β method in sand

inputs.label: Soil Strength Parameter — c_u (kPa) for clay OR N_60 for sand
inputs.description: Clay: c_u from UU triaxial or vane shear; Sand: N_60 from SPT (energy-corrected)

inputs.label: Shaft Adhesion / Friction Factor (α or β)
inputs.description: Clay α: 0.4–0.5 bored, 0.5–0.6 driven; Sand β: 0.2–0.4; EC7 Annex D / FHWA NHI-16-064

inputs.label: Unit Base (End-Bearing) Resistance (q_b)
inputs.description: Clay: q_b = N_c × c_u = 9 × c_u; Dense sand: q_b = 3000–8000 kPa; Rock: 10,000–20,000 kPa

options.label: γ_R,t = 1.3 — Driven pile, model factor correlated

standards.name: Single Pile Axial Capacity — Shaft Friction, Base Resistance & EC7 Design Verification
standards.description: Single Pile Axial Capacity — Shaft Friction, Base Resistance & EC7 Design Verification — Geotechnical Engineering / Foundation Design

formulas.expression: D_m = pile_diameter_mm / 1000; perimeter_m = Math.PI * D_m; Ab_m2 = Math.PI / 4 * Math.pow(D_m, 2)
fs_kpa = (soil_type === 'clay') ? alpha_adhesion * cu_or_N60_value : alpha_adhesion * cu_or_N60_value * 10; Qs_kn = fs_kpa * perimeter_m * pile_length_m
Rcd_kn = Rck_kn / gamma_R_t; mob_factor = (pile_type === 'bored') ? 0.9 : 1.0; Rcd_mob_kn = Rcd_kn * mob_factor
eta_group = (pile_type === 'clay') ? Math.min(1.0, 0.7 + 0.1 * Math.log10(pile_length_m / D_m)) : 1.0; R_group_kn = 4 * Rcd_mob_kn * eta_group

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Rcd_mob_kn < 0
fmea.effect: Design resistance is negative: check soil strength input — minimum c_u or N_60 must be positive and realistic
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: pile_length_m / (pile_diameter_mm / 1000) < 8
fmea.effect: L/D < 8: stubby pile — shaft friction contribution is limited; this behaves more as a spread footing; verify end bearing dominates

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: qb_kpa > cu_or_N60_value * 15 && soil_type === 'clay'
fmea.effect: Unit base resistance exceeds 15 × c_u: unrealistic for clay — theoretical maximum N_c = 9 for deep piles; cap q_b at 9 × c_u per Skempton (1951)

fmea.failureMode: End bearing > 70% of shaft: base-dominant pile — settlement at working load may be 30–50 mm before full base mobilisation; consider longer pile to increase shaft contribution
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Qb_kn > Qs_kn * 0.7

fmea.failureMode: Bored pile in soft clay (c_u < 40 kPa): construction disturbance reduces α — use α = 0.4 maximum; verify bentonite support and concrete workability
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: pile_type === 'bored' && cu_or_N60_value < 40

fmea.failureMode: Driven precast pile D > 900 mm: driving damage risk is severe for large diameters — switch to bored, CFA, or barrette; driving stresses per GRLWEAP analysis mandatory
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: pile_diameter_mm > 900 && pile_type === 'driven_concrete'

fmea.failureMode: Long bored pile with standard γ_R: consider pile testing (static or dynamic CAPWAP) to validate shaft friction distribution — testing permits γ_R reduction to 1.3 per EC7 §7.6.3.3
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: gamma_R_t === 1.5 && pile_length_m > 30

fmea.failureMode: Group efficiency < 85% in clay: block failure may govern — calculate block capacity per EC7 §7.6.2.5; Qblock = c_u × (perimeter_block × L + Ablock × 9)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: eta_group < 0.85 && soil_type === 'clay'

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Sheet Metal Press Brake Bending — Bending Force, Springback Angle & Minimum Bend Radius
subtitle: Sheet Metal / Metal Forming / Fabrication — DIN 6935:1975 (cold bending of flat bars), ISO 7438:2016 (bend testing), ASTM E290-14 (semi-guided bend test)

inScope: Sheet Metal Press Brake Bending — Bending Force, Springback Angle & Minimum Bend Radius
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Bend Line Length (L) — into page
inputs.description: Length of the press brake punch; equals workpiece width perpendicular to bend direction

inputs.label: V-Die Opening Width (V)
inputs.description: Rule of thumb: V = 8 × t for mild steel; V = 6 × t for aluminium; larger V = lower force, more springback

inputs.label: Material Ultimate Tensile Strength (R_m)
inputs.description: Mild steel DC01: Rm=270–370 MPa; S355: Rm=470–630 MPa; SS304: Rm=515–720 MPa; Al5052: Rm=230 MPa

inputs.label: Material Yield Strength (R_e / R_p0.2)
inputs.description: Springback depends on R_e/E ratio — high-strength materials spring back more

inputs.label: Punch Nose Radius (r_p)
inputs.description: Minimum radius ≥ material thickness for most materials; sharp punches cause cracking

standards.name: Sheet Metal Press Brake Bending — Bending Force, Springback Angle & Minimum Bend Radius
standards.description: Sheet Metal Press Brake Bending — Bending Force, Springback Angle & Minimum Bend Radius — Sheet Metal / Metal Forming / Fabrication

formulas.expression: E_mpa = 210000; elongation_pct = Math.max(5, (1 - Fy_mpa/Rm_mpa) * 60); r_min_mm = material_thickness_mm * (50 / elongation_pct - 1)

r_neutral_mm = punch_radius_mm + material_thickness_mm / 2; springback_factor = 1 - Fy_mpa * r_neutral_mm / (E_mpa * material_thickness_mm); Theta_punch_deg = bend_angle_deg / springback_factor; delta_theta_deg = Theta_punch_deg - bend_angle_deg
k_factor = (punch_radius_mm < 2 * material_thickness_mm) ? 0.33 : 0.41; BA_mm = (punch_radius_mm + k_factor * material_thickness_mm) * bend_angle_deg * Math.PI / 180

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: punch_radius_mm < r_min_mm
fmea.effect: Punch radius < minimum bend radius: cracking risk on outer surface — increase punch radius to r_min or pre-heat material; verify with trial bends

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: die_opening_mm < 6 * material_thickness_mm
fmea.effect: Die opening < 6t: excessive bending force and tool wear — minimum V = 6t for aluminium, 8t for mild steel; smaller die opening risks sheet damage and machine overload

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: F_required_kn > 10000
fmea.effect: Required bending force > 1000 tonnes: very heavy press brake needed — verify material and geometry; consider multi-pass incremental bending or rolling for thick plates

fmea.failureMode: Springback > 10°: significant overbend required — for high-strength steel and AHSS, springback can reach 15–25°; consider bottom bending or laser tracking closed-loop angle control on CNC press brake
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: delta_theta_deg > 10

fmea.failureMode: High-strength material with large r_min: difficult-to-form sheet — consider hot-forming, pre-annealing, or laser scoring at bend line for UHSS > 700 MPa
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Fy_mpa > 500 && r_min_mm > 3 * material_thickness_mm

fmea.failureMode: Long bend + high force: press brake deflection significant — ram crowning compensation mandatory; deflection without crowning causes trapezoidal bends on long parts
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: bend_length_mm > 3000 && F_required_kn > 2000

fmea.failureMode: Die opening > 16t: excessive — large V-opening reduces bending accuracy and increases springback; use V = 8–10t for best accuracy-to-force balance on structural steel
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: die_opening_mm > 16 * material_thickness_mm

fmea.failureMode: Large punch radius with shallow angle: wipe bending geometry — verify punch stroke and die sizing prevent material slip; consider pre-positioning stops
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: punch_radius_mm > material_thickness_mm * 3 && bend_angle_deg < 30

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: RC One-Way Slab — Flexural Design Steel Area, Depth Check & One-Way Shear Verification
subtitle: Concrete Construction / Structural Engineering — ACI 318-19 §7, §9, §22.5, EN 1992-1-1:2004 §6.2, §9.3, BS 8110-1:1997 §3.4.4

inScope: RC One-Way Slab — Flexural Design Steel Area, Depth Check & One-Way Shear Verification
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Factored Bending Moment per Metre Width (M_u / M_Ed)
Factored Shear Force per Metre Width (V_u / V_Ed)
Total Slab Thickness (h)
inputs.description: ACI 318 minimum one-way slab: L/20 (simply supported); L/28 (one end continuous)

inputs.label: Clear Cover to Flexural Steel (c_c)
inputs.description: ACI §20.6.1: interior slab min 20 mm; exterior min 40 mm; marine: 50 mm

standards.name: RC One-Way Slab — Flexural Design Steel Area, Depth Check & One-Way Shear Verification
standards.description: RC One-Way Slab — Flexural Design Steel Area, Depth Check & One-Way Shear Verification — Concrete Construction / Structural Engineering

formulas.expression: d_mm = slab_thickness_mm - cover_mm - bar_diameter_mm / 2; Rn_mpa = Mu_knm_m * 1e6 / (0.9 * 1000 * Math.pow(d_mm, 2))

rho_req = (0.85 * fc_prime_mpa / fy_mpa) * (1 - Math.sqrt(1 - 2 * Rn_mpa / (0.85 * fc_prime_mpa))); rho_min = Math.max(0.25 * Math.sqrt(fc_prime_mpa) / fy_mpa, 1.4 / fy_mpa); rho_max = 0.75 * 0.85 * 0.85 * fc_prime_mpa / fy_mpa * (600 / (600 + fy_mpa)); rho_design = Math.max(rho_req, rho_min)
As_req_mm2_m = rho_design * 1000 * d_mm; bar_area_mm2 = Math.PI / 4 * Math.pow(bar_diameter_mm, 2); spacing_mm = Math.floor(bar_area_mm2 / As_req_mm2_m * 1000)
s_max_mm = Math.min(3 * slab_thickness_mm, 450); s_ok = spacing_mm <= s_max_mm
shear_DCR = Vu_kn_m / phi_Vc_kn_m; L_d_ratio = span_m * 1000 / d_mm; L_d_limit = (fy_mpa === 420) ? 20 : 18

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: rho_req > rho_max
fmea.effect: Required steel ratio exceeds 75% of balanced — slab is over-stressed; increase slab thickness by 20–30 mm or increase f'c; compression-controlled failure mode unacceptable

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: shear_DCR > 1.0
fmea.effect: One-way shear demand exceeds concrete capacity: stirrups required in slab (unusual) — verify load/span; more practical to increase slab thickness until V_u < φV_c

fmea.failureMode: Parameter warning: d_mm < slab_thickness_mm * 0.7
fmea.effect: Effective depth < 70% of total thickness: very large cover or bar size — verify cover is correct; large bars in thin slabs reduce d significantly

fmea.failureMode: Span-to-depth ratio exceeds ACI §24.2 deflection limit — computed deflections must be checked; long-term creep deflection (×2.0 for 5+ years) may damage partitions or finishes
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: L_d_ratio > L_d_limit

fmea.failureMode: Bar spacing < 75 mm: congestion risk — concrete aggregate cannot flow between bars; ACI §25.2.1 requires clear spacing ≥ bar diameter, 25 mm, or 4/3 × aggregate size
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: spacing_mm < 75

fmea.failureMode: Bar spacing exceeds ACI maximum: cracks will be wide — use smaller diameter bars at closer spacing or redesign to meet s ≤ min(3h, 450 mm)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: !s_ok

fmea.failureMode: Minimum steel governs: slab is lightly loaded for its depth — consider reducing slab thickness to L/20 minimum per ACI Table 9.3.1.1 for efficiency
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: rho_design < rho_min * 1.1

fmea.failureMode: High moment coefficient (R_n > 30% of 0.85f'c): approaching over-reinforced zone — verify d is adequate; increase thickness before adding reinforcement
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Rn_mpa > 0.85 * fc_prime_mpa * 0.3

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Roller Bearing L10h Life & Modified Lnm Life (ISO 281)
subtitle: Machine Elements — ISO 281:2007, ISO 76:2006, SKF General Catalogue 2018

inScope: Roller Bearing L10h Life & Modified Lnm Life (ISO 281)
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

standards.name: Roller Bearing L10h Life & Modified Lnm Life (ISO 281)
standards.description: Roller Bearing L10h Life & Modified Lnm Life (ISO 281) — Machine Elements

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: equiv_load_P_kN >= dynamic_load_C_kN
fmea.effect: MECHANICAL IMPOSSIBILITY: P >= C. Operating load exceeds dynamic capacity. L10 is less than 1 million revolutions. Bearing will fail immediately; selection is incorrect.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: contamination_ec_val < 0 || contamination_ec_val > 0.80
fmea.effect: eC contamination factor must be between 0 and 0.80 (ISO 281 Annex A Table A.4).
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Load_Ratio > 0.8
fmea.effect: P/C ratio > 0.8: Bearing is severely overloaded. Minimum L10 life is less than 2 million revolutions. Select a larger bearing series.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: CRITICAL LUBRICATION DEFICIENCY: kappa < 0.4. Rolling elements are in direct contact; EHD film does not form. Metal-on-metal wear initiated. Lnm calculation unreliable. Higher viscosity oil or lower speed is mandatory.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: viscosity_ratio_kappa < 0.4

fmea.failureMode: Insufficient oil film: kappa between 0.4 and 1.0. Partial EHD film. Fatigue life is significantly below theoretical values. Optimize oil selection or evaluate viscosity-enhancing additives.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: viscosity_ratio_kappa >= 0.4 && viscosity_ratio_kappa < 1.0

fmea.failureMode: High contamination: eC < 0.30. Abrasive particles are destroying the raceways. Upgrade filter class to ISO 4406 <= 16/14/11. Evaluate shielded/sealed bearing types.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: contamination_ec_val < 0.30

fmea.failureMode: INSUFFICIENT MODIFIED LIFE: Lnm is less than 50% of target life. Bearing will fail prematurely under current conditions. Larger series, better lubrication, or lower load is required.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Lnm < target_life_hours * 0.5

fmea.failureMode: P/C > 0.5: High load ratio. A larger bearing series should be evaluated. P/C <= 0.2 is ideal for long service life.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Load_Ratio > 0.5

fmea.failureMode: L10h < 20,000 hours: This is the industrial minimum for electric motors. Current design is insufficient. Increase bearing size or reduce load.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: L10h < 20000 && target_life_hours >= 20000

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not financial/legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

standards.name: Hollow Section RHS/SHS/CHS Beam-Column Combined Loading Check — EN 1993-1-1 / AISC 360-22 / CIDECT DG2
standards.description: Hollow Section RHS/SHS/CHS Beam-Column Combined Loading Check — EN 1993-1-1 / AISC 360-22 / CIDECT DG2 — Steel Structure / Hollow Structural Sections / Beam-Column Stability

formulas.expression: A_rect_calc = section_type === 'RHS' || section_type === 'SHS' ? (B * H - (B - 2 * t) * (H - 2 * t)) : null
A_chs_calc = section_type === 'CHS' ? Math.PI / 4 * (Math.pow(D, 2) - Math.pow(D - 2 * t, 2)) : null
A = A_lookup !== null && A_lookup !== undefined ? A_lookup : (section_type === 'CHS' ? A_chs_calc : A_rect_calc)
Iy_rect_calc = section_type === 'RHS' || section_type === 'SHS' ? (B * Math.pow(H, 3) - (B - 2 * t) * Math.pow(H - 2 * t, 3)) / 12 : null
Iz_rect_calc = section_type === 'RHS' || section_type === 'SHS' ? (H * Math.pow(B, 3) - (H - 2 * t) * Math.pow(B - 2 * t, 3)) / 12 : null
I_chs_calc = section_type === 'CHS' ? Math.PI / 64 * (Math.pow(D, 4) - Math.pow(D - 2 * t, 4)) : null
Iy = Iy_lookup !== null && Iy_lookup !== undefined ? Iy_lookup : (section_type === 'CHS' ? I_chs_calc : Iy_rect_calc)
Iz = Iz_lookup !== null && Iz_lookup !== undefined ? Iz_lookup : (section_type === 'CHS' ? I_chs_calc : Iz_rect_calc)
Wy = Wy_lookup !== null && Wy_lookup !== undefined ? Wy_lookup : (section_type === 'CHS' ? I_chs_calc / (D / 2) : Iy / (H / 2))
Wz = Wz_lookup !== null && Wz_lookup !== undefined ? Wz_lookup : (section_type === 'CHS' ? I_chs_calc / (D / 2) : Iz / (B / 2))
Ncr_y = Math.pow(Math.PI, 2) * E * Iy / Math.pow(K_y * L_y, 2) / 1000
Ncr_z = Math.pow(Math.PI, 2) * E * Iz / Math.pow(K_z * L_z, 2) / 1000

chi_y_raw = 1 / (0.5 * (1 + alpha_y * (lambda_bar_y - 0.2) + Math.pow(lambda_bar_y, 2)) + Math.sqrt(Math.pow(0.5 * (1 + alpha_y * (lambda_bar_y - 0.2) + Math.pow(lambda_bar_y, 2)), 2) - Math.pow(lambda_bar_y, 2)))

chi_z_raw = 1 / (0.5 * (1 + alpha_z * (lambda_bar_z - 0.2) + Math.pow(lambda_bar_z, 2)) + Math.sqrt(Math.pow(0.5 * (1 + alpha_z * (lambda_bar_z - 0.2) + Math.pow(lambda_bar_z, 2)), 2) - Math.pow(lambda_bar_z, 2)))
UC_EC3_y = N_Ed > 0 ? N_Ed / Nb_Rd_y + k_yy * M_y_Ed / My_Rd + k_yz * M_z_Ed / Mz_Rd : Math.abs(N_Ed) / Npl_Rd + M_y_Ed / My_Rd + M_z_Ed / Mz_Rd
UC_EC3_z = N_Ed > 0 ? N_Ed / Nb_Rd_z + k_zy * M_y_Ed / My_Rd + k_zz * M_z_Ed / Mz_Rd : Math.abs(N_Ed) / Npl_Rd + M_y_Ed / My_Rd + M_z_Ed / Mz_Rd
Fcr_y = fy / Fe_y <= 2.25 ? Math.pow(0.658, fy / Fe_y) * fy : 0.877 * Fe_y
Fcr_z = fy / Fe_z <= 2.25 ? Math.pow(0.658, fy / Fe_z) * fy : 0.877 * Fe_z

UC_AISC_y = N_Ed > 0 ? (N_Ed / Pc_y >= 0.2 ? N_Ed / Pc_y + (8 / 9) * (M_y_Ed / Mc_y + M_z_Ed / Mc_z) : N_Ed / (2 * Pc_y) + (M_y_Ed / Mc_y + M_z_Ed / Mc_z)) : Math.abs(N_Ed) / (phi_c * fy * A / 1000) + M_y_Ed / Mc_y + M_z_Ed / Mc_z

UC_AISC_z = N_Ed > 0 ? (N_Ed / Pc_z >= 0.2 ? N_Ed / Pc_z + (8 / 9) * (M_y_Ed / Mc_y + M_z_Ed / Mc_z) : N_Ed / (2 * Pc_z) + (M_y_Ed / Mc_y + M_z_Ed / Mc_z)) : Math.abs(N_Ed) / (phi_c * fy * A / 1000) + M_y_Ed / Mc_y + M_z_Ed / Mc_z
UC = design_standard === 'EN_1993_1_1' ? Math.max(UC_EC3_y, UC_EC3_z) : Math.max(UC_AISC_y, UC_AISC_z)
f_res = Math.sqrt(Math.pow((N_Ed * 1000) / A, 2) + Math.pow((M_y_Ed * 1000000) / Wy, 2) + Math.pow((M_z_Ed * 1000000) / Wz, 2))
u_c_UC = UC * Math.sqrt(Math.pow(0.02, 2) + Math.pow(0.005, 2) + Math.pow(0.02, 2) + Math.pow(0.05, 2))

fmea.failureMode: Local buckling (flange/web)
fmea.effect: Loss of section capacity, sudden collapse risk
fmea.control_measure: Verify width-to-thickness ratios per EN 1993-1-1 §5.5. Check stiffener spacing.

fmea.failureMode: Global buckling (y-y axis)
fmea.effect: Loss of member stability, lateral-torsional interaction
fmea.control_measure: Verify slenderness ratio L_y/i_y. Check effective length K_yL_y per buckling curve.

fmea.failureMode: Global buckling (z-z axis)
fmea.effect: Loss of member stability, reduced compression capacity
fmea.control_measure: Verify slenderness ratio L_z/i_z. Check effective length K_zL_z per buckling curve.

fmea.failureMode: Biaxial bending overload
fmea.effect: Combined stress exceeds yield, plastic hinge formation

fmea.failureMode: Cross-section geometry input error
fmea.effect: Wrong section properties (A, I_y, I_z, W_y, W_z), invalid results
fmea.control_measure: Verify section dimensions against manufacturer catalogue. Cross-check with A_lookup, Iy_lookup from standard tables.

fmea.failureMode: Material grade mismatch
fmea.effect: Incorrect yield strength fy, wrong capacity
fmea.control_measure: Verify steel grade (S235/S275/S355/S460) per EN 10025. Check fy value against grade specification.

fmea.failureMode: Missing effective length restraint
fmea.effect: Buckling length underestimation, unconservative design
fmea.control_measure: Verify K_y, K_z factors. Check lateral restraint conditions (pinned/fixed/free). Refer to EN 1993-1-1 Table 6.2.

formulas.expression: x = A_s * f_yd / (eta * f_cd * b_w * lambda)
V_Rdc = (0.18/gamma_c * k * (100 * rho_l_limited * f_ck)^(1/3)) * b_w * d / 1000
V_Rds = A_sv / s_v * z * f_yd * cot_theta / 1000

fmea.failureMode: Flexural yielding
fmea.description: Tension steel yields before concrete crushes. Ductile failure mode if ρ_l ≤ ρ_bal.
fmea.effect: Large deflections but visible warning before collapse

fmea.failureMode: Compression-controlled brittle failure
fmea.description: Neutral axis too deep. Concrete crushes before steel yields. Low ductility.
fmea.effect: Sudden collapse without warning

fmea.failureMode: Diagonal shear failure
fmea.description: Diagonal tension crack propagates through web. Sudden failure without warning.
fmea.effect: Brittle shear failure

fmea.failureMode: Concrete strut crushing
fmea.description: Web crushing before stirrup yielding. Section too slender or stirrups too strong.
fmea.effect: Brittle web failure

fmea.failureMode: Stirrup yielding
fmea.description: Stirrups yield before concrete strut crushes. Large shear deformations.
fmea.effect: Excessive cracking before failure

fmea.failureMode: Anchorage failure
fmea.description: Insufficient development length. Bar pull-out at support.
fmea.effect: Loss of flexural capacity at support

fmea.failureMode: Bar placement error
fmea.description: Actual effective depth less than assumed due to placement tolerance or congestion.
fmea.effect: Reduced capacity vs. design

fmea.failureMode: Material strength mismatch
fmea.description: Actual concrete or steel strength below specified grade. Compliance failure.
fmea.effect: Non-compliance with design assumptions

name: Centrifugal Pump Cavitation & NPSH Analysis (API 610)
subtitle: Fluid Mechanics / Pump Systems — API 610 12th Ed., ISO 9908, Hydraulic Institute ANSI/HI 9.6.1

inScope: Centrifugal Pump Cavitation & NPSH Analysis (API 610)
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

standards.name: Centrifugal Pump Cavitation & NPSH Analysis (API 610)
standards.description: Centrifugal Pump Cavitation & NPSH Analysis (API 610) — Fluid Mechanics / Pump Systems

formulas.expression: Max_Suction_Lift = Head_Tank - Head_Vapor - suction_pipe_losses_m - npsh_required_m - safety_margin_m

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: vapor_pressure_bar >= tank_pressure_bar
fmea.effect: PHYSICAL ERROR: Vapor pressure is equal to or greater than tank pressure. Fluid is already at boiling point — pumping in liquid phase is impossible. Reduce temperature or increase tank pressure.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: fluid_density_kg_m3 < 400
fmea.effect: Fluid density < 400 kg/m³: Not a realistic liquid (LNG is ~420, others are higher). Verify input value.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: static_suction_head_m < -9.0
fmea.effect: Suction lift < -9m: Theoretical maximum vacuum lift for water is ~9m. This value exceeds physical limits. Revise pump position.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: pipe_diameter_mm > 0 && flow_rate_m3h > 0 && Flow_Velocity_m_s > 3.0
fmea.effect: Suction line velocity > 3 m/s: Friction losses will be excessive, reducing NPSHa and increasing cavitation risk. Hydraulic Institute recommendation: suction velocity < 1.5 m/s.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: CAVITATION INITIATED: NPSHa < NPSHr. Vapor bubbles are forming in the impeller eye. Micro-implosions are causing surface erosion. Stop the pump immediately and rectify suction conditions.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: NPSH_Ratio < 1.0

fmea.failureMode: CAVITATION RISK: Does not meet API 610 requirement of NPSHa >= NPSHr + 10% safety margin. Cavitation will initiate immediately on flow surges or temperature rises. Reduce suction line friction losses.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: NPSH_Ratio >= 1.0 && NPSH_Ratio < 1.1

fmea.failureMode: Insufficient NPSH margin: NPSHa / NPSHr is between 1.1 and 1.3. Cavitation may trigger during process fluctuations (temperature, flow). Hydraulic Institute recommends a minimum ratio of 1.3.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: NPSH_Ratio >= 1.1 && NPSH_Ratio < 1.3

fmea.failureMode: Hot fluid + negative suction lift: Vapor pressure increases exponentially with temperature, rapidly reducing NPSHa. This combination poses critical cavitation risk. Locate pump below tank level.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fluid_temp_c > 60 && static_suction_head_m < 0

fmea.failureMode: Suction line friction losses > 2m: Directly subtracts from NPSHa. To reduce losses: increase pipe diameter, minimize valves, shorten pipe runs, and avoid tight elbows.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: suction_pipe_losses_m > 2.0

fmea.failureMode: NPSHa < 1.0m: Operating conditions are outside normal centrifugal pump limits. Evaluate special low-NPSH pump models (with inducer) or submersible pumps.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: NPSHa < 1.0

formulas.expression: t_cyl_circ = design_pressure_mpa * vessel_diameter_mm / (2 * allowable_stress_mpa * weld_joint_efficiency - 1.2 * design_pressure_mpa)
t_cyl_long = design_pressure_mpa * vessel_diameter_mm / (4 * allowable_stress_mpa * weld_joint_efficiency + 0.8 * design_pressure_mpa)
t_hemi_head = design_pressure_mpa * vessel_diameter_mm / (4 * allowable_stress_mpa * weld_joint_efficiency - 0.4 * design_pressure_mpa) + corrosion_allowance_mm
t_ellip_head = design_pressure_mpa * vessel_diameter_mm / (2 * allowable_stress_mpa * weld_joint_efficiency - 0.2 * design_pressure_mpa) + corrosion_allowance_mm
t_toris_head = (0.885 * design_pressure_mpa * vessel_diameter_mm) / (allowable_stress_mpa * weld_joint_efficiency - 0.1 * design_pressure_mpa) + corrosion_allowance_mm

fmea.failureMode: S < P
