name: Wire Rope Sling Load and Rigging Angle Calculator — ASME B30 Sling Tension and Utilization Check
subtitle: Lifting / Rigging / Wire Rope Slings — ASME B30.9-2025, ASME B30.26-2026, ASME P30.1, OSHA Safe Sling Use guidance, ISO 80000, ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM, ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1

inScope: single_operation
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.name: Load Mass to be Lifted
inputs.hint: Mass of the load to be lifted. Include all attachments, rigging hardware, and any ancillary equipment suspended below the crane hook.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME P30.1 §4.3.1 — Load weight uncertainty: ±1.5% typical for weighed loads; ±5% for estimated loads

inputs.name: Local Gravitational Acceleration (g)
inputs.hint: Standard gravity = 9.80665 m/s². Variations: 9.764 (Equator) to 9.834 (Poles). Use local value for precision lifts.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 80000-3:2019 — Standard gravity gₙ = 9.80665 m/s²; local variation ±0.35%

inputs.name: Dynamic Amplification Factor (DAF)
inputs.hint: Factor accounting for dynamic effects during lift. ASME B30.9: 1.0 for static/controlled; 1.25 for normal crane lifts; 1.5 for rough terrain; 2.0 for sudden release or shock loading.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §8.2 — Dynamic factor uncertainty: ±5% for normal lifts, ±10% for shock loading

inputs.name: Rigging Configuration / Hitch Type
inputs.hint: Wire rope sling configuration. ASME B30.9 Table 3 defines configuration factors. Bridle: legs share load. Basket: sling wraps around load. Choker: sling passes through itself around load.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 Table 3 — Configuration definitions, zero configuration uncertainty

inputs.name: Number of Sling Legs Installed
inputs.hint: Total number of sling legs installed in the rigging arrangement. Maximum 4 legs per ASME B30.9.
inputs.uncertainty_source: Discrete count variable — zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Active Load-Bearing Legs
inputs.hint: Number of legs actually carrying the load. For 3-leg bridle with rigid load: often only 2 legs carry load due to load distribution. ASME B30.9: do not assume equal load sharing without load-balancing device.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.1.3 — Load distribution: discrete assessment, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Load-Balancing Device Present
inputs.hint: Is a load-balancing device (e.g., equalizer beam, spreader bar, load cell feedback) used to ensure equal load distribution among all sling legs?

inputs.name: Sling Angle from Horizontal (θ)
inputs.hint: Angle between the sling leg and the horizontal plane. ASME B30.9: minimum 30° from horizontal. OSHA: minimum 30° (30° = 2x tension factor). 60° = 1.155x tension factor; 90° = 1.0x tension factor.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.2.1 — Angle measurement uncertainty: ±2° typical for field measurement; ±1° for digital inclinometer

inputs.name: Sling Length (L_s)
inputs.hint: Total length of the sling from master link to hook or end fitting. Used for vertical rise and horizontal offset calculation if not directly provided.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Length measurement tolerance class m: ±0.5%

inputs.name: Vertical Rise from Load to Hook (H)
inputs.hint: Vertical distance from the load attachment point to the crane hook. Used to verify sling angle if sling_length_m and horizontal_offset_m are provided.

inputs.name: Horizontal Offset from Hook to Load (D)
inputs.hint: Horizontal distance from the crane hook centerline to the load attachment point. Combined with vertical_rise_m to verify sling angle. Zero for centered lifts.

inputs.name: Wire Rope Sling WLL — Vertical / Basket (per leg)
inputs.hint: Manufacturer-rated Working Load Limit for this sling in vertical configuration. ASME B30.9 Table 4: WLL shall be marked on sling tag or nameplate. Includes termination efficiency.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §5.1.2 — Manufacturer WLL rating uncertainty: ±3% (includes material, manufacturing, and test variability)

inputs.name: Hitch Configuration Capacity Factor
inputs.hint: Factor applied to vertical WLL for hitch type: Single Vertical = 1.0, 2-Leg Bridle = 1.0 per leg, Single Basket = 2.0, Double Basket = 2.0 per leg (depends on D/d ratio), Choker = 0.75–0.80.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 Table 3 — Hitch capacity factors, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: D/d Ratio (Bend Radius / Rope Diameter)
inputs.hint: Ratio of the diameter of curvature (D) of the pin/hook/shackle over the nominal rope diameter (d). ASME B30.26 Table 1: D/d ≥ 5 for shackles; D/d ≥ 2 for hooks. Lower D/d reduces sling capacity.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.26-2026 §8.3.1 — D/d measurement uncertainty: ±5% field estimate

inputs.name: Minimum Required D/d Ratio per Standard
inputs.hint: Minimum D/d ratio required by the applicable standard. ASME B30.26: D/d ≥ 5 for shackles, D/d ≥ 2 for hooks. EN 1677: D/d ≥ 5. Lower values require mandatory reduction factor.

inputs.name: D/d Reduction Factor (f_dd)
inputs.hint: Reduction factor for sling WLL based on D/d ratio. D/d ≥ minimum_required: f_dd = 1.0. D/d < minimum_required: apply reduction. ASME B30.9: f_dd = actual_Dd / minimum_Dd for basket hitches.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §8.3 — D/d reduction factor, ±1% for interpolation

inputs.name: Termination Efficiency Factor (η_ter)
inputs.hint: Efficiency of sling end terminations (swaged sleeves, hand-spliced eyes, mechanical splices). Swaged sleeve Flemish eye: 0.95–1.0. Hand-tucked splice (6 tucks): 0.80–0.85. Mechanical wedge socket: 0.90. Mechanical clamped: 0.75.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §5.4 — Termination efficiency: ±2% for swaged, ±5% for hand-spliced

inputs.name: Edge Protection Factor (f_edge)
inputs.hint: Factor for edge contact/corner protection. 1.0 = proper edge protection (corner protectors, saddles); 0.9 = rounded edges with padding; 0.8 = soft metal edge contact; 0.7 = sharp edges without protection. ASME B30.9 §8.5: sharp edges must be padded.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §8.5 — Edge protection: ±3% for good protection, ±10% for marginal

inputs.name: Weakest Rigging Hardware WLL
inputs.hint: Working Load Limit of the weakest component in the rigging assembly (shackle, master link, hook, ring). ASME B30.26: all rigging hardware shall be rated and marked. The system is only as strong as its weakest component.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.26-2026 §5.1 — Hardware WLL rating uncertainty: ±3%

inputs.name: Load Distribution Factor (f_dist)
inputs.hint: Factor accounting for unequal load distribution among legs. 1.0 = equal distribution (load-balancing device present and verified); 0.9 = good distribution (rigid load, symmetric geometry); 0.8 = moderate distribution; 0.67 = conservative (no load-balancing, 3+ legs, ASME B30.9 default).
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.1.3 — Load distribution factor: conservative estimate ±2%

inputs.name: Manufacturer Tag / Nameplate Present
inputs.hint: Is the manufacturer tag (with WLL, grade, serial number, manufacturer ID) present and legible on each sling as required by ASME B30.9 §5.1.2?

inputs.name: Sling Inspection Status
inputs.hint: Most recent inspection status per ASME B30.9 §6: Periodic inspection (annual minimum, monthly for heavy use). PASS = no defects found. CONDITIONAL = minor wear/damage requiring engineering review. REJECTED = failed inspection, do not use.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §6 — Inspection status: zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Load Mass Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Percentage uncertainty in load mass. Weighed loads: ±1.5%. Calculated from known density/dimensions: ±5%. Estimated: ±10% or more.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME P30.1 §4.3.1 — Load weight uncertainty categories

inputs.name: Sling Angle Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Uncertainty in sling angle measurement. Digital inclinometer: ±0.5°. Protractor: ±1°. Visual estimation: ±5°.

inputs.name: Sling Capacity Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Percentage uncertainty in sling rated capacity. Manufacturer-rated: ±3%. Derated/aging sling: ±5–10%.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: sling angle out of range (V1)
fmea.effect: Sling angle θ < 30° or > 90° — invalid geometry per ASME B30.9
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: active leg count invalid (V2)
fmea.effect: Active legs outside valid range
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: load mass zero or negative (V3)
fmea.effect: Load weight undefined — lift not possible
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: DAF < 1.0 (V4)
fmea.effect: Non-conservative dynamic factor — may underestimate load
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: no load-balancing device with ≥3 legs (V5)
fmea.effect: Unequal load distribution with multi-leg bridle, conservative assessment required
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic WARNING on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: controlled available WLL ≤ 0 (V6)
fmea.effect: No effective capacity — sling or hardware WLL invalid
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: sling WLL not positive (V7)
fmea.effect: Sling capacity undefined
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: manufacturer tag missing (V8)
fmea.effect: Sling identification not present — ASME B30.9 violation
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: sling inspection REJECTED (V9)
fmea.effect: Sling condemned — do not use
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: D/d ratio ≤ 0 (V10)
fmea.effect: Invalid D/d ratio
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: capacity factor ≤ 0 (V11)
fmea.effect: Invalid capacity or distribution factor
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: SLING OVERLOAD: UC > 1.0
fmea.effect: Design tension exceeds effective WLL — risk of sling failure and dropped load
fmea.control_measure: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.4 — UC threshold monitoring with critical alert

fmea.failureMode: SLING MARGINAL: UC > 0.9
fmea.effect: High utilization — reduced safety margin for dynamic events

fmea.failureMode: LOW SLING ANGLE: θ < 45°
fmea.effect: High tension factor increases sling load significantly
fmea.control_measure: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.2.4 — Angle warning with recommendation

fmea.failureMode: Inspected CONDITIONAL sling in use
fmea.effect: Potential hidden damage or wear affecting capacity
fmea.control_measure: ASME B30.9-2025 §6.4 — Conditional use requires engineering sign-off

fmea.failureMode: D/d below minimum required
fmea.effect: Reduced sling capacity due to tight bend radius

legalNotice: Technical simulation; not legal/engineering advice. Verify before business decisions.

name: Chain Sling Working Load Limit Calculator — EN 818 / ASME B30 Chain Sling Utilization Check
subtitle: Lifting / Rigging / Chain Slings — EN 818-4, EN 818-5, EN 818-6, ASME B30.9, ASME B30.26, ASME P30.1, ISO 80000, ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM, ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1

inScope: single_operation
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.name: Chain Sling Rating Standard
inputs.hint: Applicable standard for chain sling rating. EN 818-4: Grade 8 chain slings. ASME B30.9: includes chain slings in Table 3. ISO 1835: older standard (increasingly replaced by EN 818).

inputs.name: Chain Grade / Quality
inputs.hint: Chain material grade. Grade 8 (EN 818 / ASME B30.9): most common for overhead lifting. Grade 10/12: higher strength (less common). Grade 5: tie-down/towing (not for overhead lifting per ASME B30.9). Grade 100/120: newer high-strength grades.
inputs.uncertainty_source: Chain grade marking — zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Nominal Chain Diameter
inputs.hint: Nominal diameter of chain link material. EN 818-4: 4–45 mm. ASME B30.9: up to 32 mm typical. Measure with caliper across the link cross-section.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Precision measurement tolerance: ±0.2% for calibrated measurement

inputs.name: Single Leg WLL (Vertical, per chain sling leg)
inputs.hint: Working Load Limit of a single chain sling leg in vertical configuration per EN 818-4 Table 1 or ASME B30.9 Table 3. Grade 8, 10mm chain: ~31.5 kN. Verify from manufacturer tag.
inputs.uncertainty_source: EN 818-4 §6.2 — Manufacturer WLL rating uncertainty: ±3% (includes proof test variability)

inputs.name: Load Mass to be Lifted
inputs.hint: Mass of the load including all attachments and rigging hardware suspended below the crane hook.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME P30.1 §4.3.1 — Load weight uncertainty: ±1.5% for weighed loads

inputs.name: Local Gravitational Acceleration (g)
inputs.hint: Standard gravity = 9.80665 m/s². Use local value for precision critical lifts.

inputs.name: Dynamic Amplification Factor (DAF)
inputs.hint: Factor for dynamic effects: 1.0 for static/controlled; 1.25 for normal crane lifts; 1.5 for rough terrain; 2.0 for shock loading. EN 818-4: 1.0 for static proof load, 1.25 for normal service.
inputs.uncertainty_source: EN 818-4 §7.2 — Dynamic factor uncertainty: ±5% for normal lifts

inputs.name: Chain Sling Configuration
inputs.hint: Configuration of the chain sling assembly. EN 818-5 §5 defines assembly types. Bridle = multiple legs from master link. Basket = chain wraps around load. Choker = chain passes through itself.
inputs.uncertainty_source: EN 818-5 §5 — Configuration definitions, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Number of Installed Sling Legs
inputs.hint: Total number of chain legs in the sling assembly. ASME B30.9: maximum 4 legs. EN 818-4: single, 2-leg, 3-leg, 4-leg assemblies defined.

inputs.name: Active Load-Bearing Legs
inputs.hint: Number of legs actually carrying the load. For 3-leg or 4-leg configurations without load-balancing: often only 2 legs bear the load. EN 818-5 §6.2: if load equalizing not ensured, use reduced leg count.
inputs.uncertainty_source: EN 818-5 §6.2 — Load distribution assessment, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Load-Balancing Device Present
inputs.hint: Is a load-balancing device (e.g., equalizer beam, compensation ring) used to ensure equal load distribution among all legs?

inputs.name: Angle from Vertical (β)
inputs.hint: Angle between the chain sling leg and the vertical axis. EN 818-5: maximum 45° for rated configurations (reduces to 0° vertical). 0° = vertical, 30° = 1.155x factor, 45° = 1.414x factor, 60° = 2.0x factor.
inputs.uncertainty_source: EN 818-5 §6.1 — Angle measurement uncertainty: ±2° field measurement

inputs.name: Rated Configuration Factor (C_rated)
inputs.hint: Configuration factor from EN 818-5 Table 1 or ASME B30.9 Table 3. Single vertical: 1.0. 2-leg bridle: 1.0 per leg. Basket (choked): 0.8. Basket (full): 2.0. Choker: 0.75. Verify from sling tag if leg-specific WLL is marked.
inputs.uncertainty_source: EN 818-5 Table 1 — Configuration factors, zero measurement uncertainty for standard configurations

inputs.name: Load Distribution Factor (f_dist)
inputs.hint: Factor for unequal load distribution. 1.0 = verified equal distribution (load-balancing device). 0.9 = good distribution (symmetric geometry, rigid load). 0.8 = moderate distribution. 0.67 = conservative (no load-balancing, 3+ legs).

inputs.name: Operating Temperature
inputs.hint: Ambient/operating temperature at the chain sling during the lift. EN 818-4 §7.3: Grade 8 rated for -40°C to +200°C standard. Above 200°C: capacity reduction required. Above 300°C: special alloy chain required.

inputs.name: Manufacturer Temperature Limit
inputs.hint: Maximum operating temperature specified by the chain sling manufacturer. Grade 8 standard: 200°C. Extended temperature range chains may be rated to 300°C or higher. Verify from sling tag or manufacturer data sheet.

inputs.name: Temperature Capacity Factor (f_temp)
inputs.hint: Capacity derating factor for elevated temperature. EN 818-4 §7.3: T ≤ 200°C → 1.0; 200°C < T ≤ 300°C → 0.8; 300°C < T ≤ 400°C → 0.5. Below -40°C → use cold-temperature rated chain only.

inputs.name: Edge Contact / Bend Radius Factor (f_edge)
inputs.hint: Factor for edge contact or sharp bend radius on chain links. 1.0 = no edge contact, proper radius. 0.9 = rounded edges with padding. 0.8 = soft metal edge contact. 0.7 = sharp edges without protection. EN 818-6 §8.5: sharp edges must be protected.

inputs.name: Wear Derating Factor (f_wear)
inputs.hint: Derating for chain link wear (reduction in link diameter). ASME B30.9 §6.2.3: reject chain when wear exceeds 10% of original diameter. f_wear = (actual_diameter / original_diameter). 1.0 = no significant wear; 0.9 = 10% wear (rejection limit); 0.5 = 50% section loss.

inputs.name: Additional Derating Factor (f_add)
inputs.hint: Additional derating factor for any other conditions affecting capacity (corrosion, link deformation, nicks/gouges, fatigue cracks, or adverse environmental conditions not covered by other factors). Default 1.0 = no additional derating.

inputs.name: Weakest Rigging Hardware WLL
inputs.hint: Working Load Limit of the weakest component in the rigging assembly: master links, intermediate links, hooks, shackles, rings, or load pins. ASME B30.26: all components must be rated and marked with WLL.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.26-2026 §5.1 — Hardware WLL rating uncertainty: ±3%

inputs.name: Manufacturer Tag / Nameplate Present
inputs.hint: Is the manufacturer tag (with WLL, grade, chain size, serial number, manufacturer ID, and standard reference) present and legible on the chain sling assembly per EN 818-6 §7 or ASME B30.9 §5.1.2?

inputs.name: Manufacturer Certificate Available
inputs.hint: Is the manufacturer test certificate (EN 818-4 §8: proof load test, material traceability, batch number) available and traceable?

inputs.name: Chain Sling Inspection Status
inputs.hint: Inspection status per EN 818-6 §6 or ASME B30.9 §6. PASS = no defects. CONDITIONAL = minor issues requiring engineering review. REJECTED = failed inspection, do not use.
inputs.uncertainty_source: EN 818-6 §6 — Inspection status, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Chain Twisted or Knotted
inputs.hint: Is the chain sling twisted or knotted? EN 818-6 §8.2: chain slings must not be used if twisted, knotted, or kinked. Twisted chains have reduced capacity and may fail unexpectedly.

inputs.name: Chemical Exposure Status
inputs.hint: Exposure to chemicals that may affect chain integrity. EN 818-6 §8.6: chain slings must not be exposed to corrosive chemicals without protective coating. Acid/alkali exposure requires immediate inspection and possible replacement.

inputs.name: Personnel Lifting Application
inputs.hint: Is this chain sling used for personnel lifting? EN 818-6 §4: chain slings for personnel lifting require 10:1 design factor (vs. 4:1 for material lifting) and additional inspection/certification requirements.

inputs.name: Load Mass Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Percentage uncertainty in load mass. Weighed loads: ±1.5%. Calculated: ±5%. Estimated: ±10%.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME P30.1 §4.3.1 — Load weight uncertainty categories

inputs.name: Angle Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Uncertainty in angle from vertical measurement. Digital inclinometer: ±0.5°. Protractor: ±1°. Visual estimation: ±5°.

inputs.name: Chain Sling Capacity Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Percentage uncertainty in chain sling rated capacity. Manufacturer-rated (certified): ±3%. Used/aged sling (wear not quantified): ±5–10%.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: angle out of range 0–60° (V1)
fmea.effect: Angle exceeds EN 818-5 limit — excessive leg tension
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: active leg count invalid (V2)
fmea.effect: Invalid leg count for load distribution
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: load mass zero/negative (V3)
fmea.effect: Load weight undefined — lift not possible
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: DAF < 1.0 (V4)
fmea.effect: Non-conservative dynamic factor — may underestimate load
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: no load-balancing with ≥3 legs (V5)
fmea.effect: Unequal load distribution — conservative assessment required
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic WARNING on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: manufacturer tag missing (V8)
fmea.effect: EN 818-6 §7.1 violation — sling identity unknown
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: inspection REJECTED (V9)
fmea.effect: Chain sling condemned — do not use
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: chain twisted or knotted (V11)
fmea.effect: EN 818-6 §8.2 violation — reduced capacity, unpredictable failure
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: significant chemical exposure (V12)
fmea.effect: Potential corrosion damage — reduced integrity
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: personnel lifting without EN 818 (V13)
fmea.effect: Inadequate design factor for personnel safety
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: personnel lifting with non-PASS inspection (V14)
fmea.effect: Inadequate inspection status for personnel lifting
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: certificate not available (V15)
fmea.effect: EN 818-4 §8.1 violation — no traceability
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: CHAIN SLING OVERLOAD: UC > 1.0
fmea.effect: Tension exceeds WLL — potential chain failure and dropped load
fmea.control_measure: EN 818-5 §6.6 — UC threshold monitoring with critical alert

fmea.failureMode: CHAIN SLING MARGINAL: UC > 0.9
fmea.effect: High utilization — reduced safety margin

fmea.failureMode: HIGH ANGLE: β > 45° for chain sling
fmea.effect: Exceeds EN 818-5 rated angle range for standard assemblies

fmea.failureMode: ELEVATED TEMPERATURE: capacity derated
fmea.effect: Reduced chain strength at elevated temperature

fmea.failureMode: CHAIN WEAR: wear derating applied
fmea.effect: Reduced cross-section from wear — reduced capacity
fmea.control_measure: ASME B30.9 §6.2.3 — Wear measurement and rejection criteria

name: Plate Bending Force Calculator — Sheet Metal Forming / Press Brake Force and Capacity Utilization Check
subtitle: Sheet Metal / Press Brake / Plate Forming — DIN 8586 — Sheet metal forming by bending, ISO 7438 — Metallic materials — Bend test, ISO 16092-3 — Machine tools safety — Press brakes, ISO 12100 — Safety of machinery — General principles for design — Risk assessment and risk reduction, ISO 80000 — Quantities and units, ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 — Uncertainty of measurement (GUM), ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1 — Production and service provision — Control of production, VDI 3441 — Statistical testing of the operational and positional accuracy of machine tools, Manufacturer press brake manual — Capacity chart and tooling specifications
standardsBasis: DIN 8586 — Sheet metal forming by bending
ISO 16092-3 — Machine tools safety — Press brakes
ISO 12100 — Safety of machinery — General principles for design — Risk assessment and risk reduction
ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1 — Production and service provision — Control of production
VDI 3441 — Statistical testing of the operational and positional accuracy of machine tools
Manufacturer press brake manual — Capacity chart and tooling specifications

inScope: single_operation
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.name: Bending Process Type
inputs.hint: Select the press brake bending process. Air bending: workpiece contacts V-die at three points. Bottoming: punch forces material into die at full stroke. Coining: punch penetrates material surface for minimal springback

inputs.name: Material Family
inputs.hint: Material family classification. Selection affects recommended V-die ratio, springback characteristics, and minimum bend radius

inputs.name: Ultimate Tensile Strength (UTS)
inputs.hint: Ultimate tensile strength of the workpiece material. Low-carbon steel: 270-400 MPa. Stainless 304: 500-700 MPa. Aluminum 6061: 260-310 MPa. Use material certificate or standard reference
inputs.uncertainty_source: Material certificate or standard reference data uncertainty

inputs.name: Yield Strength (YS)
inputs.hint: Yield strength of workpiece material. Low-carbon steel: 180-250 MPa. Stainless 304: 200-350 MPa. Aluminum 6061: 140-240 MPa
inputs.uncertainty_source: Material certificate or standard reference data uncertainty

inputs.name: Elastic Modulus (Young's Modulus)
inputs.hint: Elastic modulus of workpiece material. Steel: 200 GPa. Stainless: 193 GPa. Aluminum: 69 GPa. Copper: 110 GPa. Titanium: 110 GPa

inputs.name: Workpiece Thickness
inputs.hint: Nominal thickness of the workpiece being bent. Sheet metal: 0.5-6 mm. Plate: 6-100 mm

inputs.name: Bend Length (Part Width)
inputs.hint: Length of the bend line (width of the workpiece perpendicular to the bend axis). Longer bends require higher total force

inputs.name: V-Die Opening Width
inputs.hint: Width of the V-die opening. Recommended V-die = 8× to 12× material thickness for air bending

inputs.name: Punch Tip Radius
inputs.hint: Radius at the tip of the punch. Typically 0.5-2.0 mm for sheet metal. Affects inside bend radius and force distribution

inputs.name: Target Bend Angle
inputs.hint: Desired included bend angle. 90° is a standard right-angle bend. Acute bends < 90° may require special tooling
inputs.uncertainty_source: Angle gauge or protractor measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Bending Force Coefficient (K1)
inputs.hint: Empirical bending force coefficient. Standard air bending: 1.42. Bottoming: 5. Coining: 10+. Increase for tight radii, high-strength materials

inputs.name: Process Force Multiplier
inputs.hint: Force multiplier for the selected bending process. Air bending: 1.0. Bottoming: 2.0-5.0. Coining: 5.0-10.0. Determined by process type and depth of penetration

inputs.name: Grain Direction Factor
inputs.hint: Factor accounting for material grain orientation. Across grain: 1.0 (default). With grain: 1.1-1.3 (higher force). Diagonal: 0.9-1.0. Bending with the grain increases cracking risk

inputs.name: Friction and Lubrication Factor
inputs.hint: Empirical factor for lubrication conditions. Well lubricated: 0.9-1.0. Dry: 1.0-1.15. Poor lubrication: 1.15-1.3. Affects sliding friction between workpiece and tooling

inputs.name: Safety Factor for Force Calculation
inputs.hint: Safety factor applied to the required force. ISO 12100 risk assessment: minimum 1.2. Increase for unknown material properties or worn tooling

inputs.name: Press Brake Nominal Capacity
inputs.hint: Rated maximum force of the press brake. Common capacities: 500 kN (50 ton), 1000 kN (100 ton), 2000 kN (200 ton). Check machine nameplate

inputs.name: Usable Bed Length (Between Side Frames)
inputs.hint: Maximum usable bed length between press brake side frames. Common: 2000-4000 mm. Longer beds reduce force per meter capacity

inputs.name: Upper Tool (Punch) Capacity
inputs.hint: Maximum allowable force per meter on the upper tool (punch). Varies by punch type, material, and heat treatment. Check tooling manufacturer specification

inputs.name: Lower Die (V-Die) Capacity
inputs.hint: Maximum allowable force per meter on the lower die (V-die). Check die manufacturer capacity chart

inputs.name: Minimum Permitted Inside Radius
inputs.hint: Minimum acceptable inside bend radius. Used to check if the predicted radius meets print requirement. Set to 0 if no minimum requirement

inputs.name: Off-Center Load Factor
inputs.hint: Factor for off-center loading. Centered load (symmetric): 1.0. Partially off-center: 1.25-1.5. Full off-center (one side frame): 1.5-2.0. Off-center reduces effective machine capacity

inputs.name: Operator Guarding Verified (ISO 16092-3)
inputs.hint: Operator guarding and safety devices verified per ISO 16092-3. Includes light curtains, two-hand controls, and rear guarding

inputs.name: Tooling Verification Completed
inputs.hint: Punch and die tooling verified for correct mounting, alignment, and capacity rating. Confirm in machine setup checklist

inputs.name: Thickness Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of thickness measurement. Caliper/micrometer resolution: typically ±0.03 mm. Laser gauge: ±0.01 mm

inputs.name: Material Strength Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Relative standard uncertainty of tensile strength and yield strength values. Material certificate: ±1-3%. Generic reference: ±5-10%

inputs.name: Force Coefficient Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Relative standard uncertainty of the empirical bending force coefficient. DIN standard coefficients: ±5%. Custom estimates: ±10-20%

standards: DIN 8586 — Sheet metal forming by bending
ISO 16092-3 — Machine tools safety — Press brakes
ISO 12100 — Safety of machinery — General principles for design — Risk assessment and risk reduction
ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1 — Production and service provision — Control of production
VDI 3441 — Statistical testing of the operational and positional accuracy of machine tools
Manufacturer press brake manual — Capacity chart and tooling specifications

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: thickness_positive
fmea.effect: Plate thickness must be positive.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: tensile_strength_positive
fmea.effect: Tensile strength must be positive.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: PRESS BRAKE OVERLOAD
fmea.effect: Required force exceeds machine capacity. Risk of mechanical failure, ram damage, or operator injury.
fmea.control_measure: VDI 3441 — Capacity check with utilization ratio

fmea.failureMode: UPPER TOOL OVERLOAD
fmea.effect: Force per meter exceeds punch capacity. Risk of punch fracture, tip chipping, or bending.
fmea.control_measure: Tooling manufacturer specification — force per meter check

fmea.failureMode: LOWER DIE OVERLOAD
fmea.effect: Force per meter exceeds die capacity. Risk of die cracking, V-opening deformation, or die insert failure.
fmea.control_measure: Die manufacturer specification — force per meter check

fmea.failureMode: INSIDE RADIUS BELOW MINIMUM
fmea.effect: Bend radius smaller than print requirement. Part rejection.

fmea.failureMode: V-DIE RATIO OUT OF RANGE
fmea.effect: V-die opening to thickness ratio outside safe range. Increased force, poor bend quality, or die damage.

fmea.failureMode: SIGNIFICANT SPRINGBACK
fmea.effect: Large springback causes incorrect final bend angle. Requires rework or overbend compensation.
fmea.control_measure: DIN 8586 — Overbend compensation or coining process

fmea.failureMode: OPERATOR GUARDING NOT VERIFIED
fmea.effect: Safety devices not verified. Risk of operator injury from pinch points or flying debris.

fmea.failureMode: TOOLING NOT VERIFIED
fmea.effect: Tooling condition unknown. Risk of incorrect mounting, wear damage, or capacity mismatch.

name: Press Brake Bend Allowance and K-Factor Calculator — Sheet Metal Flat Pattern Development Check
subtitle: Sheet Metal / Press Brake / Flat Pattern Development — DIN 6935 — Sheet metal — Bending, dimensions of bent parts, DIN 8586 — Sheet metal forming by bending, ISO 7438 — Metallic materials — Bend test, ISO 2768-m — General tolerances — Part 1: Tolerances for linear and angular dimensions, ISO 1101 — Geometrical product specifications (GPS) — Geometrical tolerancing, ISO 80000 — Quantities and units, ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 — Uncertainty of measurement (GUM), ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1 — Production and service provision — Control of production, Manufacturer press brake, tooling, and CAM bend table — Bend allowance and K-factor reference data
standardsBasis: DIN 6935 — Sheet metal — Bending, dimensions of bent parts
DIN 8586 — Sheet metal forming by bending
ISO 2768-m — General tolerances — Part 1: Tolerances for linear and angular dimensions
ISO 1101 — Geometrical product specifications (GPS) — Geometrical tolerancing
ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1 — Production and service provision — Control of production
Manufacturer press brake, tooling, and CAM bend table — Bend allowance and K-factor reference data

inScope: single_operation
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.name: Calculation Mode
inputs.hint: Select calculation mode. KNOWN_K_FACTOR: enter K-factor directly. DERIVE_K_FROM_KNOWN_BEND_ALLOWANCE: compute K-factor from known bend allowance. DERIVE_K_FROM_MEASURED_FLAT_LENGTH: compute K-factor from measured flat pattern length

inputs.name: Bend Method
inputs.hint: Bending method. Affects K-factor typically: Air bending K ≈ 0.33-0.42. Bottoming K ≈ 0.25-0.35. Coining K ≈ 0.20-0.30. Roll bending and wipe bending K varies by application

inputs.name: Material Family
inputs.hint: Material family. Affects K-factor reference ranges and minimum bend radius recommendations

inputs.name: Material Thickness
inputs.hint: Nominal material thickness. Sheet metal: 0.5-6 mm. Plate: 6-50 mm

inputs.name: Inside Bend Radius
inputs.hint: Inside bend radius of the finished part. For air bending, inside radius ≈ punch tip radius or V/6.67 (whichever is larger)

inputs.name: Bend Sweep Angle
inputs.hint: The angle through which the material bends (complement of the included angle). 90° sweep = right-angle bend
inputs.uncertainty_source: Angle gauge or CNC controller position feedback

inputs.name: Known K-Factor
inputs.hint: Known K-factor value. Typical values: soft materials (aluminum, copper): 0.33-0.40. Steel: 0.38-0.42. Hard materials (stainless, titanium): 0.25-0.35. Required when calculation_mode = KNOWN_K_FACTOR
inputs.uncertainty_source: Material reference table or CAM bend table

inputs.name: K-Factor Source
inputs.hint: Source of the K-factor value. MEASURED_COUPON: highest confidence. SHOP_CAM_TABLE: high confidence. MACHINE_TOOLING_TABLE: medium-high confidence. DIN_6935_REFERENCE: medium confidence. MATERIAL_SUPPLIER_DATA: medium confidence. ENGINEERING_ESTIMATE: low confidence

inputs.name: Outside Flange Length 1
inputs.hint: Length of the first outside flange (from bend tangent point to part edge). Used for outside setback calculation

inputs.name: Outside Flange Length 2
inputs.hint: Length of the second outside flange (from bend tangent point to part edge)

inputs.name: Measured Bend Allowance
inputs.hint: Measured bend allowance from a physical coupon test. Required when calculation_mode = DERIVE_K_FROM_KNOWN_BEND_ALLOWANCE

inputs.name: Measured Flat Pattern Length
inputs.hint: Measured total flat pattern length (sum of straight segments + bend allowance). Required when calculation_mode = DERIVE_K_FROM_MEASURED_FLAT_LENGTH

inputs.name: Minimum Permitted Inside Radius
inputs.hint: Minimum acceptable inside bend radius per ISO 7438 or print requirement. Set to 0 if no minimum requirement

inputs.name: V-Die Opening Width (optional)
inputs.hint: V-die opening width. Optional — used for calculated inside radius estimation if inside_bend_radius_mm not known from tooling. When provided, helps validate bend deduction

inputs.name: Grain Direction Relative to Bend Line
inputs.hint: Grain direction relative to the bend axis. ACROSS_GRAIN: preferred (bend line perpendicular to rolling direction). WITH_GRAIN: increases cracking risk. DIAGONAL: reduced effect. UNKNOWN: assumes worst case

inputs.name: Target Flat Length Tolerance
inputs.hint: Maximum acceptable deviation from the calculated flat pattern length. ISO 2768-m typical: ±0.2 mm for precision, ±0.5 mm for general tolerance

inputs.name: Thickness Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of thickness measurement

inputs.name: Radius Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of inside radius measurement

inputs.name: K-Factor Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of the K-factor. MEASURED_COUPON: ±0.005. SHOP_CAM_TABLE: ±0.01. DIN_6935_REFERENCE: ±0.02. ENGINEERING_ESTIMATE: ±0.05

inputs.name: Angle Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of bend angle measurement
inputs.uncertainty_source: Angle gauge or CNC position feedback calibration

standards: DIN 6935 — Sheet metal — Bending, dimensions of bent parts
DIN 8586 — Sheet metal forming by bending
ISO 2768-m — General tolerances — Part 1: Tolerances for linear and angular dimensions
ISO 1101 — Geometrical product specifications (GPS) — Geometrical tolerancing
ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1 — Production and service provision — Control of production
Manufacturer press brake, tooling, and CAM bend table — Bend allowance and K-factor reference data

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: thickness_positive
fmea.effect: Material thickness must be positive.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: bend_angle_valid
fmea.effect: Bend angle must be between 0.1° and 180°.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: FLAT LENGTH OUTSIDE TOLERANCE
fmea.effect: Uncertainty exceeds tolerance. Inconsistent bend results in production.

fmea.failureMode: K-FACTOR CONFIDENCE LOW
fmea.effect: Flat pattern length may be incorrect. Rework or scrap risk.

fmea.failureMode: INSIDE RADIUS BELOW RECOMMENDED
fmea.effect: Material cracking or fracture during bending.

fmea.failureMode: MODE MISMATCH — Missing input for selected mode
fmea.effect: Required input for selected calculation mode is missing.

fmea.failureMode: R/T RATIO OUT OF RANGE
fmea.effect: K-factor reference may not be reliable for extreme R/t ratios.

fmea.failureMode: GRAIN DIRECTION — With grain at low R/t
fmea.effect: Material cracking, especially in high-strength materials.

name: Sheet Metal Flat Pattern Development Calculator — Multi-Bend Blank Length and Bend Line Stack-Up Check
subtitle: Sheet Metal / Press Brake / Flat Pattern Development — DIN 6935 — Sheet metal — Bending, dimensions of bent parts, DIN 8586 — Sheet metal forming by bending, ISO 7438 — Metallic materials — Bend test, ISO 2768-m — General tolerances — Part 1: Tolerances for linear and angular dimensions, ISO 1101 — Geometrical product specifications (GPS) — Geometrical tolerancing, ISO 80000 — Quantities and units, ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 — Uncertainty of measurement (GUM), ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1 — Production and service provision — Control of production
standardsBasis: DIN 6935 — Sheet metal — Bending, dimensions of bent parts
DIN 8586 — Sheet metal forming by bending
ISO 2768-m — General tolerances — Part 1: Tolerances for linear and angular dimensions
ISO 1101 — Geometrical product specifications (GPS) — Geometrical tolerancing
ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1 — Production and service provision — Control of production

inScope: single_operation
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.name: Flat Pattern Development Method
inputs.hint: Flat pattern development method. BEND_ALLOWANCE_SUM: sum straight segments + total bend allowance. BEND_DEDUCTION_SUM: sum outside dimensions - total bend deduction. K_FACTOR_PER_BEND: computed from effective K-factor for all bends. CAM_TABLE_VERIFIED: use verified CAM bend table data

inputs.name: Dimension Convention
inputs.hint: Dimensioning convention used on the part print. OUTSIDE_VIRTUAL_SHARP: dimensions to virtual sharp intersections. INSIDE_TANGENT: dimensions to inside tangent points. CENTERLINE: dimensions to centerline. MIXED_REQUIRES_CONVERSION: mixed conventions — requires conversion to a single method

inputs.name: Bend Method
inputs.hint: Bending method used for all bends in the part. Affects K-factor range and minimum bend radius

inputs.name: Material Family
inputs.hint: Material family. Affects K-factor reference ranges and minimum bend radius recommendations

inputs.name: Material Thickness
inputs.hint: Nominal material thickness. Sheet metal: 0.5-6 mm. Plate: 6-50 mm

inputs.name: Number of Bends
inputs.hint: Number of bends in the part. 1-12 bends supported. More than 12 bends: use iterative flat pattern development or CAM software

inputs.name: Number of Straight Segments
inputs.hint: Number of straight segments in the developed part. For a part with N bends: N+1 straight segments. Minimum 2 (one bend), maximum 13 (12 bends)

inputs.name: Sum of Straight Segment Lengths
inputs.hint: Total sum of all straight segment centerline lengths in the flat pattern. For outside virtual sharp convention: this excludes bend allowance contribution

inputs.name: Total Bend Sweep Angle
inputs.hint: Sum of all individual bend sweep angles. For 4 bends at 90° each: 360°. Maximum: 12 bends × 180° = 2160°
inputs.uncertainty_source: Angle specification tolerance per angle sum

inputs.name: Average Inside Bend Radius
inputs.hint: Average inside bend radius across all bends. If radii vary, use length-weighted average. Typical: punch tip radius or V/6.67 for air bending
inputs.uncertainty_source: Radius gauge or average of individual bend measurements

inputs.name: Minimum Inside Bend Radius (any bend)
inputs.hint: The smallest inside radius among all bends in the part. Used for formability check

inputs.name: Minimum Permitted Inside Radius (material limit)
inputs.hint: Smallest inside radius permitted for the material per ISO 7438. If minimum_inside_radius_mm < minimum_permitted_inside_radius_mm, cracking risk exists

inputs.name: Effective K-Factor
inputs.hint: Effective K-factor for all bends (or average if K varies). Determined from coupon test, CAM table, or DIN 6935 reference. Used to compute bend allowance per bend
inputs.uncertainty_source: Material reference table or CAM bend table

inputs.name: K-Factor Source
inputs.hint: Source of the K-factor value. MEASURED_COUPON: highest confidence. ENGINEERING_ESTIMATE: lowest confidence

inputs.name: Total Known Bend Allowance (sum of all bends)
inputs.hint: Sum of known bend allowances for all bends. Optional — used when bend allowance data is available from CAM table or coupon test. Overrides K-factor calculation
inputs.uncertainty_source: CAM table or coupon test data

inputs.name: Total Known Bend Deduction (sum of all bends)
inputs.hint: Sum of known bend deductions for all bends. Optional — used with BEND_DEDUCTION_SUM method
inputs.uncertainty_source: CAM table or coupon test data

inputs.name: Sum of Outside Setbacks (all bends)
inputs.hint: Sum of all individual outside setback values. Optional — used for validation of flat pattern calculation
inputs.uncertainty_source: Calculated from individual bend geometry or CAM table

inputs.name: Blank Width (perpendicular to bend axis)
inputs.hint: Width of the blank perpendicular to the bend axis. Used for blank area calculation

inputs.name: Edge Trim Allowance per Side
inputs.hint: Additional material allowance per edge for post-bend trimming. Typical: 0.5-3 mm per side for clean edges

inputs.name: Saw or Laser Kerf Allowance
inputs.hint: Material removed by saw or laser cut per cut. Laser: 0.1-0.3 mm. Saw: 1-3 mm. Plasma: 1.5-3 mm

inputs.name: Grain Direction Relative to Bend Line
inputs.hint: Grain direction relative to the primary bend axis. ACROSS_GRAIN: preferred (bend line perpendicular to rolling direction). WITH_GRAIN: increases cracking risk

inputs.name: Bend Relief Provided
inputs.hint: Whether bend relief notches are provided at bend ends to prevent tearing. Recommended for flanges adjacent to bends

inputs.name: Minimum Flange Length in Part
inputs.hint: The shortest flange length in the part. Used for minimum flange length check

inputs.name: Minimum Required Flange Length
inputs.hint: Minimum flange length required for the given V-die opening (typically: V_die / 2 + flange clearance). Shorter flanges may not be formable
inputs.uncertainty_source: Die geometry and part design rule

inputs.name: Target Flat Length Tolerance
inputs.hint: Maximum acceptable deviation from the calculated flat pattern length. ISO 2768-m typical: ±0.2 mm precision, ±0.5 mm general

inputs.name: Length Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of straight segment length measurements

inputs.name: Thickness Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of thickness measurement

inputs.name: Radius Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of inside radius measurement

inputs.name: K-Factor Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of the K-factor. MEASURED_COUPON: ±0.005. SHOP_CAM_TABLE: ±0.01. DIN_6935_REFERENCE: ±0.02. ENGINEERING_ESTIMATE: ±0.05

inputs.name: Angle Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of bend angle measurement per individual bend, summed across all bends
inputs.uncertainty_source: Angle gauge or CNC position feedback calibration

standards: DIN 6935 — Sheet metal — Bending, dimensions of bent parts
DIN 8586 — Sheet metal forming by bending
ISO 2768-m — General tolerances — Part 1: Tolerances for linear and angular dimensions
ISO 1101 — Geometrical product specifications (GPS) — Geometrical tolerancing
ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1 — Production and service provision — Control of production

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: bend_count_valid
fmea.effect: Bend count must be 1-12.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: segment_count_valid
fmea.effect: Segment count must equal bend_count + 1.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: STACK-UP UC EXCEEDED
fmea.effect: Flat pattern length likely out of tolerance or flange length inadequate.

fmea.failureMode: FLANGE LENGTH INADEQUATE
fmea.effect: Flange too short to form. Part geometry not achievable.

fmea.failureMode: K-FACTOR VALIDATION REQUIRED
fmea.effect: Flat pattern length may be incorrect for multi-bend part. Rework or scrap risk.
fmea.control_measure: DIN 6935 — Coupon test recommended before production

fmea.failureMode: INSIDE RADIUS BELOW MATERIAL LIMIT
fmea.effect: Material cracking at one or more bend locations.

fmea.failureMode: TOLERANCE MARGIN LOW (multi-bend)
fmea.effect: Accumulated bend uncertainty consumes most of the tolerance band.

fmea.failureMode: GRAIN DIRECTION — Multiple with-grain bends
fmea.effect: Increased cracking risk at multiple bend locations.
fmea.control_measure: ISO 7438 — Grain direction check for all bends

name: Laser Cutting Time and Assist Gas Cost Calculator — Sheet Metal Laser Process Time, Gas Consumption and Cost Check
subtitle: Sheet Metal / Laser Cutting / Cost Engineering — ISO 9013, ISO 11145, ISO 14955-1, ISO 11553-1, IEC 60825-1, ISO 80000, ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM, ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1, Machine OEM cutting database

inScope: single_operation
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.name: Material Thickness
inputs.hint: Sheet or plate thickness in mm. Laser cutting range depends on laser power and material type.

inputs.name: Laser Power (at workpiece)
inputs.hint: Effective laser power delivered to the workpiece. Fiber: 1-12 kW, CO2: 0.5-6 kW. Check machine calibration log.

inputs.name: Machine Rated Laser Power
inputs.hint: Rated laser power from machine nameplate. Used for power utilization ratio calculation.

inputs.name: Programmed Cut Length
inputs.hint: Total programmed cutting path length per part from CAM system.

inputs.name: Lead-in / Lead-out Length
inputs.hint: Total lead-in and lead-out length per contour. Typically 1-5 mm per pierce.

inputs.name: Average Programmed Feed Rate
inputs.hint: Average cutting feed rate from CAM system for the programmed cutting path. Fiber laser mild steel 1mm: ~6000-12000 mm/min; 6mm: ~2000-4000 mm/min.

inputs.name: Feed Override Factor
inputs.hint: Actual feed rate as fraction of programmed. Operator override: 0.8-1.2 typical. Acceleration-limited paths may reduce effective feed.

inputs.name: Contour Complexity Factor
inputs.hint: Factor compensating for acceleration/deceleration losses on complex contours. Simple rectangles: 1.0; tight radii < 2mm: 1.3-1.5; high-detail with many small features: 2.0-3.0.

inputs.name: Number of Pierces
inputs.hint: Total number of pierce operations per part. Each closed contour typically requires one pierce + lead-in.

inputs.name: Average Pierce Time
inputs.hint: Average time per pierce operation. Fiber laser mild steel 1mm: 0.3-0.5 s; 6mm: 1-3 s; 12mm: 3-8 s. Pulsed piercing is faster than ramp piercing.
inputs.uncertainty_source: Machine OEM cutting database — pierce time variability

inputs.name: Rapid Traverse Distance
inputs.hint: Total rapid traverse (positioning) distance per part between cutting operations.

inputs.name: Average Rapid Traverse Speed
inputs.hint: Average rapid traverse speed. Typical: 30000-60000 mm/min for mid-range; up to 120000 mm/min for high-speed gantry.

inputs.name: Auxiliary Time per Part
inputs.hint: Additional time per part for loading/unloading, part separation (micro-joints), marking, and inspection.

inputs.name: Setup Time
inputs.hint: Job setup time including sheet loading, program selection, nozzle check, focus calibration, and gas purge.

inputs.name: Measured Cutting Gas Flow
inputs.hint: Directly measured gas flow rate at nozzle in normal cubic meters per hour. Required when gas_flow_mode = MEASURED_FLOW.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5167-1:2003 — Flow measurement uncertainty ±2% for calibrated thermal mass flow meter

inputs.name: Nozzle Diameter
inputs.hint: Cutting nozzle orifice diameter. Typical: 1.0-3.0 mm for sheet metal laser cutting.

inputs.name: Assist Gas Pressure
inputs.hint: Assist gas pressure at nozzle inlet. Nitrogen: 6-20 bar; Oxygen: 0.5-6 bar; Compressed air: 4-10 bar.
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Pressure transducer class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Calibrated Flow Coefficient (Cv_nozzle)
inputs.hint: Empirical nozzle flow coefficient for CALIBRATED_NOZZLE_PRESSURE_ESTIMATE mode. Typical range: 0.1-1.5 for conical nozzles. Derived from OEM nozzle flow characterization.
inputs.uncertainty_source: Nozzle flow calibration — empirical coefficient uncertainty

inputs.name: Pierce Gas Flow Factor
inputs.hint: Gas flow multiplier during piercing vs. steady-state cutting. Pulsed piercing at high pressure: 2-4x. Ramp piercing: 1-2x.

inputs.name: Assist Gas Unit Cost
inputs.hint: Cost per normal cubic meter of assist gas. Nitrogen (liquid): 0.10-0.50 USD/Nm³; Oxygen: 0.15-0.60 USD/Nm³; Argon: 1.0-5.0 USD/Nm³; Compressed air: 0.02-0.10 USD/Nm³.

inputs.name: Machine Hour Rate
inputs.hint: Fully loaded machine hour rate including depreciation, maintenance, floor space, labor, and overhead. Typical laser cutting: 50-200 USD/h.
inputs.uncertainty_source: Cost accounting — machine hour rate estimate

inputs.name: Average Machine Electrical Power (cutting)
inputs.hint: Average electrical power consumption during cutting (including chiller, exhaust, drives, CNC, and laser power supply). Fiber 4kW: ~15-25 kW total; CO2 4kW: ~30-50 kW total.
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61000-4-30:2015 — Power measurement uncertainty Class A: ±0.5% (system-level: ±10% including auxiliary loads)

inputs.name: Standby Electrical Power
inputs.hint: Electrical power consumption in standby/idle mode (laser off, drives idle, chiller standby). Typical: 3-10 kW for fiber laser systems.

inputs.name: Electricity Unit Cost
inputs.hint: Industrial electricity cost per kWh including demand charges, taxes, and surcharges. Typical: 0.08-0.25 USD/kWh.

inputs.name: Consumable Cost per Pierce
inputs.hint: Consumable cost attributed per pierce (nozzle wear, lens contamination, protective window degradation). Approx: 0.001-0.05 USD/pierce for fiber laser.

inputs.name: Consumable Cost per Cut Meter
inputs.hint: Consumable cost per meter of cut (protective window, nozzle wear, focus lens degradation). Approx: 0.01-0.10 USD/m.

inputs.name: Scrap / Recut Allowance Factor
inputs.hint: Factor to account for scrap parts requiring re-cutting and process inefficiency. 1.0 = no scrap; 1.05 = 5% scrap rate typical.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: feed_rate_positive
fmea.effect: Feed rate must be positive.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: rapid_speed_positive
fmea.effect: Rapid speed must be positive.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: rated_power_positive
fmea.effect: Rated power must be positive.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: laser_power_not_exceed_rated
fmea.effect: Laser power cannot exceed rated power.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: laser_safety_required
fmea.effect: Laser safety verification is required.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Laser operating well below rated power.

fmea.failureMode: Extremely slow feed rate < 100 mm/min
fmea.effect: High risk of excessive heat input and dross formation.

name: Plasma Cutting Cost and Kerf Compensation Calculator — Thermal Cutting Time, Gas, Consumable and Offset Check
subtitle: Sheet Metal / Plasma Cutting / Cost Engineering — ISO 9013, ISO 17658, IEC 60974-1, IEC 60974-8, ISO 14955-1, ISO 80000, ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM, ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1, Machine OEM cutting database

inScope: single_operation
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.name: Material Thickness
inputs.hint: Plate thickness in mm. Plasma cutting range: 0.5-150 mm depending on system type and power.

inputs.name: Nominal Part Dimension
inputs.hint: Nominal design dimension of the part in the direction relevant for kerf compensation evaluation. Used for tolerance check.

inputs.name: Programmed Cut Length
inputs.hint: Total programmed cutting path length per part from CAM system.

inputs.name: Lead-in / Lead-out Length
inputs.hint: Total lead-in and lead-out length per contour. Typically 3-10 mm per pierce for plasma.

inputs.name: Number of Pierces
inputs.hint: Total number of pierce operations per part. Each closed contour typically requires one pierce.

inputs.name: Average Cut Speed
inputs.hint: Average cutting speed from CAM cutting table. HD plasma mild steel 6mm: ~3000-5000 mm/min; 12mm: ~1500-2500 mm/min; 25mm: ~700-1200 mm/min.
inputs.uncertainty_source: CAM system — cutting speed definition per ISO 9013

inputs.name: Speed Override Factor
inputs.hint: Actual cut speed as fraction of programmed. Operator override typically 0.8-1.1.

inputs.name: Contour Complexity Factor
inputs.hint: Factor compensating for acceleration/deceleration losses on complex contours. Simple rectangles: 1.0; tight radii: 1.3-1.5; high-detail: 2.0-3.0.

inputs.name: Average Pierce Time
inputs.hint: Average time per pierce operation. HD plasma mild steel 6mm: 0.5-1.5 s; 12mm: 1.5-3 s; 25mm: 3-8 s.
inputs.uncertainty_source: Machine OEM cutting database — pierce time variability

inputs.name: Rapid Traverse Distance
inputs.hint: Total rapid traverse distance per part between cutting operations.

inputs.name: Average Rapid Traverse Speed
inputs.hint: Average rapid traverse speed. Typical plasma gantry: 20000-60000 mm/min.

inputs.name: Setup Time
inputs.hint: Job setup time including plate loading, program selection, torch height calibration, gas purge, and consumable inspection.

inputs.name: Auxiliary Time per Part
inputs.hint: Additional time per part for unloading, slag removal, marking, and inspection.

inputs.name: Measured Kerf Width
inputs.hint: Measured kerf width from cut test piece. HD plasma: 1.0-2.0 mm (12mm plate); air plasma: 2.0-5.0 mm. Use calibrated optical measurement.

inputs.name: Target Dimension Tolerance
inputs.hint: Required dimensional tolerance for the part. ISO 9013: Fine quality ±0.5mm; Medium ±1.0mm; Rough ±2.0mm.

inputs.name: Arc Current (I)
inputs.hint: Plasma arc current from power supply setting. HD plasma: 50-400 A typical; air plasma: 20-100 A.
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60974-1 — Welding/plasma power source current accuracy ±1% of setting

inputs.name: Arc Voltage (U)
inputs.hint: Plasma arc voltage. Typically 100-200 V for HD plasma cutting. Higher voltage = thicker material capacity.

inputs.name: Power Supply Efficiency
inputs.hint: Power supply efficiency factor. Modern switching power supplies: 0.85-0.94; older transformer-based: 0.70-0.80.
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60974-1 §8.3 — Power supply efficiency measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Standby Power Consumption
inputs.hint: Power supply consumption in standby/idle (fans, control circuits, gas purge). Typical: 0.5-2 kW.

inputs.name: Electricity Unit Cost
inputs.hint: Industrial electricity cost per kWh including demand charges and taxes. Typical: 0.08-0.25 USD/kWh.

inputs.name: Plasma Gas Flow Rate
inputs.hint: Plasma gas flow rate at nozzle in normal cubic meters per hour. Compressed air: 2-10 Nm³/h; Oxygen: 2-8 Nm³/h; Argon/Hydrogen: 1-5 Nm³/h.

inputs.name: Shield Gas Flow Rate
inputs.hint: Shield gas flow rate in normal cubic meters per hour. Set to 0 if no shield gas is used.

inputs.name: Plasma Gas Unit Cost
inputs.hint: Cost per normal cubic meter of plasma gas. Compressed air: 0.02-0.10 USD/Nm³; Oxygen: 0.15-0.60 USD/Nm³; Argon/Hydrogen (H35): 2-15 USD/Nm³.

inputs.name: Shield Gas Unit Cost
inputs.hint: Cost per normal cubic meter of shield gas. Set to 0 if no shield gas is used.

inputs.name: Electrode Life
inputs.hint: Expected electrode life in number of pierces before replacement. HD plasma: 500-2000 pierces; standard plasma: 200-1000 pierces.

inputs.name: Nozzle Life
inputs.hint: Expected nozzle life in total cut minutes before replacement. HD plasma: 60-300 min; standard plasma: 30-120 min.

inputs.name: Electrode Unit Cost
inputs.hint: Cost per electrode replacement. HD plasma electrode: 15-50 USD; standard: 5-20 USD.

inputs.name: Nozzle Unit Cost
inputs.hint: Cost per nozzle replacement. HD plasma nozzle: 20-80 USD; standard: 5-30 USD.

inputs.name: Additional Consumable Cost per Cut Meter
inputs.hint: Additional consumable cost per meter of cut (swirl ring, retaining cap, shield). Approx: 0.02-0.15 USD/m.

inputs.name: Machine Hour Rate
inputs.hint: Fully loaded machine hour rate including depreciation, maintenance, floor space, labor, and overhead. Typical plasma cutting: 40-150 USD/h.
inputs.uncertainty_source: Cost accounting — machine hour rate estimate

inputs.name: Scrap / Recut Allowance Factor
inputs.hint: Factor to account for scrap parts. 1.0 = no scrap; 1.05 = 5% scrap rate typical for plasma cutting.

subtitle: Electrical Engineering — IEC 60364-5-52, IEC 60228, TS HD 60364-5-52

inScope: IEC 60364 Three-Phase Cable Section Sizing & Voltage Drop
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Installation Method
options: B1 - Single-core cables in open conduit
B2 - Multi-core cable in open conduit
E - Multicore cable in free air
F - Single-core cables in horizontal cable tray
G - Single-core cables in vertical cable tray

standards.name: IEC 60364 Three-Phase Cable Section Sizing & Voltage Drop
standards.description: IEC 60364 Three-Phase Cable Section Sizing & Voltage Drop — Electrical Engineering

formulas.expression: VD_Voltage = SQRT(3) * Load_Current_A * (Resistance_R_ohm * power_factor + Reactance_X_ohm * Sin_Phi)
Recommended_Min_Section = (SQRT(3) * Load_Current_A * Resistivity * cable_length_m * power_factor) / (max_vd_pct / 100 * voltage_v)

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: power_factor < 0.5 || power_factor > 1.0
fmea.effect: Power factor must be between 0.5 and 1.0. Typically 0.8-0.9 for inductive loads. Below 0.5 is not practical.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: Load_Current_A > 1000 && cross_section_val < 70
fmea.effect: Current > 1000A requires a minimum cross-section of 70mm² to prevent thermal overload.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: cross_section_val < 1.5
fmea.effect: IEC 60228: Power circuit minimum cross-section is 1.5mm². Smaller sizes are non-standard.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: cable_length_m > 500 && cross_section_val < 25
fmea.effect: Long run + small cross-section: for lines >500m, voltage drop and reactance effects are critical. Evaluate cross-section increase or voltage level upgrade.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: VOLTAGE DROP LIMIT EXCEEDED: Motor will draw excessive current at this low voltage -> overheat -> melt insulation -> cause short circuit. Increase cross-section by at least one standard size.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: VD_Percent > max_vd_pct

fmea.failureMode: Voltage drop close to limit: Within 80% of limit. Cross-section upgrade is recommended to prevent exceeding limit on load surges or hot weather.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: VD_Percent > max_vd_pct * 0.8 && VD_Percent <= max_vd_pct

fmea.failureMode: Cable heat dissipation loss > 3%: Annual energy cost is significant. Investment in cross-section upgrade typically pays back in 2-3 years. Run economic sizing optimization (Kelvin's Law).
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Power_Loss_Pct > 3

fmea.failureMode: High ambient temperature: Cable ampacity derates. Apply correction factor (Ct) for ambient >40°C per IEC 60364. Standard catalog ratings assume 30°C.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: ambient_temp_c > 40

fmea.failureMode: Selected cross-section is insufficient: does not meet voltage drop limits. Calculated minimum cross-section is displayed. Choose next standard size up.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: cross_section_val < Recommended_Min_Section

fmea.failureMode: High current (>630A): Consider parallel cables or busbar duct system. Parallel cable routing can be complex; busduct is more reliable.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Load_Current_A > 630
