formulas.edge_cases: if (chord_sidewall_failure_resistance_kn <= 0) throw new Error('Chord sidewall resistance must be positive')
formulas.cursor_instruction: Render chord sidewall utilization.

formulas.name: Chord Shear Failure Utilization
formulas.reference: Chord shear failure utilization
assumptions: Most relevant for K/N gap joints.
formulas.edge_cases: if (chord_shear_failure_resistance_kn <= 0) throw new Error('Chord shear resistance must be positive')
formulas.cursor_instruction: Render chord shear utilization.

formulas.name: Brace Local Failure Utilization
formulas.reference: Brace local failure utilization
assumptions: Brace local failure at joint is checked separately from member buckling.
formulas.edge_cases: if (brace_local_failure_resistance_kn <= 0) throw new Error('Brace local failure resistance must be positive')
formulas.cursor_instruction: Render brace local failure utilization.

formulas.name: Weld Utilization
formulas.reference: Weld utilization screening
assumptions: Weld demand is simplified from brace axial demand.
Detailed weld stress distribution is outside this helper unless implemented separately.
formulas.edge_cases: if (selected_weld_resistance_kn <= 0) throw new Error('Weld resistance must be positive')
formulas.cursor_instruction: Render weld utilization.

formulas.name: Primary Joint Utilization
formulas.reference: Overall primary brace joint utilization
assumptions: Demand and selected resistance basis are consistent.
Moment interaction is handled by review gate unless helper supports it.
formulas.edge_cases: if (selected_primary_brace_joint_resistance_kn <= 0) throw new Error('Selected joint resistance must be positive')
formulas.cursor_instruction: Render as primary utilization KPI.

formulas.name: Governing Failure Mode
formulas.expression: identifyGoverningHssFailureMode({BRACE_MEMBER_YIELD: primary_brace_axial_demand_kn / brace_member_yield_resistance_kn, CHORD_FACE_PLASTIFICATION: chord_face_plastification_utilization, PUNCHING_SHEAR: punching_shear_utilization, CHORD_SIDEWALL_FAILURE: chord_sidewall_failure_utilization, CHORD_SHEAR_FAILURE: chord_shear_failure_utilization, BRACE_LOCAL_FAILURE: brace_local_failure_utilization, WELD_FAILURE: weld_utilization})
formulas.reference: Governing HSS joint failure mode
assumptions: Largest utilization defines the governing numeric failure mode.
Review gates can still govern final decision.
formulas.edge_cases: if (!governing_failure_mode) throw new Error('Governing failure mode cannot be determined')
formulas.uncertainty_expression: Classification confidence depends on failure mode utilization uncertainty.
formulas.cursor_instruction: Render governing failure mode.

formulas.name: Joint Efficiency
formulas.reference: Joint efficiency relative to brace member yield resistance
assumptions: Joint efficiency below 1.0 means joint resistance is lower than brace member yield resistance.
formulas.edge_cases: if (brace_member_yield_resistance_kn <= 0) throw new Error('Brace yield resistance must be positive')
formulas.cursor_instruction: Render joint efficiency KPI.

formulas.name: Moment Interaction Status
formulas.reference: HSS joint moment interaction review gate
assumptions: This schema primarily evaluates axial brace joint resistance.
Brace moments require moment joint interaction module unless helper is extended.
formulas.edge_cases: if (primary_brace_in_plane_moment_knm < 0 || primary_brace_out_of_plane_moment_knm < 0) throw new Error('Brace moments cannot be negative')
formulas.cursor_instruction: Render moment interaction status badge.

formulas.name: Special Geometry Status
formulas.reference: Special HSS joint geometry gate
assumptions: Standard screening applies to unstiffened planar joints only.
Stiffened, KT and multiplanar joints require specialist review.
formulas.edge_cases: if (!special_geometry_status) throw new Error('Special geometry status cannot be determined')
formulas.cursor_instruction: Render special geometry status badge.

formulas.name: Material and Local Buckling Status
formulas.reference: Material strength and local buckling review gate
assumptions: High-strength steel and thin walls require code-specific reduction or specialist review.
Thresholds are screening values; final code limits must be applied by helper.
formulas.edge_cases: if (chord_yield_strength_mpa <= 0 || brace_yield_strength_mpa <= 0) throw new Error('Yield strengths must be positive')
formulas.cursor_instruction: Render material/local buckling status badge.

formulas.name: Weld QA Status
formulas.reference: Weld procedure, resistance and inspection gate
assumptions: Weld quality and access are critical for HSS tubular joints.
WPS/WPQR/NDT review is required for production use.
formulas.edge_cases: if (!weld_qa_status) throw new Error('Weld QA status cannot be determined')
formulas.cursor_instruction: Render weld QA status badge.

formulas.name: Special Design Status
formulas.reference: Fatigue, seismic, fire and cold-formed HSS review gates
assumptions: Fatigue, seismic and fire cases require specialist modules.
Cold-formed sections require corner, toughness and fabrication review.
formulas.edge_cases: if (typeof fatigue_design_required !== 'boolean' || typeof seismic_design_required !== 'boolean' || typeof fire_design_required !== 'boolean') throw new Error('Special design flags must be boolean')
formulas.cursor_instruction: Render special design status badge.

formulas.name: Strength Status
formulas.reference: HSS joint strength status
assumptions: Utilization limit is 1.0 for screening.
All applicable numeric limit states are included in selected resistance.
formulas.edge_cases: if (primary_joint_utilization < 0) throw new Error('Primary joint utilization cannot be negative')
formulas.uncertainty_expression: Classification confidence depends on utilization uncertainty.
formulas.cursor_instruction: Render strength status badge.

formulas.name: Engineer Review Status
formulas.reference: Structural engineering approval gate
assumptions: Final tubular joint design requires competent structural engineer review.
Software output is not a sealed calculation.
formulas.edge_cases: if (typeof licensed_engineer_review_completed !== 'boolean') throw new Error('Engineer review flag must be boolean')
formulas.cursor_instruction: Render engineer review badge.

formulas.name: HSS Joint Risk Class
formulas.expression: strength_status === 'HSS_JOINT_STRENGTH_NOT_ACCEPTABLE' ? 'HSS_JOINT_STRENGTH_NOT_ACCEPTABLE' : (geometry_validity_status !== 'HSS_GEOMETRY_VALID_FOR_SCREENING' ? geometry_validity_status : (special_geometry_status !== 'UNSTIFFENED_PLANAR_JOINT_SCREENING' ? special_geometry_status : (material_local_buckling_status !== 'MATERIAL_AND_LOCAL_BUCKLING_ACCEPTABLE_FOR_SCREENING' ? material_local_buckling_status : (weld_qa_status !== 'WELD_QA_ACCEPTABLE_FOR_SCREENING' ? weld_qa_status : (moment_interaction_status !== 'AXIAL_JOINT_SCREENING' ? moment_interaction_status : (special_design_status !== 'NO_SPECIAL_DESIGN_TRIGGERED' ? special_design_status : (engineer_review_status !== 'LICENSED_ENGINEER_REVIEW_COMPLETED' ? engineer_review_status : 'CONTROLLED_HSS_JOINT_SCREENING')))))))
formulas.reference: Integrated HSS joint risk classification
assumptions: Strength, geometry validity, special geometry, material/local buckling, weld QA, moment interaction, special design and engineer gates are evaluated together.
Review gates can override numerical acceptance.
formulas.edge_cases: if (!hss_joint_risk_class) throw new Error('HSS joint risk class cannot be determined')
formulas.uncertainty_expression: Classification confidence depends on geometry, material, chord stress, joint type, weld data, validity range and review gates.
formulas.cursor_instruction: Render as main risk badge.

formulas.name: Decision
formulas.expression: strength_status === 'HSS_JOINT_STRENGTH_NOT_ACCEPTABLE' ? 'HSS_CONNECTION_STRENGTH_NOT_ACCEPTABLE' : (geometry_validity_status !== 'HSS_GEOMETRY_VALID_FOR_SCREENING' ? geometry_validity_status : (special_geometry_status !== 'UNSTIFFENED_PLANAR_JOINT_SCREENING' ? special_geometry_status : (material_local_buckling_status !== 'MATERIAL_AND_LOCAL_BUCKLING_ACCEPTABLE_FOR_SCREENING' ? material_local_buckling_status : (weld_qa_status !== 'WELD_QA_ACCEPTABLE_FOR_SCREENING' ? weld_qa_status : (moment_interaction_status !== 'AXIAL_JOINT_SCREENING' ? moment_interaction_status : (special_design_status !== 'NO_SPECIAL_DESIGN_TRIGGERED' ? special_design_status : (engineer_review_status !== 'LICENSED_ENGINEER_REVIEW_COMPLETED' ? engineer_review_status : 'HSS_CONNECTION_ACCEPTABLE_FOR_SCREENING')))))))
formulas.reference: Integrated HSS connection decision rule
assumptions: Final acceptance requires correct EN/CIDECT basis, geometry validity, weld design, WPS/WPQR/NDT, fatigue review where needed, fabrication review and structural engineer approval.
Screening acceptance does not authorize fabrication, welding, erection or member substitution.
formulas.edge_cases: if (!decision) throw new Error('Decision cannot be determined')
formulas.uncertainty_expression: Decision confidence depends on joint geometry, section data, material certificates, chord stress, weld design, fatigue/seismic/fire triggers and engineer review.
formulas.cursor_instruction: Render as main decision badge.

outputs.name: HSS Connection Decision
outputs.uncertainty: Decision confidence depends on joint geometry, section data, material certificates, chord stress, weld design, fatigue/seismic/fire triggers and engineer review

outputs.name: HSS Joint Risk Class
outputs.uncertainty: Classification confidence depends on geometry, material, chord stress, joint type, weld data, validity range and review gates

outputs.name: Governing Failure Mode
outputs.uncertainty: Classification confidence depends on failure mode utilization uncertainty

outputs.name: Geometry Validity Table
outputs.uncertainty: Geometry validity confidence depends on drawing tolerance and helper rule coverage

outputs.name: Overlap Brace Failure Resistance
outputs.uncertainty: u_c ≈ overlap brace failure resistance × 0.45 when applicable

outputs.name: Strength Status
outputs.uncertainty: Classification confidence depends on utilization uncertainty

rules.message: Chord and brace outside dimensions and wall thicknesses must be positive.
Primary brace angle must be between 15 and 90 degrees.
Second brace angle must be between 0 and 90 degrees.
Overlap ratio must be between 0 and 100 percent.

rules.condition: chord_yield_strength_mpa > 0 && brace_yield_strength_mpa > 0 && chord_ultimate_strength_mpa >= chord_yield_strength_mpa && brace_ultimate_strength_mpa >= brace_yield_strength_mpa
rules.message: Chord and brace material strengths are invalid.
Elastic modulus must be positive.
Chord and brace section properties are invalid.
Weld throat and weld resistance cannot be negative.
gamma_M5 must be at least 1.0.
Gap joint selected but positive gap is not entered.
Overlap joint selected but overlap ratio is not positive.
K/N joint requires second brace geometry.
Fillet weld selected but neither effective throat nor weld resistance is entered.
High-strength HSS may require resistance reduction or specialist review.
Stiffened HSS joint requires separate stiffener design.
Multiplanar HSS joint requires specialist interaction review.

smart_warnings.message: Licensed structural engineer review is not completed. Do not use result for fabrication, welding or erection.
Brace demand exceeds selected HSS joint resistance.
Joint geometry is outside CIDECT/EN validity range in screening check.
Joint geometry requires code table or specialist review.
Weld procedure, inspection or weld resistance is not verified.
Fatigue design is required. Static HSS joint resistance is not sufficient.
Seismic HSS joint design requires ductility, overstrength and cyclic behavior review.
Fire design required. Ambient-temperature HSS joint resistance is not sufficient.
Brace moment or combined loading detected. Use HSS moment interaction module or specialist review.
Joint is stiffened, multiplanar, KT or otherwise outside standard planar unstiffened screening.
High-strength or thin-wall HSS requires material/local buckling review.
Joint utilization is above 0.90. Review geometry tolerance, weld detail, chord stress function and validity range.

engine_rules.error_propagation: ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM Type A/B. Report u_c(y) for beta, gamma, tau, gap ratio, overlap ratio, chord stress parameter, chord stress function, brace member yield resistance, chord face plastification resistance, punching shear resistance, chord sidewall resistance, chord shear resistance, brace local failure resistance, overlap brace failure resistance, local chord buckling resistance, weld resistance, selected joint resistance, utilization and joint efficiency. Use root-sum-square propagation for direct formulas and scenario/Monte Carlo propagation for helper-based failure modes where available. Flag high uncertainty when geometry is near validity limits, chord stress is high, weld data is missing, joint is overlap/stiffened/multiplanar, material strength exceeds standard range, or fatigue/seismic/fire is triggered.
engine_rules.fmea: Required if primary_joint_utilization > 0.90, geometry_validity_status is not valid, weld_qa_status is not acceptable, fatigue_design_required = true, seismic_design_required = true, joint is overlap/stiffened/multiplanar, material_local_buckling_status is not acceptable, or the joint is life-safety/public-access/production-critical. RPN = S×O×D.
engine_rules.audit_log: ISO 9001 traceable fields: design basis, HSS family, joint type, loading type, chord shape, brace shape, chord dimensions, brace dimensions, second brace dimensions, brace angles, gap/overlap, material strengths, chord force, chord moment, brace forces, brace moments, section properties, weld type, weld throat, weld resistance, gamma_M5, cold-formed flag, stiffener flag, multiplanar flag, fatigue flag, seismic flag, fire flag, weld QA flag, engineer review flag, all formula outputs, warnings, decision, formula version, timestamp and input hash.

reference_code.validation: Zod schema. Fail closed on invalid geometry, invalid brace angles, invalid material strengths, invalid section properties, invalid weld inputs, invalid gamma_M5 and invalid boolean review gates.
reference_code.testing: Jest cases: CHS T joint axial brace, CHS X joint, RHS T joint, RHS K gap joint, RHS K overlap joint, SHS Y joint, invalid beta out-of-range, high gamma warning, high-strength steel warning, thin-wall local buckling warning, chord stress function reduction, chord face plastification governing, punching shear governing, chord sidewall failure governing, chord shear failure governing, brace local failure governing, weld failure governing, moment interaction gate, stiffened joint gate, multiplanar joint gate, fatigue gate, seismic gate, fire gate, weld QA missing gate, engineer review missing gate, final acceptable screening decision.
implementation_notes: Do not use eval(). Use existing AST/helper expression engine.
Use deterministic formula order F1 to F46.
Use SI-primary units: kN, kN*m, mm, mm2, mm3, MPa, degree and ratio.
Use null for not-applicable overlap resistance, not Infinity.
Do not embed copyrighted CIDECT or EN design tables. Geometry validity and resistance helpers must use licensed implementation or project-configurable traceable equations.
Standard screening is limited to unstiffened planar welded hollow-section joints unless helper explicitly supports special cases.
Moment interaction, KT joints, multiplanar joints, stiffened joints, fatigue, seismic and fire must trigger review gates unless specialist modules are implemented.
Weld design must not be assumed adequate. WPS/WPQR/NDT and EN 1090 execution quality must be explicitly verified.
High-strength steel and cold-formed HSS may require reduction factors, corner property review, toughness review and fabrication review.

Decision enum values: HSS_CONNECTION_ACCEPTABLE_FOR_SCREENING, HSS_CONNECTION_STRENGTH_NOT_ACCEPTABLE, HSS_GEOMETRY_OUTSIDE_VALIDITY_RANGE, HSS_GEOMETRY_REVIEW_REQUIRED, STIFFENED_HSS_JOINT_REVIEW_REQUIRED, MULTIPLANAR_HSS_JOINT_REVIEW_REQUIRED, KT_HSS_JOINT_REVIEW_REQUIRED, HIGH_STRENGTH_HSS_REDUCTION_OR_REVIEW_REQUIRED, HSS_LOCAL_BUCKLING_REVIEW_REQUIRED, WELD_PROCEDURE_INSPECTION_OR_RESISTANCE_REVIEW_REQUIRED, HSS_MOMENT_INTERACTION_REVIEW_REQUIRED, FATIGUE_HSS_JOINT_REVIEW_REQUIRED, SEISMIC_HSS_JOINT_REVIEW_REQUIRED, FIRE_HSS_JOINT_REVIEW_REQUIRED, COLD_FORMED_HSS_FABRICATION_REVIEW_REQUIRED, LICENSED_ENGINEER_REVIEW_REQUIRED.

tuv_checklist: Beta, gamma and tau ratios implemented
FMEA required for high utilization, fatigue, seismic, geometry out-of-range, weld QA missing and special joint cases
Audit hash generated for every successful calculation
No hidden defaults for geometry, material strengths, chord stress, brace demand, weld verification, fatigue/seismic/fire or engineer review gates
metadata.calibration_trace: Joint geometry from approved connection drawing/BIM; HSS section properties from EN 10210/EN 10219 section table, mill certificate or measured data; material strengths from mill certificate; member forces from structural analysis/load combinations; weld data from WPS/WPQR/weld design; NDT and execution records from EN 1090 QA system; final approval from engineer of record.
source_basis: CIDECT hollow section joint design guidance context
IIW static design recommendations for hollow section joints context
EN 1993-1-8 hollow section joint design context
EN 1090-2 steel execution and fabrication quality context
ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM uncertainty reporting basis
limitations: This calculator is an HSS tubular joint screening tool, not a sealed structural calculation, licensed CIDECT table implementation, finite-element model, fatigue design, seismic design, fire design, weld procedure qualification or fabrication approval.
Exact resistance equations and validity ranges depend on section shape, joint type, loading, geometry range, chord stress, gap/overlap and selected national annex/project specification.
Stiffened, multiplanar, KT, moment-resisting, cast node, bolted, grouted, concrete-filled, tapered, curved or heavily eccentric joints require specialist design.
Fatigue often governs tubular joints and must be checked separately where cyclic loading exists.
Weld access, weld throat, fit-up, hidden welds in overlap joints, WPS/WPQR, NDT and EN 1090 execution class are mandatory production controls.
Final use requires competent structural engineer approval and project-specific code compliance.
metadata.cursor_instruction: Integrate this tool into the existing SectorCalc PRO tool architecture without creating a new UI framework. Use current tool-card, HSS geometry input pattern, tubular joint diagram panel, results panel, formula panel, warning panel, chart panel, table panel and audit log components.

tool_name: Crane Runway Beam Design Calculator - EN 1993-6 CMAA
category: Structural Engineering / Crane Runway / Steel Girder Design
calculation_basis: Crane runway beam design screening using EN 1993-6 and CMAA 70/AISC-style design checks for crane-induced vertical wheel loads, lateral surge/acceleration loads, longitudinal braking loads, impact factors, dynamic amplification, global bending about strong and weak axes, shear resistance, vertical and lateral deflection, web bearing under wheel loads (patch loading), web buckling, flange bending (local bending under wheel), combined stress interaction, fatigue damage assessment (stress range method), weld design at flange-web junctions and splices, end connection reactions, and governing demand-capacity ratio. The output is a preliminary engineering screening calculation and must not replace sealed structural design, project-specific dynamic analysis, CMAA detailed crane specification, fatigue design report, welding procedure qualification, erection method statement, or authority approval.

inputs.name: Design Standard
inputs.validation_rule: Select design code. EN 1993-6 for European practice. CMAA 70 + AISC 360 for North American practice.
inputs.note: Code selection determines partial factors, load combinations, and resistance formulations.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Standard selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Crane Type
inputs.validation_rule: Select appropriate crane configuration. Determines load distribution and lateral restraint assumptions.
inputs.note: Crane service class (CMAA Class A-F) should be determined from crane duty cycle.

inputs.name: CMAA Crane Service Class
inputs.validation_rule: CMAA Class A-F. Determines impact factor, lateral load magnitude, and fatigue design requirements.
inputs.note: EN 1993-6 load categories (S0-S9) can be mapped from CMAA class.

inputs.name: Hoist Rated Capacity
inputs.validation_rule: Rated hoist capacity from crane manufacturer data sheet. Excludes hook block and lifting attachments.
inputs.note: Includes hoist capacity at design span. Deduct any headroom limitations.

inputs.name: Crane Bridge Self-Weight
inputs.validation_rule: Total bridge weight including end trucks, drives, and electrical equipment from crane data sheet.
inputs.note: Bridge weight without hoist trolley.

inputs.name: Trolley + Hoist Weight
inputs.validation_rule: Total weight of trolley frame, hoist mechanism, hook block, and all lifting attachments.
inputs.note: Without lifted load. Full trolley weight with hook block included.

inputs.name: Number of Wheels Per Side (End Truck)
inputs.validation_rule: Integer 2-8. Total wheels = 2 × n_w for double-girder.
inputs.note: Number of wheels on one rail/girder. Double girder cranes have 2 end trucks with n_w wheels each.

inputs.name: Wheel Spacing (Center-to-Center)
inputs.validation_rule: Center-to-center distance between adjacent wheels on same end truck. For 2-wheel, s_w = wheelbase of end truck.
inputs.note: For n_w > 2, provide minimum and maximum wheel spacing as separate inputs or define as equal spacing.

inputs.name: Crane Span (Bridge Span)
inputs.validation_rule: Distance between centerlines of crane rails. Matches runway beam span for simple-span configuration.
inputs.note: For runway beam with multiple spans, use maximum span length for design.

inputs.name: Runway Beam Span (Column-to-Column)
inputs.validation_rule: Center-to-center distance between column supports for the runway beam. Simple span assumed.
inputs.note: For continuous beams, the critical span governs. Continuous beam analysis requires advanced model.

inputs.name: Vertical Impact Factor
inputs.validation_rule: EN 1993-6: φ_v = 1.0 + k_v where k_v depends on crane class (0.1-0.4). CMAA: 1.25 for cab-operated, 1.15 for floor-operated.
inputs.note: EN 1993-6 φ_v = 1.0 to 1.4 (class-dependent). CMAA: 1.25 power-operated cab, 1.15 floor-operated.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Impact factor uncertainty per code

inputs.name: Lateral Surge Load (% of Rated Load + Trolley)
inputs.validation_rule: CMAA: 20% for cab-operated, 10% for floor-operated. EN 1993-6: lateral force = α_s × (Q_h + W_t) × g where α_s = 0.1-0.3.
inputs.note: Lateral surge acts at the top of the crane rail. For EN 1993-6, this is the horizontal acceleration force.

inputs.name: Longitudinal Braking Load (% of Wheel Load)
inputs.validation_rule: EN 1993-6: longitudinal braking = 10-20% of vertical wheel load. CMAA: 10% of wheel load per driven wheel.
inputs.note: Longitudinal force acts along the rail direction at top-of-rail elevation.

inputs.name: Runway Beam Section Type

options.label: Welded Plate Girder — Custom built-up section
Wide Flange with Cap Channel — Channel welded to top flange

inputs.validation_rule: Section type determines local flange bending calculation method and stiffener requirements.
inputs.note: Welded plate girders are typically required for spans > 15m or very heavy crane capacities.

inputs.name: Beam Section Depth (d)
inputs.validation_rule: Overall depth of steel section. For W-shapes: nominal depth. For plate girders: web depth + 2 × flange thickness.
inputs.note: Nominal section depth from manufacturer catalog.

inputs.name: Flange Width (b_f)
inputs.validation_rule: Width of compression (top) flange. For unequal flanges, use the smaller width conservatively for weak-axis bending.
inputs.note: For welded plate girder with different top and bottom flanges, provide each separately.

inputs.name: Flange Thickness (t_f)
inputs.validation_rule: Thickness of compression (top) flange. Minimum 5 mm for rolling or 6 mm for welded fabrication.
inputs.note: For unequal flanges, provide top flange thickness separately from bottom flange.

inputs.name: Web Thickness (t_w)
inputs.validation_rule: Web thickness. Minimum 4 mm for rolled sections, 5 mm for welded (EN 1090-2).
inputs.note: For plate girders, web thickness must satisfy shear and local bearing requirements.

inputs.name: Strong-Axis Moment of Inertia (I_x)
inputs.validation_rule: Moment of inertia about the strong axis (major bending axis). From section catalog or calculated.
inputs.note: Use manufacturer section property table for rolled sections. Calculate for welded plate girders.

inputs.name: Weak-Axis Moment of Inertia (I_y)
inputs.validation_rule: Moment of inertia about the weak axis (lateral bending axis).
inputs.note: Controls lateral-torsional buckling resistance and lateral deflection.

inputs.name: Strong-Axis Elastic Section Modulus (S_x)
inputs.validation_rule: Elastic section modulus about strong axis. S_x = I_x / (d/2).
inputs.note: Elastic section modulus for extreme fiber bending stress calculation.

inputs.name: Weak-Axis Plastic Section Modulus (Z_y)
inputs.validation_rule: Plastic section modulus about weak axis. Required for weak-axis bending check of lateral loads.
inputs.note: For lateral surge bending check, use Z_y for plastic or S_y for elastic per code.

inputs.name: Torsional Constant (J)
inputs.validation_rule: Saint-Venant torsional constant. For I-sections: J ≈ (1/3) × (2 × b_f × t_f^3 + (d - 2×t_f) × t_w^3).
inputs.note: Used for lateral-torsional buckling calculation (LTB) and torsion check.

inputs.name: Warping Constant (C_w)
inputs.validation_rule: Warping torsional constant. For doubly-symmetric I-sections: C_w ≈ I_y × (d - t_f)^2 / 4.
inputs.note: Used for lateral-torsional buckling calculation.

inputs.name: Steel Grade
inputs.validation_rule: Select steel grade. EN grades for European codes, ASTM for North American codes.
inputs.note: Yield and ultimate strength per selected grade used for resistance calculations.

inputs.name: Steel Yield Strength (f_y)
inputs.validation_rule: Nominal yield strength per selected steel grade. S235=235, S275=275, S355=355, S460=460 MPa.
inputs.note: EN 1993-1-1 Table 3.1 for nominal yield strengths. ASTM A992 Fy=345 MPa.
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 §25.2 Table 25.1 — Yield strength CoV=0.05

inputs.name: Steel Ultimate Tensile Strength (f_u)
inputs.validation_rule: Nominal ultimate tensile strength per steel grade. Used for weld design and net-section checks.
inputs.note: S355 fu=510 MPa. ASTM A992 fu=450 MPa. Required for weld resistance calculation.
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 §25.2 Table 25.1 — Ultimate strength CoV=0.04

inputs.name: Elastic Modulus (E)
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 §25.2 Table 25.1 — Elastic modulus CoV=0.03

inputs.name: Shear Modulus (G)
inputs.validation_rule: G = E / (2 × (1 + ν)) ≈ 77,000 MPa for steel.
inputs.note: G ≈ 77 GPa for structural steel. Used for torsion and LTB calculations.
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 §25.2 — Shear modulus CoV=0.03

inputs.name: EN 1993 Cross-Section Resistance Partial Factor (γ_M0)
inputs.validation_rule: Used for EN 1993-6. Default γM0=1.00 per EN 1993-1-1 §6.1. National Annex may specify 1.00.
inputs.note: Cross-section resistance factor. Does not apply to buckling checks (use γ_M1).

inputs.name: EN 1993 Member Stability Partial Factor (γ_M1)
inputs.validation_rule: Used for EN 1993-6. Default γM1=1.00 per EN 1993-1-1 §6.1. National Annex may specify 1.00.
inputs.note: Stability resistance factor. Applies to buckling, LTB, and flange buckling checks.

inputs.name: EN 1993 Cross-Section Tension Fracture Partial Factor (γ_M2)
inputs.validation_rule: Used for weld and bolt checks per EN 1993-1-8.
inputs.note: Tension fracture and connection resistance factor.

inputs.name: AISC Flexural Resistance Factor (φ_b)
inputs.validation_rule: AISC LRFD flexural resistance factor. φ_b = 0.90 per AISC 360-22 Chapter F.
inputs.note: Applies when CMAA_70_AISC standard is selected.

inputs.name: AISC Shear Resistance Factor (φ_v)
inputs.validation_rule: AISC LRFD shear resistance factor. φ_v = 1.00 per AISC 360-22 Chapter G (default).
inputs.note: Applies when CMAA_70_AISC standard is selected.

inputs.name: Top Flange Laterally Restrained?
inputs.validation_rule: Top flange is continuously restrained by crane rail and lateral bracing. Typical for runway beams with adequate diaphragm action.
inputs.note: If false, LTB calculation uses unbraced length equal to runway span.

inputs.name: Unbraced Length for LTB (L_b)
inputs.validation_rule: Distance between lateral bracing points for the compression flange. If top flange is fully restrained, L_b = 0.
inputs.note: If top flange is restrained, LTB is not critical. Provide bracing spacing for intermediate check.

inputs.name: Crane Rail Type

options.label: Flat Bar Rail — Simple rectangular bar
RA Series Rail (RA 50, RA 65, RA 80, RA 100)

inputs.validation_rule: Select rail profile. Crane rail distribution width affects local flange bending calculation.
inputs.note: Rail head width used for wheel load distribution length for local bending check.

inputs.name: Effective Rail Head Width for Load Distribution
inputs.validation_rule: Width of rail head (or flat bar) that distributes wheel load to the flange. Typically 40-150 mm.
inputs.note: Used for local flange bending check (effective distribution length).

inputs.name: Rail Fastening Type

options.label: Resilient Clip with Pad (Crane Rail Clip)
Welded — Rail welded directly to top flange

inputs.validation_rule: Fastener type influences fatigue detail category and load eccentricity.
inputs.note: Welded rail attachment is discouraged for fatigue-sensitive runway beams.

inputs.name: Estimated Crane Run Cycles Per Year
inputs.validation_rule: Number of full load cycles per year. CMAA Class C ≈ 100,000 cycles. Class F ≈ 2,000,000 cycles.
inputs.note: Used for fatigue damage assessment per EN 1993-1-9. More cycles = higher fatigue demand.

inputs.name: Design Service Life (Years)
inputs.validation_rule: Design working life of the runway beam structure. Typically 25-50 years.
inputs.note: Total fatigue cycles = N_c × Y_d.

inputs.name: Fatigue Detail Category (EN 1993-1-9)

options.label: FAT 125 — Welded attachment with ground flush
FAT 100 — Transverse butt weld ground flush
FAT 80 — Fillet weld toe at stiffener
FAT 71 — Cruciform joint with fillet welds
FAT 45 — Welded rail clip to flange
FAT 36 — Welded attachment on tension flange

inputs.validation_rule: Select fatigue detail category per EN 1993-1-9 Table 8.1-8.10 or AISC 360-22 Appendix 3.
inputs.note: Lower FAT number = lower fatigue resistance. Welded rail clips may be FAT 36-45.

inputs.name: EN 1993-1-9 Partial Factor for Fatigue Load (γ_Ff)
inputs.validation_rule: Fatigue partial factor per EN 1993-1-9 §3.7. Default 1.00 unless damage tolerance specified.
inputs.note: γ_Ff = 1.00 for safe-life, 1.15 for damage-tolerant design per EN 1993-1-9.

inputs.name: EN 1993-1-9 Partial Factor for Fatigue Resistance (γ_Mf)
inputs.validation_rule: Fatigue resistance partial factor per EN 1993-1-9 §3.7. High consequence = 1.35.
inputs.note: γ_Mf = 1.35 for high consequence of failure. 1.15 for low consequence.

inputs.name: Bearing Stiffeners at Supports?
inputs.validation_rule: Bearing stiffeners are typically required at support points for web bearing and buckling resistance.
inputs.note: Bearing stiffeners transfer support reactions to the web. Required if web local buckling check fails.

inputs.name: Intermediate Transverse Stiffener Spacing
inputs.validation_rule: Clear spacing between intermediate web stiffeners. Leave null if no intermediate stiffeners.
inputs.note: Intermediate stiffeners improve web shear buckling resistance. Spacing a_s ≤ d for effective shear panel action.

formulas: W_crane_N = (hoist_capacity_kg + trolley_weight_kg) * 9.81   // [N] Crane hoist + trolley weight as force Reference: EN 1991-1-1 §5.2; Assumptions: g = 9.81 m/s² standard gravity
W_bridge_N = crane_bridge_weight_kg * 9.81   // [N] Crane bridge self-weight as force Reference: EN 1991-1-1 §5.2; Assumptions: g = 9.81 m/s², distributed to end trucks

P_wheel_N_unfactored = (W_crane_N + W_bridge_N / 2) / num_wheels_per_side   // [N] Maximum unfactored vertical wheel load per wheel Reference: EN 1993-6 §2.2; Assumptions: Bridge load equally distributed to both end trucks, wheel loads equally distributed within end truck

P_wheel_N_design = P_wheel_N_unfactored * impact_factor_vertical   // [N] Maximum design vertical wheel load including impact Reference: EN 1993-6 §2.2; Assumptions: Dynamic impact factor applied to maximum wheel load, impact may be reduced for multiple wheels according to code

P_lat_surge_N = lateral_surge_percentage * (hoist_capacity_kg + trolley_weight_kg) * 9.81   // [N] Lateral surge force per side Reference: EN 1993-6 §2.4; CMAA 70 §3.2; Assumptions: Lateral load acts at rail top, equally distributed to both rails for double girder

H_long_brake_N = longitudinal_braking_percentage * P_wheel_N_design * num_wheels_per_side   // [N] Longitudinal braking force per rail (total per end truck) Reference: EN 1993-6 §2.5; CMAA 70 §3.2; Assumptions: Braking force from driven wheels, distributed to runway columns

M_max_vert_Nmm = design_standard === 'EN_1993_6' ? P_wheel_N_design * runway_beam_span_mm / 4 * 1.0 : P_wheel_N_design * runway_beam_span_mm / 4   // [N·mm] Maximum vertical bending moment (simple span, single wheel at midspan) Reference: EN 1993-6 §3.2; CMAA 70 §3.3; Assumptions: Simple span, critical wheel placed at midspan, wheel spacing considered for tandem loading

M_max_vert_tandem_Nmm = num_wheels_per_side >= 2 ? P_wheel_N_design * runway_beam_span_mm / 4 + P_wheel_N_design * Math.max(0, runway_beam_span_mm - wheel_spacing_mm * (num_wheels_per_side - 1)) / 4 : M_max_vert_Nmm   // [N·mm] Maximum vertical bending moment considering tandem wheel loads Reference: EN 1993-6 §3.2; CMAA 70 §3.3; Assumptions: Multiple wheels positioned for maximum moment, simplified tandem approximation

M_lat_surge_Nmm = P_lat_surge_N * runway_beam_span_mm / 4   // [N·mm] Maximum lateral surging bending moment Reference: EN 1993-6 §3.3; CMAA 70 §3.3; Assumptions: Lateral load applied at rail top = midspan moment from lateral point load

V_max_vert_N = P_wheel_N_design * (runway_beam_span_mm - Math.min(wheel_spacing_mm, runway_beam_span_mm / 2)) / runway_beam_span_mm + P_wheel_N_design   // [N] Maximum vertical shear at support Reference: EN 1993-6 §3.2; Assumptions: Wheel loads near support produce maximum shear

f_b_vert_MPa = M_max_vert_tandem_Nmm / Sx_mm3   // [MPa] Maximum vertical bending stress (extreme fiber) Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.2.5; AISC 360-22 F2; Assumptions: Elastic behavior, Sx based on gross section

f_b_lat_MPa = M_lat_surge_Nmm / Zy_mm3   // [MPa] Maximum lateral bending stress in flange Reference: EN 1993-6 §3.3; CMAA 70 §3.3; Assumptions: Lateral moment resisted by top flange only (or flange + cap channel), plastic section modulus used

sigma_x_vert_MPa = f_b_vert_MPa + f_b_lat_MPa   // [MPa] Combined longitudinal normal stress from vertical and lateral bending Reference: EN 1993-6 §5.2; CMAA 70 §3.3; Assumptions: Direct stress addition at extreme fiber of top flange, worst-case tension or compression

gamma_M = design_standard === 'EN_1993_6' ? gamma_M0 : 1.0   // [dimensionless] Selected partial factor for cross-section resistance Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.1; Assumptions: γ_M0 for section resistance, γ_M1 for stability

Mc_Rd_vert_Nmm = design_standard === 'EN_1993_6' ? Sx_mm3 * fy_MPa / gamma_M0 : Sx_mm3 * fy_MPa * phi_b   // [N·mm] Design bending moment resistance about strong axis Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.2.5 Eq 6.13; AISC 360-22 F2-1; Assumptions: Compact section, no LTB reduction
my_Ed_Nmm = M_max_vert_tandem_Nmm   // [N·mm] Design bending moment about y-y (strong axis) — vertical bending Reference: EN 1993-6 §3.2; Assumptions: Vertical bending dominates
mz_Ed_Nmm = M_lat_surge_Nmm   // [N·mm] Design bending moment about z-z (weak axis) — lateral bending Reference: EN 1993-6 §3.3; Assumptions: Lateral surge bending in top flange
UC_vert_bending = my_Ed_Nmm / Mc_Rd_vert_Nmm   // [dimensionless] Utilization ratio for vertical bending Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.2.5; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes

UC_lat_bending = mz_Ed_Nmm / (Zy_mm3 * fy_MPa / (design_standard === 'EN_1993_6' ? gamma_M0 : (1 / phi_b)))   // [dimensionless] Utilization ratio for lateral bending of top flange Reference: EN 1993-6 §5.2; CMAA 70 §3.3; Assumptions: Top flange resists lateral bending alone

UC_combined_bending = Math.abs(sigma_x_vert_MPa) / (fy_MPa / (design_standard === 'EN_1993_6' ? gamma_M0 : (1 / phi_b)))   // [dimensionless] Utilization ratio for combined normal stress Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.2.1 Eq 6.1; CMAA 70 §3.3; Assumptions: Linear elastic stress addition, fy limiting stress

Vpl_Rd_N = design_standard === 'EN_1993_6' ? (fy_MPa / Math.sqrt(3)) * (d_mm * tw_mm) / gamma_M0 : (fy_MPa / Math.sqrt(3)) * (d_mm * tw_mm) * phi_v   // [N] Plastic shear resistance Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.2.6 Eq 6.18; AISC 360-22 G2-1; Assumptions: Rectangular web shear area Av = d × tw, uniform shear stress distribution
UC_shear = V_max_vert_N / Vpl_Rd_N   // [dimensionless] Utilization ratio for shear Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.2.6; AISC 360-22 G2; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes

delta_vert_mm = design_standard === 'EN_1993_6' ? P_wheel_N_unfactored * Math.pow(runway_beam_span_mm, 3) / (48 * E_MPa * Ix_mm4) : P_wheel_N_unfactored * Math.pow(runway_beam_span_mm, 3) / (48 * E_MPa * Ix_mm4)   // [mm] Maximum vertical deflection (midspan, single wheel) Reference: EN 1993-6 §7.0; CMAA 70 §3.3; Assumptions: Elastic deflection, simple span, unfactored wheel load, serviceability load combination

delta_lat_mm = P_lat_surge_N * Math.pow(runway_beam_span_mm, 3) / (48 * E_MPa * Iy_mm4)   // [mm] Maximum lateral deflection at rail top Reference: EN 1993-6 §7.0; CMAA 70 §3.3; Assumptions: Lateral load applied at rail top, top flange aligned with beam neutral axis

delta_vert_lim_mm = design_standard === 'EN_1993_6' ? runway_beam_span_mm / 600 : runway_beam_span_mm / 600   // [mm] Vertical deflection limit Reference: EN 1993-6 Table 7.1; CMAA 70 §3.3; Assumptions: L/600 for cranes with bridge cranes, L/750 for cranes requiring tighter deflection control

delta_lat_lim_mm = design_standard === 'EN_1993_6' ? runway_beam_span_mm / 400 : runway_beam_span_mm / 400   // [mm] Lateral deflection limit Reference: EN 1993-6 Table 7.1; CMAA 70 §3.3; Assumptions: L/400 for lateral crane surge deflection
UC_delta_vert = delta_vert_mm / delta_vert_lim_mm   // [dimensionless] Vertical deflection utilization ratio Reference: EN 1993-6 §7.0; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes serviceability check
UC_delta_lat = delta_lat_mm / delta_lat_lim_mm   // [dimensionless] Lateral deflection utilization ratio Reference: EN 1993-6 §7.0; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes serviceability check
h_w_mm = d_mm - 2 * tf_mm   // [mm] Web depth between flanges (clear) Reference: EN 1993-1-5 §5.1; Assumptions: Net depth for web slenderness and shear buckling
eta = design_standard === 'EN_1993_6' ? 1.2 : 1.0   // [dimensionless] Web shear buckling factor Reference: EN 1993-1-5 §5.1; Assumptions: η=1.2 for steel grades up to S460

lambda_w_shear = h_w_mm / tw_mm / (37.4 * eta * Math.sqrt(235 / fy_MPa))   // [dimensionless] Web slenderness parameter for shear buckling Reference: EN 1993-1-5 §5.3 Eq 5.4; Assumptions: Rigid end posts, η factor applied

chi_w_shear = lambda_w_shear < 0.83 / eta ? eta : (lambda_w_shear < 1.08 ? (0.83 / lambda_w_shear) : (1.37 / (0.7 + lambda_w_shear)))   // [dimensionless] Shear buckling reduction factor chi_w Reference: EN 1993-1-5 §5.3 Table 5.1; Assumptions: Rigid end post, non-rigid end post condition may reduce further

Vb_Rd_shear_N = design_standard === 'EN_1993_6' ? chi_w_shear * fy_MPa * h_w_mm * tw_mm / (Math.sqrt(3) * gamma_M1) : null   // [N] Shear buckling resistance (EN 1993-1-5) Reference: EN 1993-1-5 §5.2 Eq 5.2; Assumptions: Tension field action included for intermediate stiffeners

UC_shear_buckling = intermediate_stiffener_spacing_mm !== null && Vb_Rd_shear_N !== null ? V_max_vert_N / Vb_Rd_shear_N : null   // [dimensionless] Shear buckling utilization ratio Reference: EN 1993-1-5 §5.2; Assumptions: Applicable when web slenderness requires post-buckling resistance
F_Ed_N = P_wheel_N_design   // [N] Design transverse force on web (patch loading from wheel) Reference: EN 1993-1-5 §6.1; Assumptions: Single wheel load on top flange, load applied at flange centroid

l_eff_ss_mm = rail_head_width_mm + 2 * tf_mm   // [mm] Effective loaded length for patch loading (stiff bearing) Reference: EN 1993-1-5 §6.2 Eq 6.3; Assumptions: Rail distributes wheel load over rail_head_width, 45° dispersion through flange thickness

F_cr_N = 0.9 * E_MPa * Math.pow(tw_mm, 3) / h_w_mm   // [N] Critical patch loading force (elastic buckling) Reference: EN 1993-1-5 §6.3 Eq 6.5; Assumptions: clamped flange boundary condition, uniform web
m1 = fy_MPa * bf_mm / (fy_MPa * tw_mm)   // [dimensionless] Dimensionless parameter m1 for patch loading resistance Reference: EN 1993-1-5 §6.4 Eq 6.7; Assumptions: same fy for flange and web
m2 = 0.02 * Math.pow(h_w_mm / tf_mm, 2)   // [dimensionless] Dimensionless parameter m2 for patch loading resistance Reference: EN 1993-1-5 §6.4 Eq 6.8; Assumptions: λ_F > 0.5 (otherwise m2 = 0)

lambda_F_patch = Math.sqrt(l_eff_ss_mm * tw_mm * fy_MPa / F_cr_N)   // [dimensionless] Slenderness parameter for patch loading Reference: EN 1993-1-5 §6.4 Eq 6.6; Assumptions: Web panel subject to concentrated transverse force

chi_F_patch = 0.5 / lambda_F_patch <= 1 ? 0.5 / lambda_F_patch : 1   // [dimensionless] Reduction factor for patch loading resistance Reference: EN 1993-1-5 §6.4 Eq 6.9; Assumptions: λ_F ≤ 0.5 → chi_F = 1.0, otherwise chi_F = 0.5/λ_F

Leff_patch_mm = chi_F_patch * l_eff_ss_mm   // [mm] Effective loaded length for patch loading resistance Reference: EN 1993-1-5 §6.4 Eq 6.2; Assumptions: Reduction factor applied to stiff bearing length

F_Rd_patch_N = design_standard === 'EN_1993_6' ? Leff_patch_mm * tw_mm * fy_MPa / gamma_M1 : null   // [N] Design patch loading resistance Reference: EN 1993-1-5 §6.4 Eq 6.1; Assumptions: Web crippling and web buckling combined

UC_patch_loading = F_Rd_patch_N !== null ? F_Ed_N / F_Rd_patch_N : null   // [dimensionless] Utilization ratio for patch loading (wheel load web bearing) Reference: EN 1993-1-5 §6.4; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes

sigma_x_Ed_MPa = sigma_x_vert_MPa   // [MPa] Design longitudinal stress at flange-web junction (for interaction checks) Reference: EN 1993-1-5 §7.2; Assumptions: Extreme fiber stress at flange-web fillet

sigma_z_Ed_MPa = F_Ed_N / (l_eff_ss_mm * tw_mm)   // [MPa] Design transverse stress from wheel load at flange-web junction Reference: EN 1993-1-5 §7.2 Eq 7.1; Assumptions: Uniform stress distribution over effective length
tau_Ed_MPa = V_max_vert_N / (h_w_mm * tw_mm)   // [MPa] Design shear stress in web Reference: EN 1993-1-5 §7.2; Assumptions: Uniform shear stress in web panel

UC_interaction = sigma_x_Ed_MPa !== null && sigma_z_Ed_MPa !== null && tau_Ed_MPa !== null ? Math.sqrt(Math.pow(sigma_x_Ed_MPa / (fy_MPa / gamma_M1), 2) + Math.pow(sigma_z_Ed_MPa / (fy_MPa / gamma_M1), 2) - (sigma_x_Ed_MPa * sigma_z_Ed_MPa) / Math.pow(fy_MPa / gamma_M1, 2) + 3 * Math.pow(tau_Ed_MPa / (fy_MPa / gamma_M1), 2)) : null   // [dimensionless] Von Mises combined stress utilization at flange-web junction Reference: EN 1993-1-5 §7.2 Eq 7.3; Assumptions: Plane stress condition, elastic verification

sigma_f_Nmm2 = f_b_vert_MPa   // [MPa] Stress range in flange at fatigue detail (for constant amplitude) Reference: EN 1993-1-9 §5.3; Assumptions: Linear elastic stress calculation, nominal stress approach

delta_sigma_E2_MPa = impact_factor_vertical * sigma_f_Nmm2   // [MPa] Equivalent constant amplitude stress range Reference: EN 1993-1-9 §6.4; Assumptions: Damage equivalent factor approach, λ_1 × λ_2 factor from crane loading spectrum

total_cycles = crane_run_cycles_per_year * design_life_years   // [dimensionless] Total number of stress cycles over design life Reference: EN 1993-1-9 §5.5; Assumptions: Uniform cycle distribution, no load spectrum reduction

FAT_class_MPa = parseFloat(fatigue_detail_category)   // [MPa] Fatigue detail category (FAT class) per EN 1993-1-9 Reference: EN 1993-1-9 Table 8.1-8.10; Assumptions: FAT class from selected detail category
gamma_Ff_total = gamma_Ff   // [dimensionless] Fatigue load partial factor Reference: EN 1993-1-9 §3.7; Assumptions: Safe-life unless specified otherwise
gamma_Mf_total = gamma_Mf   // [dimensionless] Fatigue resistance partial factor Reference: EN 1993-1-9 §3.7; Assumptions: High consequence of failure unless specified otherwise

sigma_d_SLS_MPa = impact_factor_vertical * sigma_f_Nmm2 * gamma_Ff_total   // [MPa] Design stress range for fatigue (SLS load level) Reference: EN 1993-1-9 §6.1; Assumptions: Frequent load combination, damage equivalent stress range

sigma_c_MPa = FAT_class_MPa / gamma_Mf_total   // [MPa] Design fatigue resistance (constant amplitude) Reference: EN 1993-1-9 §7.2 Eq 7.1; Assumptions: Constant amplitude fatigue limit (CAFL), 5×10^6 cycles reference

UC_fatigue = delta_sigma_E2_MPa / sigma_c_MPa   // [dimensionless] Fatigue damage utilization ratio (stress range check) Reference: EN 1993-1-9 §6.2; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes, simplified stress range check

Mc_Rd_ltb_Nmm = design_standard === 'EN_1993_6' ? (top_flange_bracing_restrained ? Mc_Rd_vert_Nmm : Mc_Rd_vert_Nmm * 0.85) : Mc_Rd_vert_Nmm   // [N·mm] Lateral-torsional buckling resistance moment (simplified) Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.2; Assumptions: Top flange continuously restrained → full moment resistance, unrestrained → simplified 0.85 reduction factor

UC_LTB = top_flange_bracing_restrained ? 0 : my_Ed_Nmm / Mc_Rd_ltb_Nmm   // [dimensionless] Lateral-torsional buckling utilization ratio Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.2; Assumptions: If top flange fully restrained, LTB is prevented (UC=0)

M_Rd_end_Nmm = design_standard === 'EN_1993_6' ? Sx_mm3 * fy_MPa / gamma_M0 : Sx_mm3 * fy_MPa * phi_b   // [N·mm] End connection moment resistance Reference: EN 1993-1-8 §4.0; Assumptions: End plate or bracket connection, reduced for bolt hole effects if applicable

V_Rd_end_N = design_standard === 'EN_1993_6' ? Vpl_Rd_N : (fy_MPa / Math.sqrt(3)) * (d_mm * tw_mm) * phi_v   // [N] End connection shear resistance Reference: EN 1993-1-8 §4.0; Assumptions: Web shear at end connection
UC_end_reaction = V_max_vert_N / V_Rd_end_N   // [dimensionless] End reaction utilization ratio Reference: EN 1993-1-8 §4.0; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes

UC_governing = Math.max(UC_vert_bending, UC_lat_bending, UC_combined_bending, UC_shear, UC_delta_vert, UC_delta_lat, UC_patch_loading !== null ? UC_patch_loading : 0, UC_shear_buckling !== null ? UC_shear_buckling : 0, UC_interaction !== null ? UC_interaction : 0, UC_fatigue, UC_LTB, UC_end_reaction)   // [dimensionless] Governing (maximum) utilization ratio across all checks Reference: SectorCalc engine rule; Assumptions: All individual UC values calculated correctly, governing UC defines overall PASS/FAIL
status = UC_governing <= 1 ? 'PASS' : 'FAIL'   // [dimensionless] Overall PASS/FAIL status Reference: ISO 9001 §8.6; Assumptions: All validation checks passed

u_c_UC_governing = UC_governing * Math.sqrt(Math.pow(0.03, 2) + Math.pow(0.02, 2) + Math.pow(0.05, 2) + Math.pow(0.02, 2) + Math.pow(0.05, 2))   // [dimensionless] Combined standard uncertainty of governing UC Reference: ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM; Assumptions: Independent uncertainty contributions from loads, geometry, material, and model uncertainty

f_local_flange_MPa = P_wheel_N_design / (4 * Math.pow(tf_mm, 2)) * (Math.pow(bf_mm - tw_mm, 2) / (bf_mm * tf_mm))   // [MPa] Local flange bending stress under wheel load  Reference: AISC Design Guide 7 §3.2; Assumptions: Top flange as cantilevered plate under wheel load, elastic analysis (Timoshenko plate bending)

UC_local_flange_bending = f_local_flange_MPa / (fy_MPa / (design_standard === 'EN_1993_6' ? gamma_M0 : (1 / phi_b)))   // [dimensionless] Local flange bending utilization Reference: AISC Design Guide 7 §3.2; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes, stress limit at fy
R_react_support_N = V_max_vert_N   // [N] Support reaction for column design Reference: EN 1993-6 §3.2; Assumptions: Simple support reaction for column bracket design

M_total_lat_Nmm = P_lat_surge_N * runway_beam_span_mm / 4   // [N·mm] Total lateral moment for torsional check Reference: EN 1993-6 §3.3; Assumptions: Eccentric lateral load causes torsion about beam axis

stress_ratio_combined = design_standard === 'EN_1993_6' ? (sigma_x_Ed_MPa / (fy_MPa / gamma_M1) + sigma_z_Ed_MPa / (fy_MPa / gamma_M1) <= 1.4 ? sigma_x_Ed_MPa / (fy_MPa / gamma_M1) + sigma_z_Ed_MPa / (fy_MPa / gamma_M1) : 1.4) : sigma_x_vert_MPa / (fy_MPa * phi_b / phi_b)   // [dimensionless] Combined stress ratio (linear interaction, EN 1993-1-5 §7.2 Eq 7.2) Reference: EN 1993-1-5 §7.2 Eq 7.2; Assumptions: Linear interaction for combined stresses

UC_torsion = M_total_lat_Nmm / (G_MPa * J_mm4 * 1000000) > 0.01 ? 0.5 : 0   // [dimensionless] Simplified torsion utilization Reference: AISC Design Guide 7 §3.3; Assumptions: Torsional effects are secondary for restrained sections, simplified flag

k_yy_bc = design_standard === 'EN_1993_6' ? 1.0 : null   // [dimensionless] Beam-column interaction factor k_yy (for combined axial + bending if applicable) Reference: EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.3 Annex A; Assumptions: Unity if no significant axial load in runway beam

cranked_effect_Nmm = runway_beam_span_mm > 15000 ? M_max_vert_tandem_Nmm * 0.05 : 0   // [N·mm] Second-order P-Δ effect allowance for very long span runways Reference: SectorCalc engine rule; Assumptions: 5% moment magnification for spans > 15m, detailed P-Δ analysis required for critical cases

rules.message: All crane loads must be positive. Check crane data sheet values.
Number of wheels per side must be integer between 2 and 8.
Wheel spacing must be positive and less than runway beam span.
Spans must be positive.
Vertical impact factor must be between 1.0 and 1.5 per EN 1993-6 and CMAA 70.
Lateral surge percentage must be between 5% and 50% of rated load + trolley.
All section dimensions must be positive.
Section properties must be positive. Check section catalog values.
Material properties must be positive.
Effective rail head width must be positive.
Minimum crane cycles (100/yr) or design life (5 years) not met.
Crane runway beam governing utilization exceeds 1.00. Section fails design check.
Combined stress exceeds 90% of yield with unrestrained top flange. LTB check critical.
Vertical deflection approaching serviceability limit. Consider deeper section or pre-camber.
Fatigue utilization exceeds 0.70. Consider improved detail category or reduced stress range.

smart_warnings.message: Crane service class C or higher requires fatigue design assessment per EN 1993-1-9 and CMAA 70.
Fatigue detail category FAT <= 50 is very low for crane runway. Consider redesigning the detail or increasing flange thickness.
Governing utilization exceeds 0.90. FMEA and independent design review required before release.
Web slenderness h_w/t_w high. Consider intermediate web stiffeners to improve shear buckling resistance.
Bearing stiffeners recommended at supports if patch loading utilization is critical.
Lateral-torsional buckling check is simplified. For detailed LTB design, use EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.2 AISC F2 with full L_b and C_b factors.
Crane span > 15m. Verify second-order effects (P-Δ) and dynamic response including crane-induced vibration.
Verify crane rail attachment is not welded to the tension flange of the runway beam to avoid fatigue crack initiation.

engine_rules.error_propagation: ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM Type A/B. Report u_c(UC_governing), input uncertainty table, sensitivity contributors for wheel loads, span, section modulus, yield strength, impact factor, cycle count, and fatigue detail category.

fmea.failureMode: Wheel load bearing (patch loading) web crippling
fmea.effect: Local web yielding or crippling under wheel load, progressive flange or web deformation
fmea.control_measure: Verify EN 1993-1-5 §6 patch loading resistance. Check stiff bearing length from rail head. Add web stiffeners if required.

fmea.failureMode: Flange local bending failure
fmea.effect: Top flange bending under concentrated wheel load, plastic hinge formation at web-flange junction
fmea.control_measure: Verify local flange bending stress per AISC Design Guide 7. Increase flange thickness if critical.

fmea.failureMode: Fatigue crack at flange-web weld
fmea.effect: Crack initiation at fillet weld toe from cyclic wheel loading, propagation through web
fmea.control_measure: Verify fatigue detail category. Use improved weld profile. Schedule periodic NDT inspection per EN 1090-2.

fmea.failureMode: Web shear buckling
fmea.effect: Loss of web stability under high shear, diagonal tension field formation
fmea.control_measure: Verify EN 1993-1-5 §5 shear buckling resistance. Check h_w/t_w ratio. Add intermediate stiffeners.

fmea.failureMode: Lateral-torsional buckling of runway beam
fmea.effect: Loss of beam stability, lateral displacement and twist under service loads
fmea.control_measure: Verify top flange lateral restraint. If unrestrained, perform full LTB check per EN 1993-1-1 §6.3.2.

fmea.failureMode: Excessive vertical deflection
fmea.effect: Crane operation degradation, rail misalignment, skewing, wheel flange wear
fmea.control_measure: Verify EN 1993-6 §7 serviceability limits. Consider pre-camber for spans > L/600.

fmea.failureMode: Design standard mismatch
fmea.effect: Incorrect load factors, resistance factors, or interaction equations from wrong code selection
fmea.control_measure: Verify code selection matches project specification and jurisdiction.

fmea.failureMode: End web panel buckling at support
fmea.effect: Web crippling at support reaction point, buckling of unstiffened end panel
fmea.control_measure: Provide bearing stiffeners at all support locations per EN 1993-1-5 §9.

reference_code.interface: interface CraneRunwayBeamInput { design_standard: 'EN_1993_6' | 'CMAA_70_AISC'; crane_type: string; crane_service_class: 'A'|'B'|'C'|'D'|'E'|'F'; hoist_capacity_kg: number; crane_bridge_weight_kg: number; trolley_weight_kg: number; num_wheels_per_side: number; wheel_spacing_mm: number; crane_span_mm: number; runway_beam_span_mm: number; impact_factor_vertical: number; lateral_surge_percentage: number; longitudinal_braking_percentage: number; beam_section_type: string; d_mm: number; bf_mm: number; tf_mm: number; tw_mm: number; Ix_mm4: number; Iy_mm4: number; Sx_mm3: number; Zy_mm3?: number; J_mm4?: number; Cw_mm6?: number; steel_grade: string; fy_MPa: number; fu_MPa?: number; E_MPa: number; G_MPa?: number; gamma_M0: number; gamma_M1: number; gamma_M2?: number; phi_b: number; phi_v: number; top_flange_bracing_restrained: boolean; Lb_mm?: number; rail_type: string; rail_head_width_mm: number; rail_fastener_type?: string; crane_run_cycles_per_year: number; design_life_years: number; fatigue_detail_category: string; gamma_Ff: number; gamma_Mf: number; support_stiffener_provided: boolean; intermediate_stiffener_spacing_mm?: number; } interface CraneRunwayBeamOutput { P_wheel_N_unfactored: number; P_wheel_N_design: number; M_max_vert_tandem_Nmm: number; M_lat_surge_Nmm: number; V_max_vert_N: number; sigma_x_vert_MPa: number; UC_vert_bending: number; UC_lat_bending: number; UC_combined_bending: number; UC_shear: number; UC_patch_loading: number; UC_shear_buckling: number; UC_interaction: number; UC_delta_vert: number; UC_delta_lat: number; UC_fatigue: number; UC_LTB: number; UC_local_flange_bending: number; UC_end_reaction: number; UC_torsion: number; UC_governing: number; status: 'PASS'|'FAIL'; u_c_UC_governing: number; delta_vert_mm: number; delta_lat_mm: number; }
reference_code.validation: Zod schema. Throw on invalid wheel count, negative loads, zero dimensions, invalid code selection, engineering UC > 1.0, invalid fatigue category string.
reference_code.testing: Jest. 6 cases: nominal medium-duty crane PASS, heavy-duty crane with high utilization PASS, unrestrained top flange LTB check PASS, web buckling failure scenario FAIL, fatigue critical detail FAIL, invalid dimension BLOCK.

tuv_checklist: Dimensional check completed for every formula
Crane load combination verified vs EN 1991-1-1/CMAA 70
Fatigue assessment methodology verified against EN 1993-1-9
limitations: Simple span analysis only. Continuous runway beams require stiffness-proportional moment distribution.
Crane-induced vibration, acceleration, and dynamic amplification beyond single impact factor not included.
Fatigue assessment is stress-range-based (nominal stress method). Notch class / hot-spot stress method not included.
Seismic load combination not included. Refer to EN 1998-6 for crane runway seismic design.
Column bracket, connection, anchor bolt, and foundation design outside scope.
Crane rail alignment, tolerance, and wear effects outside scope.
Thermal effects and fire design outside scope.

tool_name: Lifting Lug and Padeye Design Calculator - ASME BTH-1 DNV
category: Lifting & Rigging / Padeye and Lifting Lug Design / Structural Steel
calculation_basis: Lifting lug and padeye design screening using ASME BTH-1, DNV-ST-0378, and AISC-style design checks for pin-hole bearing stress, tearout failure, net-section tension rupture, gross-section yielding, double-plane shear (lug ear), weld group demand at lug-to-base-plate connection, pin bending and shear, cheek plate contribution, combined biaxial stresses at lug hole, sling angle resolution into in-plane and out-of-plane components, fatigue screening (high-cycles), and governing demand-capacity ratio. The output is a preliminary engineering screening calculation and must not replace sealed engineering design, ASME BTH-1 detailed lifting device design, DNV lifting appliance certification, AWS D1.1 weld procedure qualification, load test, proof test, periodic inspection, or competent person review.

inputs.name: Design Code / Standard
inputs.validation_rule: Select governing design code for lifting lug design. Determines safety factors, allowable stress or LRFD factors.
inputs.note: ASME BTH-1 for below-the-hook lifting devices in North America. DNV-ST-0378 for offshore lifting.

inputs.name: Lifting Service Classification

options.label: Severe — > 500,000 cycles (ASME BTH-1 Category D — fatigue required)

inputs.validation_rule: Service class determines safety factors per ASME BTH-1 Table 3-1 or DNV-ST-0378 Table 2-1.
inputs.note: Higher cycle counts require lower allowable stresses and fatigue assessment.

inputs.name: Design Load (Maximum Static Load)
inputs.validation_rule: Maximum static load on lug. Does not include sling angle factor or dynamic factor. Must be positive.
inputs.note: Use maximum lifted weight including rigging hardware but before sling angle magnification.

inputs.name: Sling Angle from Horizontal (θ)
inputs.validation_rule: Angle between the sling leg and the horizontal plane. 0° = horizontal sling, 90° = vertical sling.
inputs.note: Sling angle measured from horizontal. Lower angle = higher tension in sling = higher lug force. Minimum 30° recommended (60° from horizontal = 30° from vertical convention varies).

inputs.name: Sling Out-of-Plane Angle (β)
inputs.validation_rule: Angle of the sling out of the plane of the lug. 0° for in-plane pull.
inputs.note: Out-of-plane loading significantly reduces padeye capacity. Minimize in rigging design.

inputs.name: Dynamic / Impact Factor
inputs.validation_rule: ASME BTH-1: 1.25 for powered hoisting, 1.0 for manual. DNV: 1.30 for offshore lifts.
inputs.note: Applied as multiplier to design load for dynamic effects. Include if lifted by powered crane.

inputs.name: Consequence / Risk Factor
inputs.validation_rule: DNV: 1.0 for personnel not under load, 1.2 for personnel exclusion zone. DNV-OS-H205: 1.3 for critical lifts.
inputs.note: Applied to factored load for critical lifts. For ASME, use Category factor from Table 3-1.

inputs.name: Lug Material Grade

options.label: A36 / S235JR — Fy=250 MPa, Fu=400 MPa
A572 Gr.50 / S355JR — Fy=345 MPa, Fu=450 MPa
A514 Gr.100 / S690QL — Fy=690 MPa, Fu=760 MPa
A709 Gr.50 (Bridge) — Fy=345 MPa, Fu=450 MPa
A709 Gr.70 (Bridge) — Fy=485 MPa, Fu=585 MPa
Custom — Enter Fy and Fu manually

inputs.validation_rule: Select material grade. Applies to both lug plate and cheek plates if same material.
inputs.note: ASME BTH-1 requires minimum notch toughness for Category B and above. Verify CVN requirements per BTH-1 §3.4.

inputs.name: Lug Yield Strength (F_y)
inputs.validation_rule: Minimum specified yield strength per material grade. For A572 Gr.50, Fy=345 MPa.
inputs.note: Use nominal minimum yield per ASTM or EN standard.
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 §25.2 — Yield strength CoV=0.05

inputs.name: Lug Ultimate Tensile Strength (F_u)
inputs.validation_rule: Minimum specified tensile strength per material grade. For A572 Gr.50, Fu=450 MPa.
inputs.note: Used for net-section tension rupture and bearing checks.
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 §25.2 — Ultimate strength CoV=0.04

inputs.name: Pin Hole Diameter (d_h)
inputs.validation_rule: Diameter of the hole machined in the lug for the pin. Typically pin diameter + 1-3 mm clearance.
inputs.note: ASME BTH-1: maximum clearance is 3 mm (1/8 inch) for pin-to-hole fit. Less clearance reduces bearing stress.

inputs.name: Lug Plate Width (w)
inputs.validation_rule: Total width of lug plate perpendicular to load direction. Must exceed d_h + 2× edge distance.
inputs.note: Minimum width typically 2.5 × d_h per ASME BTH-1 or DNV. Width controls net-section and tearout capacity.

inputs.name: Lug Plate Thickness (t)
inputs.validation_rule: Thickness of the main lug plate (or sum of thickness for double-plate padeye). Minimum 6 mm per ASME BTH-1.
inputs.note: Thicker plates increase bearing and tension capacity. Check plate rolling/drilling capability.

inputs.name: Edge Distance — Hole Center to Plate Edge (a)
inputs.validation_rule: Distance from center of pin hole to the nearest edge of lug plate in the load direction (a = L - d_h/2 for symmetrical lug, where L = distance from edge to hole center).
inputs.note: Minimum edge distance per ASME BTH-1 Table 3-2: a_min = 1.5 × d_h typically.

inputs.name: Lug Length from Hole Center to Base (L)
inputs.validation_rule: Distance from pin hole center to the lug base (weld line). Controls bending moment at base weld.
inputs.note: Longer eccentricity increases base weld demand. Keep as short as practical for given rigging configuration.

inputs.name: Cheek Plate Thickness (per side)
inputs.validation_rule: Thickness of one cheek plate (reinforcing plate welded around hole). Set to 0 if no cheek plates.
inputs.note: Cheek plates increase bearing area but the pin transfers load through cheek plates with less efficiency. Typical cheek plates: 0.5 × t.

inputs.name: Number of Cheek Plates
inputs.validation_rule: 0 = no cheek plates, 1 = one side, 2 = both sides.
inputs.note: When 2 cheek plates are used, total effective thickness for bearing = t + 2 × t_c × efficiency_factor.

inputs.name: Cheek Plate Bearing Efficiency Factor
inputs.validation_rule: Factor between 0 and 1 accounting for load distribution inefficiency to cheek plates. DNV/ASME recommend 0.5-0.75.
inputs.note: 0.5 = 50% efficiency (common for ASME). 0.75 for tightly fitted cheek plates ground to match lug contour.

inputs.name: Pin Diameter (d_p)
inputs.validation_rule: Diameter of the pin or clevis pin. Must be ≤ d_h - clearance.
inputs.note: Pin bearing area = d_p × t_effective. Pin bending check uses simple-span or double-shear model.

inputs.name: Pin Yield Strength (F_y_pin)
inputs.validation_rule: Minimum specified yield strength of pin material. Typically higher than lug material.
inputs.note: Common pin materials: AISI 4140 (Fy≈600 MPa), AISI 4340 (Fy≈800 MPa), or stainless for corrosion resistance.
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 §25.2 — Yield strength CoV=0.05

inputs.name: Pin Bearing Length / Clevis Gap Width
inputs.validation_rule: Total bearing length of the pin against the lug. For single-shear: w_b = lug_thickness + gap. For double-shear: w_b = total lug + cheek thickness.
inputs.note: Pin bending span is typically the gap between outer support plates of the clevis or shackle.

inputs.name: Pin Bearing Support Span (g — gap between yoke plates)
inputs.validation_rule: Clear span between the inner faces of the clevis or shackle plates. Controls pin bending moment (simple span).
inputs.note: For symmetric double-shear connections, the pin bending span = g (clevis internal width).

inputs.name: Lug Base Weld Class

options.label: Fillet Weld — All around lug base

inputs.validation_rule: Select weld type at lug-to-base-plate connection.
inputs.note: Fillet welds are common. PJP/CJP provides full-strength connection. AWS D1.1 Table 8.1 applies.

inputs.name: Weld Leg Size (s)
inputs.validation_rule: Fillet weld leg size. Minimum 3 mm (1/8 inch) per AWS D1.1.
inputs.note: Effective throat = 0.707 × s for fillet weld. Two-side welds use both longitudinal weld lengths.

inputs.name: Weld Electrode Ultimate Strength (FEXX)
inputs.validation_rule: Select electrode classification. E70XX is standard for A36/A572 applications.
inputs.note: Weld metal strength must match or exceed base metal strength per AWS D1.1 §5.3.

inputs.name: Base Plate Thickness (t_b)
inputs.validation_rule: Thickness of plate to which the lug is welded. Affects weld heat sink and load distribution.
inputs.note: Base plate must be thick enough to develop full weld strength. Minimum t_b ≥ s + 2 mm for fillet welds.

inputs.name: Base Plate Yield Strength (F_y_base)
inputs.validation_rule: Minimum yield strength of base plate material. Typically matches lug material.
inputs.note: Base plate material should be compatible with lug material for welding (similar strength and chemistry).
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 §25.2 — Yield strength CoV=0.05

inputs.name: ASME BTH-1 Minimum Safety Factor
inputs.validation_rule: ASME BTH-1 Table 3-1: Category A (infrequent) SF=3, Category B (moderate) SF=3, Category C (frequent) SF=4, Category D (severe) SF=5.
inputs.note: SF applies to yield-based checks (gross yielding). For ultimate-based (rupture, bearing), effective SF = SF_t × F_y/F_u or per code table.

inputs.name: ASME BTH-1 Bearing Safety Factor
inputs.validation_rule: ASME BTH-1 Table 3-2: Category A SF=1.1, Category B/C SF=1.25, Category D SF=1.5 (bearing on pins).
inputs.note: Bearing safety factor is typically lower than tension SF because bearing is a less brittle failure mode.

inputs.name: Weld Quality Level

options.label: Standard — AWS D1.1 visual inspection only
Enhanced — AWS D1.1 VT + MT/PT on root pass and finished weld
Premium — AWS D1.1 VT + UT/RT + MT/PT full volumetric NDE

inputs.validation_rule: Weld quality level determines resistance factor on weld capacity.
inputs.note: PREMIUM for critical lifts / personnel-rated lifting devices per ASME B30.20.

inputs.name: Design Fatigue Cycles (if applicable)
inputs.validation_rule: Number of load cycles for fatigue check. Enter 0 for static-only design (no fatigue check).
inputs.note: Fatigue assessment per ASME BTH-1 §3.6 or DNV-RP-C203. Required for service classes C, D and dynamic lifting.

formulas: P_total_N = design_load_N * dynamic_factor * consequence_factor   // [N] Factored design load including dynamic and consequence factors Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.1; DNV-ST-0378 §2.2; Assumptions: Static load multiplied by all applicable load factors
theta_rad = sling_angle_deg * Math.PI / 180   // [rad] Sling in-plane angle in radians Reference: ISO 80000; Assumptions: Standard radian conversion
beta_rad = sling_angle_out_of_plane_deg * Math.PI / 180   // [rad] Sling out-of-plane angle in radians Reference: ISO 80000; Assumptions: Standard radian conversion

P_in_plane_N = P_total_N * Math.cos(beta_rad)   // [N] In-plane component of sling force on lug Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.2; Assumptions: Out-of-plane component resolved separately, load acts at hole center
P_out_of_plane_N = P_total_N * Math.sin(beta_rad)   // [N] Out-of-plane component of sling force on lug Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.2; Assumptions: Out-of-plane loading determined from β angle

P_sling_tension_N = P_total_N / Math.sin(theta_rad) / Math.cos(beta_rad)   // [N] Tension in sling leg from vertical load resolution Reference: DNV-OS-H205 §7.3; Assumptions: Two-leg bridle sling symmetrical about vertical, single sling assumption for lug

P_axial_N = P_in_plane_N * Math.cos(theta_rad)   // [N] Horizontal (axial) component on lug pin hole from sling tension Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.2; Assumptions: Lateral load component creates bending at base
P_transverse_N = P_in_plane_N * Math.sin(theta_rad)   // [N] Vertical (transverse) component on lug pin hole from sling tension Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.2; Assumptions: Primary lifting load direction

t_eff_bearing_mm = lug_thickness_mm + cheek_plate_count * cheek_plate_thickness_mm * cheek_plate_efficiency   // [mm] Effective bearing thickness including cheek plate contribution Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.3; DNV-ST-0378 §5.3; Assumptions: Cheek plates share load with reduced efficiency
A_bearing_mm2 = pin_diameter_mm * t_eff_bearing_mm   // [mm^2] Pin bearing area (projected) Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.3.1; DNV-ST-0378 §5.3; Assumptions: Projected area = pin dia × effective thickness
sigma_bearing_MPa = P_total_N / A_bearing_mm2   // [MPa] Pin-hole bearing stress Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.3.1 Eq 3-3; DNV-ST-0378 §5.3; Assumptions: Uniform bearing stress over projected area
F_bearing_all_MPa = fu_MPa / min_safety_factor_bearing   // [MPa] Allowable bearing stress Reference: ASME BTH-1 Table 3-2; Assumptions: Bearing limit based on ultimate strength divided by bearing SF
UC_bearing = sigma_bearing_MPa / F_bearing_all_MPa   // [dimensionless] Pin-hole bearing utilization ratio Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.3.1; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes

A_net_mm2 = (lug_width_mm - lug_hole_diameter_mm) * lug_thickness_mm   // [mm^2] Net (tension rupture) area at hole section Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.1 Eq 3-5; AISC 360-22 D2; Assumptions: Net section through hole centerline, straight line failure path
A_gross_mm2 = lug_width_mm * lug_thickness_mm   // [mm^2] Gross (yield) area of lug section Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.1; Assumptions: Full cross-sectional area away from hole
sigma_net_MPa = P_total_N / A_net_mm2   // [MPa] Net-section tensile stress at hole Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.1; AISC 360-22 D2; Assumptions: Net area controls for ductile rupture
sigma_gross_MPa = P_total_N / A_gross_mm2   // [MPa] Gross-section tensile stress away from hole Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.1; Assumptions: Gross area check for overall yielding
F_net_all_MPa = fu_MPa / min_safety_factor_tension   // [MPa] Allowable net-section tension stress (rupture) Reference: ASME BTH-1 Table 3-1; Assumptions: Rupture limit state
F_gross_all_MPa = fy_MPa / min_safety_factor_tension   // [MPa] Allowable gross-section tension stress (yielding) Reference: ASME BTH-1 Table 3-1; Assumptions: Yield limit state
UC_net_tension = sigma_net_MPa / F_net_all_MPa   // [dimensionless] Net-section tension rupture utilization Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.1; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes
UC_gross_yield = sigma_gross_MPa / F_gross_all_MPa   // [dimensionless] Gross-section yield utilization Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.1; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes

A_tearout_mm2 = 2 * edge_distance_from_hole_mm * lug_thickness_mm   // [mm^2] Double-plane shear tearout area (both sides of hole) Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.2; Assumptions: Two shear planes each of width = edge_distance_from_hole_mm

sigma_tearout_MPa = P_total_N / A_tearout_mm2   // [MPa] Tearout shear stress Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.2 Eq 3-7; Assumptions: Concentrated load equal to total load resolved into shear on both planes

F_tearout_all_MPa = fu_MPa / (Math.sqrt(3) * min_safety_factor_tension)   // [MPa] Allowable tearout shear stress Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.2; Assumptions: Shear yield = 0.58 × F_y, but code uses 0.577 × F_u / SF typically
UC_tearout = sigma_tearout_MPa / F_tearout_all_MPa   // [dimensionless] Tearout failure utilization ratio Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.2; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes

M_base_Nmm = P_transverse_N * lug_length_mm   // [N·mm] Bending moment at lug base from vertical load Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.6; Assumptions: Cantilever action, eccentricity = L (hole center to base)
P_base_N = P_axial_N   // [N] Axial (horizontal) force at lug base Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.6; Assumptions: Horizontal component transferred as shear or tension in weld
V_base_N = P_transverse_N   // [N] Vertical shear at lug base Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.6; Assumptions: Direct shear transferred to weld
M_base_lat_Nmm = P_out_of_plane_N * lug_length_mm   // [N·mm] Out-of-plane bending moment at lug base Reference: AISC 360-22; Assumptions: Out-of-plane load creates lateral bending at base

A_weld_mm2 = weld_class === 'FILLET_ALL_AROUND' ? 2 * (lug_width_mm + lug_thickness_mm) * weld_size_mm * 0.707 : weld_class === 'FILLET_TWO_SIDES' ? 2 * lug_thickness_mm * weld_size_mm * 0.707 : 0   // [mm^2] Effective weld throat area Reference: AWS D1.1 §2.4; AISC 360-22 J2; Assumptions: Fillet weld throat = 0.707 × leg size, all-around or two-side weld configuration

S_weld_mm3 = weld_class === 'FILLET_ALL_AROUND' ? (2 * (lug_width_mm + lug_thickness_mm) * Math.pow(weld_size_mm * 0.707, 2) / 6 + lug_width_mm * (weld_size_mm * 0.707) * ((lug_thickness_mm + weld_size_mm * 0.707) / 2)) : weld_class === 'FILLET_TWO_SIDES' ? (2 * (weld_size_mm * 0.707) * Math.pow(lug_thickness_mm, 2) / 6) : 0   // [mm^3] Weld group section modulus for bending Reference: AISC Manual §8-2; AWS D1.1 Table 8.1; Assumptions: Simplified weld group elastic section modulus

f_weld_bending_MPa = M_base_Nmm / Math.max(S_weld_mm3, 1)   // [MPa] Bending stress in weld (from vertical moment) Reference: AWS D1.1 §2.4; Assumptions: Linear elastic stress distribution in weld throat
f_weld_axial_MPa = P_base_N / Math.max(A_weld_mm2, 1)   // [MPa] Axial stress in weld (from horizontal load) Reference: AWS D1.1 §2.4; Assumptions: Uniform axial stress on weld throat
f_weld_shear_MPa = V_base_N / Math.max(A_weld_mm2, 1)   // [MPa] Shear stress in weld (from vertical shear) Reference: AWS D1.1 §2.4; Assumptions: Uniform shear stress on weld throat

f_weld_lat_bending_MPa = M_base_lat_Nmm / Math.max(S_weld_mm3, 1)   // [MPa] Out-of-plane bending stress in weld Reference: AWS D1.1 §2.4; Assumptions: Lateral bending stress combined with other weld stresses

f_weld_resultant_MPa = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(f_weld_bending_MPa, 2) + Math.pow(f_weld_axial_MPa, 2) + Math.pow(f_weld_shear_MPa, 2) + Math.pow(f_weld_lat_bending_MPa, 2))   // [MPa] Resultant weld stress (4-component vector sum) Reference: AWS D1.1 §2.4.1.6; Assumptions: Elastic vector addition, instantaneous center of rotation method alternative

F_weld_all_MPa = design_code === 'ASME_BTH_1' ? parseInt(weld_electrode_fu_MPa) * 0.3 / min_safety_factor_tension * 3 : parseInt(weld_electrode_fu_MPa) * 0.3   // [MPa] Allowable weld stress Reference: AWS D1.1 Table 8.1; AISC 360-22 J2-4; Assumptions: Fillet weld allowable = 0.30 × FEXX for tension/shear combined
UC_weld = f_weld_resultant_MPa / Math.max(F_weld_all_MPa, 0.1)   // [dimensionless] Weld group utilization ratio Reference: AWS D1.1 §2.4; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes

M_pin_max_Nmm = P_total_N * pin_support_width_mm / 4   // [N·mm] Maximum pin bending moment (simply supported beam, center point load) Reference: AISC Steel Design Guide; Assumptions: Simple-span between yoke plates, point load at pin center
Z_pin_mm3 = Math.PI * Math.pow(pin_diameter_mm, 3) / 32   // [mm^3] Elastic section modulus of pin (circular section) Reference: ISO 80000; Assumptions: Circular cross-section, solid pin
sigma_pin_bending_MPa = M_pin_max_Nmm / Z_pin_mm3   // [MPa] Pin bending stress Reference: AISC 360-22 F11; Assumptions: Elastic bending stress, circular beam
F_pin_bending_all_MPa = pin_material_fy_MPa / min_safety_factor_tension   // [MPa] Allowable pin bending stress Reference: ASME BTH-1 Table 3-1; Assumptions: Yield-based allowable stress
UC_pin_bending = sigma_pin_bending_MPa / F_pin_bending_all_MPa   // [dimensionless] Pin bending utilization ratio Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.6; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes
A_pin_mm2 = Math.PI * Math.pow(pin_diameter_mm, 2) / 4   // [mm^2] Pin cross-sectional area Reference: ISO 80000; Assumptions: Circular solid pin
tau_pin_shear_MPa = P_total_N / (2 * A_pin_mm2)   // [MPa] Pin double-shear stress Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.2; Assumptions: Double shear, two shear planes
F_pin_shear_all_MPa = pin_material_fy_MPa / (Math.sqrt(3) * min_safety_factor_tension)   // [MPa] Allowable pin shear stress Reference: AISC 360-22; Assumptions: Von Mises shear yield criterion
UC_pin_shear = tau_pin_shear_MPa / F_pin_shear_all_MPa   // [dimensionless] Pin shear utilization ratio Reference: ASME BTH-1; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes
P_double_plane_N = P_total_N   // [N] Double-plane shear load through lug ears Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.2; Assumptions: Total load resolved into two shear planes

A_double_plane_mm2 = 2 * edge_distance_from_hole_mm * t_eff_bearing_mm   // [mm^2] Double-plane shear area (both sides of hole) Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.2; Assumptions: Shear-failure planes extend from hole edge to plate edge on both sides

tau_double_plane_MPa = P_double_plane_N / Math.max(A_double_plane_mm2, 1)   // [MPa] Double-plane shear stress in lug ear Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.2; Assumptions: Uniform shear stress over both failure planes
F_double_plane_all_MPa = fu_MPa / (Math.sqrt(3) * min_safety_factor_tension)   // [MPa] Allowable double-plane shear stress Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.2; Assumptions: Shear based on ultimate strength
UC_double_plane_shear = tau_double_plane_MPa / F_double_plane_all_MPa   // [dimensionless] Double-plane shear utilization in lug ear Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.5.2; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes

sigma_combined_MPa = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(sigma_net_MPa, 2) + Math.pow(sigma_bearing_MPa, 2) - 2 * sigma_net_MPa * sigma_bearing_MPa * Math.cos(theta_rad))   // [MPa] Combined stress at hole edge (net tension + bearing interaction) Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.7; Assumptions: Vector sum of net-section tension and bearing components

UC_combined_stress = sigma_combined_MPa / (fu_MPa / min_safety_factor_tension)   // [dimensionless] Combined stress interaction utilization at hole Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.7; Assumptions: UC ≤ 1.0 passes
