UC_governing = Math.max(UC_bearing, UC_net_tension, UC_gross_yield, UC_tearout, UC_weld, UC_pin_bending, UC_pin_shear, UC_double_plane_shear, UC_combined_stress)   // [dimensionless] Governing (maximum) utilization ratio across all lug checks Reference: SectorCalc engine rule; Assumptions: All individual UC values calculated correctly
status = UC_governing <= 1 ? 'PASS' : 'FAIL'   // [dimensionless] Overall PASS/FAIL status Reference: ISO 9001 §8.6; Assumptions: All validation checks passed

u_c_UC_governing = UC_governing * Math.sqrt(Math.pow(0.02, 2) + Math.pow(0.01, 2) + Math.pow(0.05, 2) + Math.pow(0.02, 2))   // [dimensionless] Combined standard uncertainty of governing UC Reference: ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM; Assumptions: Independent uncertainty from loads, geometry, material, code safety factor

P_allowable_ASME_N = design_load_N / Math.max(UC_governing, 0.01)   // [N] Allowable load based on governing utilization Reference: ASME BTH-1 §3.1; Assumptions: Linear scaling, all UC ratios proportionally

M_base_total_Nmm = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(M_base_Nmm, 2) + Math.pow(M_base_lat_Nmm, 2))   // [N·mm] Resultant bending moment at base (in-plane + out-of-plane) Reference: AISC 360-22; Assumptions: Vector sum of orthogonal moments

rules.message: Design load must be positive. Enter the maximum static lift load.
Pin and hole diameters must be positive.
Pin diameter must be less than hole diameter to allow pin insertion.
Lug width must exceed hole diameter. Minimum width ≈ 2.5 × d_h per ASME BTH-1.
All lug dimensions must be positive.
Weld size and base plate thickness must be positive.
Material strengths must be positive.
Pin support span must exceed pin diameter. Check clevis / shackle geometry.
Sling angle < 10° from horizontal is extremely shallow. Very high sling tension. Angle must be ≥ 30° (60° from vertical) per standard rigging practice.
Lifting lug/padeye governing utilization exceeds 1.00. Lug design fails. Increase lug thickness, width, or reduce load.
Out-of-plane sling angle exceeds 15°. Out-of-plane loading significantly reduces padeye capacity. Verify rigging to eliminate or minimize out-of-plane pull.

smart_warnings.message: Net-section tension and tearout are the critical ductile failure modes for lifting lugs. Verify these limits govern.
Governing utilization exceeds 0.90. FMEA and independent design review required before fabrication and use.
Edge distance-to-hole-diameter ratio a/d_h < 1.5. ASME BTH-1 Table 3-2 requires minimum edge distance. Increase edge distance or reduce hole diameter.
Bearing stress governs. Consider increasing lug thickness, adding cheek plates, or increasing pin diameter.
Design load includes dynamic factor of DYN. Verify dynamic factor is appropriate for the lifting equipment and lift category per ASME B30.20.
Fatigue cycles entered. Verify fatigue assessment per ASME BTH-1 §3.6 or DNV-RP-C203 for the selected detail category.
Lifting lug safety factor is below ASME BTH-1 minimum of 3.0. Increase lug capacity or reduce safe working load.

engine_rules.error_propagation: ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM Type A/B. Report u_c(UC_governing), input uncertainty table, sensitivity contributors for design load, hole diameter, pin diameter, lug width, lug thickness, edge distance, yield strength, weld size, and sling angle.

fmea.failureMode: Pin-hole bearing failure (elongation)
fmea.effect: Ovalization of pin hole, progressive deformation, loss of alignment, sudden tearout initiation
fmea.control_measure: Verify bearing stress ≤ allowable per ASME BTH-1 Table 3-2. Increase t or add cheek plates.

fmea.failureMode: Net-section tension rupture
fmea.effect: Brittle or ductile rupture through the reduced section at hole, catastrophic failure
fmea.control_measure: Verify net-section stress ≤ allowable per ASME BTH-1 §3.5.1. Increase width or thickness.

fmea.failureMode: Tearout (double-plane shear)
fmea.effect: Shear failure of lug ear from hole edge to plate edge, sudden load drop
fmea.control_measure: Verify edge distance ≥ 1.5 × d_h per ASME BTH-1 Table 3-2. Increase edge distance.

fmea.failureMode: Weld failure at lug base
fmea.effect: Complete separation of lug from base plate, dropped load
fmea.control_measure: Verify weld resultant stress ≤ allowable per AWS D1.1 §2.4. Increase weld size or add PJP/CJP groove. NDT required.

fmea.failureMode: Pin bending failure
fmea.effect: Pin plastic deformation, binding in hole, inability to rotate, local stress concentration on lug
fmea.control_measure: Verify pin bending stress ≤ allowable. Increase pin diameter or reduce support span.

fmea.failureMode: Pin shear failure
fmea.effect: Catastrophic pin fracture at shear plane, complete loss of lifting capacity
fmea.control_measure: Verify pin double-shear stress ≤ allowable. Use higher strength pin material if critical.

fmea.failureMode: Out-of-plane loading overload
fmea.effect: Lateral bending of lug, buckling of thin plate, weld tearing on one side
fmea.control_measure: Minimize out-of-plane angle. Verify combined stress from in-plane + out-of-plane components. Use check plates for stiffening.

fmea.failureMode: Sling angle measurement error
fmea.effect: Actual sling tension higher than calculated, overload of lug and pin
fmea.control_measure: Verify sling angle from lift plan. Use inclinometer or measured sling length and hook height. Minimum 30° from horizontal.

reference_code.interface: interface LiftingLugPadeyeInput { design_code: 'ASME_BTH_1'|'DNV_ST_0378'|'EN_1993_AISC'; service_class: 'INFREQUENT'|'MODERATE'|'FREQUENT'|'SEVERE'; design_load_N: number; sling_angle_deg: number; sling_angle_out_of_plane_deg?: number; dynamic_factor: number; consequence_factor?: number; lug_material_grade: string; fy_MPa: number; fu_MPa: number; lug_hole_diameter_mm: number; lug_width_mm: number; lug_thickness_mm: number; edge_distance_from_hole_mm: number; lug_length_mm: number; cheek_plate_thickness_mm?: number; cheek_plate_count?: number; cheek_plate_efficiency?: number; pin_diameter_mm: number; pin_material_fy_MPa: number; pin_bearing_width_mm: number; pin_support_width_mm: number; weld_class: string; weld_size_mm: number; weld_electrode_fu_MPa: string; base_plate_thickness_mm: number; base_plate_fy_MPa: number; min_safety_factor_tension: number; min_safety_factor_bearing: number; weld_quality_level?: string; fatigue_cycles?: number; } interface LiftingLugPadeyeOutput { P_total_N: number; UC_bearing: number; UC_net_tension: number; UC_gross_yield: number; UC_tearout: number; UC_double_plane_shear: number; UC_pin_bending: number; UC_pin_shear: number; UC_weld: number; UC_combined_stress: number; UC_governing: number; status: 'PASS'|'FAIL'; u_c_UC_governing: number; P_allowable_ASME_N: number; }
reference_code.validation: Zod schema. Throw on negative/dimension mismatch, pin ≥ hole, width ≤ hole, zero material properties, missing required field, UC > 1.0, invalid safety factor.
reference_code.testing: Jest. 7 cases: nominal padeye PASS with all ASME checks, double-plane shear critical FAIL, single-shear pin bending PASS, out-of-plane load critical FAIL, edge distance too small WARN, weld only configuration PASS, invalid geometry BLOCK.

tuv_checklist: Dimensional check completed for every formula
Pin double-shear and bending model verified
metadata.calibration_trace: NIST/DKD/PTB traceable pin load cell verification
limitations: Single-pin/single-hole padeye only. Multiple-hole or spreader beam configurations outside scope.
Fatigue assessment is nominal stress screening only. Detailed S-N curve method per DNV-RP-C203 not implemented.
Weld design uses simplified elastic vector method. Instantaneous center of rotation method for eccentric weld groups not implemented.
Shackle/clevis selection and capacity outside scope.
Lift beam, spreader bar, and multiple-point lift systems outside scope.
Base plate bending and anchor bolt design outside scope.
Corrosion allowance, wear, and thermal effects outside scope.
Seismic design, accidental load cases, and progressive collapse outside scope.

tool_name: Wire Rope Sling Load and Rigging Angle Calculator — ASME B30 Sling Tension and Utilization Check

inputs.name: Load Mass to be Lifted
inputs.hint: Mass of the load to be lifted. Include all attachments, rigging hardware, and any ancillary equipment suspended below the crane hook.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME P30.1 §4.3.1 — Load weight uncertainty: ±1.5% typical for weighed loads; ±5% for estimated loads

inputs.name: Local Gravitational Acceleration (g)
inputs.hint: Standard gravity = 9.80665 m/s². Variations: 9.764 (Equator) to 9.834 (Poles). Use local value for precision lifts.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 80000-3:2019 — Standard gravity gₙ = 9.80665 m/s²; local variation ±0.35%

inputs.name: Dynamic Amplification Factor (DAF)
inputs.hint: Factor accounting for dynamic effects during lift. ASME B30.9: 1.0 for static/controlled; 1.25 for normal crane lifts; 1.5 for rough terrain; 2.0 for sudden release or shock loading.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §8.2 — Dynamic factor uncertainty: ±5% for normal lifts, ±10% for shock loading

inputs.name: Rigging Configuration / Hitch Type
inputs.hint: Wire rope sling configuration. ASME B30.9 Table 3 defines configuration factors. Bridle: legs share load. Basket: sling wraps around load. Choker: sling passes through itself around load.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 Table 3 — Configuration definitions, zero configuration uncertainty

inputs.name: Number of Sling Legs Installed
inputs.hint: Total number of sling legs installed in the rigging arrangement. Maximum 4 legs per ASME B30.9.
inputs.uncertainty_source: Discrete count variable — zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Active Load-Bearing Legs
inputs.hint: Number of legs actually carrying the load. For 3-leg bridle with rigid load: often only 2 legs carry load due to load distribution. ASME B30.9: do not assume equal load sharing without load-balancing device.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.1.3 — Load distribution: discrete assessment, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Load-Balancing Device Present
inputs.hint: Is a load-balancing device (e.g., equalizer beam, spreader bar, load cell feedback) used to ensure equal load distribution among all sling legs?

inputs.name: Sling Angle from Horizontal (θ)
inputs.hint: Angle between the sling leg and the horizontal plane. ASME B30.9: minimum 30° from horizontal. OSHA: minimum 30° (30° = 2x tension factor). 60° = 1.155x tension factor; 90° = 1.0x tension factor.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.2.1 — Angle measurement uncertainty: ±2° typical for field measurement; ±1° for digital inclinometer

inputs.name: Sling Length (L_s)
inputs.hint: Total length of the sling from master link to hook or end fitting. Used for vertical rise and horizontal offset calculation if not directly provided.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Length measurement tolerance class m: ±0.5%

inputs.name: Vertical Rise from Load to Hook (H)
inputs.hint: Vertical distance from the load attachment point to the crane hook. Used to verify sling angle if sling_length_m and horizontal_offset_m are provided.

inputs.name: Horizontal Offset from Hook to Load (D)
inputs.hint: Horizontal distance from the crane hook centerline to the load attachment point. Combined with vertical_rise_m to verify sling angle. Zero for centered lifts.

inputs.name: Wire Rope Sling WLL — Vertical / Basket (per leg)
inputs.hint: Manufacturer-rated Working Load Limit for this sling in vertical configuration. ASME B30.9 Table 4: WLL shall be marked on sling tag or nameplate. Includes termination efficiency.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §5.1.2 — Manufacturer WLL rating uncertainty: ±3% (includes material, manufacturing, and test variability)

inputs.name: Hitch Configuration Capacity Factor
inputs.hint: Factor applied to vertical WLL for hitch type: Single Vertical = 1.0, 2-Leg Bridle = 1.0 per leg, Single Basket = 2.0, Double Basket = 2.0 per leg (depends on D/d ratio), Choker = 0.75–0.80.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 Table 3 — Hitch capacity factors, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: D/d Ratio (Bend Radius / Rope Diameter)
inputs.hint: Ratio of the diameter of curvature (D) of the pin/hook/shackle over the nominal rope diameter (d). ASME B30.26 Table 1: D/d ≥ 5 for shackles; D/d ≥ 2 for hooks. Lower D/d reduces sling capacity.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.26-2026 §8.3.1 — D/d measurement uncertainty: ±5% field estimate

inputs.name: Minimum Required D/d Ratio per Standard
inputs.hint: Minimum D/d ratio required by the applicable standard. ASME B30.26: D/d ≥ 5 for shackles, D/d ≥ 2 for hooks. EN 1677: D/d ≥ 5. Lower values require mandatory reduction factor.

inputs.name: D/d Reduction Factor (f_dd)
inputs.hint: Reduction factor for sling WLL based on D/d ratio. D/d ≥ minimum_required: f_dd = 1.0. D/d < minimum_required: apply reduction. ASME B30.9: f_dd = actual_Dd / minimum_Dd for basket hitches.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §8.3 — D/d reduction factor, ±1% for interpolation

inputs.name: Termination Efficiency Factor (η_ter)
inputs.hint: Efficiency of sling end terminations (swaged sleeves, hand-spliced eyes, mechanical splices). Swaged sleeve Flemish eye: 0.95–1.0. Hand-tucked splice (6 tucks): 0.80–0.85. Mechanical wedge socket: 0.90. Mechanical clamped: 0.75.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §5.4 — Termination efficiency: ±2% for swaged, ±5% for hand-spliced

inputs.name: Edge Protection Factor (f_edge)
inputs.hint: Factor for edge contact/corner protection. 1.0 = proper edge protection (corner protectors, saddles); 0.9 = rounded edges with padding; 0.8 = soft metal edge contact; 0.7 = sharp edges without protection. ASME B30.9 §8.5: sharp edges must be padded.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §8.5 — Edge protection: ±3% for good protection, ±10% for marginal

inputs.name: Weakest Rigging Hardware WLL
inputs.hint: Working Load Limit of the weakest component in the rigging assembly (shackle, master link, hook, ring). ASME B30.26: all rigging hardware shall be rated and marked. The system is only as strong as its weakest component.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.26-2026 §5.1 — Hardware WLL rating uncertainty: ±3%

inputs.name: Load Distribution Factor (f_dist)
inputs.hint: Factor accounting for unequal load distribution among legs. 1.0 = equal distribution (load-balancing device present and verified); 0.9 = good distribution (rigid load, symmetric geometry); 0.8 = moderate distribution; 0.67 = conservative (no load-balancing, 3+ legs, ASME B30.9 default).
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.1.3 — Load distribution factor: conservative estimate ±2%

inputs.name: Manufacturer Tag / Nameplate Present
inputs.hint: Is the manufacturer tag (with WLL, grade, serial number, manufacturer ID) present and legible on each sling as required by ASME B30.9 §5.1.2?

inputs.name: Sling Inspection Status
inputs.hint: Most recent inspection status per ASME B30.9 §6: Periodic inspection (annual minimum, monthly for heavy use). PASS = no defects found. CONDITIONAL = minor wear/damage requiring engineering review. REJECTED = failed inspection, do not use.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §6 — Inspection status: zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Load Mass Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Percentage uncertainty in load mass. Weighed loads: ±1.5%. Calculated from known density/dimensions: ±5%. Estimated: ±10% or more.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME P30.1 §4.3.1 — Load weight uncertainty categories

inputs.name: Sling Angle Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Uncertainty in sling angle measurement. Digital inclinometer: ±0.5°. Protractor: ±1°. Visual estimation: ±5°.

inputs.name: Sling Capacity Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Percentage uncertainty in sling rated capacity. Manufacturer-rated: ±3%. Derated/aging sling: ±5–10%.

formulas.name: Static Load Weight (W)
formulas.reference: ASME B30.9-2025 §8.1 — Static load weight: W = m × g. Conversion from kg to kN using gravity.
assumptions: Mass uniformly distributed
Standard or local gravity value used
edge_cases: load_mass_kg <= 0: Invalid mass — lift not possible
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Design Lift Load (W_design)
formulas.reference: ASME B30.9-2025 §8.2 — Design lift load includes dynamic amplification: W_design = W × DAF
assumptions: DAF captures all dynamic effects within expected lift profile
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Sling Angle from Horizontal (θ_rad)
formulas.reference: ISO 80000-1:2009 — Degree to radian conversion: θ_rad = θ_deg × π/180
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Angle Tension Factor (K_angle)
formulas.reference: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.2.2 — Angle tension factor: K = 1/sin(θ). At θ=30°: K=2.0, θ=60°: K=1.155, θ=90°: K=1.0
assumptions: Sling is straight line between attachment points (no sag)
edge_cases: sling_angle_rad <= 0: Division by zero — invalid angle
sling_angle_rad >= Math.PI: Invalid angle beyond horizontal
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Ideal Tension per Leg (T_ideal)
formulas.reference: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.2.3 — Tension per leg: T_leg = (W_total / n) × K_angle. Assumes equal distribution among active legs.
assumptions: Equal load distribution among active bearing legs
Sling legs symmetric around vertical axis
edge_cases: active_load_bearing_legs <= 0: Division by zero — invalid leg count
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Design Tension per Leg (T_design)
formulas.reference: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.1.3 — Design tension with load distribution: T_design = T_ideal / f_dist. Conservatively increases tension when distribution is uncertain.
edge_cases: load_distribution_factor <= 0: Division by zero — invalid factor
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Horizontal Force Component per Leg (H_leg)
formulas.reference: Vector resolution: H = T × cos(θ). Horizontal component creates crushing force on the load and determines spreader bar/sling loading.
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Vertical Force Component per Leg (V_leg)
formulas.reference: Vector resolution: V = T × sin(θ). Sum of vertical components across all active legs equals design lift load.
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Effective Sling WLL (WLL_eff per leg)
formulas.expression: sling_effective_wll_kN = wire_rope_sling_wll_vertical_kN * hitch_capacity_factor * dd_reduction_factor * termination_efficiency_factor * edge_protection_factor
formulas.reference: ASME B30.9-2025 §8.3–8.5 — Effective WLL = WLL_vertical × C_hitch × f_dd × η_ter × f_edge. Compounding of all capacity reduction factors.
assumptions: All reduction factors are independent and multiplicative
edge_cases: Any factor <= 0: Invalid — all factors must be positive
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Controlled Available WLL (WLL_system)
formulas.reference: ASME B30.26-2026 §4.1 — System capacity limited by weakest component: WLL_system = min(WLL_sling, WLL_hardware). Chain is only as strong as its weakest link.
assumptions: Hardware WLL is for the same loading configuration (vertical) — adjust if angled
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Required Vertical Sling WLL per Leg
formulas.reference: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.3 — Required vertical WLL = T_design / (combined factors). Used for sling selection: specify a sling with at least this vertical rating.
edge_cases: Combined factor product <= 0: Division by zero
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Rigging Utilization Ratio (UC)
formulas.reference: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.4 — Utilization Ratio UC = T_design / WLL_system. UC ≤ 1.0 = safe. UC > 1.0 = overload. UC > 0.9 = marginal/review recommended.
edge_cases: controlled_available_wll_kN <= 0: Division by zero — no capacity available
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Combined Standard Uncertainty in UC (u_c_UC)
formulas.reference: ISO/IEC Guide 98-3:2008 GUM §5.1 — Combined standard uncertainty: u_c = f × √(Σ(c_i × u_i)²). Sensitivity coefficient for angle: c_θ = cot(θ). Load and capacity sensitivities: c_W = c_C = 1.
assumptions: Input uncertainties are uncorrelated
First-order Taylor expansion valid (linear approximation)
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Capacity Margin (CM)
formulas.reference: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.5 — Capacity margin: CM = WLL_system - T_design. Positive = excess capacity. Negative = overload. Should be ≥ 0.
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Status Code Determination
formulas.reference: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.6 — Status thresholds: ≤0.7 PASS, ≤0.9 CAUTION (monitor), ≤1.0 MARGINAL (review), ≤1.2 OVERLOAD (stop lift if possible), >1.2 CRITICAL_OVERLOAD (immediate stop).
assumptions: Thresholds per ASME B30.9 conservative guidance
edge_cases: UC is NaN or Infinity: Return 'ERROR' status
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

outputs.name: Design Tension per Leg (with distribution factor)
Effective Sling WLL (capacity per leg with factors)
Required Vertical Sling WLL per Leg

standards: ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM — Uncertainty of measurement
ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1 — Control of production and service provision
ISO 2768-1:1989 — General tolerances for linear and angular dimensions

V1.error_msg: Sling angle from horizontal must be between 30° and 90° (ASME B30.9 §9.2.1). Angle <30° creates excessive tension (K_angle > 2.0). Angle >90° is physically impossible for a sling leg.

V2.error_msg: Active load-bearing legs must be between 1 and the number of installed sling legs. Cannot exceed total legs installed.

V3.error_msg: Load mass must be positive. A zero or negative mass produces zero or negative load weight — lift not possible.

V4.error_msg: Dynamic Amplification Factor must be ≥ 1.0. DAF < 1.0 would reduce the static load, which is non-conservative.

V5.error_msg: Without a load-balancing device, ASME B30.9 §9.1.3 recommends assuming only 2 legs bear the load in 3-leg or 4-leg bridles unless specific load distribution analysis is performed. Consider reducing active_load_bearing_legs or adding a load-balancing device.

V6.error_msg: Controlled available WLL must be positive. Check that sling WLL and hardware WLL are positive values and capacity factors are valid.

V7.error_msg: Wire rope sling WLL must be positive. Verify sling manufacturer rating is correctly entered.

V8.error_msg: ASME B30.9 §5.1.2 requires sling manufacturer tag/nameplate to be present and legible. Do not use a sling without identification of rated capacity. Replace or re-certify sling before use.

V9.error_msg: Sling inspection status is REJECTED per ASME B30.9 §6. Do not use this sling for any lifting operation. Remove from service immediately.

V10.error_msg: D/d ratio must be positive. Verify bend radius and rope diameter measurements.

V11.error_msg: All capacity and distribution factors must be positive. Zero or negative factors invalidate the capacity calculation.

smart_warnings.message: UC > 1.0: SLING OVERLOAD — Design tension exceeds effective WLL. Stop the lift. Reduce load, add more sling legs, increase sling angle, or use higher capacity sling.

UC > 0.9: SLING UTILIZATION MARGINAL — Utilization ratio exceeds 0.9 (ASME recommended limit for controlled lifts). Engineering review recommended. Verify all inputs and consider increasing capacity margin.
Sling angle < 45°: HIGH TENSION — Angle factor exceeds 1.414. Tension per leg increases rapidly below 45°. Consider increasing angle to ≥ 60° for optimal capacity utilization.
Inspection status CONDITIONAL: Sling requires engineering review before use per ASME B30.9 §6.4. Verify that conditions noted in inspection do not affect the specific lift geometry.
D/d ratio below minimum required: Apply D/d reduction factor and verify that derated capacity is adequate. Consider using larger hardware (shackle/hook) to meet minimum D/d requirement.
Three or more legs without load-balancing device: ASME B30.9 recommends assuming only 2 legs bear the load. Verify that active_load_bearing_legs input reflects this conservative assumption.
Horizontal force component present: Ensure load is capable of resisting horizontal crushing force. Use spreader beam if load is not rigid enough to withstand horizontal component without damage.
Capacity margin < 10% of design load: Margin is tight, even if UC ≤ 1.0. Consider increasing sling capacity or reducing load for additional safety margin.

error_propagation.method: GUM ISO/IEC Guide 98-3:2008 — LPU first-order Taylor expansion

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: sling angle out of range (V1)
fmea.effect: Sling angle θ < 30° or > 90° — invalid geometry per ASME B30.9
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: active leg count invalid (V2)
fmea.effect: Active legs outside valid range
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: load mass zero or negative (V3)
fmea.effect: Load weight undefined — lift not possible
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: DAF < 1.0 (V4)
fmea.effect: Non-conservative dynamic factor — may underestimate load
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: no load-balancing device with ≥3 legs (V5)
fmea.effect: Unequal load distribution with multi-leg bridle, conservative assessment required
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic WARNING on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: controlled available WLL ≤ 0 (V6)
fmea.effect: No effective capacity — sling or hardware WLL invalid
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: sling WLL not positive (V7)
fmea.effect: Sling capacity undefined
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: manufacturer tag missing (V8)
fmea.effect: Sling identification not present — ASME B30.9 violation
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: sling inspection REJECTED (V9)
fmea.effect: Sling condemned — do not use
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: D/d ratio ≤ 0 (V10)
fmea.effect: Invalid D/d ratio
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: capacity factor ≤ 0 (V11)
fmea.effect: Invalid capacity or distribution factor
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: SLING OVERLOAD: UC > 1.0
fmea.effect: Design tension exceeds effective WLL — risk of sling failure and dropped load
fmea.control_measure: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.4 — UC threshold monitoring with critical alert

fmea.failureMode: SLING MARGINAL: UC > 0.9
fmea.effect: High utilization — reduced safety margin for dynamic events

fmea.failureMode: LOW SLING ANGLE: θ < 45°
fmea.effect: High tension factor increases sling load significantly
fmea.control_measure: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.2.4 — Angle warning with recommendation

fmea.failureMode: Inspected CONDITIONAL sling in use
fmea.effect: Potential hidden damage or wear affecting capacity
fmea.control_measure: ASME B30.9-2025 §6.4 — Conditional use requires engineering sign-off

reference_code.validation: Zod schema. Fail closed on: mass ≤ 0, angle < 30° or > 90°, DAF < 1.0, WLL ≤ 0, inspection_status REJECTED, active legs > installed legs, load_distribution_factor ≤ 0.
reference_code.testing: Jest cases: vertical lift (θ=90°), 2-leg bridle at 60° and 30° (edge of range), 4-leg with and without load-balancing, choker configuration, D/d reduction scenario, rejected sling, overload UC > 1.0, marginal UC = 0.95, uncertainty propagation case.
implementation_notes: Do not use eval(). Use existing AST expression engine.
Run formulas F1-F15 in deterministic order.
All tension outputs in kN. All angles internally in radians for JS Math functions.
UC = T_design / WLL_system. UC > 1.0 = overload.
Required vertical WLL = T_design / (combined factor product). Used for sling selection.
System WLL = min(effective_sling_WLL, hardware_WLL) — chain is only as strong as weakest link.
Capacity margin = WLL_system - T_design. Should be ≥ 0 for safe lift.
Status code: PASS (UC ≤ 0.7), CAUTION (0.7 < UC ≤ 0.9), MARGINAL (0.9 < UC ≤ 1.0), OVERLOAD (1.0 < UC ≤ 1.2), CRITICAL_OVERLOAD (UC > 1.2).
For choker configuration: hitch_capacity_factor = 0.75 (without verification), up to 0.80 (with manufacturer verification).
For basket configuration: hitch_capacity_factor = 2.0 (requires D/d ≥ minimum_required).
Load uncertainty propagation uses root-sum-square of load, angle, and capacity uncertainties.
All boolean gate outputs use strict comparison. Use null for unavailable values, not Infinity.

metadata.description: Wire Rope Sling Load and Rigging Angle Calculator per ASME B30.9-2025 and ASME B30.26-2026. Computes sling tension per leg considering rigging configuration, sling angle, load distribution, D/d ratio, termination efficiency, edge protection, and dynamic effects. Validates sling utilization against rated capacity with combined uncertainty analysis.
metadata.calibration_trace: Load cell measurements traceable to national standards (NIST/PTB). Sling angle measurement via digital inclinometer (calibrated per ISO 17123). Uncertainty values per ASME P30.1 and ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM.
tuv_checklist: ASME B30.9-2025 sling configuration and angle factor tables verified
ASME B30.26-2026 rigging hardware D/d requirements confirmed
ASME P30.1 lift planning methodology reviewed
OSHA 29 CFR 1910.184 safe sling use guidance incorporated
Sling angle tension factor derivation (1/sin θ) verified against ASME B30.9 Table 3
Combined uncertainty calculation per ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM confirmed
Load distribution factor guidance per ASME B30.9 §9.1.3 verified
D/d reduction factor methodology confirmed per ASME B30.9 §8.3
Termination efficiency factors verified per ASME B30.9 §5.4
Edge protection factor assessment per ASME B30.9 §8.5 confirmed
limitations: Single rigging configuration evaluated per calculation. Multi-configuration comparison not supported.
Assumes all sling legs have identical material, diameter, construction, and termination.
Sling sag and catenary effects not modeled — sling assumed straight between attachment points.
Load weight assumed static and uniformly distributed. Out-of-plane loading not modeled.
Wind loads, snow loads, and other environmental effects not included.
Personnel lifting not addressed — requires additional regulations (e.g., OSHA 1926.1501).
Crane/crane hook capacity not included in system WLL check.
Sling stretch under load not modeled (elastic elongation).
Fatigue life, cycle counting, and remaining service life not evaluated.
Seismic effects not considered.
This is a technical calculation tool. It does not replace a lift plan per ASME P30.1 or competent person review per OSHA.

ui_result_policy.warnings_display: smart_warnings only with severity CRITICAL or WARNING in red/yellow banners under verdict
ui_result_policy.uncertainty_display: u_c_UC shown with UC as ± expanded uncertainty at 95.45% confidence

tool_name: Chain Sling Working Load Limit Calculator — EN 818 / ASME B30 Chain Sling Utilization Check

inputs.name: Chain Sling Rating Standard
inputs.hint: Applicable standard for chain sling rating. EN 818-4: Grade 8 chain slings. ASME B30.9: includes chain slings in Table 3. ISO 1835: older standard (increasingly replaced by EN 818).

inputs.name: Chain Grade / Quality
inputs.hint: Chain material grade. Grade 8 (EN 818 / ASME B30.9): most common for overhead lifting. Grade 10/12: higher strength (less common). Grade 5: tie-down/towing (not for overhead lifting per ASME B30.9). Grade 100/120: newer high-strength grades.
inputs.uncertainty_source: Chain grade marking — zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Nominal Chain Diameter
inputs.hint: Nominal diameter of chain link material. EN 818-4: 4–45 mm. ASME B30.9: up to 32 mm typical. Measure with caliper across the link cross-section.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Precision measurement tolerance: ±0.2% for calibrated measurement

inputs.name: Single Leg WLL (Vertical, per chain sling leg)
inputs.hint: Working Load Limit of a single chain sling leg in vertical configuration per EN 818-4 Table 1 or ASME B30.9 Table 3. Grade 8, 10mm chain: ~31.5 kN. Verify from manufacturer tag.
inputs.uncertainty_source: EN 818-4 §6.2 — Manufacturer WLL rating uncertainty: ±3% (includes proof test variability)

inputs.name: Load Mass to be Lifted
inputs.hint: Mass of the load including all attachments and rigging hardware suspended below the crane hook.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME P30.1 §4.3.1 — Load weight uncertainty: ±1.5% for weighed loads

inputs.name: Local Gravitational Acceleration (g)
inputs.hint: Standard gravity = 9.80665 m/s². Use local value for precision critical lifts.

inputs.name: Dynamic Amplification Factor (DAF)
inputs.hint: Factor for dynamic effects: 1.0 for static/controlled; 1.25 for normal crane lifts; 1.5 for rough terrain; 2.0 for shock loading. EN 818-4: 1.0 for static proof load, 1.25 for normal service.
inputs.uncertainty_source: EN 818-4 §7.2 — Dynamic factor uncertainty: ±5% for normal lifts

inputs.name: Chain Sling Configuration
inputs.hint: Configuration of the chain sling assembly. EN 818-5 §5 defines assembly types. Bridle = multiple legs from master link. Basket = chain wraps around load. Choker = chain passes through itself.
inputs.uncertainty_source: EN 818-5 §5 — Configuration definitions, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Number of Installed Sling Legs
inputs.hint: Total number of chain legs in the sling assembly. ASME B30.9: maximum 4 legs. EN 818-4: single, 2-leg, 3-leg, 4-leg assemblies defined.

inputs.name: Active Load-Bearing Legs
inputs.hint: Number of legs actually carrying the load. For 3-leg or 4-leg configurations without load-balancing: often only 2 legs bear the load. EN 818-5 §6.2: if load equalizing not ensured, use reduced leg count.
inputs.uncertainty_source: EN 818-5 §6.2 — Load distribution assessment, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Load-Balancing Device Present
inputs.hint: Is a load-balancing device (e.g., equalizer beam, compensation ring) used to ensure equal load distribution among all legs?

inputs.name: Angle from Vertical (β)
inputs.hint: Angle between the chain sling leg and the vertical axis. EN 818-5: maximum 45° for rated configurations (reduces to 0° vertical). 0° = vertical, 30° = 1.155x factor, 45° = 1.414x factor, 60° = 2.0x factor.
inputs.uncertainty_source: EN 818-5 §6.1 — Angle measurement uncertainty: ±2° field measurement

inputs.name: Rated Configuration Factor (C_rated)
inputs.hint: Configuration factor from EN 818-5 Table 1 or ASME B30.9 Table 3. Single vertical: 1.0. 2-leg bridle: 1.0 per leg. Basket (choked): 0.8. Basket (full): 2.0. Choker: 0.75. Verify from sling tag if leg-specific WLL is marked.
inputs.uncertainty_source: EN 818-5 Table 1 — Configuration factors, zero measurement uncertainty for standard configurations

inputs.name: Load Distribution Factor (f_dist)
inputs.hint: Factor for unequal load distribution. 1.0 = verified equal distribution (load-balancing device). 0.9 = good distribution (symmetric geometry, rigid load). 0.8 = moderate distribution. 0.67 = conservative (no load-balancing, 3+ legs).

inputs.name: Operating Temperature
inputs.hint: Ambient/operating temperature at the chain sling during the lift. EN 818-4 §7.3: Grade 8 rated for -40°C to +200°C standard. Above 200°C: capacity reduction required. Above 300°C: special alloy chain required.

inputs.name: Manufacturer Temperature Limit
inputs.hint: Maximum operating temperature specified by the chain sling manufacturer. Grade 8 standard: 200°C. Extended temperature range chains may be rated to 300°C or higher. Verify from sling tag or manufacturer data sheet.

inputs.name: Temperature Capacity Factor (f_temp)
inputs.hint: Capacity derating factor for elevated temperature. EN 818-4 §7.3: T ≤ 200°C → 1.0; 200°C < T ≤ 300°C → 0.8; 300°C < T ≤ 400°C → 0.5. Below -40°C → use cold-temperature rated chain only.

inputs.name: Edge Contact / Bend Radius Factor (f_edge)
inputs.hint: Factor for edge contact or sharp bend radius on chain links. 1.0 = no edge contact, proper radius. 0.9 = rounded edges with padding. 0.8 = soft metal edge contact. 0.7 = sharp edges without protection. EN 818-6 §8.5: sharp edges must be protected.

inputs.name: Wear Derating Factor (f_wear)
inputs.hint: Derating for chain link wear (reduction in link diameter). ASME B30.9 §6.2.3: reject chain when wear exceeds 10% of original diameter. f_wear = (actual_diameter / original_diameter). 1.0 = no significant wear; 0.9 = 10% wear (rejection limit); 0.5 = 50% section loss.

inputs.name: Additional Derating Factor (f_add)
inputs.hint: Additional derating factor for any other conditions affecting capacity (corrosion, link deformation, nicks/gouges, fatigue cracks, or adverse environmental conditions not covered by other factors). Default 1.0 = no additional derating.

inputs.name: Weakest Rigging Hardware WLL
inputs.hint: Working Load Limit of the weakest component in the rigging assembly: master links, intermediate links, hooks, shackles, rings, or load pins. ASME B30.26: all components must be rated and marked with WLL.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.26-2026 §5.1 — Hardware WLL rating uncertainty: ±3%

inputs.name: Manufacturer Tag / Nameplate Present
inputs.hint: Is the manufacturer tag (with WLL, grade, chain size, serial number, manufacturer ID, and standard reference) present and legible on the chain sling assembly per EN 818-6 §7 or ASME B30.9 §5.1.2?

inputs.name: Manufacturer Certificate Available
inputs.hint: Is the manufacturer test certificate (EN 818-4 §8: proof load test, material traceability, batch number) available and traceable?

inputs.name: Chain Sling Inspection Status
inputs.hint: Inspection status per EN 818-6 §6 or ASME B30.9 §6. PASS = no defects. CONDITIONAL = minor issues requiring engineering review. REJECTED = failed inspection, do not use.
inputs.uncertainty_source: EN 818-6 §6 — Inspection status, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Chain Twisted or Knotted
inputs.hint: Is the chain sling twisted or knotted? EN 818-6 §8.2: chain slings must not be used if twisted, knotted, or kinked. Twisted chains have reduced capacity and may fail unexpectedly.

inputs.name: Chemical Exposure Status
inputs.hint: Exposure to chemicals that may affect chain integrity. EN 818-6 §8.6: chain slings must not be exposed to corrosive chemicals without protective coating. Acid/alkali exposure requires immediate inspection and possible replacement.

inputs.name: Personnel Lifting Application
inputs.hint: Is this chain sling used for personnel lifting? EN 818-6 §4: chain slings for personnel lifting require 10:1 design factor (vs. 4:1 for material lifting) and additional inspection/certification requirements.

inputs.name: Load Mass Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Percentage uncertainty in load mass. Weighed loads: ±1.5%. Calculated: ±5%. Estimated: ±10%.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME P30.1 §4.3.1 — Load weight uncertainty categories

inputs.name: Angle Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Uncertainty in angle from vertical measurement. Digital inclinometer: ±0.5°. Protractor: ±1°. Visual estimation: ±5°.

inputs.name: Chain Sling Capacity Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Percentage uncertainty in chain sling rated capacity. Manufacturer-rated (certified): ±3%. Used/aged sling (wear not quantified): ±5–10%.

formulas.name: Static Load Weight (W)
formulas.reference: EN 818-4 §7.1 / ASME B30.9 §8.1 — Static load weight: W = m × g
assumptions: Mass uniformly distributed
Standard or local gravity value used
edge_cases: load_mass_kg <= 0: Invalid mass — lift not possible
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Design Lift Load (W_design)
formulas.reference: EN 818-4 §7.2 — Design lift load: W_design = W × DAF
assumptions: DAF captures all dynamic effects within expected lift profile
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Angle from Vertical in Radians
formulas.reference: ISO 80000-1:2009 — Degree to radian conversion: β_rad = β_deg × π/180
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Angle from Horizontal (complementary)
formulas.reference: Complementary angle: θ = 90° - β. Used for cross-reference with wire rope sling convention.
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Geometric Leg Factor (K_angle)
formulas.reference: EN 818-5 §6.1 — Geometric leg factor from angle from vertical: K = 1/cos(β). β=0°: K=1.0, β=30°: K=1.155, β=45°: K=1.414, β=60°: K=2.0.
assumptions: Sling leg assumed straight line between attachments
edge_cases: angle_from_vertical_rad >= Math.PI/2: Division by zero — cos(90°) = 0, infinite tension factor
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Design Tension per Leg (T_design)
formulas.reference: EN 818-5 §6.2 — Design tension per leg: T_leg = (W_design × K_angle) / (n_active × f_dist)
assumptions: All active legs share load according to distribution factor
edge_cases: active_load_bearing_legs <= 0 || load_distribution_factor <= 0: Division by zero
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Table Assembly WLL per Leg (from standard tables)
formulas.reference: EN 818-5 Table 1 — Assembly WLL per leg = single_leg_WLL × C_rated. Standard factor determined by configuration, not angle.
assumptions: Configuration factor correctly selected for the rigging arrangement
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Combined Derating Factor (f_comb)
formulas.reference: EN 818-4 §7.3 / EN 818-6 §8 — Combined derating: f_comb = f_temp × f_edge × f_wear × f_add. All derating factors are multiplicative.
assumptions: All derating factors are independent and multiplicative
edge_cases: Any factor <= 0: Invalid — all derating factors must be positive
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Effective Chain Sling WLL per Leg (WLL_eff)
formulas.reference: EN 818-5 §6.3 — Effective WLL per leg = assembly_WLL × f_comb. Applies all derating factors to the standard table WLL.
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Controlled Available WLL (WLL_system)
formulas.reference: ASME B30.26-2026 §4.1 / EN 818-5 §6.4 — System capacity limited by weakest component: WLL_system = min(WLL_sling, WLL_hardware).
assumptions: Hardware WLL is for the same loading configuration — adjust if angled
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Required Single Leg WLL for Application
formulas.reference: EN 818-5 §6.5 — Required single leg WLL = T_design / (C_rated × f_comb). Used for chain sling selection.
edge_cases: Combined factor product <= 0: Division by zero
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Per-Leg Utilization Ratio (UL)
formulas.reference: EN 818-5 §6.6 — Per-leg utilization: UL = T_design / WLL_system_per_leg. UL ≤ 1.0 = safe.
edge_cases: controlled_available_wll_kN <= 0: Division by zero
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Sling Utilization Ratio (UC)
formulas.reference: Overall utilization = max(per_leg_utilization across all legs). In this single-configuration check, UC = per_leg_utilization (all legs assumed identical).
assumptions: All legs have identical capacity and loading
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Combined Standard Uncertainty in UC (u_c_UC)
formulas.reference: ISO/IEC Guide 98-3:2008 GUM §5.1 — Combined standard uncertainty. Sensitivity coefficient for angle from vertical: c_β = tan(β).
assumptions: Input uncertainties are uncorrelated
First-order Taylor expansion valid
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Capacity Margin (CM)
formulas.reference: EN 818-5 §6.7 — Capacity margin: CM = WLL_system - T_design. Positive = excess capacity.
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Status Code Determination
formulas.reference: EN 818-5 §6.8 / ASME B30.9 §9.6 — Status thresholds: ≤0.7 PASS, ≤0.9 CAUTION, ≤1.0 MARGINAL, ≤1.2 OVERLOAD, >1.2 CRITICAL_OVERLOAD.
assumptions: Thresholds per conservative industry practice
edge_cases: UC is NaN or Infinity: Return 'ERROR'
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

outputs.name: Table Assembly WLL (per leg from standard table)
Effective Chain Sling WLL (per leg with all deratings)
Controlled Available WLL (system limit per leg)
Required Single Leg WLL for this Application
Sling Utilization Ratio (max UL per leg)

standards: EN 818-4 — Short link chain for lifting purposes — Grade 8
EN 818-6 — Chain slings — Specification for information for use and maintenance
ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM — Uncertainty of measurement
ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1 — Control of production and service provision

V1.error_msg: Angle from vertical must be between 0° and 60° (EN 818-5 §6.1). Angles > 60° produce excessive leg tension (K_angle > 2.0).

V2.error_msg: Active load-bearing legs must be between 1 and the number of installed legs. Cannot exceed total installed legs.

V3.error_msg: Load mass must be positive. Zero or negative mass is invalid for a lifting operation.

V4.error_msg: Dynamic Amplification Factor must be ≥ 1.0. DAF < 1.0 is non-conservative.

V5.error_msg: Without a load-balancing device, EN 818-5 §6.2 recommends assuming only 2 legs bear the load for 3-leg or 4-leg configurations unless specific load distribution analysis is performed.

V6.error_msg: Controlled available WLL must be positive. Check chain WLL and hardware WLL inputs.

V7.error_msg: Single leg WLL must be positive. Verify chain sling manufacturer rating.

V8.error_msg: EN 818-6 §7.1 / ASME B30.9 §5.1.2 requires manufacturer tag/nameplate on the chain sling assembly. Do not use a sling without rated capacity identification.

V9.error_msg: Chain sling inspection status is REJECTED. Do not use this chain sling for any lifting operation. Remove from service immediately per EN 818-6 §6.3.

V10.error_msg: All WLL values must be positive. Verify chain sling and hardware ratings.

V11.error_msg: EN 818-6 §8.2: chain slings must not be used if twisted, knotted, or kinked. Straighten or replace before use.

V12.error_msg: EN 818-6 §8.6: chain slings with significant or unknown chemical exposure status must be inspected and cleared by a competent person before use.

V13.error_msg: Personnel lifting requires EN 818 rated chain slings with 10:1 design factor. EN 818-6 §4: additional certification and inspection requirements apply.

V14.error_msg: Personnel lifting requires chain sling inspection status of PASS. EN 818-6 §4: enhanced inspection criteria apply for personnel lifting applications.

V15.error_msg: EN 818-4 §8.1: manufacturer test certificate must be available for the chain sling assembly. Traceability from raw material to proof load test is required.

V16.error_msg: All derating and distribution factors must be positive. Zero or negative factors invalidate the capacity calculation.

smart_warnings.message: UC > 1.0: CHAIN SLING OVERLOAD — Design tension exceeds effective WLL. Stop the lift. Reduce load, increase angle (reduce from horizontal), add more legs, or use higher capacity chain sling.
UC > 0.9: CHAIN SLING UTILIZATION MARGINAL — Utilization ratio exceeds 0.9. Engineering review recommended. Verify all inputs and consider additional margin.
Angle from vertical > 45° for chain sling: EN 818-5 Table 1 load ratings apply only up to 45°. Angles beyond 45° require special engineering review and reduced capacity.
Temperature exceeds standard rated range: Chain sling capacity is derated per temperature_capacity_factor. Verify manufacturer temperature rating for the specific chain grade.
Inspection status CONDITIONAL: Chain sling requires engineering review before use per EN 818-6 §6.2 or ASME B30.9 §6.4.
Wear derating factor < 1.0: Chain link wear detected. ASME B30.9 §6.2.3: chain must be removed from service when wear exceeds 10% of original diameter. Verify remaining cross-section.
Edge contact without protection: EN 818-6 §8.5 requires edge protection for chain slings in contact with sharp corners or edges of the load.
Three or more legs without load-balancing device: EN 818-5 §6.2 recommends assuming 2 legs. Verify active_load_bearing_legs input.

error_propagation.method: GUM ISO/IEC Guide 98-3:2008 — LPU first-order Taylor expansion

reference_code.validation: Zod schema. Fail closed on: mass ≤ 0, angle < 0° or > 60°, DAF < 1.0, WLL ≤ 0, inspection REJECTED, twisted/knotted chain, significant chemical exposure, personnel lifting without EN 818, certificate missing.
reference_code.testing: Jest cases: vertical lift (β=0°), 2-leg bridle at 30° and 45° (standard limits), 3-leg and 4-leg configurations with/without load-balancing, basket configuration, choker configuration, temperature derating (200°C, 300°C, 400°C), wear derating at 10% wear, twisted/knotted chain (blocked), chemical exposure blocked, personnel lifting scenarios, overload UC > 1.0, uncertainty propagation.
implementation_notes: Do not use eval(). Use existing AST expression engine.
Run formulas F1-F16 in deterministic order.
All tension outputs in kN. Angle internally in radians for JS Math functions.
Angle from vertical (β) is primary for chain slings (EN 818 convention, 0° = vertical).
Geometric leg factor K = 1/cos(β). β=0° → K=1.0, β=45° → K=1.414.
UC = T_design / WLL_system. UC > 1.0 = overload.
Combined derating = product of temperature, edge contact, wear, and additional factors.
System WLL = min(effective_chain_WLL, hardware_WLL).
Personnel lifting requires EN 818 standard, PASS inspection, and 10:1 design factor.
Wear derating: f_wear = actual/original diameter. Reject at 10% loss (f_wear ≤ 0.9).
Status code thresholds: PASS (≤0.7), CAUTION (≤0.9), MARGINAL (≤1.0), OVERLOAD (≤1.2), CRITICAL_OVERLOAD (>1.2).
Uncertainty propagation via GUM LPU with sensitivity c_β = tan(β) for angle.
All boolean gate outputs use strict comparison. Use null for unavailable values, not Infinity.

metadata.description: Chain Sling Working Load Limit Calculator per EN 818-4/5/6 and ASME B30.9. Computes leg tension, effective WLL with temperature derating, edge contact, wear, and additional derating factors, system capacity (limited by weakest component), and utilization ratio for chain sling lifting operations.
metadata.calibration_trace: Chain sling proof load test certificates traceable to national standards. Temperature measurement via calibrated thermocouple (ISO 17025). Wear measurement via calibrated caliper (ISO 2768-1).
tuv_checklist: EN 818-4 Grade 8 chain sling WLL and proof load requirements verified
EN 818-5 chain sling assembly configuration factors from Table 1 confirmed
EN 818-6 chain sling use, maintenance, and inspection criteria incorporated
ASME B30.9 chain sling provisions verified
ASME B30.26 rigging hardware requirements confirmed
Geometric leg factor (1/cos β) derivation verified against EN 818-5 §6.1
Temperature derating factors per EN 818-4 §7.3 confirmed
Wear derating and rejection criteria per ASME B30.9 §6.2.3 verified
Combined uncertainty calculation per ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM confirmed
Personnel lifting safety factor and additional requirements per EN 818-6 §4 confirmed
limitations: Single chain sling configuration evaluated per calculation.
Assumes all chain legs are from same batch and have identical properties.
Chain sling stretch under load not modeled.
Fatigue life and remaining service life not evaluated.
Environmental effects (wind, snow, ice) not included.
Corrosion rate modeling not included — chemical exposure treated as binary assessment.
Wear assessment requires physical measurement — calculator does not estimate wear rate.
Crane/crane hook capacity not included in system WLL check.
Dynamic effects simplified to a single DAF factor — hoist speed effects not separately modeled.
This is a technical calculation tool. Does not replace pre-lift inspection per EN 818-6 §6 or competent person review.

ui_result_policy.uncertainty_display: u_c_UC shown with UC as ± expanded uncertainty at 95.45% confidence

tool_name: Plate Bending Force Calculator — Sheet Metal Forming / Press Brake Force and Capacity Utilization Check
category: Sheet Metal / Press Brake / Plate Forming
standards: DIN 8586 — Sheet metal forming by bending
ISO 16092-3 — Machine tools safety — Press brakes
ISO 12100 — Safety of machinery — General principles for design — Risk assessment and risk reduction
ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1 — Production and service provision — Control of production
VDI 3441 — Statistical testing of the operational and positional accuracy of machine tools
Manufacturer press brake manual — Capacity chart and tooling specifications

inputs.name: Bending Process Type
inputs.hint: Select the press brake bending process. Air bending: workpiece contacts V-die at three points. Bottoming: punch forces material into die at full stroke. Coining: punch penetrates material surface for minimal springback

inputs.name: Material Family
inputs.hint: Material family classification. Selection affects recommended V-die ratio, springback characteristics, and minimum bend radius

inputs.name: Ultimate Tensile Strength (UTS)
inputs.hint: Ultimate tensile strength of the workpiece material. Low-carbon steel: 270-400 MPa. Stainless 304: 500-700 MPa. Aluminum 6061: 260-310 MPa. Use material certificate or standard reference
inputs.uncertainty_source: Material certificate or standard reference data uncertainty

inputs.name: Yield Strength (YS)
inputs.hint: Yield strength of workpiece material. Low-carbon steel: 180-250 MPa. Stainless 304: 200-350 MPa. Aluminum 6061: 140-240 MPa
inputs.uncertainty_source: Material certificate or standard reference data uncertainty

inputs.name: Elastic Modulus (Young's Modulus)
inputs.hint: Elastic modulus of workpiece material. Steel: 200 GPa. Stainless: 193 GPa. Aluminum: 69 GPa. Copper: 110 GPa. Titanium: 110 GPa

inputs.name: Workpiece Thickness
inputs.hint: Nominal thickness of the workpiece being bent. Sheet metal: 0.5-6 mm. Plate: 6-100 mm

inputs.name: Bend Length (Part Width)
inputs.hint: Length of the bend line (width of the workpiece perpendicular to the bend axis). Longer bends require higher total force

inputs.name: V-Die Opening Width
inputs.hint: Width of the V-die opening. Recommended V-die = 8× to 12× material thickness for air bending

inputs.name: Punch Tip Radius
inputs.hint: Radius at the tip of the punch. Typically 0.5-2.0 mm for sheet metal. Affects inside bend radius and force distribution

inputs.name: Target Bend Angle
inputs.hint: Desired included bend angle. 90° is a standard right-angle bend. Acute bends < 90° may require special tooling
inputs.uncertainty_source: Angle gauge or protractor measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Bending Force Coefficient (K1)
inputs.hint: Empirical bending force coefficient. Standard air bending: 1.42. Bottoming: 5. Coining: 10+. Increase for tight radii, high-strength materials

inputs.name: Process Force Multiplier
inputs.hint: Force multiplier for the selected bending process. Air bending: 1.0. Bottoming: 2.0-5.0. Coining: 5.0-10.0. Determined by process type and depth of penetration

inputs.name: Grain Direction Factor
inputs.hint: Factor accounting for material grain orientation. Across grain: 1.0 (default). With grain: 1.1-1.3 (higher force). Diagonal: 0.9-1.0. Bending with the grain increases cracking risk

inputs.name: Friction and Lubrication Factor
inputs.hint: Empirical factor for lubrication conditions. Well lubricated: 0.9-1.0. Dry: 1.0-1.15. Poor lubrication: 1.15-1.3. Affects sliding friction between workpiece and tooling

inputs.name: Safety Factor for Force Calculation
inputs.hint: Safety factor applied to the required force. ISO 12100 risk assessment: minimum 1.2. Increase for unknown material properties or worn tooling

inputs.name: Press Brake Nominal Capacity
inputs.hint: Rated maximum force of the press brake. Common capacities: 500 kN (50 ton), 1000 kN (100 ton), 2000 kN (200 ton). Check machine nameplate

inputs.name: Usable Bed Length (Between Side Frames)
inputs.hint: Maximum usable bed length between press brake side frames. Common: 2000-4000 mm. Longer beds reduce force per meter capacity

inputs.name: Upper Tool (Punch) Capacity
inputs.hint: Maximum allowable force per meter on the upper tool (punch). Varies by punch type, material, and heat treatment. Check tooling manufacturer specification

inputs.name: Lower Die (V-Die) Capacity
inputs.hint: Maximum allowable force per meter on the lower die (V-die). Check die manufacturer capacity chart

inputs.name: Minimum Permitted Inside Radius
inputs.hint: Minimum acceptable inside bend radius. Used to check if the predicted radius meets print requirement. Set to 0 if no minimum requirement

inputs.name: Off-Center Load Factor
inputs.hint: Factor for off-center loading. Centered load (symmetric): 1.0. Partially off-center: 1.25-1.5. Full off-center (one side frame): 1.5-2.0. Off-center reduces effective machine capacity

inputs.name: Operator Guarding Verified (ISO 16092-3)
inputs.hint: Operator guarding and safety devices verified per ISO 16092-3. Includes light curtains, two-hand controls, and rear guarding

inputs.name: Tooling Verification Completed
inputs.hint: Punch and die tooling verified for correct mounting, alignment, and capacity rating. Confirm in machine setup checklist

inputs.name: Thickness Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of thickness measurement. Caliper/micrometer resolution: typically ±0.03 mm. Laser gauge: ±0.01 mm

inputs.name: Material Strength Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Relative standard uncertainty of tensile strength and yield strength values. Material certificate: ±1-3%. Generic reference: ±5-10%

inputs.name: Force Coefficient Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Relative standard uncertainty of the empirical bending force coefficient. DIN standard coefficients: ±5%. Custom estimates: ±10-20%

formulas.name: V-Die Opening to Thickness Ratio
formulas.reference: DIN 8586 — Recommended V-die opening to thickness ratio for air bending: 8:1 to 12:1
edge_cases: plate_thickness_mm <= 0: Cannot compute ratio — invalid thickness
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Base Air Bending Force (kN)
formulas.reference: DIN 8586 — Base air bending force formula: F = K1 × UTS × t² × L / V
assumptions: Plane strain condition
Uniform material properties across bend length
Isotropic material behavior (first-order approximation)
edge_cases: v_die_opening_mm <= 0: Cannot compute force — invalid V-die opening
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Process-Adjusted Force (kN)
formulas.reference: DIN 8586 — Process-adjusted force: air bending × multiplier for bottoming or coining
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Required Press Force with Adjustment Factors
formulas.expression: required_press_force_kN = process_adjusted_force_kN * material_direction_factor * friction_lubrication_factor * safety_factor_force * off_center_load_factor
formulas.reference: DIN 8586 — Final required force including material, friction, safety, and loading adjustment factors
assumptions: Factors are multiplicative and independent
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Required Tonnage (Metric Tons)
formulas.reference: ISO 80000 — Force conversion: 1 kN = 0.10197 metric ton-force (using standard gravity 9.80665 m/s²)
assumptions: Standard gravity g = 9.80665 m/s²
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Force Per Meter of Bend Length
formulas.reference: DIN 8586 — Specific force per unit bend length for tooling capacity comparison
edge_cases: bend_length_mm <= 0: Cannot compute force per meter
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Predicted Inside Radius (Air Bending)
formulas.reference: DIN 8586 — Air bending inside radius ≈ V/6.67. Bottoming inside radius ≈ V/12. Coining: punch radius + 0.1 mm
assumptions: Standard V-die geometry
Sharp punch tip assumed for air bending
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Springback Index
formulas.reference: DIN 8586 — Springback tendency index. Higher values indicate greater springback. Typical threshold: >0.01 = significant springback expected
assumptions: Linear elastic unloading (Hooke's law)
Simplified bending stress distribution
edge_cases: plate_thickness_mm <= 0: Cannot compute springback index
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Machine Utilization Ratio
formulas.reference: VDI 3441 — Machine capacity utilization: required force / rated capacity. Target: <0.8 for safe operation
edge_cases: machine_nominal_capacity_kN <= 0: Cannot compute utilization
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Upper Tool Utilization Ratio
formulas.reference: VDI 3441 — Upper tool utilization: force per meter / punch capacity per meter. Target: <0.9 for safe operation
edge_cases: upper_tool_capacity_kN_per_m <= 0: Cannot compute tool utilization
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Lower Die Utilization Ratio
formulas.reference: VDI 3441 — Lower die utilization: force per meter / die capacity per meter. Target: <0.9 for safe operation
edge_cases: lower_die_capacity_kN_per_m <= 0: Cannot compute die utilization
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Combined Utilization Coefficient (UC)
formulas.reference: VDI 3441 — Combined utilization coefficient: the highest utilization among all three capacity constraints
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Combined Utilization Uncertainty (u_c_UC)
formulas.reference: ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM — Combined standard uncertainty for UC using RSS method with sensitivity coefficients = 1
assumptions: First-order Taylor expansion
Uncorrelated input quantities
Coverage factor k=2 for 95.45% confidence
edge_cases: plate_thickness_mm <= 0: Cannot compute thickness relative uncertainty
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Capacity Margin
formulas.reference: VDI 3441 — Capacity margin: positive value indicates machine has reserve capacity for this operation
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Status Code Determination
formulas.reference: VDI 3441 — Force capacity limit status classification
edge_cases: UC calculation failed: status_code = ERROR
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

tensile_strength_positive.error_msg: Ultimate tensile strength must be positive.

machine_capacity_positive.error_msg: Machine nominal capacity must be positive.

usable_bed_length_positive.error_msg: Usable bed length must be positive.

upper_tool_capacity_positive.error_msg: Upper tool capacity per meter must be positive.

lower_die_capacity_positive.error_msg: Lower die capacity per meter must be positive.

bend_angle_valid.error_msg: Target bend angle must be between 1° and 180°.

v_die_ratio_minimum.error_msg: V-die opening to thickness ratio is below the safe minimum of 4:1. Risk of die damage and poor bend quality.

operator_guarding_required.error_msg: Operator guarding must be verified per ISO 16092-3 before proceeding.

tooling_verification_required.error_msg: Tooling verification must be completed before proceeding.

smart_warnings.message: PRESS BRAKE OVERLOAD: The required bending force exceeds the machine rated capacity. Reduce bend length, use a larger V-die opening, select a different process (air bending instead of bottoming/coining), or move to a larger capacity machine.
PRESS BRAKE NEAR CAPACITY LIMIT: The required bending force approaches the machine rated capacity. Proceed with caution. Consider increasing V-die opening or reducing bend length for a safety margin.
UPPER TOOL OVERLOAD: Force per meter exceeds the upper tool (punch) capacity. Risk of tool damage or failure. Use a stronger punch or distribute the load over a longer tool section.
LOWER DIE OVERLOAD: Force per meter exceeds the lower die (V-die) capacity. Risk of die deformation or cracking. Use a stronger die or larger V-die opening.
V-DIE TO THICKNESS RATIO BELOW RECOMMENDED RANGE: For air bending, V/die ratio should be 6:1 to 12:1. Below 6:1 increases force requirement and risk of die damage.
V-DIE TO THICKNESS RATIO ABOVE RECOMMENDED RANGE: For air bending, ratio >12:1 may result in poor dimensional accuracy and excessive springback.

INSIDE RADIUS BELOW MINIMUM REQUIREMENT: The predicted inside bend radius is smaller than the minimum permitted radius. Increase V-die opening or use a different punch radius to meet print requirements.
SIGNIFICANT SPRINGBACK EXPECTED: Springback index exceeds 0.01. Large springback is expected. Consider overbending, coining, or reducing the bend radius. CNC compensation may be required.
PROCESS FORCE MULTIPLIER REVIEW REQUIRED: Air bending typically requires a process multiplier of 1.0. Values >3 suggest bottoming or coining. Verify process selection and multiplier are correct.
MATERIAL TENSILE-TO-YIELD RATIO VERY LOW: The UTS/YS ratio is below 1.05, indicating limited strain-hardening capacity. Increased risk of cracking during bending. Verify material properties.
OPERATOR GUARDING NOT VERIFIED: Operator safety devices must be verified per ISO 16092-3 before starting bending operations.
TOOLING NOT VERIFIED: Punch and die tooling must be verified for correct mounting and capacity rating per ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1.
SIGNIFICANT OFF-CENTER LOAD: Off-center load factor exceeds 1.3. Off-center bending reduces effective machine capacity and increases ram deflection risk. Verify load distribution.

smart_warnings.condition: UC >= 0 && UC <= 0.8 && operator_guarding_verified == true && tooling_verified == true
smart_warnings.message: PRESS BRAKE CAPACITY ADEQUATE: All capacity checks within safe limits (UC ≤ 0.8). Guarding and tooling verified. Safe to proceed.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: thickness_positive
fmea.effect: Plate thickness must be positive.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: tensile_strength_positive
fmea.effect: Tensile strength must be positive.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: PRESS BRAKE OVERLOAD
fmea.effect: Required force exceeds machine capacity. Risk of mechanical failure, ram damage, or operator injury.
fmea.control_measure: VDI 3441 — Capacity check with utilization ratio

fmea.failureMode: UPPER TOOL OVERLOAD
fmea.effect: Force per meter exceeds punch capacity. Risk of punch fracture, tip chipping, or bending.
fmea.control_measure: Tooling manufacturer specification — force per meter check

fmea.failureMode: LOWER DIE OVERLOAD
fmea.effect: Force per meter exceeds die capacity. Risk of die cracking, V-opening deformation, or die insert failure.
fmea.control_measure: Die manufacturer specification — force per meter check

fmea.failureMode: INSIDE RADIUS BELOW MINIMUM
fmea.effect: Bend radius smaller than print requirement. Part rejection.

fmea.failureMode: V-DIE RATIO OUT OF RANGE
fmea.effect: V-die opening to thickness ratio outside safe range. Increased force, poor bend quality, or die damage.

fmea.failureMode: SIGNIFICANT SPRINGBACK
fmea.effect: Large springback causes incorrect final bend angle. Requires rework or overbend compensation.
fmea.control_measure: DIN 8586 — Overbend compensation or coining process

fmea.failureMode: OPERATOR GUARDING NOT VERIFIED
fmea.effect: Safety devices not verified. Risk of operator injury from pinch points or flying debris.

fmea.failureMode: TOOLING NOT VERIFIED
fmea.effect: Tooling condition unknown. Risk of incorrect mounting, wear damage, or capacity mismatch.

error_propagation.method: GUM ISO/IEC Guide 98-3:2008 — LPU first-order Taylor expansion

reference_code.validation: Zod schema. Fail closed on zero/negative thickness, zero bend length, zero V-die opening, zero/negative material strengths, zero machine/tooling capacities. Block on missing operator guarding or tooling verification.
reference_code.testing: Jest cases: standard air bending 90° in low-carbon steel, bottoming process, coining, high-strength steel edge case, machine overload scenario, tooling overload, combined UC > 1.0, uncertainty computation, springback classification.
implementation_notes: Do not use eval(). Use existing AST expression engine.
Run formulas F1-F15 in deterministic order.
Force formula: F = K1 × UTS × t² × L / V × multiplier × material_factor × friction_factor × safety × off_center_factor.
All factor adjustments default to 1.0 except safety factor (1.2).
V-die ratio must be >= 4:1 minimum for any process.
Inside radius prediction differs by process: air bending ≈ V/6.67, bottoming ≈ V/12, coining ≈ punch radius + 0.1 mm.
Springback index = YS × R_i / (E × t). Threshold: >0.01 = significant springback.
Utilization = required / capacity for machine, upper tool, and lower die separately.
Combined UC = highest utilization among all three constraints.
Capacity margin = machine capacity - required force (positive = safe).
Status code based on UC thresholds: >1.0 overload, >0.9 near limit, >0.8 adequate monitor, <=0.8 adequate.
Uncertainty from thickness, material strength, and force coefficient combined via RSS.
Safety gates: operator guarding and tooling verification must be true to proceed.
All utilization values are dimensionless ratios.
Use null for unavailable values, not Infinity.

metadata.description: Press brake plate bending force and capacity utilization calculator per DIN 8586, VDI 3441, and ISO 16092-3. Computes required bending force, machine capacity utilization, upper and lower tooling utilization, predicted inside radius, springback index, and combined utilization coefficient (UC) for air bending, bottoming, and coining processes on sheet metal and plate.
tuv_checklist: ISO 12100 risk assessment for force overload considered
ISO 7438 minimum bend radius check verified
ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM uncertainty propagation implemented
Material property data quality assessment outlined
Tooling capacity verification per manufacturer specification
metadata.calibration_trace: Force calculations based on DIN 8586 empirical coefficients. Machine capacity references traceable to manufacturer nameplate. Uncertainty values per ISO 80000 and ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM. Thickness uncertainty: ±0.03 mm, material strength uncertainty: ±3%, force coefficient uncertainty: ±5%.
