tool_name: Press Brake Bend Allowance and K-Factor Calculator — Sheet Metal Flat Pattern Development Check
category: Sheet Metal / Press Brake / Flat Pattern Development
standards: DIN 6935 — Sheet metal — Bending, dimensions of bent parts
DIN 8586 — Sheet metal forming by bending
ISO 2768-m — General tolerances — Part 1: Tolerances for linear and angular dimensions
ISO 1101 — Geometrical product specifications (GPS) — Geometrical tolerancing
ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1 — Production and service provision — Control of production
Manufacturer press brake, tooling, and CAM bend table — Bend allowance and K-factor reference data

inputs.name: Calculation Mode
inputs.hint: Select calculation mode. KNOWN_K_FACTOR: enter K-factor directly. DERIVE_K_FROM_KNOWN_BEND_ALLOWANCE: compute K-factor from known bend allowance. DERIVE_K_FROM_MEASURED_FLAT_LENGTH: compute K-factor from measured flat pattern length

inputs.name: Bend Method
inputs.hint: Bending method. Affects K-factor typically: Air bending K ≈ 0.33-0.42. Bottoming K ≈ 0.25-0.35. Coining K ≈ 0.20-0.30. Roll bending and wipe bending K varies by application

inputs.name: Material Family
inputs.hint: Material family. Affects K-factor reference ranges and minimum bend radius recommendations

inputs.name: Material Thickness
inputs.hint: Nominal material thickness. Sheet metal: 0.5-6 mm. Plate: 6-50 mm

inputs.name: Inside Bend Radius
inputs.hint: Inside bend radius of the finished part. For air bending, inside radius ≈ punch tip radius or V/6.67 (whichever is larger)

inputs.name: Bend Sweep Angle
inputs.hint: The angle through which the material bends (complement of the included angle). 90° sweep = right-angle bend
inputs.uncertainty_source: Angle gauge or CNC controller position feedback

inputs.name: Known K-Factor
inputs.hint: Known K-factor value. Typical values: soft materials (aluminum, copper): 0.33-0.40. Steel: 0.38-0.42. Hard materials (stainless, titanium): 0.25-0.35. Required when calculation_mode = KNOWN_K_FACTOR
inputs.uncertainty_source: Material reference table or CAM bend table

inputs.name: K-Factor Source
inputs.hint: Source of the K-factor value. MEASURED_COUPON: highest confidence. SHOP_CAM_TABLE: high confidence. MACHINE_TOOLING_TABLE: medium-high confidence. DIN_6935_REFERENCE: medium confidence. MATERIAL_SUPPLIER_DATA: medium confidence. ENGINEERING_ESTIMATE: low confidence

inputs.name: Outside Flange Length 1
inputs.hint: Length of the first outside flange (from bend tangent point to part edge). Used for outside setback calculation

inputs.name: Outside Flange Length 2
inputs.hint: Length of the second outside flange (from bend tangent point to part edge)

inputs.name: Measured Bend Allowance
inputs.hint: Measured bend allowance from a physical coupon test. Required when calculation_mode = DERIVE_K_FROM_KNOWN_BEND_ALLOWANCE

inputs.name: Measured Flat Pattern Length
inputs.hint: Measured total flat pattern length (sum of straight segments + bend allowance). Required when calculation_mode = DERIVE_K_FROM_MEASURED_FLAT_LENGTH

inputs.name: Minimum Permitted Inside Radius
inputs.hint: Minimum acceptable inside bend radius per ISO 7438 or print requirement. Set to 0 if no minimum requirement

inputs.name: V-Die Opening Width (optional)
inputs.hint: V-die opening width. Optional — used for calculated inside radius estimation if inside_bend_radius_mm not known from tooling. When provided, helps validate bend deduction

inputs.name: Grain Direction Relative to Bend Line
inputs.hint: Grain direction relative to the bend axis. ACROSS_GRAIN: preferred (bend line perpendicular to rolling direction). WITH_GRAIN: increases cracking risk. DIAGONAL: reduced effect. UNKNOWN: assumes worst case

inputs.name: Target Flat Length Tolerance
inputs.hint: Maximum acceptable deviation from the calculated flat pattern length. ISO 2768-m typical: ±0.2 mm for precision, ±0.5 mm for general tolerance

inputs.name: Thickness Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of thickness measurement

inputs.name: Radius Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of inside radius measurement

inputs.name: K-Factor Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of the K-factor. MEASURED_COUPON: ±0.005. SHOP_CAM_TABLE: ±0.01. DIN_6935_REFERENCE: ±0.02. ENGINEERING_ESTIMATE: ±0.05

inputs.name: Angle Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of bend angle measurement
inputs.uncertainty_source: Angle gauge or CNC position feedback calibration

formulas.name: Bend Angle in Radians
formulas.reference: ISO 80000 — Angle conversion: degrees to radians
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Included Angle (Complementary)
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Included angle is the complement of bend sweep angle
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Radius to Thickness Ratio
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Ratio of inside bend radius to material thickness: key parameter for K-factor and bend allowance
edge_cases: material_thickness_mm <= 0: Cannot compute ratio
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: V-Die to Thickness Ratio (optional)
formulas.expression: v_to_thickness_ratio = v_die_opening_mm != null ? v_die_opening_mm / MAX(material_thickness_mm, 0.001) : null
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — V-die opening to thickness ratio for reference
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs. Set to null if v_die_opening_mm is not provided.

formulas.name: Effective K-Factor Determination
formulas.expression: effective_k_factor = calculation_mode == 'KNOWN_K_FACTOR' ? known_k_factor : calculation_mode == 'DERIVE_K_FROM_KNOWN_BEND_ALLOWANCE' ? (measured_bend_allowance_mm / (bend_angle_rad * material_thickness_mm)) - (inside_bend_radius_mm / material_thickness_mm) : calculation_mode == 'DERIVE_K_FROM_MEASURED_FLAT_LENGTH' ? (measured_flat_length_mm - outside_flange_1_mm - outside_flange_2_mm) / (bend_angle_rad * material_thickness_mm) - (inside_bend_radius_mm / material_thickness_mm) : known_k_factor
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — K-factor = (BA / (θ × t)) - (R_i / t) where BA = bend allowance, θ = bend angle in radians, t = thickness, R_i = inside radius
assumptions: Neutral axis shift model
Constant K-factor across bend arc
edge_cases: material_thickness_mm <= 0: Cannot compute K-factor
bend_angle_rad <= 0: Cannot compute K-factor
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Neutral Axis Radius
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Neutral axis radius: R_N = R_i + K × t. The neutral axis is where material length remains unchanged during bending
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Bend Allowance
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 §4 — Bend allowance: BA = θ × (R_i + K × t). The arc length of the neutral axis through the bend
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Outside Setback (OSSB)
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 §5 — Outside setback (bend allowance method): OSSB = tan(θ/2) × (R_i + t). Distance from bend tangent to virtual sharp intersection
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Bend Deduction (BD)
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 §6 — Bend deduction: BD = 2 × OSSB - BA. Amount subtracted from the sum of outside dimensions to obtain flat pattern length
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Flat Pattern Length (Bend Allowance Method)
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Flat pattern length using bend allowance method: L_flat = L1 + L2 - 2 × OSSB + BA
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Inside Arc Length
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Inside arc length (compression side): L_i = θ × R_i
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Outside Arc Length
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Outside arc length (tension side): L_o = θ × (R_i + t)
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: K-Factor Confidence Score
formulas.expression: k_factor_confidence_score = k_factor_source == 'MEASURED_COUPON' ? 1.0 : k_factor_source == 'SHOP_CAM_TABLE' ? 0.9 : k_factor_source == 'MACHINE_TOOLING_TABLE' ? 0.8 : k_factor_source == 'DIN_6935_REFERENCE' ? 0.7 : k_factor_source == 'MATERIAL_SUPPLIER_DATA' ? 0.6 : k_factor_source == 'ENGINEERING_ESTIMATE' ? 0.3 : 0.5
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — K-factor confidence scoring: MEASURED_COUPON = 1.0, SHOP_CAM_TABLE = 0.9, MACHINE_TOOLING_TABLE = 0.8, DIN_6935_REFERENCE = 0.7, MATERIAL_SUPPLIER_DATA = 0.6, ENGINEERING_ESTIMATE = 0.3
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Flat Length Uncertainty (RSS)
formulas.reference: ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM — Combined standard uncertainty for flat pattern length using RSS method
assumptions: First-order Taylor expansion
Uncorrelated input quantities
Coverage factor k=2 for 95.45% confidence

formulas.reference: ISO 2768-m — Tolerance utilization: measurement uncertainty / target tolerance. Target: < 0.5 for reliable inspection

inside_radius_positive.error_msg: Inside bend radius must be positive.

bend_angle_valid.error_msg: Bend sweep angle must be between 0.1° and 180°.

flange_lengths_positive.error_msg: Both outside flange lengths must be positive.

k_factor_required_in_known_mode.error_msg: Known K-factor is required when calculation_mode is KNOWN_K_FACTOR.

bend_allowance_required_for_derive.error_msg: Measured bend allowance must be provided and positive for DERIVE_K_FROM_KNOWN_BEND_ALLOWANCE mode.

flat_length_required_for_derive.error_msg: Measured flat length must be provided and positive for DERIVE_K_FROM_MEASURED_FLAT_LENGTH mode.

tolerance_positive.error_msg: Target length tolerance must be positive.

smart_warnings.message: FLAT LENGTH OUTSIDE TOLERANCE: The combined measurement uncertainty exceeds the target tolerance. The calculated flat pattern length cannot be reliably verified. Consider improving measurement methods or relaxing the tolerance requirement.
TOLERANCE MARGIN LOW: Measurement uncertainty uses more than 80% of the tolerance band. Consider more precise measurement methods or K-factor validation.
K-FACTOR CONFIDENCE LOW: The K-factor source is an engineering estimate. Recommended to perform a coupon test to validate K-factor for production.
K-FACTOR CONFIDENCE HIGH: The K-factor source is validated (measured coupon or CAM table). Flat length within tolerance. Proceed with production.
INSIDE RADIUS BELOW RECOMMENDED RANGE: R/t ratio < 1 indicates a very tight bend. Risk of material cracking. Check material formability limits.
LARGE R/T RATIO: R/t > 10. The K-factor approaches 0.5 (neutral axis at mid-thickness). Large radii may require special tooling or roll bending.
MEASURED DATA REQUIRED FOR VALIDATION: K-factor derived from calculation should be validated with a physical coupon test per DIN 6935 before production.

BENDING WITH GRAIN AT LOW R/T RATIO: Bending with the grain direction (bend line parallel to rolling direction) at R/t < 2 significantly increases cracking risk. Consider using a larger radius or rotating the part.
STAINLESS STEEL BEND RADIUS CONCERN: Stainless steel requires minimum R/t of ~1.5 for adequate formability. Below this ratio, risk of cracking and excessive work hardening.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: bend_angle_valid

fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: FLAT LENGTH OUTSIDE TOLERANCE
fmea.effect: Uncertainty exceeds tolerance. Inconsistent bend results in production.

fmea.failureMode: K-FACTOR CONFIDENCE LOW
fmea.effect: Flat pattern length may be incorrect. Rework or scrap risk.

fmea.failureMode: INSIDE RADIUS BELOW RECOMMENDED
fmea.effect: Material cracking or fracture during bending.

fmea.failureMode: MODE MISMATCH — Missing input for selected mode
fmea.effect: Required input for selected calculation mode is missing.

fmea.failureMode: R/T RATIO OUT OF RANGE
fmea.effect: K-factor reference may not be reliable for extreme R/t ratios.

fmea.failureMode: GRAIN DIRECTION — With grain at low R/t
fmea.effect: Material cracking, especially in high-strength materials.

error_propagation.method: GUM ISO/IEC Guide 98-3:2008 — LPU first-order Taylor expansion

reference_code.validation: Zod schema. Fail closed on zero/negative thickness, zero/negative inside radius, invalid bend angle, zero flange lengths. Mode-dependent required fields must be present.
reference_code.testing: Jest cases: 90° bend in low-carbon steel with known K-factor, derive K-factor from bend allowance, derive K-factor from measured flat length, tight radius with cracking risk, tolerance exceeded case, uncertainty computation, grain direction warnings.
implementation_notes: Do not use eval(). Use existing AST expression engine.
Run formulas F1-F16 in deterministic order.
Three calculation modes: KNOWN_K_FACTOR, DERIVE_K_FROM_KNOWN_BEND_ALLOWANCE, DERIVE_K_FROM_MEASURED_FLAT_LENGTH.
Bend allowance = θ × (R_i + K × t) per DIN 6935.
Outside setback = tan(θ/2) × (R_i + t).
Bend deduction = 2 × OSSB - BA.
Flat pattern length = L1 + L2 - 2 × OSSB + BA.
K-factor range: 0 to 1. Typical: 0.25-0.45 for sheet metal.
Neutral axis radius = R_i + K × t.
K-factor confidence depends on source: measured coupon = 1.0, engineering estimate = 0.3.
Uncertainty from thickness, radius, K-factor, and angle combined via RSS.
Tolerance utilization = uncertainty / tolerance. Target < 0.5.
R/t ratio < 1 triggers cracking risk warning.
V-die opening is optional for this calculator.
All gate outputs are boolean or string. Use null for unavailable values, not Infinity.

metadata.description: Press brake bend allowance, K-factor, and flat pattern development calculator per DIN 6935 and DIN 8586. Computes bend allowance, outside setback, bend deduction, flat pattern length, neutral axis radius, and K-factor from known K-factor, known bend allowance, or measured flat length. Includes uncertainty analysis and tolerance utilization check.
tuv_checklist: ISO 7438 minimum bend radius check included
ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM uncertainty propagation implemented
Uncertainty budget with RSS combination confirmed
metadata.calibration_trace: Flat pattern length calculations based on DIN 6935 neutral axis model. K-factor references traceable to DIN standard tables and CAM bend table data. Uncertainty values per ISO 80000 and ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM. Thickness uncertainty: ±0.03 mm, radius uncertainty: ±0.05 mm, K-factor uncertainty: ±0.02, angle uncertainty: ±0.2°.

tool_name: Sheet Metal Flat Pattern Development Calculator — Multi-Bend Blank Length and Bend Line Stack-Up Check
category: Sheet Metal / Press Brake / Flat Pattern Development
standards: DIN 6935 — Sheet metal — Bending, dimensions of bent parts
DIN 8586 — Sheet metal forming by bending
ISO 2768-m — General tolerances — Part 1: Tolerances for linear and angular dimensions
ISO 1101 — Geometrical product specifications (GPS) — Geometrical tolerancing
ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1 — Production and service provision — Control of production

inputs.name: Flat Pattern Development Method
inputs.hint: Flat pattern development method. BEND_ALLOWANCE_SUM: sum straight segments + total bend allowance. BEND_DEDUCTION_SUM: sum outside dimensions - total bend deduction. K_FACTOR_PER_BEND: computed from effective K-factor for all bends. CAM_TABLE_VERIFIED: use verified CAM bend table data

inputs.name: Dimension Convention
inputs.hint: Dimensioning convention used on the part print. OUTSIDE_VIRTUAL_SHARP: dimensions to virtual sharp intersections. INSIDE_TANGENT: dimensions to inside tangent points. CENTERLINE: dimensions to centerline. MIXED_REQUIRES_CONVERSION: mixed conventions — requires conversion to a single method

inputs.name: Bend Method
inputs.hint: Bending method used for all bends in the part. Affects K-factor range and minimum bend radius

inputs.name: Material Family
inputs.hint: Material family. Affects K-factor reference ranges and minimum bend radius recommendations

inputs.name: Material Thickness
inputs.hint: Nominal material thickness. Sheet metal: 0.5-6 mm. Plate: 6-50 mm

inputs.name: Number of Bends
inputs.hint: Number of bends in the part. 1-12 bends supported. More than 12 bends: use iterative flat pattern development or CAM software

inputs.name: Number of Straight Segments
inputs.hint: Number of straight segments in the developed part. For a part with N bends: N+1 straight segments. Minimum 2 (one bend), maximum 13 (12 bends)

inputs.name: Sum of Straight Segment Lengths
inputs.hint: Total sum of all straight segment centerline lengths in the flat pattern. For outside virtual sharp convention: this excludes bend allowance contribution

inputs.name: Total Bend Sweep Angle
inputs.hint: Sum of all individual bend sweep angles. For 4 bends at 90° each: 360°. Maximum: 12 bends × 180° = 2160°
inputs.uncertainty_source: Angle specification tolerance per angle sum

inputs.name: Average Inside Bend Radius
inputs.hint: Average inside bend radius across all bends. If radii vary, use length-weighted average. Typical: punch tip radius or V/6.67 for air bending
inputs.uncertainty_source: Radius gauge or average of individual bend measurements

inputs.name: Minimum Inside Bend Radius (any bend)
inputs.hint: The smallest inside radius among all bends in the part. Used for formability check

inputs.name: Minimum Permitted Inside Radius (material limit)
inputs.hint: Smallest inside radius permitted for the material per ISO 7438. If minimum_inside_radius_mm < minimum_permitted_inside_radius_mm, cracking risk exists

inputs.name: Effective K-Factor
inputs.hint: Effective K-factor for all bends (or average if K varies). Determined from coupon test, CAM table, or DIN 6935 reference. Used to compute bend allowance per bend
inputs.uncertainty_source: Material reference table or CAM bend table

inputs.name: K-Factor Source
inputs.hint: Source of the K-factor value. MEASURED_COUPON: highest confidence. ENGINEERING_ESTIMATE: lowest confidence

inputs.name: Total Known Bend Allowance (sum of all bends)
inputs.hint: Sum of known bend allowances for all bends. Optional — used when bend allowance data is available from CAM table or coupon test. Overrides K-factor calculation
inputs.uncertainty_source: CAM table or coupon test data

inputs.name: Total Known Bend Deduction (sum of all bends)
inputs.hint: Sum of known bend deductions for all bends. Optional — used with BEND_DEDUCTION_SUM method
inputs.uncertainty_source: CAM table or coupon test data

inputs.name: Sum of Outside Setbacks (all bends)
inputs.hint: Sum of all individual outside setback values. Optional — used for validation of flat pattern calculation
inputs.uncertainty_source: Calculated from individual bend geometry or CAM table

inputs.name: Blank Width (perpendicular to bend axis)
inputs.hint: Width of the blank perpendicular to the bend axis. Used for blank area calculation

inputs.name: Edge Trim Allowance per Side
inputs.hint: Additional material allowance per edge for post-bend trimming. Typical: 0.5-3 mm per side for clean edges

inputs.name: Saw or Laser Kerf Allowance
inputs.hint: Material removed by saw or laser cut per cut. Laser: 0.1-0.3 mm. Saw: 1-3 mm. Plasma: 1.5-3 mm

inputs.name: Grain Direction Relative to Bend Line
inputs.hint: Grain direction relative to the primary bend axis. ACROSS_GRAIN: preferred (bend line perpendicular to rolling direction). WITH_GRAIN: increases cracking risk

inputs.name: Bend Relief Provided
inputs.hint: Whether bend relief notches are provided at bend ends to prevent tearing. Recommended for flanges adjacent to bends

inputs.name: Minimum Flange Length in Part
inputs.hint: The shortest flange length in the part. Used for minimum flange length check

inputs.name: Minimum Required Flange Length
inputs.hint: Minimum flange length required for the given V-die opening (typically: V_die / 2 + flange clearance). Shorter flanges may not be formable
inputs.uncertainty_source: Die geometry and part design rule

inputs.name: Target Flat Length Tolerance
inputs.hint: Maximum acceptable deviation from the calculated flat pattern length. ISO 2768-m typical: ±0.2 mm precision, ±0.5 mm general

inputs.name: Length Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of straight segment length measurements

inputs.name: Thickness Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of thickness measurement

inputs.name: Radius Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of inside radius measurement

inputs.name: K-Factor Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of the K-factor. MEASURED_COUPON: ±0.005. SHOP_CAM_TABLE: ±0.01. DIN_6935_REFERENCE: ±0.02. ENGINEERING_ESTIMATE: ±0.05

inputs.name: Angle Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Standard uncertainty of bend angle measurement per individual bend, summed across all bends
inputs.uncertainty_source: Angle gauge or CNC position feedback calibration

formulas.name: Total Bend Angle in Radians
formulas.reference: ISO 80000 — Angle conversion: degrees to radians
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Radius to Thickness Ratio (minimum radius)
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Ratio of minimum inside bend radius to material thickness
edge_cases: material_thickness_mm <= 0: Cannot compute ratio
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Average Neutral Axis Radius
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Neutral axis radius: R_N = R_avg + K × t
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Calculated Total Bend Allowance (from K-factor)
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 §4 — Total bend allowance: BA_total = θ_total × (R_avg + K × t). Sum of neutral axis arc lengths through all bends
assumptions: Uniform K-factor across all bends
Average radius approximation for variable radii
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Effective Total Bend Allowance (method-dependent)
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Effective bend allowance: uses measured/CAM data if available; otherwise K-factor calculated value
assumptions: Known bend allowance, when provided, overrides calculation
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Calculated Total Bend Deduction (from K-factor)
formulas.expression: calculated_total_bend_deduction_mm = 2 * bend_count * TAN((total_bend_sweep_angle_deg / MAX(bend_count, 1) * PI / 180) / 2) * (average_inside_radius_mm + material_thickness_mm) - effective_total_bend_allowance_mm
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 §6 — Total bend deduction: BD_total = sum of (2 × OSSB_i) - BA_total. Using average per-bend angle
assumptions: Uniform bend angle across all bends (average approximation)
edge_cases: bend_count <= 0: Cannot compute per-bend angle
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Effective Total Bend Deduction (method-dependent)
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Effective bend deduction: uses known data if available; otherwise calculated value
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Developed Flat Length (Bend Allowance Sum Method)
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Flat pattern length (bend allowance sum): L_flat = sum(L_straight) + total_BA
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Developed Flat Length (Bend Deduction Sum Method — validation)
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Flat pattern length (bend deduction sum): L_flat = sum(L_outside) - total_BD
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Cut Length (flat pattern + trim + kerf)
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Cut length: flat pattern length + edge trim allowance (both sides) + kerf allowance
assumptions: Linear addition of trim and kerf allowances
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Blank Area (approximate)
formulas.reference: Simple area calculation: area = length × width
assumptions: Rectangular blank shape
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: K-Factor Confidence Score
formulas.expression: k_factor_confidence_score = k_factor_source == 'MEASURED_COUPON' ? 1.0 : k_factor_source == 'SHOP_CAM_TABLE' ? 0.9 : k_factor_source == 'MACHINE_TOOLING_TABLE' ? 0.8 : k_factor_source == 'DIN_6935_REFERENCE' ? 0.7 : k_factor_source == 'MATERIAL_SUPPLIER_DATA' ? 0.6 : k_factor_source == 'ENGINEERING_ESTIMATE' ? 0.3 : 0.5
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — K-factor confidence scoring by source
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Flat Length Uncertainty (RSS multi-bend)
formulas.expression: flat_length_uncertainty_mm = SQRT((length_measurement_uncertainty_mm)^2 + (bend_count * thickness_uncertainty_mm)^2 + (bend_count * radius_uncertainty_mm)^2 + (bend_count * k_factor_uncertainty)^2 + (angle_uncertainty_deg * (PI / 180) * (average_inside_radius_mm + effective_k_factor * material_thickness_mm))^2)
formulas.reference: ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM — Combined standard uncertainty for multi-bend flat pattern using RSS method. Each bend contributes thickness, radius, and K-factor uncertainty
assumptions: First-order Taylor expansion
Uncorrelated input quantities
Coverage factor k=2 for 95.45% confidence
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Tolerance Utilization
formulas.reference: ISO 2768-m — Tolerance utilization: measurement uncertainty / target tolerance. Target: < 0.5 for reliable inspection
edge_cases: target_flat_length_tolerance_mm <= 0: Cannot compute tolerance utilization
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Minimum Flange Utilization
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 — Flange utilization: actual flange length / required flange length. Target: >= 1.0 (flange is long enough)
edge_cases: minimum_required_flange_length_mm <= 0: Cannot compute flange utilization
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

formulas.name: Combined Utilization Coefficient (UC) and Status Code
formulas.expression: UC = MAX(tolerance_utilization, 1 / MAX(minimum_flange_utilization, 0.001)). status_code = UC > 1.0 ? 'STACK_UP_UC_EXCEEDED' : UC > 0.8 ? 'STACK_UP_WARNING' : UC >= 0 ? 'STACK_UP_ACCEPTABLE' : 'ERROR'
formulas.reference: DIN 6935 / ISO 2768-m — Combined utilization coefficient considering both tolerance and flange length constraints
edge_cases: minimum_flange_utilization <= 0: Cannot compute combined UC
formulas.cursor_instruction: Compute this formula deterministically using the inputs.

bend_count_valid.error_msg: Bend count must be between 1 and 12.

segment_count_valid.error_msg: Straight segment count must equal bend_count + 1 (range: 2-13).

straight_length_positive.error_msg: Sum of straight segment lengths must be positive.

total_angle_valid.error_msg: Total bend sweep angle must be between 0.1° and 2160° (max 12 bends × 180°).

avg_radius_positive.error_msg: Average inside bend radius must be positive.

k_factor_range.error_msg: Effective K-factor must be between 0 and 1.

flange_length_positive.error_msg: Both minimum and required flange lengths must be positive.

tolerance_positive.error_msg: Target flat length tolerance must be positive.

smart_warnings.message: STACK-UP UC EXCEEDED: The combined utilization coefficient exceeds 1.0. Either the tolerance margin is insufficient or the minimum flange length requirement is not met. Review part design or tolerance specification.
TOLERANCE MARGIN LOW: Multi-bend flat pattern uncertainty uses more than 80% of the tolerance band. Consider K-factor validation or tighter process control.
FLANGE LENGTH INADEQUATE: The minimum flange length is shorter than the required minimum for the given die opening. Flange may not be formable. Increase flange length or reduce V-die opening.
K-FACTOR VALIDATION REQUIRED: The K-factor source is an engineering estimate (confidence < 0.5). Perform a coupon test to validate K-factor before production, especially for multi-bend parts.
FLAT LENGTH WITHIN TOLERANCE: All checks passed. K-factor confidence high, tolerance margin adequate, flange lengths sufficient. Proceed with production.
INSIDE RADIUS BELOW MATERIAL LIMIT: The minimum inside bend radius is below the permitted limit. Risk of cracking in one or more bends. Consider using a larger radius.
BENDING WITH GRAIN AT LOW R/T RATIO: Multiple bends with grain direction at low R/t ratio significantly increases cracking risk. Consider rotating the blank or using a larger bend radius.
DEVELOPMENT METHOD MISMATCH: CAM_TABLE_VERIFIED method selected but K-factor confidence is below 0.8. Verify CAM bend table data matches the actual material and process conditions.
COMPLEX PART WITH LOW K-FACTOR CONFIDENCE: Part has more than 6 bends and the K-factor confidence is below 0.7. Accumulated uncertainty may be significant. Recommend physical flat pattern validation.
BEND RELIEF RECOMMENDED: Short flanges without bend relief are prone to tearing at bend ends. Consider adding bend relief notches.
NO EDGE TRIM ALLOWANCE: Edge trim allowance is set to 0. For critical dimension parts, consider adding trim allowance for post-bend edge finishing.

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: bend_count_valid
fmea.effect: Bend count must be 1-12.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: segment_count_valid
fmea.effect: Segment count must equal bend_count + 1.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: STACK-UP UC EXCEEDED
fmea.effect: Flat pattern length likely out of tolerance or flange length inadequate.

fmea.failureMode: FLANGE LENGTH INADEQUATE
fmea.effect: Flange too short to form. Part geometry not achievable.

fmea.failureMode: K-FACTOR VALIDATION REQUIRED
fmea.effect: Flat pattern length may be incorrect for multi-bend part. Rework or scrap risk.
fmea.control_measure: DIN 6935 — Coupon test recommended before production

fmea.failureMode: INSIDE RADIUS BELOW MATERIAL LIMIT
fmea.effect: Material cracking at one or more bend locations.

fmea.failureMode: TOLERANCE MARGIN LOW (multi-bend)
fmea.effect: Accumulated bend uncertainty consumes most of the tolerance band.

fmea.failureMode: GRAIN DIRECTION — Multiple with-grain bends
fmea.effect: Increased cracking risk at multiple bend locations.
fmea.control_measure: ISO 7438 — Grain direction check for all bends

error_propagation.method: GUM ISO/IEC Guide 98-3:2008 — LPU first-order Taylor expansion

reference_code.validation: Zod schema. Fail closed on zero/negative thickness, invalid bend count (1-12), segment count != bend_count+1, zero straight length, invalid total angle, invalid K-factor range, zero blank width, zero/negative flange lengths, zero tolerance.
reference_code.testing: Jest cases: 2-bend simple channel part, 4-bend box part, 6-bend complex part, bend allowance sum method, bend deduction sum method, tolerance exceeded case, flange length inadequate case, K-factor confidence scoring, uncertainty RSS computation, combined UC > 1.0.
implementation_notes: Do not use eval(). Use existing AST expression engine.
Run formulas F1-F16 in deterministic order.
Four development methods: BEND_ALLOWANCE_SUM, BEND_DEDUCTION_SUM, K_FACTOR_PER_BEND, CAM_TABLE_VERIFIED.
For N bends: straight_segment_count = N + 1.
Total BA = sum of all individual bend allowances (K-factor or known).
Total BD = sum of all individual bend deductions.
Flat pattern (BA method) = sum straight segments + total BA.
Flat pattern (BD method) = sum outside dimensions - total BD.
Cut length = flat pattern + 2 × edge trim + kerf.
Blank area = cut length × blank width.
K-factor confidence scored 0.3-1.0 based on source.
Multi-bend uncertainty: thickness, radius, and K-factor uncertainties scale with sqrt(bend_count).
Angle uncertainty contribution scaled by total bend angle in radians.
Flange utilization = actual / required. Target >= 1.0.
Combined UC = max(tolerance_utilization, 1/flange_utilization).
All gate outputs are boolean or string. Use null for unavailable values, not Infinity.
Minimum flange length check depends on V-die geometry approximation.
Bend relief boolean affects formability warning only (not calculation).

metadata.description: Multi-bend sheet metal flat pattern development calculator per DIN 6935. Computes developed flat length, total bend allowance, total bend deduction, cut length (including trim and kerf), blank area, K-factor confidence, tolerance utilization, minimum flange length check, and combined utilization coefficient (UC) for parts with 1-12 bends.
tuv_checklist: DIN 6935 flat pattern development methodology verified
ISO 7438 minimum bend radius check included
ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM uncertainty propagation implemented
Multi-bend bend allowance sum method validated
Multi-bend bend deduction sum method validated
Combined UC with tolerance and flange constraints verified
metadata.calibration_trace: Flat pattern calculations based on DIN 6935 neutral axis model. K-factor references traceable to DIN standard tables, CAM bend table data, and physical coupon tests. Uncertainty values per ISO 80000 and ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM. Multi-bend uncertainty accumulation modeled with bend_count scaling.

tool_name: Laser Cutting Time and Assist Gas Cost Calculator — Sheet Metal Laser Process Time, Gas Consumption and Cost Check
category: Sheet Metal / Laser Cutting / Cost Engineering

inputs.name: Material Thickness
inputs.hint: Sheet or plate thickness in mm. Laser cutting range depends on laser power and material type.

inputs.name: Laser Power (at workpiece)
inputs.hint: Effective laser power delivered to the workpiece. Fiber: 1-12 kW, CO2: 0.5-6 kW. Check machine calibration log.

inputs.name: Machine Rated Laser Power
inputs.hint: Rated laser power from machine nameplate. Used for power utilization ratio calculation.

inputs.name: Programmed Cut Length
inputs.hint: Total programmed cutting path length per part from CAM system.

inputs.name: Lead-in / Lead-out Length
inputs.hint: Total lead-in and lead-out length per contour. Typically 1-5 mm per pierce.

inputs.name: Average Programmed Feed Rate
inputs.hint: Average cutting feed rate from CAM system for the programmed cutting path. Fiber laser mild steel 1mm: ~6000-12000 mm/min; 6mm: ~2000-4000 mm/min.

inputs.name: Feed Override Factor
inputs.hint: Actual feed rate as fraction of programmed. Operator override: 0.8-1.2 typical. Acceleration-limited paths may reduce effective feed.

inputs.name: Contour Complexity Factor
inputs.hint: Factor compensating for acceleration/deceleration losses on complex contours. Simple rectangles: 1.0; tight radii < 2mm: 1.3-1.5; high-detail with many small features: 2.0-3.0.

inputs.name: Number of Pierces
inputs.hint: Total number of pierce operations per part. Each closed contour typically requires one pierce + lead-in.

inputs.name: Average Pierce Time
inputs.hint: Average time per pierce operation. Fiber laser mild steel 1mm: 0.3-0.5 s; 6mm: 1-3 s; 12mm: 3-8 s. Pulsed piercing is faster than ramp piercing.
inputs.uncertainty_source: Machine OEM cutting database — pierce time variability

inputs.name: Rapid Traverse Distance
inputs.hint: Total rapid traverse (positioning) distance per part between cutting operations.

inputs.name: Average Rapid Traverse Speed
inputs.hint: Average rapid traverse speed. Typical: 30000-60000 mm/min for mid-range; up to 120000 mm/min for high-speed gantry.

inputs.name: Auxiliary Time per Part
inputs.hint: Additional time per part for loading/unloading, part separation (micro-joints), marking, and inspection.

inputs.name: Setup Time
inputs.hint: Job setup time including sheet loading, program selection, nozzle check, focus calibration, and gas purge.

inputs.name: Measured Cutting Gas Flow
inputs.hint: Directly measured gas flow rate at nozzle in normal cubic meters per hour. Required when gas_flow_mode = MEASURED_FLOW.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 5167-1:2003 — Flow measurement uncertainty ±2% for calibrated thermal mass flow meter

inputs.name: Nozzle Diameter
inputs.hint: Cutting nozzle orifice diameter. Typical: 1.0-3.0 mm for sheet metal laser cutting.

inputs.name: Assist Gas Pressure
inputs.hint: Assist gas pressure at nozzle inlet. Nitrogen: 6-20 bar; Oxygen: 0.5-6 bar; Compressed air: 4-10 bar.
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Pressure transducer class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Calibrated Flow Coefficient (Cv_nozzle)
inputs.hint: Empirical nozzle flow coefficient for CALIBRATED_NOZZLE_PRESSURE_ESTIMATE mode. Typical range: 0.1-1.5 for conical nozzles. Derived from OEM nozzle flow characterization.
inputs.uncertainty_source: Nozzle flow calibration — empirical coefficient uncertainty

inputs.name: Pierce Gas Flow Factor
inputs.hint: Gas flow multiplier during piercing vs. steady-state cutting. Pulsed piercing at high pressure: 2-4x. Ramp piercing: 1-2x.

inputs.name: Assist Gas Unit Cost
inputs.hint: Cost per normal cubic meter of assist gas. Nitrogen (liquid): 0.10-0.50 USD/Nm³; Oxygen: 0.15-0.60 USD/Nm³; Argon: 1.0-5.0 USD/Nm³; Compressed air: 0.02-0.10 USD/Nm³.

inputs.name: Machine Hour Rate
inputs.hint: Fully loaded machine hour rate including depreciation, maintenance, floor space, labor, and overhead. Typical laser cutting: 50-200 USD/h.
inputs.uncertainty_source: Cost accounting — machine hour rate estimate

inputs.name: Average Machine Electrical Power (cutting)
inputs.hint: Average electrical power consumption during cutting (including chiller, exhaust, drives, CNC, and laser power supply). Fiber 4kW: ~15-25 kW total; CO2 4kW: ~30-50 kW total.
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61000-4-30:2015 — Power measurement uncertainty Class A: ±0.5% (system-level: ±10% including auxiliary loads)

inputs.name: Standby Electrical Power
inputs.hint: Electrical power consumption in standby/idle mode (laser off, drives idle, chiller standby). Typical: 3-10 kW for fiber laser systems.

inputs.name: Electricity Unit Cost
inputs.hint: Industrial electricity cost per kWh including demand charges, taxes, and surcharges. Typical: 0.08-0.25 USD/kWh.

inputs.name: Consumable Cost per Pierce
inputs.hint: Consumable cost attributed per pierce (nozzle wear, lens contamination, protective window degradation). Approx: 0.001-0.05 USD/pierce for fiber laser.

inputs.name: Consumable Cost per Cut Meter
inputs.hint: Consumable cost per meter of cut (protective window, nozzle wear, focus lens degradation). Approx: 0.01-0.10 USD/m.

inputs.name: Scrap / Recut Allowance Factor
inputs.hint: Factor to account for scrap parts requiring re-cutting and process inefficiency. 1.0 = no scrap; 1.05 = 5% scrap rate typical.

laser_safety_required.error_msg: Laser safety verification (IEC 60825-1) is required before proceeding. Safety audit must confirm laser enclosure, interlocks, and beam path shielding.

machine_parameters_required.error_msg: Machine parameter table must be verified against current machine setup.

smart_warnings.message: Low power utilization ratio (< 40%): Laser is operating well below rated power. Consider using a lower-rated laser machine for better energy efficiency or verify that the cutting parameters are optimal for the material/thickness.
Effective feed rate < 100 mm/min: Extremely slow cutting. Verify material thickness and laser power combination. High risk of excessive heat input, dross formation, and wide kerf.
Total production time exceeds 8 hours: Job spans multiple shifts. Verify machine availability and shift planning. Consider increasing shift utilization for cost efficiency.

Argon assist gas cost > 2 USD/Nm³: Argon is significantly more expensive than nitrogen or compressed air. For laser cutting applications, verify that argon is required (e.g., titanium cutting) rather than a less expensive alternative.
Gas cost exceeds 30% of total job cost: Assist gas consumption is a dominant cost driver. Review gas flow rate, nozzle condition, and consider alternative gas types or lower pressure settings.

Contour complexity factor > 2.0: High complexity factor indicates significant acceleration/deceleration losses. Consider optimizing part geometry for fewer tight radii or evaluating if CAM feed rate optimization could reduce effective complexity.
Scrap/recut allowance > 15%: High scrap rate indicates process instability or quality issues. Review cutting parameters, material quality, nozzle condition, and machine calibration before production.

ISO 9013 Fine quality required for material > 6mm: Achieving fine quality (Rz ≤ 40µm for thin sheet) becomes increasingly difficult above 6mm. Verify that achievable with current laser power and gas selection. Consider reducing quality class for thicker materials.

error_propagation.method: GUM ISO/IEC Guide 98-3:2008 — LPU first-order Taylor expansion

reference_code.validation: Zod schema. Fail closed on zero/negative feed rate, zero rated power, negative costs, unverified safety gate.
reference_code.testing: Jest cases: thin sheet fiber cutting time (1mm mild steel), thick plate CO2 cutting time (12mm), nitrogen vs oxygen gas cost comparison, scrap allowance effect on total cost, OEM nozzle table vs measured flow comparison, safety gate block on unverified, power utilization low warning.
implementation_notes: Do not use eval(). Use existing AST expression engine.
Run formulas F1-F28 in deterministic order.
All time values in minutes internally; convert hours for display.
Gas flow rate precedence: MEASURED_FLOW > OEM_NOZZLE_TABLE > CALIBRATED_NOZZLE_PRESSURE_ESTIMATE.
Power utilization ratio is a diagnostic KPIs only — does not affect calculations.
Machine cost, gas cost, energy cost, and consumable cost summed for total job cost.
Scrap/recut allowance factor applies to all variable elements (time, gas, consumables).
All gate outputs are boolean. Use null for unavailable values, not Infinity.
Uncertainty propagation: combine time, gas flow, and cost uncertainties via RSS for each cost component.

metadata.description: Laser Cutting Time and Assist Gas Cost Calculator — Sheet Metal Laser Process Time, Gas Consumption and Cost Check per ISO 9013, ISO 11145, and ISO 14955-1. Computes effective feed rates, cycle times, assist gas consumption (via measured flow, OEM nozzle table, or calibrated pressure estimate), machine cost, energy cost, consumable cost, and total job cost for sheet metal laser cutting operations.
tuv_checklist: ISO 9013 thermal cutting quality classification confirmed
ISO 11145 laser gas flow measurement specification verified
ISO 14955-1 machine tool energy efficiency methodology confirmed
ISO 11553-1 laser processing machine safety audit reviewed
IEC 60825-1 laser product safety verification confirmed
Machine OEM cutting database parameter correlation checked
Power utilization ratio assessed per ISO 14955-1
Cost uncertainty propagation per ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM verified
metadata.calibration_trace: Feed rate and time references from CAM system and machine control logs. Gas flow calibration traceable to thermal mass flow meter standards (ISO 5167-1). Pressure transducer calibration per IEC 61298-2 Class 1.0. Uncertainty propagation per ISO/IEC Guide 98-3:2008 GUM.

tool_name: Plasma Cutting Cost and Kerf Compensation Calculator — Thermal Cutting Time, Gas, Consumable and Offset Check
category: Sheet Metal / Plasma Cutting / Cost Engineering

inputs.name: Material Thickness
inputs.hint: Plate thickness in mm. Plasma cutting range: 0.5-150 mm depending on system type and power.

inputs.name: Nominal Part Dimension
inputs.hint: Nominal design dimension of the part in the direction relevant for kerf compensation evaluation. Used for tolerance check.

inputs.name: Programmed Cut Length
inputs.hint: Total programmed cutting path length per part from CAM system.

inputs.name: Lead-in / Lead-out Length
inputs.hint: Total lead-in and lead-out length per contour. Typically 3-10 mm per pierce for plasma.

inputs.name: Number of Pierces
inputs.hint: Total number of pierce operations per part. Each closed contour typically requires one pierce.

inputs.name: Average Cut Speed
inputs.hint: Average cutting speed from CAM cutting table. HD plasma mild steel 6mm: ~3000-5000 mm/min; 12mm: ~1500-2500 mm/min; 25mm: ~700-1200 mm/min.
inputs.uncertainty_source: CAM system — cutting speed definition per ISO 9013

inputs.name: Speed Override Factor
inputs.hint: Actual cut speed as fraction of programmed. Operator override typically 0.8-1.1.

inputs.name: Contour Complexity Factor
inputs.hint: Factor compensating for acceleration/deceleration losses on complex contours. Simple rectangles: 1.0; tight radii: 1.3-1.5; high-detail: 2.0-3.0.

inputs.name: Average Pierce Time
inputs.hint: Average time per pierce operation. HD plasma mild steel 6mm: 0.5-1.5 s; 12mm: 1.5-3 s; 25mm: 3-8 s.
inputs.uncertainty_source: Machine OEM cutting database — pierce time variability

inputs.name: Rapid Traverse Distance
inputs.hint: Total rapid traverse distance per part between cutting operations.

inputs.name: Average Rapid Traverse Speed
inputs.hint: Average rapid traverse speed. Typical plasma gantry: 20000-60000 mm/min.

inputs.name: Setup Time
inputs.hint: Job setup time including plate loading, program selection, torch height calibration, gas purge, and consumable inspection.

inputs.name: Auxiliary Time per Part
inputs.hint: Additional time per part for unloading, slag removal, marking, and inspection.

inputs.name: Measured Kerf Width
inputs.hint: Measured kerf width from cut test piece. HD plasma: 1.0-2.0 mm (12mm plate); air plasma: 2.0-5.0 mm. Use calibrated optical measurement.

inputs.name: Target Dimension Tolerance
inputs.hint: Required dimensional tolerance for the part. ISO 9013: Fine quality ±0.5mm; Medium ±1.0mm; Rough ±2.0mm.

inputs.name: Arc Current (I)
inputs.hint: Plasma arc current from power supply setting. HD plasma: 50-400 A typical; air plasma: 20-100 A.
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60974-1 — Welding/plasma power source current accuracy ±1% of setting

inputs.name: Arc Voltage (U)
inputs.hint: Plasma arc voltage. Typically 100-200 V for HD plasma cutting. Higher voltage = thicker material capacity.

inputs.name: Power Supply Efficiency
inputs.hint: Power supply efficiency factor. Modern switching power supplies: 0.85-0.94; older transformer-based: 0.70-0.80.
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 60974-1 §8.3 — Power supply efficiency measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Standby Power Consumption
inputs.hint: Power supply consumption in standby/idle (fans, control circuits, gas purge). Typical: 0.5-2 kW.

inputs.name: Electricity Unit Cost
inputs.hint: Industrial electricity cost per kWh including demand charges and taxes. Typical: 0.08-0.25 USD/kWh.

inputs.name: Plasma Gas Flow Rate
inputs.hint: Plasma gas flow rate at nozzle in normal cubic meters per hour. Compressed air: 2-10 Nm³/h; Oxygen: 2-8 Nm³/h; Argon/Hydrogen: 1-5 Nm³/h.

inputs.name: Shield Gas Flow Rate
inputs.hint: Shield gas flow rate in normal cubic meters per hour. Set to 0 if no shield gas is used.

inputs.name: Plasma Gas Unit Cost
inputs.hint: Cost per normal cubic meter of plasma gas. Compressed air: 0.02-0.10 USD/Nm³; Oxygen: 0.15-0.60 USD/Nm³; Argon/Hydrogen (H35): 2-15 USD/Nm³.

inputs.name: Shield Gas Unit Cost
inputs.hint: Cost per normal cubic meter of shield gas. Set to 0 if no shield gas is used.

inputs.name: Electrode Life
inputs.hint: Expected electrode life in number of pierces before replacement. HD plasma: 500-2000 pierces; standard plasma: 200-1000 pierces.

inputs.name: Nozzle Life
inputs.hint: Expected nozzle life in total cut minutes before replacement. HD plasma: 60-300 min; standard plasma: 30-120 min.

inputs.name: Electrode Unit Cost
inputs.hint: Cost per electrode replacement. HD plasma electrode: 15-50 USD; standard: 5-20 USD.

inputs.name: Nozzle Unit Cost
inputs.hint: Cost per nozzle replacement. HD plasma nozzle: 20-80 USD; standard: 5-30 USD.

inputs.name: Additional Consumable Cost per Cut Meter
inputs.hint: Additional consumable cost per meter of cut (swirl ring, retaining cap, shield). Approx: 0.02-0.15 USD/m.

inputs.name: Machine Hour Rate
inputs.hint: Fully loaded machine hour rate including depreciation, maintenance, floor space, labor, and overhead. Typical plasma cutting: 40-150 USD/h.
inputs.uncertainty_source: Cost accounting — machine hour rate estimate

inputs.name: Scrap / Recut Allowance Factor
inputs.hint: Factor to account for scrap parts. 1.0 = no scrap; 1.05 = 5% scrap rate typical for plasma cutting.

kerf_width_positive.error_msg: Measured kerf width must be positive.

tolerance_positive.error_msg: Target dimension tolerance must be positive.

efficiency_valid.error_msg: Power supply efficiency must be between > 0 and ≤ 1 (0-100%).

consumable_life_valid.error_msg: Electrode life and nozzle life must be positive values.

plasma_gas_flow_positive.error_msg: Plasma gas flow rate must be positive.

plasma_gas_cost_positive.error_msg: Plasma gas unit cost must be positive.

electricity_cost_positive.error_msg: Electricity unit cost must be positive.

plasma_safety_required.error_msg: Plasma safety verification (IEC 60974-1 / ISO 11553-1) is required. Must confirm arc flash protection, fume extraction, noise protection, and fire safety.

thc_required.error_msg: Torch height control (THC) verification is required. THC calibration and operational check must be confirmed for consistent cut quality.

machine_parameters_required.error_msg: Machine parameter table must be verified against current machine setup.

kerf_compensation_side_required.error_msg: Kerf compensation is enabled but the compensation side (left/right of path) has not been verified. Verify CAM toolpath compensation direction to avoid scrapped parts.

smart_warnings.message: KERF COMPENSATION FAILS TOLERANCE CHECK: The kerf measurement uncertainty exceeds the allowable limit (≤ tolerance/4). Dimensional accuracy cannot be guaranteed. Improve kerf measurement accuracy or revise tolerance requirement.

DIMENSIONAL ACCURACY MARGIN NEGATIVE: The sum of kerf offset and measurement uncertainty exceeds the target tolerance. Parts will likely be out of tolerance. Review kerf compensation settings, reduce kerf width, or relax tolerance.

Kerf compensation is disabled: Parts will be undersized by approximately one kerf width (2 × offset). For outside profiles, parts will be smaller than nominal. Enable kerf compensation with verified side and measured kerf data.
Total production time exceeds 8 hours: Job spans multiple shifts. Verify machine availability and shift planning.
Gas cost exceeds 30% of total job cost: Review plasma and shield gas flow rates. Consider lower-cost gas alternatives where applicable.
Active power consumption > 50 kW: High-powered plasma system. Verify electrical supply capacity and cooling system adequacy. Consider demand charge implications.
Scrap/recut allowance > 15%: High scrap rate indicates process instability. Check consumable condition, gas quality, THC function, and material condition before production.
Consumable cost exceeds 20% of total job cost: Electrode/nozzle consumption is a significant cost driver. Verify consumable life data and consider higher-quality consumables for extended life.

Electrode life < 300 pierces: Lower than expected for most plasma systems. Verify gas purity (water content degrades electrode life), check for cooling issues, and review manufacturer life specifications.

ISO 9013 Fine quality required for material > 25mm: Fine tolerances on thick plate plasma cutting are challenging. Verify that current system and parameters can achieve the required quality. Consider if torch height control and gas quality are optimal.

error_propagation.method: GUM ISO/IEC Guide 98-3:2008 — LPU first-order Taylor expansion

reference_code.validation: Zod schema. Fail closed on zero/negative cut speed, zero kerf, zero tolerance, invalid efficiency, unverified safety/THC gates.
reference_code.testing: Jest cases: HD plasma 12mm mild steel time & cost, air plasma thin sheet cost comparison, kerf compensation PASS/FAIL check, plasma vs shield gas cost split, consumable life uncertainty effect on total cost, THC gate block on unverified, safety block on unverified, kerf compensation side verification block, scrap allowance effect on total cost.
implementation_notes: Do not use eval(). Use existing AST expression engine.
Run formulas F1-F36 in deterministic order.
All time values in minutes internally; convert hours for display.
Arc power = I × U (DC assumed). Active power = arc power / efficiency.
Plasma gas and shield gas consumption calculated separately and summed.
Electrode cost proportional to pierce count; nozzle cost proportional to cut minutes.
Kerf compensation offset = kerf_width / 2 (one side).
Effective offset = 0 when kerf_compensation_enabled is false.
Kerf compensation PASS check: enabled + side verified + uncertainty ≤ tolerance/4.
Dimensional accuracy margin = tolerance - 2 * (offset + uncertainty).
All gate outputs are boolean. Use null for unavailable values, not Infinity.
Uncertainty propagation: RSS combine time, gas flow, consumable life, and cost rate uncertainties.

metadata.description: Plasma Cutting Cost and Kerf Compensation Calculator — Thermal Cutting Time, Gas, Consumable and Offset Check per ISO 9013, ISO 17658, and IEC 60974-1. Computes cutting cycle times, arc energy consumption, plasma and shield gas costs, electrode/nozzle consumable costs, and kerf compensation accuracy check for plasma cutting operations.
tuv_checklist: ISO 9013 thermal cutting quality and tolerances classification confirmed
ISO 17658 kerf width measurement standard verified
IEC 60974-1 arc power supply efficiency and safety confirmed
IEC 60974-8 gas control system requirements reviewed
ISO 14955-1 machine tool energy efficiency methodology confirmed
Machine OEM cutting database parameter correlation checked
Plasma and shield gas consumption model verified
Electrode and nozzle life model per manufacturer data confirmed
Kerf compensation and dimensional tolerance check per ISO 9013 verified
THC calibration and plasma safety verification gates confirmed
metadata.calibration_trace: Cut speed and time references from CAM system and machine control logs. Kerf width measurement traceable to calibrated optical measurement (ISO 17658). Consumable life data from manufacturer specifications. Power measurement per IEC 60974-1. Uncertainty propagation per ISO/IEC Guide 98-3:2008 GUM.
